### feat(mechanic): spec-v660 max RPM from a piston-speed limit (mean-piston-speed inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `max-rpm-from-piston-speed` (Group K, calc-mechanic.js), the inverse of `mean-piston-speed` (spec-v324). The forward tile reads out a qualitative regime band; the engine builder's question is the reverse -- "to keep mean piston speed under 4,000 ft/min, what redline does that give my stroke?" Inverting MPS = stroke x RPM / 6 gives RPM_max = 6 x MPS_limit / stroke. First-principles; the only constant (6) is already in the sibling. The pinned example: a 3.48 in stroke at a 4,000 ft/min ceiling redlines at **6,897 rpm**; a longer 4.00 in stroke at the same ceiling caps lower at 6,000 rpm (the stroker trade). The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the RPM cap back through `computeMeanPistonSpeed` to recover the 4,000 ft/min limit, and pins the stroke and ceiling scaling. ### feat(water): spec-v659 UV required intensity or contact time (uv-dose inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `uv-required-exposure` (Group M, calc-water.js), the inverse of `uv-dose` (spec-v116). The forward tile takes both intensity and time and checks the dose; this solves the operand you are missing -- given a target dose, how long a lamp of a known intensity must expose the flow (time = dose/intensity), or how strong a lamp must be for a fixed contact time (intensity = dose/time). First-principles; the 40 mJ/cm^2 default target is already in the sibling. The pinned example: a 40 mJ/cm^2 target at 10 mW/cm^2 needs **4 s** of contact; at a fixed 8 s it needs a 5 mW/cm^2 lamp. The bounds-fuzzer proves both solve directions and the exact inverse round-trip through `computeUvDose` (the solved 4 s at 10 mW/cm^2 delivers 40 mJ/cm^2 and meets the target). ### feat(water): spec-v658 weir head from a target flow (weir-flow inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `weir-head-from-flow` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the inverse of `weir-flow` (spec-v20 M.1). The forward tile gives the flow over a weir from the head; sizing a weir box or setting a staff-gauge mark for a design flow is the reverse. Inverting the weir equations: a 90-degree V-notch Q = C H^2.48 gives H = (Q/C)^(1/2.48); a suppressed rectangular weir Q = C L H^1.5 gives H = (Q/(C L))^(2/3); a contracted rectangular weir's effective crest L - 0.2 H depends on the head, so it is solved by a few fixed-point passes seeded from the suppressed form. The coefficients (2.49, 3.33) are already in the sibling. The pinned example: a 90-degree V-notch passing 0.446 cfs needs **0.50 ft** of head. The bounds-fuzzer pins the closed-form V-notch and suppressed inverses, round-trips the contracted fixed-point through `computeWeirFlow` (1 ft / 3 ft crest), and pins the low-head flag. Full wiring: `tools-data` (Group M block), `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> weir-flow / detention-time / manning-slope), the `app.js` treatment declare, the hand-written renderer with a weir-type select, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, the fuzzer block, and the Group M citation-audit count bump (33 -> 34). Catalog 1,106 -> 1,107 (version 0.248.0).

Lands `conductivity-from-tds` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the inverse of `tds-from-conductivity` (spec-v407). The forward tile estimates TDS from a conductivity reading; this predicts the conductivity a target or permit TDS should read, or sets an EC alarm setpoint. Inverting TDS = k EC gives EC = TDS / k. ### feat(trucking): spec-v656 bridge formula minimum axle spread (bridge-formula inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `bridge-formula-min-spacing` (Group J, calc-trucking.js), the planning inverse of `bridge-formula`. The array tile reports pass/fail on axle weights and spacings; this answers the question a permit officer or heavy hauler starts with -- "I need N axles to carry W pounds, how far apart must they be?" Solving Federal Bridge Formula B W = 500 (L N/(N-1) + 12 N + 36) for the spread gives L = ((W/500) - 12 N - 36)(N-1)/N. First-principles, the 500/12/36 constants already in the sibling. The pinned example: a 5-axle group at 80,000 lb needs at least **51.2 ft** outer-to-outer; a 2-axle group at 34,000 lb needs 4 ft. The bounds-fuzzer proves the result satisfies the forward formula (feed 51.2 ft back, recover 80,000 lb), cross-checks the array `computeBridgeFormula` (a [17000,17000] pair at 4 ft has no violation), and pins the fits-at-zero and over-80,000-lb-permit flags. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.trucking + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> bridge-formula / axle-load / gcwr-check), the `app.js` trucking declare, the `_simpleRenderer` registration, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, the fuzzer block, and the Group J citation-audit count bump (18 -> 19 in citations.test.js). ### feat(hvac): spec-v655 furnace airflow to temperature rise (temp-rise inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `furnace-airflow-to-rise` (Group C, calc-hvacservice.js), the inverse of `furnace-temp-rise` (spec-v110). The forward tile is the diagnostic direction (measure supply/return temps, back out airflow); this is the setup direction -- pick a blower tap or target CFM and predict the rise you should see, before measuring. Inverting CFM = output/(1.08 dT) gives dT = output/(1.08 CFM). First-principles, no new constant. The pinned example: a 100,000 BTU/hr furnace at 80% on 1,200 CFM (70 F return) produces a **61.7 F rise** and a **131.7 F supply** -- inside the 40-70 F rating-plate band. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the airflow `furnace-temp-rise` derives (1,481.5 CFM) back and recovering the 50 F rise, and pins the rise-high (900 CFM) / rise-low (3,000 CFM) verdicts. ### feat(motor): spec-v654 motor pole count from nameplate RPM (sync-speed inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `motor-pole-identification` (Group A, calc-motor.js), the inverse of `motor-synchronous-speed-slip` (spec-v121). The forward tile requires the pole count as an input; a tech reading a nameplate has the full-load rpm, not the poles -- the pole count is the unknown. Because the synchronous speed sits just above the running speed, inverting Ns = 120 f / P identifies it: pole-pairs = round(60 f / rpm), poles = 2 x pole-pairs. First-principles deterministic rounding, no table. The pinned example: a 1750 rpm 60 Hz motor is a **4-pole** machine (Ns 1800, 2.78% slip); 1150 rpm is 6-pole, 3450 rpm is 2-pole, a 1450 rpm 50 Hz motor is 4-pole. A speed at or above the identified synchronous speed (zero/negative slip) is flagged, since an induction nameplate never shows it. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the identified 4 poles back through `computeMotorSyncSlip` to recover the 1800 rpm Ns and 2.78% slip, and pins the common-speed snaps and the over-synchronous flag. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the sync-slip / shaft-torque / FLA cluster), the `app.js` motor declare, the hand-written renderer, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,102 -> 1,103 (version 0.244.0).

### feat(agriculture): spec-v653 two-stroke mix ratio check (two-stroke-mix inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `two-stroke-mix-ratio-check` (Group L, calc-agriculture.js), the inverse of `two-stroke-mix` (spec-v35). The forward tile prescribes the oil to add for a target ratio; this verifies a mix you already made -- you poured some oil into a can of gas and want the ratio you actually achieved and whether it is safe. Inverting oil = fuel/ratio gives ratio = fuel volume / oil volume. First-principles, no new constant. The pinned example: 2.56 fl oz of oil in a US gallon is **50:1** (on spec); 3.2 fl oz is a richer **40:1** (too much oil for a 50:1 target). The tile flags the safety point: a LEAN mix (higher X:1, too little oil) starves the bearings and risks seizure -- the dangerous error -- while a RICH mix (lower X:1) only smokes and fouls. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the oil `two-stroke-mix` prescribes for 50:1 back and recovering 50:1, and pins the lean (64:1) / rich (40:1) verdicts and the liter path. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> two-stroke-mix / tank-mix / fuel-range), the `app.js` agriculture declare, the hand-written renderer with a fuel-unit select, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lands `gas-meter-clock-target` (Group C, calc-hvacservice.js), the inverse of `gas-meter-clock` (spec-v110). ### feat(concrete): spec-v651 concrete cracking moment Mcr (ACI 318-19); 2026-07-13

Lands `concrete-cracking-moment` (Group E, calc-concrete.js), the companion the `concrete-modulus-of-rupture` tile (spec-v379) names in its own note ("fr sets the cracking moment Mcr behind deflection and minimum-reinforcement checks") but never computes. Mcr is the moment a designer needs: the threshold in the effective-moment-of-inertia (Ie) deflection analysis and the ACI minimum-flexural-reinforcement check (design strength >= 1.2 Mcr). It is basic mechanics on the sibling's constant: Mcr = fr Ig/yt = fr b h^2/6 with fr = 7.5 lambda sqrt(f'c). The pinned example: a 12 x 20 in section at 4000 psi normalweight (fr 474 psi, S 800 in^3) cracks at **31.6 kip-ft**. The bounds-fuzzer pins the h^2 growth (a 24 in section cracks at 1.44x the moment) and asserts the fr it uses is identical to `computeConcreteModulusOfRupture` at the same inputs (1e-12). ### feat(shop): spec-v650 taper missing diameter, lathe setup (taper-calc inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `taper-diameter` (Group K, calc-shop.js), the inverse of `taper-calc` (spec-v40). The forward tile computes the taper per foot and angle from both end diameters; this is the actual lathe-setup problem -- you have a taper spec (TPF), one known end diameter, and the length, and need the other diameter to turn the part. Inverting TPF = 12(D-d)/L gives the missing end = known -/+ (TPF/12) x L. Pure trigonometry, no new constant. The pinned example: a 1.000 in large end with a 0.600 in/ft taper over 3 in makes a **0.850 in** small end, and the compound-slide angle per side is atan(0.600/24) = 1.432 deg -- which depends only on the TPF, so the same setting cuts the taper at any length. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the solved end diameters back through `computeTaperCalc` to recover the 0.600 in/ft TPF, and confirms the either-end solve and the TPF-only angle. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> taper-calc / sine-bar / cutting-speed), the `app.js` shop declare, the hand-written renderer with a known-end select, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. ### feat(machining): spec-v649 gear identification, pitch from teeth and OD (gear-geometry inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `gear-identification` (Group K, calc-machining.js), the inverse of `spur-gear-geometry` (spec-v401). The forward tile needs the diametral pitch Pd as an input; this is the universal shop task of identifying an *unknown* gear, where the pitch is the number you are trying to find. Inverting the sibling's OD = (N+2)/Pd gives Pd = (N+2)/OD from a counted tooth count and a measured outside diameter. Pure algebra, no table constant. The pinned example: count 40 teeth on a gear measuring 4.200 in and it identifies as **Pd 10** (pitch diameter 4.000 in, module 2.54 mm); the measured Pd is snapped to the nearest standard diametral pitch so a caliper a hair off still identifies the gear. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the identified Pd back through `computeSpurGearGeometry` to recover the measured OD, and pins the nearest-standard snap. ### feat(geotech): spec-v648 allowable load for a settlement limit (consolidation inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `settlement-limit-load` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the inverse of `soil-consolidation-settlement` (spec-v308). The forward tile gives the settlement from a stress increase; this answers the design question -- "my allowable settlement is 2 inches, what is the largest load increment the clay can take?" -- by solving the same Terzaghi relation for d_sigma: d_sigma = sigma'0 (10^(Sc(1 + e0)/(Cc H)) - 1). Every constant already lives in the sibling. The pinned example: to hold a 10 ft NC clay (Cc 0.25, e0 0.90, sigma'0 2,000 psf) to a 2 in settlement, the maximum added stress is **677 psf**; tighten the limit to 1 in and only **314 psf** is allowed -- because settlement grows with the log of the stress ratio, a tighter limit allows disproportionately less load. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the returned 677 psf back through `computeSoilConsolidationSettlement` to recover the 2 in settlement, and round-trips the settlement tile's own example (2.7804 in -> 1,000 psf). Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the settlement / consolidation-time / bearing tiles), the `app.js` geotech declare, the `_simpleRenderer` registration, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. This commit also raises two gzip caps in `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` to lock in this session's growth (the check reads dist/ and no-ops in CI where lint runs before build, so these are caught only by a local post-build lint): `calc-solar.js` 23000 -> 26000 (the spec-v647 pv-array-sizing tile took the built module to ~23.3 KB gz, 101.1% of the old cap; current + ~12% headroom) and `citations.js` 380000 -> 410000 (the 10 landed single-tile citation blocks this session took CITATIONS to ~380.1 KB gz, 100.0% of the old cap; current + ~8% headroom). Both modules are lazy-loaded and not in the home-view payload.

### feat(solar): spec-v647 PV array size from a target annual energy (PVWatts inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `pv-array-sizing` (Group A, calc-solar.js), the inverse of `pv-energy-yield` (spec-v221). The forward tile runs the NREL PVWatts model array-kW -> annual-kWh; this answers the customer's actual starting question -- "I use 12,000 kWh/yr, what size array offsets it?" -- by solving the same model for the DC nameplate: DC = target_annual_kWh / (PSH x 365 x PR). Pure algebra; the PVWatts PR default 0.77 and the E = P_dc x PSH x 365 x PR relation are already in the sibling. The pinned example: to offset 12,000 kWh/yr at 5 peak-sun-hours and a 0.77 performance ratio takes an **8.54 kW DC** array (specific yield 1,405 kWh/kWp). The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the sized array back through `computePvEnergyYield` to recover the 12,000 kWh target, and round-trips the energy tile's own desert example (14,235 kWh -> exactly 8 kW). Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 7 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the energy-yield / inverter / battery cluster), the `app.js` solar declare, the renderer registered in the SOLAR_RENDERERS map literal, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,095 -> 1,096 (version 0.237.0).

### feat(realestate): spec-v646 required face rent from a target net effective rent (inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `required-face-rent` (Group X, calc-realestate.js), the inverse of `net-effective-rent` (spec-v526). The forward tile spreads concessions to the rate a tenant pays; this answers the landlord's pricing question -- "I want to net $30/SF and I'll give 20 months free plus a TI credit, what FACE rent must I quote?" -- by solving the same straight-line spread for the face: face = (target_NER x term + one_time_credit) / (term - free_periods). Pure business algebra, no constants. The pinned example: to net $30/SF over 120 months with 20 months free and no credit, the landlord must quote a **$36.00** face (16.7% above the effective rate); a $240/SF TI credit pushes it to **$38.40**. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the returned face back through `computeNetEffectiveRent` across several term/free/credit combinations and recovering the target NER to 1e-9. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 6 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> net-effective-rent and the commercial-lease cluster), the `app.js` real-estate declare, the hand-written renderer, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,094 -> 1,095 (version 0.236.0).

### feat(geotech): spec-v645 consolidation degree from elapsed time (Terzaghi inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `consolidation-degree` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the inverse of `consolidation-time-rate` (spec-v414). The forward tile gives the time to reach a target degree of consolidation U; this answers the field/monitoring question -- "the surcharge has been down 200 days, how consolidated is it now?" -- by inverting Terzaghi's time factor: Tv = cv t / Hdr^2, then U = 100 sqrt(4 Tv/pi) for Tv <= 0.283 (U <= 60%) and 100 - 10^((1.781 - Tv)/0.933) above. Every constant already lives in the sibling, so this is a pure algebraic inversion. The pinned example: cv 0.1 ft^2/day, Hdr 10 ft, 848 days gives Tv 0.848 and **U 90%** -- exactly the sibling's own example run backwards; at 196 days the same layer is only 50% consolidated (the decelerating curve). The bounds-fuzzer round-trips U = 30/45/75/90 through the forward tile and recovers each to 1e-6 (kept off the U=60 branch seam, where Terzaghi's two-branch series has an inherent ~0.35% jump), and pins the sub-100% asymptote. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 7 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the time tile and the settlement tiles), the `app.js` geotech declare, the `_simpleRenderer` registration, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,093 -> 1,094 (version 0.235.0).

### feat(gas): spec-v644 fuel-gas pipe capacity from allowable drop (Spitzglass inverse); 2026-07-13

Lands `gas-pipe-max-flow` (Group B, calc-gas.js), the inverse of `gas-pipe-pressure-drop` (spec-v20 B.3). The forward tile gives the Spitzglass drop for a flow; this answers "the line is 1 inch and I can spend 0.5 in w.c., how much gas does it carry?" by solving the same equation for the flow: Q = 3550 sqrt(dH D^5 / (SG L K')), K' = 1 + 3.6/D + 0.03 D -- no iteration, and the constants are already in the sibling. The pinned example: a 1 in bore (ID 1.049), 100 ft, 0.60 SG natural gas at a 0.5 in w.c. allowable drop carries **173 CFH** at **480 ft/min**. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding the ~16.73 in w.c. drop the forward tile computes for 1,000 CFH back through this tile and recovering 1,000 CFH to 1e-6; it also pins the sqrt(dH) scaling and the ~1.5 psi low-pressure validity flag the forward tile carries. The tile id is deliberately `gas-pipe-max-flow`, distinct from the existing `gas-pipe-capacity.json` NFPA-table data shard. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.plumbing + editionNote + 2 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 8 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the pressure-drop / sizing / leak / demand cluster), the `app.js` gas declare, the hand-written renderer, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,092 -> 1,093 (version 0.234.0).

### feat(pipefit): spec-v643 steam main capacity from size and velocity (inverse of the steam-main sizer); 2026-07-13

Lands `steam-pipe-capacity` (Group B, calc-pipefit.js), the inverse of `steam-pipe-velocity` (spec-v158). The forward tile sizes a main from a mass flow; this answers the question a fitter on an existing plant asks -- "the main is already 2 inch, how much steam can it carry within the velocity band?" It solves the same continuity relation the other way: capacity = velocity x 60 x internal area / specific volume, with the area from the ASME B36.10M Sch 40 ID (the `_SCH40_ID_IN` table already in the module, no new constant). The pinned example: a 2 in Sch 40 main (ID 2.067) at a 6,000 ft/min ceiling and 13.7 ft^3/lb specific volume carries **612 lb/hr**. The bounds-fuzzer proves the exact inverse by feeding that capacity back through `computeSteamPipeVelocity` and re-picking the 2 in main at ~6,000 ft/min; a second cross-check confirms a 4 in main carries 2,323 lb/hr, so the sizer's "2,000 lb/hr needs 4 in" fits with margin. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 8 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the steam-main sizer, flash-steam, trap sizing, boiler pipe sizing), the `app.js` pipefit declare, the hand-written renderer (a Sch 40 size select), the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,091 -> 1,092 (version 0.233.0).

### feat(mechanic): spec-v642 hydraulic pump output flow (inverse of the motor speed relation); 2026-07-13

Lands `hydraulic-pump-flow` (Group K, calc-mechanic.js), the missing flow member of the fluid-power cluster. The cluster shipped the motor (`hydraulic-motor-torque-speed`), the cylinder (`hydraulic-cylinder-force-speed`), and the pump power sizer (`hydraulic-pump-horsepower`) -- but the last takes the delivered gpm as a given input with no tile to produce it. This computes it from displacement and speed: theoretical flow = disp x rpm / 231 (231 in^3/gal, exact, already used verbatim in the motor tile), delivered = theoretical x volumetric efficiency, the exact algebraic inverse of the motor's `rpm = 231 x gpm / disp x vol_eff`. The pinned example: a 2.0 in^3/rev pump at 1800 rpm and 0.95 volumetric efficiency displaces **15.58 gpm** but delivers **14.81 gpm**, the 0.78 gpm difference being internal slip. The bounds-fuzzer proves the inverse relationship by feeding the theoretical flow through the actual `computeHydraulicMotorTorqueSpeed` at vol_eff 1 and recovering the 1800 rpm drive speed to 1e-9. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 8 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the pump-horsepower / motor / cylinder cluster), the `app.js` mechanic declare, the `_simpleRenderer` registration, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. The delivered gpm now feeds straight into `hydraulic-pump-horsepower`. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,090 -> 1,091 (version 0.232.0).

### feat(plumbing): spec-v641 rectangular channel normal depth, Manning (the depth the Froude tile leaves out); 2026-07-13

Lands `channel-normal-depth` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), the named companion the `channel-froude-number` tile explicitly defers ("it does not compute the normal depth -- that is Manning"). It solves Manning's `Q = (1.486/n) A R^(2/3) sqrt(S)` for the rectangular normal (uniform-flow) depth by bisection (A = b y, R = A/(b + 2y) both rise monotonically with depth), then reports the velocity and Froude number at that depth and classes the slope against the critical depth `yc = (q^2/g)^(1/3)`: yn > yc is a mild slope (subcritical), yn < yc is steep. This is the second in-file root-find tile (specific-energy, spec-v637, established the pattern). The pinned example: a 10 ft rectangular channel carrying 200 cfs at n 0.015 on a 0.1% slope runs **3.82 ft deep at 5.24 ft/s, Fr 0.47**, and because that normal depth exceeds the 2.32 ft critical depth the reach is a mild slope. The bounds-fuzzer proves correctness algorithm-independently by feeding the returned depth back through Manning's equation and recovering the input 200 cfs to 1e-4, plus a cross-check that steepening the slope to 0.4% shrinks the depth to 2.34 ft. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 8 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> `channel-froude-number`, `manning-slope`, `manning-pipe-capacity`, `specific-energy`), the `app.js` plumbing declare, the inline renderer registration beside the Froude tile, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and the fuzzer block. This completes the rectangular open-channel set (Froude/regime, specific energy, hydraulic jump, and now normal depth); trapezoidal sections and backwater profiles remain out of scope by design. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,089 -> 1,090 (version 0.231.0).

### feat(plumbing): spec-v640 Manning pipe capacity, full-bore gravity flow (inverse of manning-slope); 2026-07-13

Lands `manning-pipe-capacity` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), completing the Manning pipe pair. The existing `manning-slope` tile solves `V = (1.486/n) R^(2/3) sqrt(S)` for the *slope* given a target flow (at half-full); this tile solves the same equation for the *discharge* given a fixed grade -- the full-bore capacity `Q = V A` with `R = D/4` and `A = (pi/4) D^2`, reusing the `MANNING_ROUGHNESS` table (USGS WSP-2339) the slope tile already ships, so no new constant. The pinned example: an 8 in concrete pipe (n 0.013) at a 1% grade carries **1.21 cfs (542 gpm)** flowing full at **3.46 ft/s**. Two cross-checks pin the physics: doubling the grade to 2% raises the capacity only 1.41x (`sqrt 2`, the `sqrt(S)` law), and the same pipe in PVC (n 0.009) carries 1.75 cfs (the linear `1/n` scaling). Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.plumbing + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 9 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> `manning-slope` and the open-channel / stormwater cluster), the `app.js` plumbing declare, the renderer registered in the map literal beside `renderManningSlope`, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and a bounds-fuzzer block pinning the example, the full-bore geometry, the `sqrt(S)` and `1/n` laws, and the error seams. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,088 -> 1,089 (version 0.230.0).

### feat(plumbing): spec-v639 orifice diameter for a target flow (inverse of orifice-flow); 2026-07-13

Lands `orifice-diameter-for-flow` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), the inverse of spec-v303 (`orifice-flow`). The forward tile answers "given a diameter and head, what flow"; the question a detention-pond or restrictor-plate designer actually starts with is the allowable release rate, and they need the diameter that delivers it. Solving the same orifice equation `Q = Cd A sqrt(2 g h)` for the area `A = Q / (Cd sqrt(2 g h))` and then `d = sqrt(4 A / pi)` closes the pair with pure algebra and no new constant. The pinned example: releasing 1.5 cfs under a 4 ft head at Cd 0.60 wants a **5.34 in** orifice (0.156 ft^2). The cross-check pins an **exact round-trip** against the forward tile -- the `orifice-flow` pinned example (6 in, 4 ft, Cd 0.60) discharges 1.8908... cfs, and feeding that flow back sizes the orifice to 6.00 in exactly. A notable property the tile surfaces: because the flow scales with `sqrt(h)`, the required area scales as `1/sqrt(h)`, so quadrupling the head shrinks the diameter to only 0.71x, not 0.5x. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples (the pinned release and the round-trip), 9 collision-checked aliases plus the existing "size an orifice to release a set cfs" question **repointed** from the forward tile to this dedicated inverse, related-tiles (<-> `orifice-flow` and the `tank-drain-time` / site-hydraulics cluster), the `app.js` plumbing declare, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and a bounds-fuzzer block pinning the example, the exact round-trip, the `1/sqrt(h)` area law, and the error seams. With `tank-drain-time` (spec-v630) the orifice family now answers "how fast," "how long," and "how big." Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,087 -> 1,088 (version 0.229.0).

### fix(shells): cap group-shell descriptions on escaped length too; 2026-07-13

Hardens the second half of the escaped-length class the `<title>` fix above closed. `groupShell` in `scripts/build-shells.mjs` capped its `<meta name="description">` against the **raw** string length (`if (description.length > 220) ... slice(0, 217)`), but the shell renders it through `escapeHtml` and `check-shells` measures the escaped `<meta content>` -- the same raw-vs-escaped mismatch that broke `main` on the tile-title path. The sibling `metaDescription` (tile shells) was already escape-aware; the group path was missed. This was not theoretical: the `accounting` group's escaped description sits at **exactly 220**, the cap, with zero headroom, so a single-character change to its first three sample tile names (or any name gaining an apostrophe/ampersand) would have pushed the rendered description past 220 and turned CI red. Fixed by shaving against `escapeHtml(description).length` in a loop, mirroring `metaDescription` and `buildTitle`. Zero output change today (all 21 group descriptions already render at &lt;= 220 escaped); a pure robustness fix. Build + check-shells + check-dist + check-shell-mobile all green. Version 0.228.0 -> 0.228.1.

### feat(civil): spec-v638 sag vertical curve comfort and drainage criteria (AASHTO); 2026-07-13

Lands `sag-vertical-curve-comfort` (Group E, calc-civil.js), closing the AASHTO sag-curve control set that spec-v337 (crest SSD) and spec-v636 (`sag-vertical-curve`, headlight SSD) opened. The headlight tile's own citation named the two remaining controls as "separate checks" and closed by leaving them "for a future tile": the **comfort** criterion `L = A V^2 / 46.5` (A the algebraic grade difference in %, V the design speed in mph, L in ft) is the length that holds the vertical acceleration on the sag to about 1 ft/s^2, giving `K = L/A = V^2/46.5`; the **drainage** maximum `K <= 167` bounds it from the other side (a 0.30% minimum grade within 50 ft of the low point, `50/0.30 = 166.7`), so the drainage-max length is `167 A`. The pinned example: a 4% break at 60 mph wants at least a **310 ft** curve (K **77.4**) for comfort and no more than **668 ft** for drainage; a notable property the tile surfaces is that the comfort K depends only on the design speed (`V^2/46.5`), not on the grade break, and only an unrealistic design speed above ~88 mph would push the comfort length past the drainage maximum. Both constants were web-verified against the AASHTO Green Book and multiple state DOT design manuals (TxDOT, Iowa SUDAS, MoDOT, IDOT), not just the sibling spec's note. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 machine-verified worked examples, 10 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (<-> the headlight sibling and the crest/SSD cluster), the `app.js` civil declare, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + citation-strings + derivations, and a bounds-fuzzer block pinning both examples, the V-only K dependence, the drainage flag, and the error seams. Lazy-loaded, absent from home first paint. Catalog 1,086 -> 1,087 (version 0.228.0).

### fix(shells): count the escaped &lt;title&gt; length against the 70-char cap; 2026-07-13

Unbreaks `main`: the `masonry-prism-fm` landing (0.227.0) turned the `integration` job red at the `check-shells` gate -- `tools/masonry-prism-fm/index.html: <title> length 74 exceeds 70 cap`. `buildTitle` (in `scripts/build-shells.mjs`) sized every title against its **raw** character count, but the shell renders the title through `escapeHtml` and `check-shells` measures that escaped text. `masonry-prism-fm` is the first tile whose name carries an apostrophe (`f'm`) *and* is long enough to hit the truncation fallback, so the `'` -> `&#39;` expansion (+4 chars) pushed the rendered title to 74 while `buildTitle` still thought it was 70. Fixed by measuring against `escapeHtml(...).length` at every branch and growing the truncated name one character at a time so an escapable char can never slip the rendered title past the cap. The masonry title now renders at exactly 70 (`Masonry Compressive Strength f&#39;m, Unit-Strength M... - Rough Logic`); `npm run build` + `check-shells` + `check-shell-mobile` + `check-dist` all green. No catalog change (1,086); version 0.227.0 -> 0.227.1.

### feat(masonry): spec-v551 masonry compressive strength f'm, unit-strength method (TMS 602); 2026-07-12

Lands `masonry-prism-fm` (Group E, calc-masonry.js), the last unbuilt spec in the masonry bench and a genuine functional hole: every CMU wall tile (`cmu-wall-axial`, `cmu-wall-flexure`, `cmu-shear-wall`, `masonry-anchor-bolt`) *consumes* the net-area masonry compressive strength f'm, but until now none *derived* it. Enter the net-area unit strength and the mortar type; get the f'm the TMS 602-16 Table 2 unit-strength method assigns, no prism test. The point the tile exists to make: f'm is **not** the unit strength, and the mortar type moves it -- a 2,000 psi net-area concrete unit gives f'm 2,000 psi in Type M or S mortar but only **1,750** in Type N (a 12.5% cut from the mortar alone, the pinned example + cross-check), and a 3,250 psi unit in Type M/S gives 2,500 (77% of the block). Table 2 (concrete masonry) is stored as the verified rows -- Type M/S 2000/2600/3250/3900/4500 -> 2000/2250/2500/2750/3000; Type N 2000/2650/3400/4350 -> 1750/2000/2250/2500 -- and interpolated linearly (TMS-permitted); 2,000 psi is the ASTM C90 net-area minimum (a weaker unit is flagged below-range) and the table caps design f'm (a stronger unit is capped, a note points at the prism test). The clay-masonry branch stays a future follow-on (clay selection returns an error). Table values cross-checked against CMHA/NCMA TEK notes and state-masonry-association bulletins, not just self-consistency. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general + editionNote + 3 assumptions), compute-map, 2 worked-example rows (Type S + Type N), 8 collision-checked aliases, related-tiles (-> the four consumers), the `app.js` masonry declare, the `// dims:` annotation, regenerated corpus + tile-index + derivations, and a bounds-fuzzer block pinning the Table 2 rows, the interpolation, the Type N reduction, the below/above-range flags, and the error seams. The tile tipped `calc-masonry.js` to ~9.56 KB gzipped, so its `check-module-sizes` cap is raised 9,000 -> 11,000 B (~15% headroom); the module stays lazy-loaded and out of the home-view payload. Catalog 1,085 -> 1,086 (version 0.227.0).

The prior drift-proofing entry (below) moved the home count to "1,000+" precisely because nothing pinned it; this reverses that trade by *pinning* it instead -- `check-readme-counts` now also asserts both "&lt;N&gt; free calculators for" strings in `index.html` equal the live tile count derived from `tools-data.js`, so a landing that forgets to bump the home view fails `npm run lint` rather than shipping a stale figure. ### docs: trim the home browser-tab title and meta description to "Rough Logic"; 2026-07-12

Sets the home view's `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` -- plus their Open Graph / Twitter mirrors in `index.html`, and the `HOME_TITLE` / `HOME_DESC` constants the SPA (`app.js`) swaps in on the home route -- to the bare wordmark **"Rough Logic"**, replacing the former count-bearing marketing string. That string had also drifted between the two sources: `index.html` read "1,000+" (bumped by the count drift-proofing entry below) while `app.js` still read the stale "600+" frozen since an early landing, so a phone opening the SPA home saw a different tab title than a crawler reading the static shell. ### feat(plumbing): spec-v637 open-channel specific energy and alternate depth; 2026-07-12

Lands `specific-energy` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), the specific-energy diagram that `channel-froude-number` (spec-v304) and `hydraulic-jump` (spec-v632) both named as a deliberate follow-on. Enter the width, discharge, and a flow depth; get the specific energy E = y + q^2/(2 g y^2), the critical depth yc = (q^2/g)^(1/3), the minimum specific energy Ec = 1.5 yc, the regime, and the alternate depth that carries the same energy. For one discharge the same E occurs at two depths - a deep subcritical and a shallow supercritical - meeting at the critical depth where E bottoms out; this energy conjugate is physically distinct from the momentum sequent depth of a jump (which dissipates energy). A 10 ft channel at 100 cfs and a 3 ft depth carries 3.17 ft of energy (Fr 0.34); the same energy also passes at 0.81 ft (supercritical), and the minimum is 2.19 ft at the 1.46 ft critical depth. Feeding the alternate depth back returns the original depth exactly (fuzzer pins the round-trip and the Ec = 1.5 yc identity). Pure open-channel algebra, only universal g. Completes the open-channel trio. Catalog 1,084 -> 1,085 (version 0.226.0).

### docs: drift-proof the home-view catalog count and correct stale internal-doc figures; 2026-07-12

Follows the README/MCP count-drift cleanup below. `index.html` still advertised **942** calculators in its title, meta description, Open Graph / Twitter cards, JSON-LD, and the home lede - frozen since spec-v488 while the catalog grew past 1,080. Switches all eight spots to the drift-proof **1,000+** phrasing the README and `mcp/README.md` now use, so the home view never rots on a landing again. No source or catalog change.

### docs: README overhaul + MCP catalog-count drift fix; 2026-07-12

Rewrites `README.md` from a short overview into a full, drift-safe project reference: the 21-bench catalog map, a representative-calculator cheat sheet with the formula each tile is grounded in, a Mermaid architecture diagram (client-side runtime + build-time data/shell pipeline), the correctness-as-a-build-gate story (30+ static gates plus the four-job CI pipeline), and a design-decisions table. Exact tile/URL counts are kept out of the prose so it does not drift on every landing; the one count it does state (`56 per-group calculator modules`) is the gate-verified stable value. Also fixes stale catalog counts in `mcp/README.md` (`929` -> `1,000+`, made count-free so it stops rotting). No source or catalog change; version unchanged.

### feat(civil): spec-v636 sag vertical curve minimum length (AASHTO); 2026-07-11

Lands `sag-vertical-curve` (Group E, calc-civil.js), the sag (valley) companion the crest tile `vertical-curve-sight-distance` (spec-v337) explicitly hands off ("sag curves use headlight/comfort/drainage criteria"). A sag curve is limited at night by headlight reach: L = A S^2/(400 + 3.5 S) for S<=L, L = 2 S - (400 + 3.5 S)/A for S>L, K = L/A (400 and 3.5 embed the 2.0 ft headlight height and 1-degree beam divergence, the same class of Green Book constant the crest tile bakes into C = 2158). A 4% grade break needing 400 ft SSD wants a 350 ft sag curve (K 87.5). Headlight-SSD control; comfort and drainage are separate. Catalog 1,083 -> 1,084 (version 0.225.0).

### feat(lab): spec-v635 substrate for a target fraction of Vmax (Michaelis-Menten inverse); 2026-07-11

Lands `substrate-for-velocity` (Group T, calc-lab.js), the assay-design inverse of `michaelis-menten`. Enter Km and the fraction of Vmax you want, get the substrate concentration: [S] = Km x f/(1 - f). Half of Vmax takes exactly one Km (its definition), but 90% takes 9 x Km and 99% takes 99 x Km - the runaway excess that shows why an enzyme approaches Vmax but never reaches it. Feeding the result back into the forward tile returns the target fraction exactly (fuzzer pins the round-trip and the [S]=Km-at-50% identity). Pure algebra, no constants. Catalog 1,082 -> 1,083 (version 0.224.0).

### feat(structural): spec-v634 required plastic section modulus for a steel beam; 2026-07-11

Lands `required-section-modulus` (Group E, calc-steel.js), the design inverse of `steel-beam-flexure` (spec-v314). Enter the demand moment and Fy, get the plastic section modulus Zx to pick a W-shape: LRFD Zx >= 12 Mu/(0.90 Fy); ASD Zx >= 12(1.67)Ma/Fy (AISC 360 Ch. F, Mp = Fy Zx, inverted). A 200 kip-ft LRFD demand on 50 ksi steel needs Zx 53.3 in^3 (a W16x31 works); by ASD it jumps to 80.2. Feeding the result back into the forward tile returns the demand exactly (fuzzer pins the round-trip). Pure algebra; phi_b = 0.90 and Omega = 1.67 are the same universal factors the forward tile hardcodes. Catalog 1,081 -> 1,082 (version 0.223.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v633 isolator static deflection for a target isolation efficiency; 2026-07-11

Lands `isolator-deflection` (Group C, calc-hvac.js), the mount-selection inverse of `vibration-isolation` (spec-v483). Enter the running speed and the isolation efficiency you want, get the static deflection to specify: T = 1 - efficiency, ratio = sqrt(1 + 1/T), fn = (rpm/60)/ratio, deflection = (3.13/fn)^2 in. 90% isolation of a 900 rpm fan needs 0.48 in (a 4.5 Hz mount); tighten to 95% and it nearly doubles to 0.91 in. Feeding the result back into the forward tile returns the target efficiency exactly (fuzzer pins the round-trip). Pure single-DOF algebra, same universal constant (3.13 = (1/2pi)sqrt(g)) as the forward tile. Catalog 1,080 -> 1,081 (version 0.222.0).

### feat(plumbing): spec-v632 hydraulic jump sequent depth and energy loss; 2026-07-11

Lands `hydraulic-jump` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), the conjugate-depth follow-on spec-v304 named beside `channel-froude-number`. Belanger's momentum relation gives the sequent depth y2 = (y1/2)(sqrt(1 + 8 Fr1^2) - 1) and the specific-energy loss dE = (y2 - y1)^3/(4 y1 y2). A 10 ft channel at 100 cfs and a shooting 0.8 ft depth (Fr1 2.46) jumps to 2.42 ft and throws away 0.55 ft of head; at 200 cfs (Fr1 4.93) it jumps to 5.19 ft killing 5.1 ft - why a stilling basin is sized to the Froude number. Pure Belanger momentum, universal g, no proprietary constants. Catalog 1,079 -> 1,080 (version 0.221.0).

### feat(survey): spec-v631 level-loop misclosure distribution (compass rule); 2026-07-11

Lands `level-loop-adjustment` (Group P, calc-survey.js), the adjustment-distribution follow-on spec-v311 named inside `differential-leveling` ("a proportional adjustment is a follow-on"). Correction to each turning point = -misclosure x (distance leveled to it / total distance) - the vertical analog of the compass rule the `traverse-closure` tile applies to horizontal work. A three-leg loop closing 0.05 ft high over 2,000 ft puts -0.0125 ft on the first point at 500 ft and the full -0.05 ft on the last, so the adjusted elevations land exactly on the known benchmark. Pure distance-weighted arithmetic, no constants. Raises the calc-survey.js gzip cap 8,000 -> 9,000 (array in/out renderer). Catalog 1,078 -> 1,079 (version 0.220.0).

### feat(plumbing): spec-v630 tank drain time (falling-head orifice); 2026-07-11

Lands `tank-drain-time` (Group B, calc-plumbing.js), the falling-head follow-on spec-v303 named inside `orifice-flow` ("the time-to-drain is a follow-on"). Integrating the orifice equation over the falling head of a prismatic tank gives t = 2 A_t (sqrt(h1) - sqrt(h2)) / (Cd A_o sqrt(2 g)), g = 32.2 ft/s^2. A 100 ft^2 tank draining a 6 in orifice empties from 9 ft in 10.6 min, but reaches the last foot in only 7.1 - the flow slows as sqrt(h), so the final foot alone takes another 3.5. Pure Torricelli integration; g universal and Cd the same user-entered coefficient orifice-flow uses. Catalog 1,077 -> 1,078 (version 0.219.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v629 pump suction specific speed (Nss); 2026-07-11

Lands `pump-suction-specific-speed` (Group C, calc-hvac.js), the suction-side companion spec-v307 named as separate from `pump-specific-speed`. Nss = N sqrt(Q) / NPSHr^(3/4) - the same definitional form as Ns with required NPSH in place of head - indexes how hard a pump works its suction. The Hydraulic Institute guideline caps Nss near 8,500 (and ~11,000 marks a reliability concern). A 1,750 rpm, 2,000 gpm pump with 25 ft NPSHr sits at a safe 7,000, but on a tighter 16 ft NPSHr jumps to 9,783 - above the limit, nothing changed but the suction margin. Zero empirical constants (pure definitional index). Catalog 1,076 -> 1,077 (version 0.218.0).

### feat(construction): spec-v628 Coulomb active earth pressure with wall friction and batter; 2026-07-11

Lands `coulomb-earth-pressure` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the Coulomb wall-friction form spec-v261 named beside `lateral-earth-pressure`, completing the five-tile earth-pressure limit-state cluster (at-rest, submerged, sloped-backfill, seepage-slope, Coulomb). Ka = cos^2(phi - theta) / [cos^2(theta) cos(delta + theta) (1 + sqrt(sin(phi + delta) sin(phi - alpha) / (cos(delta + theta) cos(theta - alpha))))^2] credits wall friction delta, a battered face theta, and a sloped backfill alpha; the thrust acts at delta + theta from horizontal with the horizontal (overturning) and downward (sliding-resisting) components reported. A rough vertical wall on a phi = 30 level backfill carries 1,676 lb/ft horizontal vs Rankine's 2,000 - a 16% cut; it reduces exactly to Rankine when smooth/vertical/level. Pure Coulomb trig (1776 / Das / NAVFAC DM-7.02), no proprietary constants. Raises the calc-geotech.js gzip cap 16,500 -> 18,000. Catalog 1,075 -> 1,076 (version 0.217.0).

### feat(construction): spec-v627 infinite-slope stability with seepage; 2026-07-11

Lands `slope-stability-seepage` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the seepage follow-on spec-v289 named inside `slope-stability-infinite` (the submerged gamma - gamma_w case). With steady seepage parallel to the slope and the water table at the surface, FS = (c' + (gamma_sat - 62.4) H cos^2 beta tan phi') / (gamma_sat H sin beta cos beta): the pore pressure cuts the friction term to the buoyant weight while the driving weight stays saturated, so a cohesionless factor of safety drops to (gamma_sat - 62.4)/gamma_sat of the dry value - about half. A phi = 32 sand at 18 degrees is a safe 1.92 dry but a failing 0.96 wet; the tile shows both. Pure effective-stress slope mechanics (Das / NAVFAC DM-7), no proprietary constants. Catalog 1,074 -> 1,075 (version 0.216.0).

### feat(construction): spec-v626 sloped-backfill Rankine earth pressure; 2026-07-11

Lands `sloped-backfill-earth-pressure` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the sloped-backfill Rankine coefficient spec-v261/v262 named beside `lateral-earth-pressure`. A backfill rising behind the wall at slope beta pushes harder than a level one: Ka = cos b (cos b - sqrt(cos^2 b - cos^2 phi)) / (cos b + sqrt(cos^2 b - cos^2 phi)), the thrust Pa = 0.5 x Ka x gamma x H^2 acting parallel to the slope, and its horizontal (overturning) and vertical (heel) components, with the level Ka0 for contrast. A 15 deg backfill on a phi = 30 sand raises Ka from 0.333 to 0.373 - 12% heavier and tilted; beta must stay below phi. Pure Rankine trig, no proprietary constants. Catalog 1,073 -> 1,074 (version 0.215.0).

### feat(construction): spec-v625 submerged-backfill earth pressure (buoyant + hydrostatic); 2026-07-11

Lands `submerged-earth-pressure` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the submerged-backfill companion spec-v261 named beside `lateral-earth-pressure` ("a submerged zone must be run with buoyant unit weight plus separate hydrostatic pressure"). Below the water table the soil skeleton pushes with its buoyant weight (gamma_sat - 62.4) at the Ka reduction while the water pushes with the full hydrostatic pressure at no reduction: Pa' = 0.5 x Ka x gamma_buoy x H^2, Pw = 0.5 x 62.4 x H^2, plus the Ka q H surcharge, combined resultant, and the dry-case thrust for contrast. A 10 ft phi = 30, 125 pcf sand pushes 2,083 lb/ft dry but 4,163 lb/ft submerged - almost exactly 2x, three-quarters of it water. Pure effective-stress soil mechanics, no proprietary constants. Catalog 1,072 -> 1,073 (version 0.214.0).

### feat(construction): spec-v624 at-rest earth pressure (Jaky K0) for a braced wall; 2026-07-11

Lands `at-rest-earth-pressure` (Group E, calc-geotech.js), the at-rest limit-state follow-on spec-v261 named beside `lateral-earth-pressure`. A basement wall, braced excavation, or rigid culvert cannot deflect to the Rankine active limit, so it carries the higher at-rest pressure: Jaky's K0 = 1 - sin phi, the triangular thrust P0 = 0.5 x K0 x gamma x H^2 at H/3, the surcharge term K0 x q x H at H/2, the combined resultant and its height. A 10 ft phi = 30 sand wall carries 3,000 lb/ft at rest against only 2,000 lb/ft active - exactly 1.5x more, the error of reaching for the active tile on a braced wall. Pure first-principles soil mechanics, no proprietary constants. Catalog 1,071 -> 1,072 (version 0.213.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v623 buffer tank with distribution-loop credit; 2026-07-11

Lands `buffer-tank-loop-credit` (Group C, calc-hvacsystems.js), the distribution-volume-credit follow-on spec-v587 named beside `hydronic-buffer-tank`. It sizes the gross anti-short-cycle buffer (V = on_time x (source_min - zone_load) / (500 x delta_T)), then credits the water already circulating in the distribution loop (0.0408 x d^2 x L gal, the same pure-geometry constant the pipe-volume tile uses) to return the net tank actually needed. A 60 gal gross on a 1.5 in x 200 ft primary loop holds 18.36 gal, so only a 42 gal tank is required; a 2 in x 1000 ft loop holds 163 gal and wipes the requirement out entirely. Catalog 1,070 -> 1,071 (version 0.212.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v622 draft-hood dilution ratio; 2026-07-11

Lands `draft-hood-dilution` (Group C, calc-hvacservice.js), the follow-on spec-v584 named: how much room air the draft hood pulled in, from two O2 readings. dilution_ratio = (20.9 - O2_appliance) / (20.9 - O2_diluted); dilution_air_fraction = (O2_diluted - O2_appliance) / (20.9 - O2_appliance) (the 20.9% ambient-oxygen mass balance). A clean appliance at 5% O2 reading 12% at the vent has 44% dilution air, so a CO reading there is only 56% of the true flue value - why the air-free CO sample belongs before the draft hood. Catalog 1,069 -> 1,070 (version 0.211.0).

### feat(water): spec-v621 tapered flocculation G schedule; 2026-07-10

Lands `tapered-flocculation-g` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the follow-on spec-v575 named: the multi-stage schedule the single-basin G tile can't give. P_stage = G_stage^2 x mu(T) x V (the Camp-Stein relation inverted, reusing the module's water-viscosity table) across 2-3 stages of decreasing G, with the composite Gt and a tapered/in-band check. A 50/30/20 per-second taper in three 100 m3 stages at 15 C needs 285 / 102 / 46 W. Group M audit count 31 -> 32. Catalog 1,068 -> 1,069 (version 0.210.0).

### feat(wastewater): spec-v620 digester volatile-acid to alkalinity ratio; 2026-07-10

Lands `va-alkalinity-ratio` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the follow-on spec-v573 named: the early-warning stability index the pH meter misses. ratio = volatile acids / alkalinity (common CaCO3 basis); bands < 0.1 stable, 0.1-0.25 acceptable, 0.25-0.4 corrective action, > 0.4 souring; plus the bicarbonate buffer margin. The pH lags because the buffer holds it steady until the alkalinity is consumed. Group M audit count 30 -> 31. Catalog 1,067 -> 1,068 (version 0.209.0).

### feat(forestry): spec-v619 thinning target TPA from a target SDI; 2026-07-10

Lands `thinning-target-tpa` (Group L, calc-arborist.js), the follow-on spec-v564 named: the Reineke relation inverted to the residual-stand number. TPA_target = (SDI_max x target%) / (QMD/10)^1.605, trees to cut, and the residual basal area; round-trips through reineke-sdi exactly. A ponderosa stand (SDI_max 450) at the 35% floor and 10 in QMD keeps 157.5 TPA. Catalog 1,066 -> 1,067 (version 0.208.0).

### feat(steel): spec-v618 panel-zone shear under high column axial (AISC 360-16 J10-10/J10-12); 2026-07-10

Lands `steel-panel-zone-axial` (Group E, calc-steel.js), the follow-on spec-v555 named: the axial reduction its low-axial tile flags but does not carry. Py = Fy Ag; past Pr = 0.4 Pc the basic strength takes (1.4 - Pr/Pc) (Eq. J10-10), past 0.75 Pc the deformation-modeled strength takes (1.9 - 1.2 Pr/Pc) (Eq. J10-12); below the thresholds it reproduces steel-panel-zone-shear exactly. Pinned: a 14 in / 0.5 in web column at Pr 600 of Py 1,325 checks phiRn 179.0 kip. Catalog 1,065 -> 1,066 (version 0.207.0).

### feat(concrete): spec-v617 concrete anchor side-face blowout (ACI 318-19 17.6.4); 2026-07-10

Lands `concrete-anchor-blowout` (Group E, calc-concrete.js), completing the headed-anchor tension triad spec-v548 named (breakout v548, pullout v612, blowout here): the deep-anchor-near-an-edge mode. Nsb = 160 x c_a1 x sqrt(Abrg) x lambda_a x sqrt(f'c); corner cut (1 + c_a2/c_a1)/4 where c_a2 < 3 c_a1; phiNsb = 0.70 x Nsb; applicability flag hef > 2.5 c_a1 (shallower anchors are governed by breakout). The v612 example anchor at a 3 in edge checks 17.2 kip design, 10.0 kip in a corner. Catalog 1,064 -> 1,065 (version 0.206.0).

### feat(rigging): spec-v616 beam clamp reaction and side-pull check; 2026-07-10

Lands `beam-clamp-side-pull` (Group Z, calc-rigging.js), the last spec-v544-named follow-on: the check at the steel. V = T sin(angle), H = T cos(angle), each against the clamp's vertical WLL and the manufacturer's side-pull allowance (0 = not rated, the default); a horizontal component on an unrated clamp returns a re-rig verdict, not a pass. The steep 860 lb leg of the v544 example loads its clamp 38% vertically but 77% of a generous 500 lb side-pull rating. Catalog 1,063 -> 1,064 (version 0.205.0).

### feat(rigging): spec-v615 three-point bridle leg tension (3-D); 2026-07-10

Lands `three-point-bridle` (Group Z, calc-rigging.js), the follow-on spec-v544 named: the out-of-plane three-point resolution its two-leg tile explicitly excludes. Li = sqrt(e^2 + n^2 + rise^2); ui = (e, n, rise)/Li; T1 u1 + T2 u2 + T3 u3 = (0, 0, W) by Cramer's rule; errors on coplanar geometry and on any non-positive tension (the apex must hang inside the attachment triangle - a rope can only pull). Symmetric check recovers W / (3 sin angle) exactly; the asymmetric example splits 1,000 lb as 497 / 419 / 244. Raises the calc-rigging.js gzip cap 21000 -> 24000. Catalog 1,062 -> 1,063 (version 0.204.0).

### feat(rescue): spec-v614 sweep width correction for weather, speed, and fatigue; 2026-07-10

Lands `sweep-width-correction` (Group P, calc-rescue.js), the follow-on spec-v540 named and spec-v595 re-named: the corrected sweep width that search-track-spacing requires as its W input. W = Wu x f_weather x f_speed x f_fatigue (IAMSAR Manual Vol. II / US National SAR Supplement practice); weather and fatigue factors in (0, 1], speed in (0, 1.5]. A 120 ft raw detection width in rain with a tired crew (0.5 / 1.0 / 0.9) is a 54 ft effective width - a 55% haircut before spacing is chosen. Group P audit count 12 -> 13. Catalog 1,061 -> 1,062 (version 0.203.0).

### feat(water): spec-v613 paddle flocculator power from geometry; 2026-07-10

Lands `flocculator-paddle-power` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the follow-on spec-v575 named: the power flocculation-g-value needs, from the paddle wheel. P = 0.5 x Cd x rho x A x v_rel^3; v_rel = v_tip x (1 - k) (the water slips). Cd and slip are user inputs (references disagree); the drag is exact once chosen. A 6-ft wheel at 3 rpm with 40 ft2 of blade delivers ~267 W. Raises the calc-treatment.js gzip cap 18000 -> 21000 and the tools-data.js registry cap 155000 -> 170000. Group M audit count 29 -> 30. Catalog 1,060 -> 1,061 (version 0.202.0).

### feat(concrete): spec-v612 concrete headed-anchor pullout (ACI 318-19 17.6.3); 2026-07-10

Lands `concrete-anchor-pullout` (Group E, calc-concrete.js), the companion spec-v548 named: the head-crushing tension failure mode. Np = 8 x Abrg x f'c; Npn = psi_cP x Np (1.4 uncracked, 1.0 cracked); phiNpn = 0.70 x Npn. Unlike breakout it does not depend on embedment. Raises the calc-concrete.js gzip cap 20000 -> 23000. Catalog 1,059 -> 1,060 (version 0.201.0).

### feat(electrical): spec-v611 EV charger throttled-current schedule; 2026-07-10

Lands `ev-charger-throttle` (Group A, calc-feeder.js), the follow-on spec-v561 named: what each car gets when the EVEMS throttles. throttled = min(charger_max, aggregate_limit / active_chargers); full_rate_count = floor(aggregate_limit / charger_max). Four 40-A chargers on a 100-A budget throttle to 25 A each; drop to two active and both get their full 40 A. Catalog 1,058 -> 1,059 (version 0.200.0).

### feat(electrical): spec-v610 ground potential rise screen (IEEE 80); 2026-07-10

Lands `ground-potential-rise` (Group A, calc-elecdesign.js), the follow-on spec-v558 named: the yard-voltage side of step-touch-voltage. GPR = grid_current x grid_resistance, screened against the tolerable touch voltage (the IEEE 80 shortcut: a GPR at or below the tolerable touch clears the whole yard without mesh/step analysis). Repoints the "ground potential rise" alias to the dedicated tile. Catalog 1,057 -> 1,058 (version 0.199.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v609 combustion lambda and air-fuel ratio; 2026-07-10

Lands `combustion-lambda` (Group C, calc-hvacservice.js), the follow-on spec-v583 named: the lambda and air-fuel ratio a modern analyzer shows beside excess air. lambda = 20.9 / (20.9 - O2); excess air = (lambda - 1) x 100; AFR_actual = lambda x AFR_stoich (17.2 natural gas, 15.5 propane, 14.5 #2 oil, by mass). Completes the combustion-analysis readout set. Catalog 1,056 -> 1,057 (version 0.198.0).

### feat(arborist): spec-v608 critical root zone encroachment percent; 2026-07-10

Lands `tree-crz-encroachment` (Group L, calc-arborist.js), the follow-on spec-v566 named: how much of the CRZ a construction limit line cuts off, judged against species tolerance. A limit line at distance d slices a segment R^2 x acos(d/R) - d x sqrt(R^2 - d^2); encroach = segment / (pi x R^2) x 100, over when past the species threshold (tolerant 40 / intermediate 30 / sensitive 20). Catalog 1,055 -> 1,056 (version 0.197.0).

### feat(arborist): spec-v607 open-cavity trunk strength loss (Smiley & Fraedrich); 2026-07-10

Lands `tree-open-cavity` (Group L, calc-arborist.js), the follow-on spec-v565 named: the open-cavity correction the closed-hollow Wagener screen understates. loss = (hollow_d^3 + R x (D^3 - hollow_d^3)) / D^3 x 100, R = opening arc / circumference (Smiley & Fraedrich 1992). Collapses to Wagener at R=0; reproduces the paper's example (4-in stem 70% hollow, 2-in opening -> 45%). Repoints the "cavity strength loss" alias to the dedicated tile; raises the calc-arborist.js gzip cap 13000 -> 16000. Catalog 1,054 -> 1,055 (version 0.196.0).

### feat(agriculture): spec-v606 manure storage covered-vs-open roof savings; 2026-07-10

Lands `manure-cover-savings` (Group L, calc-agriculture.js), the follow-on spec-v582 named: the storage a roof buys back. roof_saving = area x (net_precip + storm)/12 - the whole rainfall term an open pit banks on its own surface. An 8,000 ft2 pit in a 10-in wet season saves 6,667 ft3 (~50,000 gal, 19%); a bigger pit in a wetter climate saves a third. Group L audit count 29 -> 30. Catalog 1,053 -> 1,054 (version 0.195.0).

### feat(fire): spec-v605 tanker shuttle fill-site-limited fleet size; 2026-07-10

Lands `tanker-fleet-size` (Group F, calc-fire.js), the follow-on spec-v580 named: how many tankers is enough. bottleneck = max(tank/fill_gpm, tank/dump_gpm); fleet = ceil(cycle / bottleneck); ceiling flow = tank / bottleneck. A 3,000-gal operation at 1,000 gpm fill and dump over a 2-mile haul needs 5 tankers to reach its 1,000-gpm ceiling; a sixth just queues. Completes the rural water-shuttle trio. Group F audit count 34 -> 35. Catalog 1,052 -> 1,053 (version 0.194.0).

### feat(irrigation): spec-v604 center-pivot percent-timer to depth; 2026-07-10

Lands `pivot-timer-depth` (Group L, calc-agriculture.js), the follow-on spec-v568 named: the end-tower percent timer converted to applied depth. revolution = revolution_100_hr x 100 / timer_pct; depth = flow x revolution / (452.6 x area). The depth is inversely proportional to the timer - dropping it from 100% to 50% doubles the depth. Closes the center-pivot cluster (runtime, application-rate, timer). Group L audit count 28 -> 29. Catalog 1,051 -> 1,052 (version 0.193.0).

### feat(steel): spec-v603 panel-zone doubler-plate thickness sizer; 2026-07-10

Lands `steel-doubler-plate` (Group E, calc-steel.js), the how-thick spec-v555 left open. Strength t = (Vu - phiRn_bare) / (0.90 x 0.60 Fy dc); stability (AISC 360-16 Eq. J10-12) t >= (dz + wz)/90; t_required = max of the two, rounded up to the next 1/16-in plate. On a stocky column with a small shortfall the stability minimum governs; on a deep beam the strength governs. Repoints the "doubler plate" search alias from steel-panel-zone-shear to the dedicated tile; raises the calc-steel.js gzip cap 21000 -> 24000. Catalog 1,050 -> 1,051 (version 0.192.0).

### feat(irrigation): spec-v602 center-pivot outer-span application rate vs soil intake; 2026-07-10

Lands `pivot-application-rate` (Group L, calc-agriculture.js), the runoff check spec-v568's note promised: the outer end sweeps the biggest circle fastest, so app_rate = pass_depth x 2 x pi x pivot_length / (revolution_hr x wetted_band). A 1-in pass on a quarter-mile pivot turning once a day through a 100-ft band applies 3.5 in/hr at the end tower - about 7x a silt loam's 0.5 in/hr intake. Group L audit count 27 -> 28. Catalog 1,049 -> 1,050 (version 0.191.0).

### feat(fire): spec-v601 Iowa rate-of-flow (volume method); 2026-07-10

Lands `iowa-rate-of-flow` (Group F, calc-fire.js), the follow-on spec-v577 named: the Royer-Nelson volume-method fire flow beside the area-based NFA formula. volume = L x W x H; total_gal = volume / 200; rate_gpm = volume / 100 (the 30-second knockdown burst). A 20 x 30 x 10 ft room is 6,000 ft^3 -> 30 gal, 60 gpm (vs the NFA's 200 gpm sustained). Group F audit count 33 -> 34. Catalog 1,048 -> 1,049 (version 0.190.0).

### feat(wastewater): spec-v600 settleability-based RAS rate from SVI; 2026-07-10

Lands `ras-svi-settleability` (Group M, calc-water.js), the follow-on spec-v571 named: the RAS number from the settleometer, no RAS_SS lab result needed. Xr = 1,000,000 / SVI; R = MLSS / (Xr - MLSS); Q_RAS = R x Q. A good SVI of 100 holding 2,500 mg/L needs a 33% return; a bulking sludge at SVI 150 wanting 3,000 mg/L needs 82%. Group M audit count 28 -> 29. Catalog 1,047 -> 1,048 (version 0.189.0).

### feat(fire): spec-v599 tanker shuttle cycle time; 2026-07-10

Lands `tanker-shuttle-cycle` (Group F, calc-fire.js), the follow-on spec-v580 named: builds the cycle time tanker-shuttle-flow needs. fill = tank/fill_gpm; dump = tank/dump_gpm; travel = 2 x distance/speed (round trip); cycle = fill + dump + travel; one tanker sustains tank/cycle. A 3,000-gal tanker over a 2-mile haul at 35 mph runs a 12.9-min cycle and sustains 233 gpm alone. Group F audit count 32 -> 33. Catalog 1,046 -> 1,047 (version 0.188.0).

### feat(forester): spec-v598 quadratic mean diameter from a tally; 2026-07-10

Lands `quadratic-mean-diameter` (Group L, calc-arborist.js), the follow-on spec-v564 named: the QMD reineke-sdi requires, from the tally sheet. QMD = sqrt(sum(count x DBH^2) / trees); returns the QMD, the arithmetic mean beside it, the tree count, and the tallied basal area. Accepts a diameter tally (one token per tree "12" or per class "12:40" / "12 x 40", commas or new lines). Repoints the pre-existing "quadratic mean diameter" search alias from reineke-sdi to the dedicated tile. Catalog 1,045 -> 1,046 (version 0.187.0).

### feat(fire): spec-v597 vacuum gauge to drafting lift readout; 2026-07-10

Lands `vacuum-lift-reading` (Group F, calc-fire.js), the follow-on spec-v579 named: reads the compound (vacuum) gauge during a draft. suction_head_ft = vacuum_inhg x 1.13, against the altitude-corrected attainable ceiling (factor x (33.9 - elevation/1000)) with the margin left. 10 in Hg at sea level is 11.3 ft (half the ~22.6 ft ceiling); 18 in Hg at 3,000 ft is 98% of the ceiling. Group F audit count 31 -> 32. Catalog 1,044 -> 1,045 (version 0.186.0).

### feat(wastewater): spec-v596 digester gas and methane production; 2026-07-10

Lands `digester-gas-production` (Group M, calc-treatment.js), the follow-on spec-v573 named: the payoff side of anaerobic digestion. VS_destroyed = VS_fed x reduction/100; gas = VS_destroyed x yield (12-18 ft^3/lb, default 15); methane = gas x ~65%; energy = methane x 960 BTU/ft^3. A digester destroying 5,500 lb VS/day makes about 82,500 ft^3 of gas and 51.5 MMBtu/day. Group M audit count 27 -> 28. Catalog 1,043 -> 1,044 (version 0.185.0).

### feat(field): spec-v595 search effort in searcher-hours; 2026-07-10

Lands `searcher-hours` (Group P, calc-rescue.js), the follow-on spec-v540 named: what sweeping a segment costs. track_ft = 43,560 x acres / spacing; searcher_hours = track_ft / (mph x 5,280); team clock = effort / searchers. Prices the spacing search-track-spacing sets for a POD (a 160-acre segment at 40 ft spacing is 33 miles of track line). Group P audit count 11 -> 12. Catalog 1,042 -> 1,043 (version 0.184.0).

### feat(hvac): spec-v594 flue-gas combustion efficiency (stack loss); 2026-07-10

Lands `flue-gas-combustion-eff` (Group C, calc-hvacservice.js), the tile spec-v583 and spec-v584 both named as the third leg of the combustion-analysis triad: the analyzer's O2 and stack-temperature readings to steady-state combustion efficiency via the Siegert stack loss (CO2 = CO2max x (1 - O2/20.9); qA = dT_C x (A1/CO2 + B); per-fuel DIN coefficients for natural gas, propane, and #2 oil), reported on both the net (LHV, European convention) and approximate gross (HHV, US analyzer) bases. The gross conversion cross-checks within half a point of the published TSI fuel-oil efficiency table. Catalog 1,041 -> 1,042 (version 0.183.0).

### feat(search): spec-v592 follow-on -- preview map 17 -> 44 entries; 2026-07-10

Answer previews now cover 44 of the 49 slot tiles (asphalt tonnage, breaker sizing, dim weight, drywall sheets, base plates, and peers). Five slot tiles stay preview-less on principle: four whose renderers convert US inputs into SI-native computes (the preview runtime maps args identically, so a direct mapping would show numerically wrong answers -- amp-power-spl, dyno-correction-sae, flocculation-g-value, spl-atmospheric; a conversion field in the schema is the recorded follow-on if demand appears), and tankless-gpm (its preview would need the inverse solve mode, which the worked-example gate check cannot verify). All 44 verified two ways: the check-slots worked-example run and a slot-mapped preview simulation (44/44 finite, leak-free).

### feat(search): spec-v591 follow-on -- slot batch 2 (factory tiles unlocked); 2026-07-10

The slot table grows 17 -> 49 tiles / 85 slots, the first batch able to target factory-rendered tiles now that their inputs carry real ids (0.181.0): dim-weight, freight-density, breaker-sizing, transformer-sizing, asphalt-tonnage, rebar, sump-basin-sizing, brake-pad-life, and peers. `check-slots` learns the two legitimate param shapes -- an id-position literal of ANY field-builder call (aliases like `_mnF` included; the old make*-only regex missed the v593-remediated rescue renderer) or a factory field-spec `key:` literal (which IS the DOM id since the factory-id fix). Verified in-browser: "dim weight 40 in 25 lb" arrives on the factory tile as `#dim-weight?v=1&length_in=40&actual_weight_lb=25` with both inputs populated; same for "breaker for 3500w at 240v". Shard 1.6 KB gz.

### feat(search): spec-v590 Phase 2 COMPLETE -- final small-groups batch; 2026-07-10

2,548 question-phrase aliases covering the last 241 uncovered tiles (Groups F/H/J/M/N/O/P/R/T/X/Y/Z, 9-12 phrasings each; 8 rows dropped by the pipeline). **Every one of the catalog's 1,041 tiles now carries question-phrase coverage** -- the "ask it a question" search answers plain-language questions across the whole catalog, deterministically and offline. A full shard-wide top-3 re-audit after the merge pruned 20 older rows the bigger corpus outcompeted (one previously pinned phrase now correctly prefers the literal max-offer tile). The ranker also gains a verbatim-alias bonus (+4 when the normalized query IS a tile's committed alias phrase; terms are globally unique so at most one tile qualifies, and coverage still ranks first): a committed question phrase typed word-for-word now surfaces its own tile first for 99.8% of the 11,608 question rows. Shard 13,611 -> 16,139 rows, 240.8 KB gz: within but approaching the spec's ~250 KB per-shard threshold, so the next corpus growth should take the spec's designated remediation (per-group shard split) first.
