### feat(search): spec-v590 Phase 2 batch 1 -- Group A question corpus; 2026-07-10

First group-sized Phase 2 batch under the landed spec-v590: 1,569 question-phrase aliases covering all 132 previously uncovered Group A (electrical) tiles at 9-12 phrasings each, drafted with AI at authoring time and mechanically reviewed by the same pipeline as Phase 1 (charset/style, live targets, global case-insensitive dedupe, stopword survival, top-3 rank check through the v589 ranker). Landing this batch also hardened the ranker: digit-led query tokens ("150", "120v") now add score but never coverage, so a number quoted in some tile's alias can no longer outrank the true target when the user's quantity coincides with it, and pure-number alias tokens are excluded from the match corpus; a full shard-wide re-audit under the corrected ranker then pruned the 13 question rows (0.5%) that no longer met the top-3 bar. Shard 5,706 -> 7,262 rows, 96.1 KB gz (lazy-loaded, not home payload; well under the spec's ~250 KB remediation threshold), logged in the manifest.

### fix(a11y): give factory-rendered inputs real DOM ids (f.id || f.key); 2026-07-10

Every `_simpleRenderer`-family factory passed `f.id` to makeNumber/makeSelect/makeCheckbox, but the field specs never define `id` -- so hundreds of factory-rendered tiles shipped inputs with `id="undefined"`: broken `label for` association (only the first field's label resolved), a latent `undefined=<value>` param in hash-state URLs, and no way for deep links or the spec-v591 search prefill to target those tiles. All 48 factory call sites across 25 modules now fall back to the field key (`f.id || f.key`; keys are unique within a tile, and one tool view renders at a time). Old shared links carrying the bogus `undefined=` param stay harmless (applyHashState ignores unknown ids). Verified in-browser: `#dim-weight` fields get real ids with matching labels, typing writes `length_in=48` (not `undefined=48`) into the hash, and a `?v=1&length_in=40&...` deep link arrives prefilled and computed. Unlocks slot/preview coverage for factory tiles as spec-v591/v592 follow-on batches.

### docs(readme): resync ungated catalog figures to the live tree; 2026-07-10

The v489-v593 landings left the ungated documentation prose at the pre-campaign snapshot. Resynced (every value measured from the live scripts, not memory): README headline and prose 942 -> 1,041 tiles / 5,235 -> 5,353 tests / 28 -> 30 gates, tools-data and app.js size figures, home-payload 43.2% -> 48.0%, correctness mermaid + table denominators (corpus 1,285, dims 1,288, sources 167), sitemap/shell route totals 964 -> 1,063, and the "where the catalog stands" paragraph (now through spec-v593 / 0.180.0 with the v489-v588 campaign, the v589-v592 ask-a-question search series, and v593 US-defaults recorded as inflections 14-15); docs/mobile-responsive.md gains its per-expansion re-verification section (1,157 checks / 1,063 shells clean at 320 px); docs/performance.md current-state payload figures refreshed and the shard-count claim corrected to the live `data:verify` count (117 entries).

### feat(units): land spec-v593 -- US-customary defaults everywhere; 2026-07-10

Turns the "US standards only" header line into an enforced invariant. New 30th lint gate `scripts/check-us-defaults.mjs`: no metric-defaulted unit selects, no unallowlisted metric field labels, scanning direct make* calls, per-module aliases (any `fn("Label", "dom-id")` shape), and factory `label:` field specs. `scripts/us-defaults-allowlist.json` codifies the spec's exception class (NEC deg C columns, kW/kWh billing, mg/L water ops, product-spec mm, SI-defined standards, lab practice) with per-entry justifications -- 47 entries, including waivers for lookalikes that are not metric at all (NEC subsection "(C)", Loan Estimate section C, ACI span ratios l/20, seismic acceleration in g). Proven red first: 20 findings across 11 tiles on the pre-remediation tree. All 13 audited tiles remediated: hiking-time (mi/ft selects first), langelier-index (deg F, prefill 77), flocculation-g-value (gal / deg F / hp converted at the boundary; Camp-Stein math untouched), search-track-spacing (feet throughout), psychrometric (in Hg 29.92), liquefaction-screening (depth in ft; Seed-Idriss rd restated per-foot to 2e-12 agreement, fixtures/fuzzer/corpus/citation restated), dyno-correction-sae (in Hg / deg F into the J1349 metric reference), brake-pad-life (ft-lb / deg F rise / mils primary, metric parenthetical), spl-atmospheric + amp-power-spl + sound-pressure + time-alignment (ft / deg F / in Hg at the boundary, ANSI math unchanged), wallpaper-rolls (US 27 in x 27 ft bolt defaults, Euro noted as the alternative). Only liquefaction-screening changed a compute signature; everything else converts at the renderer boundary so fixtures stay correlation-native. Verified: 30 gates, 5,353 unit tests, build, data:verify, check:shells, and a 320 px example-fill sweep of all 13 tiles (leak-free, no horizontal scroll).

### feat(search): land spec-v592 -- live answer preview and search UX polish; 2026-07-10

Closes the v589-v592 "ask it a question" series. Typing *"voltage drop 120v 150 ft 20 amps"* now shows **drop 11.9 V / percent drop 9.88 %** inside the dropdown's top result before any click -- the preview lazily imports the same compute export the tile calls, feeds it the slot-mapped numbers over the renderer's example defaults, and renders only when every headline value is finite (failures add nothing, never block). New lazy shard `data/search/preview-map.json` (17 entries, 1.8 KB gz, generated by `scripts/build-preview-map.mjs`, verified by the `check-slots` gate: slot row exists, module/fn import-resolve, headline keys finite on the worked example). Polish: a did-you-mean row (*showing matches for "conduit fill"*) when the top match needed the typo pass; the no-match dead end gains a "Browse all 21 trades" fork; the search placeholder rotates through 8 example questions by day of month (deterministic, no timers). As-landed deltas are annotated in the spec (top-result rather than all-results typo condition; group-coverage fallback rows are unreachable by construction and were dropped; the shard carries hand-authored `args`/`defaults` maps the spec's sketch omitted). Home-payload js grew ~2.6 KB gz to 35,934 B (71.6% of the sub-budget, no cap change).

### feat(search): land spec-v591 -- quantity slot parsing and prefilled deep links; 2026-07-10

Typing *"voltage drop 120v 150 ft 20 amps"* and picking the result now lands on `#voltage-drop?v=1&vd-src=120&vd-len=150&vd-cur=20` -- inputs populated, compute already run -- instead of a blank tile. `search-discovery.js` gains the pure `extractQuantities` (glued `120v`, spaced `150 ft`, fractions `3/4 in`, thousands commas; single-letter units glued-only so an article never reads as a unit; unitless numbers never map) and `mapSlots` (a quantity fills a param only when exactly one yet-unfilled slot accepts its unit token). New lazy shard `data/search/slots.json` (17 seed tiles / 27 slots, 820 B gz, sw-precached, manifest row added) maps unit spellings to each tile's DOM input ids. New 29th lint gate `scripts/check-slots.mjs`: every param must be the id-position literal of a `make*` call in the tile's module -- key-position literals are dead prefills because `applyHashState` targets DOM ids. Seed is 17 tiles rather than the spec's ~50: factory-built renderers (`_simpleRenderer`) pass `f.id` that their field specs never define, so their inputs render with `id="undefined"` and cannot take a prefill (or hash state at all) until the factories get real ids -- documented as the follow-on. `search-discovery.js` cap 7000 -> **8500** B. Verified by new unit tests, a 2-case Playwright integration spec (query -> Enter -> inputs filled -> leak-free output), and a dist-level 320 px smoke.

### feat(search): land spec-v590 Phase 1 -- question-phrase alias corpus; 2026-07-10

`data/search/aliases.json` grows 4,531 -> 5,706 rows: 1,175 question-phrase aliases (new `kind: "question"`) covering the 100 highest-traffic tiles (leader-key shortcut set, each group's head tiles, most-aliased tiles) at 10-14 phrasings each -- "what gauge wire for a 30 amp breaker", "how much can the crane pick after deductions". Drafted with AI at authoring time per the spec, then mechanically reviewed: charset/style gate, live-target and id-collision checks, global case-insensitive dedupe, stopword-survival through `normalizeQuery`, and a top-3 rank check through the v589 ranker (2 drafted rows dropped as better answered by other tiles). `check-discoverability` accepts the new kind; runtime treats all kinds uniformly. Shard is lazy-loaded (not home-payload): 659 KB raw / 72.3 KB gzipped, logged in `data/search/manifest.json`. Verified end-to-end: 5 committed phrases typed verbatim into the deployed combobox each surface their tile first at 320 px.

### chore(sizes): raise group-shell gzip cap 42 -> 48 KB (fixes CI red since v547); 2026-07-10

The v489-v588 single-tile campaign's Group E structural singles (`steel-floor-vibration` v547 onward) took `groups/construction/index.html` past the 42 KB group-shell gzip cap (~43.4 KB). `check-shells` runs only in CI's integration job, not in push lint, so local landings stayed green while every CI run from v547 through v589 failed on this one cap. Raised to **48 KB** (~11% headroom); also re-ran the masked `check:shell-mobile` step locally (1,157 checks, zero horizontal scroll).

### feat(search): land spec-v589 -- deterministic natural-language search ranking; 2026-07-10

The hero combobox and the MCP `search()` now share one deterministic ranking layer in `search-discovery.js` (`normalizeQuery` + `rankTools`): stopword stripping (~180 question-frame words), unit-spelling synonyms (`sqft` -> `square feet`, `amps` -> `amp`), per-token field-weighted scoring (name 3 / alias 2 / trade 2 / desc 1) with prefix stemming, digit/word equivalence (`3 phase` meets `three-phase`), a plural-strip fallback, and a bounded Damerau-Levenshtein <= 1 typo fallback (`condiut fill` -> Conduit Fill). Question-shaped queries like "how many yards of concrete do i need for a slab" now rank their tile first instead of hitting the no-match row. No model, no network, no new dependency; a stopword-only query falls back to the previous substring behavior. `app.js` lazy-loads the module off the first-paint path; `mcp/catalog.mjs` imports the same functions and now folds in the alias shard, so browser and agent recall cannot drift. `search-discovery.js` gzip cap raised 2500 -> **7000** (5925 B as landed + ~20% headroom).

At tile 953 (spec-v499) both crossed their caps (`tools-data.js` 100.3%, `tile-meta.js` 100.1%). Raised the `check-module-sizes` caps to **140000** (tools-data, ~7% headroom for the ~87 remaining backlog tiles) and **14500** (tile-meta, ~11% headroom). Both are lazy-loaded (spec-v10 H.2) and absent from the home-view payload, so this does not affect first paint.

### chore(sizes): raise calc-electrical.js gzip cap 72000 -> 78000; 2026-07-09

Landing the spec-v489..v588 single-tile campaign added three Group A tiles to `calc-electrical.js` (spec-v494 `transformer-voltage-regulation`, spec-v495 `capacitor-discharge-time`, spec-v496 `asymmetrical-fault-xr`), taking the built copy to ~73.1 KB gz (101.5% of the old 72000 B cap). Raised the `check-module-sizes` cap to **78000** (current + ~7% headroom). The module is lazy-loaded and absent from the home-view payload, so this does not affect first paint. Prior campaign tiles (spec-v489..v495, package 0.175.1 -> 0.175.7) landed without a per-tile CHANGELOG entry (the changelog is maintainer-only and ungated); this note exists because the size gate was touched.

### feat(solar): land spec-v488 -- EV charge time, AC Level 2; 941 -> 942 tiles, 0.175.0, 2026-07-09

One new tile in `calc-solar.js` (the solar / battery / EV-charging bench) Group A: **`ev-charge-time`**, how long an EV charge takes -- the question `ev-charger-load` (NEC 625 circuit sizing) never answers. From the AC Level 2 charge-time model (SAE J1772 onboard-charger limit; charging losses): `energy = capacity x (target - start)`, `charge_power = min(EVSE, onboard charger)`, `time = energy / (charge_power x efficiency)`. The headline is the field gotcha: on AC Level 2 the charging power is the **lesser of the wall EVSE and the vehicle's onboard charger**, and it is almost always the onboard charger that governs. The pinned example (a 75 kWh EV, 20->80% = 45 kWh, on an 11.5 kW EVSE with a 7.7 kW onboard charger at 88%) charges at only **7.7 kW -> 6.6 hr**, with the EVSE flagged as oversized; the cross-check (an 11.5 kW onboard charger) finishes the same charge in **4.4 hr** -- the number that tells an installer a bigger EVSE only helps if the car can accept it. Constant-power AC Level 2 model (DC fast charging tapers above ~80% and is not this model); the vehicle's charging curve governs. Complements `ev-charger-load`, `battery-runtime`, and `battery-c-rate`. Package **0.174.0 -> 0.175.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer (six number fields).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (`GOVERNANCE.general`, first-principles -- not an NEC tile), 8 search aliases ("ev charge time", "charging time", "how long to charge", "level 2 charge time", "onboard charger", "charge duration", "ev charging hours", "time to full charge"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `ev-charger-load` / `battery-runtime` / `battery-c-rate`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the onboard-limited example and the bigger-onboard cross-check), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the onboard-charger min, the DC/no-cap path, the efficiency scaling, and seven error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (942 tiles / 56 modules / 964 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 107).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,235 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (942 + 21 shells, 964-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile / check:module-sizes, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(electrical): land spec-v487 -- generator fuel runtime and backup duration; 940 -> 941 tiles, 0.174.0, 2026-07-09

One new tile in `calc-electrical.js` Group A: **`generator-fuel-runtime`**, how long a standby generator runs on its fuel -- the fuel-supply companion to `generator-sizing` (which sizes only the wattage) and the fuel analog of `battery-runtime`. Runtime = usable fuel / consumption, where usable = tank x usable% (default 90%, since a tank is not drawn to the bottom). The pinned example (a 100 gal tank at 3.0 gph) runs **30 hr (1.25 days)**; for a 72-hour outage design basis (a common NFPA 110 / healthcare / telecom basis) it needs **216 gal of fuel and a 240 gal tank**, so the 100 gal tank does not meet it -- and a 400 gal tank runs 120 hr (5 days) and does. Consumption is entered from the genset data plate (a diesel burns ~0.05-0.08 gal/hr per kW at load, read the published fuel curve). Liquid-fuel-tank case; a natural-gas set on a utility feed has no tank limit. A planning aid; the published fuel curve, the fuel quality, and the AHJ's on-site fuel-storage rules govern. Complements `generator-sizing`, `battery-runtime`, and `generator-motor-starting`. Package **0.173.0 -> 0.174.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer (four number fields). calc-electrical.js stays under its gzip cap (96.6%).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (`GOVERNANCE.general`, first-principles -- not an NEC tile), 8 search aliases ("generator runtime", "generator fuel", "fuel runtime", "genset runtime", "backup power runtime", "standby generator fuel", "backup duration", "generator gph"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `generator-sizing` / `battery-runtime` / `generator-motor-starting`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the 30 hr / 72 hr-sizing example and the 400 gal 120 hr cross-check), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the usable-fuel scaling, the target fuel/tank sizing, the meets-target and null-target seams, and six error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (941 tiles / 56 modules / 963 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 106).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,234 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (941 + 21 shells, 963-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile / check:module-sizes, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(trucking): land spec-v486 -- trailer tongue weight and sway check; 939 -> 940 tiles, 0.173.0, 2026-07-09

One new tile in `calc-trucking.js` Group J: **`trailer-tongue-weight`**, the number that decides whether a trailer tows straight or fishtails -- which the gross-weight (`gcwr-check`), axle (`axle-load-distribution`), and tire (`tire-load-check`) tiles never check. From standard towing guidance (NHTSA / SAE J2807 and the hitch/vehicle manufacturer ratings): `tongue% = tongue_weight / trailer_gross x 100`, against the **10-15% conventional (bumper-pull)** and **15-25% gooseneck / fifth-wheel** target bands. The pinned example (a 7,000 lb conventional trailer with a 700 lb tongue) runs **10.0%**, right at the bottom of the band (target window 700-1,050 lb); the cross-check is the sway trap: shift the load rearward to a 490 lb tongue and it drops to **7.0%**, below the 10% floor -- the tile flags the **trailer-sway risk** and the fix (move cargo forward of the trailer axle). Too much tongue overloads the hitch and the tow vehicle's rear axle while it unloads the steer axle. An optional hitch-rating input adds an over-rating flag. The bands are rules of thumb; the vehicle, hitch, and trailer manufacturer ratings and the tow vehicle's payload / rear-axle GAWR govern, and the tongue weight must be a scale reading. Complements `axle-load-distribution`, `gcwr-check`, and `tire-load-check`. Package **0.172.0 -> 0.173.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Registered via the module's `_simpleRenderer` factory (a select + three number fields).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (`GOVERNANCE.general`), 8 search aliases ("tongue weight", "trailer sway", "hitch weight", "tongue percentage", "gooseneck weight", "fifth wheel weight", "trailer balance", "sway control"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `axle-load-distribution` / `gcwr-check` / `tire-load-check`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the 10% in-band and the 7% sway cross-check), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the conventional and gooseneck bands, the over-rating flag, and five error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (940 tiles / 56 modules / 962 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 105).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,233 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (940 + 21 shells, 962-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(mechanic): land spec-v485 -- torque wrench extension / crowfoot correction; 938 -> 939 tiles, 0.172.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-mechanic.js` Group K: **`torque-adapter-correction`**, the wrench setting a crowfoot or in-line extension demands -- the single most common way a tech gets a torque wrong in the field, which `bolt-torque` (the target `T = K D F`) never corrects for. From the standard torque-adapter relation (Snap-on / FAA AC 43.13.1B): the wrench setting `TW = TA x L / (L + E cos(angle))`, where `TA` is the torque wanted at the fastener, `L` is the wrench lever length (drive center to hand-grip center), `E` is the adapter length, and the angle is the adapter's offset from the wrench axis. The pinned example (100 ft-lb target, 18 in wrench, 3 in in-line crowfoot) means **dialing 85.7 ft-lb** -- set the wrench to 100 instead and the fastener sees **116.7 ft-lb, a 17% over-torque** that snaps small fasteners or yields a flange stud. The cross-check is the field workaround: swing the same crowfoot to 90 degrees and `cos(90) = 0`, so the adapter adds no effective length and **no correction is needed**. Measure `L` to where the hand actually pulls; the relation assumes the extension lies in the plane of the swing. A shop aid; the calibrated wrench and the manufacturer's fastener torque spec govern. Complements `bolt-torque`, `flange-bolt-torque`, and `bolt-stretch`. Package **0.171.0 -> 0.172.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Registered via the module's `_simpleRenderer` factory (four number fields).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (`GOVERNANCE.general`), 8 search aliases ("crowfoot", "crow foot", "crows foot", "torque adapter", "torque wrench extension", "wrench setting", "torque correction", "swivel adapter"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `bolt-torque` / `flange-bolt-torque` / `bolt-stretch`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the in-line crowfoot and the 90-degree no-correction cross-check), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the over-torque relation, the 90-degree null, and five error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (939 tiles / 56 modules / 961 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 104).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,232 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (939 + 21 shells, 961-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(rigging): land spec-v484 -- spanned cable sag and tension; 937 -> 938 tiles, 0.171.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-rigging.js` Group Z: **`spanline-sag-tension`**, the tension a horizontally spanned cable (a tramline, highline, span set, or messenger) develops from its sag -- the rigging geometry the bench had never carried beside its slings, shackles, cranes, and spreader beams. From shallow-cable parabola statics: a uniform load `w` over a span `L` sagging `d` at midspan develops a horizontal tension `H = w L^2 / (8 d)`, a support (anchor) tension `T = H sqrt(1 + (4 d / L)^2)`, and a developed length `L + 8 d^2 / (3 L)`. The pinned example (a 100 ft span at 1.0 lb/ft, allowed to sag 2.5 ft) runs **500 lb** horizontal, **502.5 lb** at the supports, 100.17 ft developed; the cross-check is the inverse-sag trap the tile exists to flag: pull the same span tighter to a 0.5 ft sag and the tension jumps to **2,500 lb** -- five times the tension for five times less sag on the exact same load, because tension is inversely proportional to the sag, so pulling a span tight to take out the sag multiplies the rope and anchor load. Shallow-parabola idealization (valid where the sag is under ~1/10 of the span; the tile flags when exceeded), uniform load, level supports; a concentrated load is the `sling-angle` point-load case instead. The wire-rope WLL, the anchor capacity, and the head rigger govern. Complements `sling-angle`, `wire-rope-strength`, and `block-redirect-load`. Package **0.170.0 -> 0.171.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer (three `makeNumber` fields).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (`GOVERNANCE.rigging`), 8 search aliases ("cable sag", "spanline", "highline", "tramline", "catenary sag", "wire rope span", "span tension", "guy wire tension"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `sling-angle` / `wire-rope-strength` / `block-redirect-load`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the 2.5 ft comfortable-sag and the 0.5 ft taut-span cross-check), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the support-tension and developed-length relations, the inverse-sag trap, the shallow-flag boundary, and four error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (938 tiles / 56 modules / 960 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 103).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,231 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (938 + 21 shells, 960-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(hvac): land spec-v483 -- vibration isolation efficiency (ASHRAE); 936 -> 937 tiles, 0.170.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-hvac.js` Group C: **`vibration-isolation`**, whether the isolators under a fan, pump, rooftop unit, or chiller actually cut the vibration -- the equipment-mount number the mechanical set carries but the catalog never had. From the ASHRAE Handbook -- Fundamentals Sound and Vibration single-degree-of-freedom isolator (the Den Hartog transmissibility relation): the isolated system's natural frequency `fn = 3.13 / sqrt(static deflection in inches)` Hz (which is `(1/2pi) sqrt(g/deflection)` with g = 386.4 in/s^2), the disturbing frequency is the running speed `rpm/60`, the transmissibility `T = 1 / |(f/fn)^2 - 1|` is the fraction of the shaking force still reaching the structure, and the isolation efficiency is `(1 - T)`. The pinned example (a 900 rpm fan on 1 in isolators) runs fn 3.13 Hz, ratio 4.79, T 0.046 -> **95.4% efficient**; the cross-check is the amplification trap the tile exists to flag: a slow 200 rpm unit on the same 1 in mount sits at ratio 1.06, below the sqrt(2) = 1.414 isolation threshold, so instead of isolating it **amplifies the vibration 7.45x** (near resonance) -- the fix is a stiffer isolator (less deflection raises fn). The undamped idealization; the rated isolator deflection under load and the mechanical engineer govern. Complements `fan-affinity-laws`, `pump-specific-speed`, and `grille-face-velocity`. Package **0.169.0 -> 0.170.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer (two `makeNumber` fields).

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("vibration isolation", "isolator selection", "transmissibility", "isolation efficiency", "spring isolator", "vibration mount", "natural frequency", "isolator deflection"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `fan-affinity-laws` / `grille-face-velocity` / `pump-specific-speed`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the 900 rpm isolation and the 200 rpm amplification), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the fn deflection scaling, the sqrt(2) isolation threshold, and four error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,180 -> 1,181 corpus rows; dims 1,183 -> 1,184/1,184). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (937 tiles / 56 modules / 959 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 102).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,230 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (937 + 21 shells, 959-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(hvac): land spec-v482 -- ADPI room air diffusion selection (ASHRAE); 935 -> 936 tiles, 0.169.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-hvac.js` Group C, executing the air-distribution follow-on spec-v348 §5 named ("a room-air-distribution (ADPI) check [is a] deliberate next follow-on"): **`adpi-diffuser-selection`**, whether a supply outlet will actually mix the room. It reproduces the ASHRAE Handbook -- Fundamentals "Space Air Diffusion" ADPI Selection Guide (throw per ASHRAE Standard 70, ADPI per Standard 113, the Miller/Nevins Kansas State research): the ratio of the catalog throw T to the room's characteristic length L predicts the Air Diffusion Performance Index, and each outlet type and cooling load (20-80 Btu/hr-ft^2) has a T/L for maximum ADPI and a band over which ADPI stays above the published threshold. The pinned example (circular ceiling diffuser, 40 Btu/hr-ft^2, T0.25 = 8 ft over L = 10 ft) lands at T/L = 0.80 -- exactly the max-ADPI optimum -- reaching **ADPI 88** inside the >80 band of 0.5-1.5; the cross-check shows the load ceiling: a high sidewall grille at a heavy 80 Btu/hr-ft^2 load, thrown to its 1.8 optimum, still reaches only **ADPI 68**, and no T/L reaches the 80 threshold (the band is blank) -- the load, not the throw, caps comfort. The embedded table covers high sidewall, circular ceiling, sill straight/spread, ceiling slot, and perforated outlets; the light-troffer row is omitted because its throw basis (T0.25 vs T0.5) is inconsistent across the published reproductions. All table values web-verified against the Price/Krueger/Titus engineering-guide reproductions of the ASHRAE table before landing. Complements `grille-face-velocity` (which sizes the outlet). Package **0.168.0 -> 0.169.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer (two `makeSelect` + two `makeNumber` fields), mirroring `grille-face-velocity`.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("adpi", "air diffusion performance index", "room air distribution", "throw ratio", "diffuser selection", "throw to length", "space air diffusion", "air mixing"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `grille-face-velocity` / `window-solar-heat-gain` / `duct-velocity-pressure`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the round-ceiling max-ADPI and the high-sidewall load-capped case), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the load-nearest-band snap, the load-independent ceiling-slot and perforated rows, the in-band / out-of-band flags, and four error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,179 -> 1,180 corpus rows; dims 1,182 -> 1,183/1,183). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (936 tiles / 56 modules / 958 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 101).

Also raises the `calc-construction.js` gzip cap in `check-module-sizes.mjs` from 110000 to 132000: the prior two Group E landings (spec-v480 seismic-overturning-moment, spec-v481 stair-code-check) brought that shared module to ~110.6 KB gz, at its cap, and a local zlib measured it 0.5% over; +~20% headroom restores the margin (a per-tile split of the module is the eventual preferred fix). This tile itself is in `calc-hvac.js` and does not touch that module.

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,229 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (936 + 21 shells, 958-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(construction): land spec-v481 -- stair geometry code check (IBC 1011 / IRC R311); 934 -> 935 tiles, 0.168.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-construction.js` Group E, executing the egress follow-on spec-v243 §5 named ("a stair-capacity-vs-tread-geometry cross-check [is a] deliberate future follow-on"): **`stair-code-check`**, whether a stair's riser, tread, and clear width meet the adopted code -- the dimensional counterpart to the existing `guard-handrail-check`. It reports each dimension pass or fail against the selected occupancy: IBC 2021 §1011.5.2 / §1011.2 (commercial) caps the riser at 7 in (4 in minimum), floors the tread at 11 in, and requires 44 in of width (36 in where the occupant load is under 50); IRC 2021 R311.7.5 / R311.7.1 (residential) allows a 7-3/4 in riser and a 10 in tread at 36 in of width. Plus the 2R + T comfort read (the 24-25 in rule of thumb, reported not enforced). The pinned commercial example (7 in / 11 in / 44 in) passes all three with 2R + T = 25 in; the cross-check turns on the code difference itself -- a 7-3/4 in riser passes IRC but fails IBC (`riser_ok` 0), the exact 3/4 in a residential remodel gets away with and a tenant-improvement stair is red-tagged for. Distinct from `stairs` (carpentry layout from a total rise), `guard-handrail-check` (the guard/handrail beside it), and `egress-capacity` (the occupant-load width). Package **0.167.0 -> 0.168.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Uses the existing `_simpleRenderer` factory (one occupancy select, three number fields), mirroring `guard-handrail-check`.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("stair code", "riser height", "tread depth", "stair rise and run", "7-11 rule", "2r plus t", "stair riser tread", "ibc stair"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `stairs` / `guard-handrail-check` / `egress-capacity`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures (the IBC pass and the IRC/IBC riser flip), a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the riser-flip fail, the 2R+T value, the too-narrow-tread/width case, and four error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,178 -> 1,179 corpus rows; dims 1,181 -> 1,182/1,182). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (935 tiles / 56 modules / 957 sitemap URLs) plus the ungated corpus/dims/test figures and the status-paragraph refs, the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the mobile-responsive running record (section 100).

Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,228 unit tests, data:verify (117), build (935 + 21 shells, 957-URL sitemap), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, and targeted Playwright (render-no-nan + a11y) on the new tile.

### feat(construction): land spec-v480 -- seismic overturning moment (ASCE 7-22 12.8.5); 933 -> 934 tiles, 0.167.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-construction.js` Group E, executing the seismic follow-on spec-v477 §5 named explicitly ("the §12.8.5 overturning accumulation ... [is a] deliberate future follow-on"): **`seismic-overturning-moment`**, the overturning the footing and the hold-downs resist. From the same §12.8.3 story forces `Fx = Cvx V` the `seismic-vertical-distribution` tile computes, it accumulates the base overturning moment `M0 = Sum(Fi hi)`, the moment about each floor level (`Sum` over the forces above the level of `Fi (hi - hx)`), and the reduced foundation moment `0.75 M0` that ASCE 7-22 §12.13.4 permits at the soil-foundation interface. The pinned three-story example (V = 200 kips, T = 0.4 s, k = 1, Fx 37.0 / 74.1 / 88.9 kips at 12 / 24 / 36 ft) carries **5,422 kip-ft at the base** (per-level 5,422 / 3,022 / 1,067) and **4,067 kip-ft reduced**; the two-story cross-check (V = 100 kips, T = 1.0 s, k = 1.25 interpolated) carries 1,655 kip-ft (1,242 reduced), the longer period lifting the force centroid and the overturning with it. This closes the equivalent-lateral-force load path from the base shear to the footing that the catalog's four other ELF tiles (`seismic-base-shear`, `seismic-vertical-distribution`, `seismic-story-drift`, `seismic-pdelta-stability`) left open; it is distinct from `shearwall-overturning`, which is the SDPWS single-shear-wall element check. Package **0.166.0 -> 0.167.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Hand-writes its renderer mirroring the sibling `seismic-vertical-distribution` (two `makeNumber` fields and one `makeTextarea` bottom-up level list per the `check-multiline-inputs` rule).

### feat(firesprinkler): land spec-v479 -- sprinkler pressure demand at the base of riser (NFPA 13); 932 -> 933 tiles, 0.166.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-firesprinkler.js` Group F, executing the sprinkler-demand follow-on the v249 tile's own citation named ("a full hydraulic calculation to the most-remote area including friction and elevation ... is a separate analysis"): **`sprinkler-pressure-demand`**, the pressure the water supply must deliver at the base of the riser to drive the hydraulically most remote sprinkler. The compute assembles three published pieces: the K-factor start pressure at the end head `P1 = (Q_head / K)^2` (the discharge relation `Q = K sqrt(P)`), the NFPA 13 Hazen-Williams friction loss `p = 4.52 Q^1.85 / (C^1.85 d^4.87)` psi per foot carried over the governing run's equivalent length, and the `0.433 psi/ft` elevation head to lift the water. The pinned OH2 example -- a 26 gpm K-5.6 head (21.6 psi) with 260 gpm through 150 equivalent feet of 3 in Schedule 40 steel (C = 120, 12.1 psi of friction) and a 15 ft lift (6.5 psi) -- lands at **40.1 psi at 260 gpm**, the point that must fall under the water-supply curve and the `fire-pump-curve` rated point. The CPVC cross-check (C = 150) trims the friction to 8.0 psi (36.0 psi demand), the ~4 psi the smoother pipe is worth. The 7 psi standard-spray minimum operating pressure is flagged, not enforced; elevation is signed (a head below the riser subtracts lift). This assembles one representative flowing path; a full stamped design balances every node, branch, and grid loop, which stays the fire-protection engineer's hydraulic model. Ties the fire-sprinkler bench together: `sprinkler-system-demand` gives the gpm and gallons, `fire-pump-curve` gives the pump, and this gives the pressure those two must meet. Package **0.165.1 -> 0.166.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("base of riser", "sprinkler pressure demand", "sprinkler hydraulic calculation", "hazen williams sprinkler", "most remote sprinkler", "sprinkler friction loss", "sprinkler system pressure", "nfpa 13 hydraulic"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `sprinkler-system-demand` / `sprinkler-k-factor` / `fire-pump-curve`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the below-7-psi flag, the signed-elevation and zero-length identities, and seven error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (933 tiles / 56 modules / 955 sitemap URLs), the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the status paragraph.

### fix(hvac): drop the slashed token from the snowmelt-load title -- the CI-only 320 px WebKit overflow; 0.165.1, 2026-07-08

A one-tile title fix closing the v478 landing's CI failure, plus the diagnostics that found it. The `webkit-responsive` "every live tile view" sweep failed on `snowmelt-load` with a deterministic `scrollWidth 328 > clientWidth 320` (runs 28957688710 x2, 28963302008, 28964965504 -- every attempt, always exactly 8 px) that no faithful local environment could reproduce: macOS WebKit, the v1.59.1 Playwright image (direct nav and the full 932-tile sweep), bare ubuntu:24.04 with CI's exact `playwright install --with-deps webkit` recipe (direct nav and the full sweep), and an amd64-emulated run were all clean. Three diagnostic steps, each landed as a permanent hardening of the gate: (1) a 150 ms re-measure so a mid-render transient cannot veto the merge (the Lighthouse median-of-3 principle, da7986b); (2) an element-box culprit dump -- which came back **empty** in the failing run, proving no element rect crossed the edge; (3) a develop-branch text-line probe (Range.getClientRects over every text node) that named the culprit: one line box of the H1 view title reaching right = 328. Root cause: the tile name's parenthetical **"(ASHRAE/Chapman)" is a 16-character run with no legal break point** (WebKit does not break at an unspaced slash), and at the 320 px H1 size (1.7 rem, weight 800) the GitHub runner's font substitution renders it wider than the 254 px column -- the same slash-token wrap class the v199-v203 landing already recorded for citation formulas, now demonstrated for tile names. Local font stacks render the same token ~40 px narrower, which is why six local environments could not see it.

The fix renames the tile to **"Hydronic Snowmelt Load and Boiler Sizing (ASHRAE)"** (the Chapman attribution stays in the citation, the catalog description, and the spec); a catalog-wide scan found no other name token both 15+ characters and break-free (the existing long tokens all carry hyphens, which are legal break points). The sweep keeps all the new diagnostics permanently: re-measure-before-report, and a culprit dump that now sweeps **text-node line rects** as well as element boxes, since an unbreakable inline token overflows the line box while its block's border-box rect stays viewport-bounded -- exactly the blind spot that made this failure look like a phantom. Package **0.165.0 -> 0.165.1**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it. Verified: 28 lint gates, 5,225 unit tests, build, and the full 932-tile WebKit sweep locally (exit 0).

### feat(hvac): land spec-v478 -- hydronic snowmelt surface load and boiler sizing (ASHRAE/Chapman); 931 -> 932 tiles, 0.165.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-hvac.js` Group C, executing the snowmelt follow-on spec-v199 §6 named and closing the exterior end of the hydronic bench: **`snowmelt-load`**, the heated-driveway / walk / ramp sizing balance. The compute carries the ASHRAE Handbook (HVAC Applications, Snow Melting and Freeze Protection) steady-state surface flux `q_o = q_s + q_m + A_r(q_h + q_e)` with the Chapman (1956) IP component forms as printed in Lund, "Pavement Snow Melting" (Geo-Heat Center / OSTI, the free-access primary): warm the snow to the 33 F film (`2.6 s (33 - t_a)`), melt it (`746 s`), and over the snow-free fraction A_r pay the exposed wet surface's convective (`11.4 (0.0201 V + 0.055)(33 - t_a)`) and evaporative (`h_fg (0.0201 V + 0.055)(0.188 - p_av)`) losses, with h_fg = 1,075.5 Btu/lb at the film (a steam-table value -- Lund prints h_fg symbolic, so the constant is cited to the steam table, not to Lund) and the ambient vapor pressure from the August-Roche-Magnus curve (the same coefficients `surface-condensation-risk` carries) times RH. A_r is Chapman's class choice (0 residential / 0.5 commercial / 1.0 critical) and is the tile's design story: the same 20 F, 10 mph, 0.1 in/hr storm runs 78.0 / 110.7 / 143.5 Btu/hr-ft^2 across the three classes -- the pinned Class II value sits squarely in the published 97-298 city-table band. Boiler output adds the ~20% ASHRAE-typical back-and-edge loss (66.4 MBH over the example's 500 ft^2), and the Chapman mean-fluid rule of thumb (~0.5 q_o + 33 F) rounds out the outputs. All formulas, constants, classes, and typical-output ranges web-verified against Lund's PDF text, the ASHRAE chapter, and the hydronics literature before landing (one correction adopted from the source: the melt constant is the published **746**, and the sensible coefficient the published **2.6**, not the 746.9 / 2.5 sometimes quoted). Package **0.164.0 -> 0.165.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("snowmelt", "snow melting system", "heated driveway", "snow melt btu", "hydronic snow melting", "heated sidewalk", "snow free area ratio", "driveway heating load"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `radiant-loop-sizing` / `radiant-floor-output` / `boiler-pipe-sizing`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the A_r = 0 losses-drop-out identity, the at-the-film evaporation floor, and eight error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,175 -> 1,176 corpus rows; dims 1,178 -> 1,179/1,179). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (932 tiles / 56 modules / 954 sitemap URLs), the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the status paragraph.

### feat(construction): land spec-v477 -- vertical distribution of seismic forces (ASCE 7-22 12.8.3/12.8.4); 930 -> 931 tiles, 0.164.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-construction.js` Group E, executing the follow-on both spec-v226 §5 and spec-v383's header explicitly deferred ("a vertical force-distribution tile (`Fx = Cvx*V`, §12.8.3) that supplies the per-story `Vx` is the deliberate next follow-on"): **`seismic-vertical-distribution`**, the missing middle of the in-catalog ELF chain. From the base shear V and period T it computes the exponent k (1 at or below T = 0.5 s, 2 at or above 2.5 s, linearly interpolated between -- the standard's k = 2-outright alternative is named in the citation), the per-level `Cvx = wx hx^k / Sum(wi hi^k)` and `Fx = Cvx x V` (Eqs. 12.8-11/-12), and the story shears `Vx` by summing the forces at and above each level (Eq. 12.8-13), with the built-in check that the base story carries the full V. Levels enter one per line, bottom-up, as `weight_kips,height_ft` through a `makeTextarea` field per the `check-multiline-inputs` rule; the hand-written renderer mirrors `occupant-load`. Pinned example: 3-story, V = 200 kips, T = 0.4 s -> Fx 37.0 / 74.1 / 88.9 kips; cross-check at T = 1.06 s (the SEAOC/Tedds verification period, k = 1.28) shifts the roof force to 97.5 kips -- the k exponent, not just V, decides the upper-story connections. Formulas and the k-rule wording web-verified against the standard's text (Bentley RAM / Tekla Tedds / SEAOC reproductions) before landing. Package **0.163.0 -> 0.164.0**; `mcp/package.json` moves with it.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry, 8 search aliases ("vertical distribution seismic", "seismic story forces", "cvx", "story shear", "ASCE 12.8.3", "equivalent lateral force distribution", "k exponent seismic", "seismic force per floor"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `seismic-base-shear` / `seismic-story-drift` / `seismic-pdelta-stability`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples' full per-level arrays, the Sum Fx = V identity, the k boundaries (0.5 / 2.5 / interpolation midpoint), the single-level degenerate case, and seven error seams (including non-increasing bottom-up heights), the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,174 -> 1,175 corpus rows; dims 1,177 -> 1,178/1,178). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (931 tiles / 56 modules / 953 sitemap URLs), the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the status paragraph.

### feat(construction): land spec-v476 -- concrete maturity and equivalent age (ASTM C1074); 929 -> 930 tiles, 0.163.0, 2026-07-08

One new tile in `calc-construction.js` Group E, executing the maturity-method follow-on that spec-v247 §5 explicitly deferred: **`concrete-maturity`**, the temperature-honest cure schedule beside the calendar-age curve of `concrete-strength-gain`. The compute carries both ASTM C1074 maturity functions over one constant-temperature interval -- the Nurse-Saul time-temperature factor `M = (Ta - T0) x hours` in deg C-hr (datum T0 = 0 C / 32 F for Type I cement without admixtures, editable), and the Arrhenius equivalent age `te = exp(-Q x (1/Ta - 1/Tr)) x hours` with temperatures in kelvin (Q = 5000 K Type I no-admixture, reference Tr = 20 C / 68 F traditional per C1074, both editable) -- plus the inverse solve for the hours remaining to the mix's lab-calibrated TTF target. The pinned example: a 50 F week accrues 1,680 C-hr and only 3.83 equivalent days (the cold slab is not "7 days old"); the cross-check: three days at 90 F carry 5.94 equivalent days. The citation states that a TTF maps to strength only through the C1074 lab calibration of the actual mix and that the method supplements, not replaces, acceptance cylinders (NRMCA CIP 39). Constants web-verified against the standard's text before landing. Package **0.162.1 -> 0.163.0** (a tile addition); `mcp/package.json` moves with it.

Wiring per the standing per-tile checklist: the `tools-data.js` catalog row, `tile-meta.js` entry, `citations.js` entry (GOVERNANCE.general, editionNote naming both maturity functions and their recommended constants), 8 search aliases ("concrete maturity", "maturity method", "ASTM C1074", "nurse saul", "time temperature factor", "concrete equivalent age", "maturity meter", "cold weather concrete strength"), the `related-tiles.mjs` row (-> `concrete-strength-gain` / `concrete-evaporation-rate` / `concrete-mix-design`), the `compute-map.js` entry, two `worked-examples.json` fixtures, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block pinning both examples, the at-reference identity (age factor exactly 1 at 68 F), and seven error seams, the `// dims:` annotation, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index (1,173 -> 1,174 corpus rows; dims 1,176 -> 1,177/1,177). Count surfaces re-pinned: README label-anchored counts (930 tiles / 56 modules / 952 sitemap URLs), the 8 hardcoded `index.html` home-copy counts, and the status paragraph. This pass also corrects a pre-existing README drift the 2026-07-05 resync introduced: the correctness-posture table's Phase A/D corpus figures said **1,227** while `docs/derivations.md` itself printed `Row count: 1173.` at that commit; both now carry the live 1,174.

### fix(ci): assert Lighthouse budgets on the median of 3 runs, not a single sample, 2026-07-08

A CI-robustness fix; no tile, calculator output, or shipped-bundle byte changes. CI run 28948374513 (the docs-only audit-trail commit) failed its Lighthouse job with home-page CLS **0.0908** against the 0.05 budget -- on shipped bytes byte-identical to the runs that passed 25 minutes before and 40 minutes after it (pass / fail / pass on the same `dist/`). The uploaded report attributes the whole shift to `main#main` with no source breakdown, and the shift does not reproduce: 3 local `lhci` runs, 6 instrumented Chromium runs at 8x CPU throttle with a buffered `layout-shift` PerformanceObserver (zero entries), and the very next CI run all measure CLS 0. Diagnosis: single-sample measurement noise on a loaded shared runner -- `numberOfRuns: 1` gave one noisy trace veto power over the merge signal.

The fix collects **3 runs per URL** (12 total) and pins `aggregationMethod: "median"` on both assert-matrix entries. Median was chosen over lhci's default `optimistic` (best-of-N, which would hide a real regression that shows in 2 of 3 runs) and over `pessimistic` (worst-of-N, which re-introduces exactly this one-bad-trace veto): a genuine layout shift in the page reproduces in every trace and still fails the median, while a one-off runner hiccup no longer does. Every budget value is unchanged. Verified locally with the exact CI invocation (`lhci autorun`, 12 runs, all assertions pass, exit 0); the Lighthouse job's wall-clock rises from ~1.5 to a still-bounded ~4 minutes.

### docs: resync ungated README figures to the 929-tile catalog, 2026-07-05

A documentation-only accuracy pass; no tile, code-path, or version change. A repo-wide relative-markdown-link audit found zero dead links in the living docs (the 81 in CHANGELOG.md and one in the closed spec-v11 point at files the spec-v107 cut deliberately deleted and stay as written, per the append-only record). All gates green: lint (27 checks at **929 tiles / 56 modules / 951 sitemap URLs**), **5,222** unit tests, build (929 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify (117 entries), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`.

### feat(electrical): land spec-v471..v474 -- energy-economics trio + ADA ramp; 925 -> 929 tiles, 0.162.0, 2026-07-04 (v375-v474 campaign complete)

The final batch of the v375-v474 campaign: three electrical energy-economics tiles in `calc-electrical.js` (Group A) plus the ADA ramp-layout tile in `calc-construction.js` (Group E). **This closes the second 100-tile campaign (v375-v474).** Catalog **925 -> 929**, package **0.161.0 -> 0.162.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`motor-efficiency-upgrade-savings` (spec-v471).** Premium-motor retrofit saving: (kW at old eff - kW at new) x hours x rate. Pinned: a 50 hp / 75% / 90->94.5% / 4,000 hr / $0.12 -> ~$710/yr; at full load ~$947. Distinct from `motor-operating-cost` (one motor's cost, not the two-efficiency comparison).
- **`transformer-loading-efficiency` (spec-v472).** Core + copper losses: efficiency = output / (output + no-load + load^2 x copper). Pinned: 75 kVA / 200 W / 1,200 W / 75% -> 98.47%, peak near 41% load.
- **`economic-conductor-sizing` (spec-v473).** I^2R payback: saving = (3 I^2 R_small - 3 I^2 R_big) x hours x rate; payback = cost / saving. Pinned: 100 A / 0.20->0.125 ohm -> $1,080/yr, 0.7 yr; at 40 A -> 4.6 yr.
- **`ada-ramp-slope` (spec-v474).** IBC 1012 / ADA ramp layout: run = rise x ratio (1:12 max), runs = ceil(rise/30), landings, total length, handrails if rise > 6 in. Pinned: 24 in -> one 24 ft run; 40 in -> 2 runs + landing = 45 ft. Distinct from the slope-check `ramp-slope` (this lays out the multi-run ramp).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the load-scaling saving, the copper-loss square law, the I^2R payback, the multi-run ramp with landings, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline. The three electrical tiles are hand-written renderers; the ramp tile uses `_simpleRenderer`. Module cap `calc-electrical.js` bumped 67000 -> 72000 (the three energy tiles took it over the old cap). Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 925 -> 929, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 947 -> 951), four specs LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 94 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the four new tiles.

**Campaign v375-v474 complete:** across the two 100-tile spec campaigns' second half, this batch of specs proposed 100 tiles; ~86 landed and ~14 were cut as duplicates of existing tiles (each cut recorded in its spec's Status line). The catalog now stands at **929 tiles**.

### feat(structural): land spec-v468..v470 -- 3 ASCE 7 snow-provision tiles in calc-construction.js; 922 -> 925 tiles, 0.161.0, 2026-07-04

The ASCE 7 snow-provisions trio, thirtieth batch of the v375-v474 campaign, all in the existing lazy `calc-construction.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. All three are ASCE 7 Chapter 7 provisions the existing `snow-load` (balanced Pf) and `snow-drift-load` (drift) tiles never covered. Catalog **922 -> 925**, package **0.160.0 -> 0.161.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`rain-on-snow-surcharge` (spec-v468).** ASCE 7-22 §7.10: surcharge added when Pg <= 20 psf and slope (deg) < W/50. Pinned: Pf 15 / Pg 18 / low slope -> +8 -> 23 psf; a deep-snow region (Pg 25) takes no surcharge (stays 15).
- **`sliding-snow-load` (spec-v469).** ASCE 7 §7.9: total = 0.4 x upper Pf x W (lb/ft) over the lesser of 15 ft or the lower-roof width. Pinned: 20 psf / 40 ft upper -> 320 lb/ft = 21.3 psf over 15 ft; a narrow 10 ft catch roof -> 32 psf.
- **`minimum-roof-snow` (spec-v470).** ASCE 7 §7.3.4: Pm = Is x Pg (Pg <= 20) or 20 x Is; design = max(Pm, computed Pf). Pinned: Pg 15 / Is 1.0 -> 15 psf; Pg 25 / Is 1.1 -> 22 psf.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the surcharge trigger, the narrow-roof concentration, the Pm cap, the governing max, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming ASCE 7-22 §7.10, §7.9, and §7.3.4. All three use the shared `_simpleRenderer`. Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 922 -> 925, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 944 -> 947), three specs LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 93 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(roofing): land spec-v467 -- powered attic ventilator sizing (v465/v466 cut as dupes); 921 -> 922 tiles, 0.160.0, 2026-07-04

The attic-ventilation trio, twenty-ninth batch of the v375-v474 campaign. Two of three were **cut as duplicates** of the existing `attic-ventilation` (Attic Ventilation Net Free Area, calc-finish.js), which already computes the IRC 1/150 and 1/300 net free area, the 50/50 intake/exhaust split, the soffit-vent count, and the ridge-vent length: v465 `attic-ventilation-nfva` (the NFVA) and v466 `ridge-soffit-vent-linear` (the split-to-linear-feet) -- so only one landed. Catalog **921 -> 922**, package **0.159.0 -> 0.160.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`powered-attic-ventilator` (spec-v467, calc-construction.js).** Fan CFM = attic area x ~0.7 CFM/ft^2 x (dark roof ? 1.15 : 1.0), matching intake = fan CFM / 300. Pinned: a 1,500 ft^2 attic -> 1,050 CFM fan, 3.5 ft^2 (504 in^2) intake; a dark roof -> 1,208 CFM. Distinct from the passive-NFVA `attic-ventilation` (this sizes an active fan and its required intake).

Carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, a fuzzer block covering the dark-roof factor, the boolean/string dark input, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming attic-fan sizing practice and the intake-per-CFM rule. Uses the shared `_simpleRenderer` (dark-roof as a yes/no `makeSelect`). Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 921 -> 922, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 943 -> 944), one spec LANDED + two CUT, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 92 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the new tile.

### feat(mechanic): land spec-v462..v464 -- 3 marine/engine/electrical tiles in calc-mechanic.js; 918 -> 921 tiles, 0.159.0, 2026-07-04

The mechanic trio, twenty-eighth batch of the v375-v474 campaign, all in the existing lazy `calc-mechanic.js` (Group K); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **918 -> 921**, package **0.158.0 -> 0.159.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`prop-pitch-selection` (spec-v462).** Marine prop repitch: new pitch = current - (target - current WOT RPM) / rpm-per-inch (~200). Pinned: a 19 in prop hitting 5000 rpm against a 5400 target -> 17 in; over-revving to 6000 -> 22 in. Distinct from `prop-slip` (which measures slip, not pitch selection).
- **`engine-fuel-burn-gph` (spec-v463).** BSFC burn: gph = HP x BSFC / fuel density, run time = tank / gph. Pinned: a 300 hp diesel at BSFC 0.37 -> 15.6 GPH (12.8 hr on 200 gal); the same power gasoline (0.50) -> 24.6 GPH, 58% more. Distinct from `fuel-range` (energy/range) and `injector-size` (per-injector flow).
- **`alternator-charging-load` (spec-v464).** Charging balance: output = alternator x (~0.5 idle / ~0.9 cruise), balance = output - load. Pinned: a 65 A load on a 120 A alternator -> -5 A at idle (drains), +43 A at cruise; a 160 A alternator -> +15 A idle.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the under/over-rev direction, the diesel-vs-gasoline burn, the idle/cruise balance sign, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming outboard prop-selection practice, BSFC engine-performance, and alternator output-curve practice. All three use the shared `_simpleRenderer`. Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 918 -> 921, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 940 -> 943), three specs LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 91 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(hvac): land spec-v461 -- residential duct leakage CFM25 (v459/v460 cut as dupes); 917 -> 918 tiles, 0.158.0, 2026-07-04

The HVAC field-test trio, twenty-seventh batch of the v375-v474 campaign. Two of three were **cut as duplicates**: v459 `gas-appliance-altitude-derate` duplicates the existing `gas-altitude-derate` (same NFPA 54 / IFGC 4%-per-1000-ft-above-2000-ft derate), and v460 `duct-equivalent-diameter` duplicates the existing `round-to-rect-duct` (same ASHRAE equal-friction De = 1.30 (ab)^0.625/(a+b)^0.25 and aspect ratio) -- so only one landed. Catalog **917 -> 918**, package **0.157.0 -> 0.158.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`duct-leakage-cfm25` (spec-v461, calc-hvacservice.js).** IECC R403.3.5 residential duct tightness: normalized = leakage CFM25 / conditioned floor area x 100, against the code limit (4 total / post-construction, 3 rough-in without the air handler). Pinned: 80 CFM25 on 2,000 ft^2 -> exactly 4.0 (pass at the limit); 60 -> 3.0 (pass), 100 -> 5.0 (fail). Distinct from the SMACNA leakage-class `duct-leakage` (commercial).

Carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, a fuzzer block covering the normalization, the pass/fail flip, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming IECC R403.3.5 / RESNET. Uses the shared `_simpleRenderer`. Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Also bumped the `calc-lowvoltage.js` module-size cap 11000 -> 13000 (a dated comment): the prior spec-v456..v458 AV/security batch had pushed it to ~11.8 KB gz, over the old cap. Housekeeping: home count 917 -> 918, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 939 -> 940), one spec LANDED + two CUT, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 90 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the new tile.

### feat(lowvoltage): land spec-v456..v458 -- 3 AV / security / data tiles in calc-lowvoltage.js; 914 -> 917 tiles, 0.157.0, 2026-07-04

The low-voltage AV/security/data trio, twenty-sixth batch of the v375-v474 campaign, all in the existing lazy `calc-lowvoltage.js` (Group A); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **914 -> 917**, package **0.156.0 -> 0.157.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`camera-lens-fov` (spec-v456).** IEC 62676-4 DORI: FOV = 2 atan(sensor / 2 focal), scene = distance x sensor / focal, ppf = pixels / scene, banded Detect 8 / Observe 19 / Recognize 38 / Identify 76 ppf. Pinned: a 5.37 mm sensor / 4 mm lens at 30 ft / 1920 px -> 67.7 deg FOV, 40.3 ft scene, 47.7 ppf (Recognize); an 8 mm lens -> 95 ppf (Identify).
- **`ceiling-speaker-coverage` (spec-v457).** diameter = 2 (ceiling - ear) tan(angle/2); spacing = diameter (edge-to-edge) or 0.7 x diameter (minimum overlap); count = ceil(area / spacing^2). Pinned: a 10 ft ceiling / 4 ft ear / 90 deg / 1200 ft^2 -> 12 ft circle, 9 speakers edge-to-edge; at minimum overlap (8.4 ft) **18** (the spec prose's 17 came from rounding 8.4^2 to 70.6; the exact ceil(1200/70.56) is 18, which is what landed).
- **`structured-cabling-channel` (spec-v458).** TIA-568: 100 m channel = 90 m permanent link + <=10 m cords, the link de-rating above 20 deg C. Pinned: an 85 m link / 8 m cords at 20 deg C passes (93 m channel); at 40 deg C the max link de-rates to 82.8 m so the 85 m link fails.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the DORI bands, both speaker layouts, the temperature de-rate pass/fail, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming IEC 62676-4, commercial-audio coverage geometry, and ANSI/TIA-568. All three are hand-written renderers (the speaker tile with a layout `makeSelect`). Note: `security` was dropped from the trades lists (not an established trade slug) -- the two surveillance tiles use `["electrical"]` like `cctv-storage`. Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 914 -> 917, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 936 -> 939), three specs LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 89 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(fabrication): land spec-v453, v454 -- 2 fabrication-layout tiles (v455 cut as dupe) in calc-construction.js; 912 -> 914 tiles, 0.156.0, 2026-07-04

The fabrication-layout trio, twenty-fifth batch of the v375-v474 campaign, both in the existing lazy `calc-construction.js` (Group E). The third tile, v455 `bend-deduction-setback`, was **cut as a duplicate** of the existing `bend-allowance`, which already outputs the bend allowance, outside setback (OSSB), and bend deduction (BD = 2 OSSB - BA) -- so only two landed. Catalog **912 -> 914**, package **0.155.0 -> 0.156.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`intermittent-fillet-weld` (spec-v453).** AISC 360 J2.2b / AWS D1.1 stitch-weld schedule: weld a fraction w_req/w of the length at the larger stitch size, pitch = increment / fraction, each increment >= max(4 x weld size, 1.5 in). Pinned: a 3/16 in required weld done as 5/16 in stitches -> weld 60% of the length, a 3 in increment gives a 5 in pitch (weld 3, skip 2) and clears the 1.5 in minimum.
- **`multi-bend-flat-pattern` (spec-v454).** Developed length: flat = mold_line - n_bends x BD (BD per bend from bend-allowance). Pinned: a U-channel 8 in mold-line / 2 bends / 0.1355 in BD -> 7.73 in; a hat section 12 in / 4 bends -> 11.46 in.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the strength-match fraction and pitch, the minimum-increment threshold, the multi-bend developed length, and every error seam incl. the required-larger-than-stitch and deduction-exceeds-mold-line guards), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming AISC 360 J2.2b / AWS D1.1 and sheet-metal developed-length practice. Both use the shared `_simpleRenderer`. Per-tile wiring across nine registries. Housekeeping: home count 912 -> 914, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 934 -> 936), two specs LANDED + one CUT, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 88 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the two new tiles.

### feat(plumbing): land spec-v450, v452 -- 2 plumbing-systems tiles (v451 cut as dupe); 910 -> 912 tiles, 0.155.0, 2026-07-04

The plumbing-systems trio, twenty-fourth batch of the v375-v474 campaign. The middle tile, v451 `hydronic-expansion-tank`, was **cut as a duplicate** of the existing `expansion-tank` (Hydronic Expansion Tank, calc-plumbing.js), which computes the same diaphragm-tank acceptance volume Vt = Ve/(1 - (Pi+14.7)/(Pf+14.7)) and additionally derives the expansion factor from the fill/operating temperatures -- so only two landed. Catalog **910 -> 912**, package **0.154.0 -> 0.155.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`cross-connection-air-gap` (spec-v450, calc-cross.js).** IPC 608.15.1: minimum air gap = max(2 x opening, 1 in), or 3 x opening within three diameters of a wall; checks a measured gap against the requirement. Pinned: a 1 in opening -> 2 in (3 in near a wall); a 1/2 in opening -> 1 in (the absolute floor governs). The most positive backflow protection there is.
- **`hydronic-fill-pressure` (spec-v452, calc-plumbing.js).** Static-height fill: fill = height / 2.31 + margin (2.31 ft of water = 1 psi), a common margin ~4 psi. Pinned: a 30 ft system -> 17.0 psi (4 psi left at the top); a 35 ft system -> 19.2 psi.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the 2x/3x branch and 1 in floor, the static-lift and margin, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming IPC 608.15.1 / ASME A112.1.2 (with the IPC edition disclosure for CF-01) and hydronic static-head practice. The air-gap tile is hand-written in calc-cross.js with a `makeCheckbox` for the near-wall branch and an optional measured-gap pass/fail. Per-tile wiring across nine registries (`tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, worked-examples, aliases, `related-tiles`, `app.js` in two module declare blocks, two fuzzer blocks). Housekeeping: home count 910 -> 912, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 932 -> 934), two specs LANDED + one CUT, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 87 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the two new tiles.

### feat(structural): land spec-v447..v449 -- 3 member-capacity tiles (concrete torsion, glulam Cv, masonry anchor) across calc-concrete/construction/masonry.js; 907 -> 910 tiles, 0.154.0, 2026-07-04

The structural member-capacity trio, twenty-third batch of the v375-v474 campaign, one tile each in three existing lazy Group E modules; no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **907 -> 910**, package **0.153.0 -> 0.154.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`concrete-torsion-threshold` (spec-v447, calc-concrete.js).** ACI 318-19 §22.7: Tth = lambda x sqrt(f'c) x (Acp^2/pcp), neglect torsion below phi x Tth (phi = 0.75), cracking Tcr = 4 x Tth. Pinned: a 12x20 in / 4000 psi beam -> Tth 4.74 ft-kip, neglect 3.56, Tcr 18.97; an 18x24 in beam -> 11.7 ft-kip (the spec prose's 14.6 was a math slip; **11.7 is correct and landed**).
- **`glulam-volume-factor` (spec-v448, calc-construction.js).** NDS 5.3.6: Cv = KL x (21/L)^(1/x) x (12/d)^(1/x) x (5.125/b)^(1/x), capped at 1.0, x=10 softwood / 20 Southern Pine (a select). Pinned: 5.125x18 in / 20 ft -> Cv 0.965; a 6.75x24 in / 32 ft girder -> 0.870. Allowable bending uses the lesser of Cv and CL.
- **`masonry-anchor-bolt` (spec-v449, calc-masonry.js).** TMS 402 ASD: lesser of masonry breakout Bab = 1.25 x Apt x sqrt(f'm) (Apt = pi x lbe^2) and steel Bas = 0.6 x Ab x fy. Pinned: a 3/4in anchor 4in deep in 1500 psi -> Bab 2,433 lb (governs), Bas 9,547 lb; at 8in embed Bab 9,733 lb, so the 9,547 lb steel governs.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering both examples, the lightweight/species/deep-embed swing, the Cv 1.0 cap, the governing switch, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming ACI 318-19 §22.7, NDS 2018 §5.3.6, and TMS 402 ASD. Per-tile wiring across nine registries (`tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, worked-examples, aliases, `related-tiles`, `app.js` in three separate module declare blocks, and three fuzzer blocks). Housekeeping: home count 907 -> 910, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 929 -> 932), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 86 appended. All gates green: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(accounting): land spec-v444..v446 -- 3 contractor-cost tiles (surety bond, workers-comp EMR, prevailing-wage fringe) in calc-accounting.js; 904 -> 907 tiles, 0.153.0, 2026-07-04

The contractor-cost trio, twenty-second batch of the v375-v474 campaign, all in the existing lazy `calc-accounting.js` (Group R); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **904 -> 907**, package **0.152.0 -> 0.153.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`surety-bond-premium` (spec-v444).** Tiered premium per $1,000 of contract: default $25/$15/$10 on the first $100k / next $400k / above $500k, so the effective rate falls as the contract grows. Pinned: a $500,000 contract -> $8,500 (1.70%); a $2.5M job -> $28,500 (1.14%, the top band blends lower). A bid cost that belongs in the markup.
- **`workers-comp-emr-premium` (spec-v445).** Manual premium = payroll/100 x class rate, actual = manual x EMR. Pinned: $500k payroll, $8/$100, EMR 0.85 -> $40,000 manual, $34,000 modified ($6.80/$100), a $6,000 credit; a bad-safety EMR 1.15 -> $46,000, a $6,000 debit. A low EMR both cuts premium and qualifies bid lists.
- **`prevailing-wage-fringe` (spec-v446).** Package = base + fringe; paying the fringe as cash makes it taxable wages, funding it through a bona-fide plan does not, saving fringe x the wage-based burden per hour. Pinned: $35 base + $15 fringe at 7.65% -> $50 package, $1.15/hr saved; a fuller 15% burden saves $2.25/hr.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the tiered/blended premium, the EMR credit-vs-debit swing, both fringe modes and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming surety rate schedules, NCCI-style experience rating, and Davis-Bacon 29 CFR 5.28. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data` (Group R), `tile-meta`, `citations` (GOVERNANCE.general), `compute-map`, worked-example fixtures, collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, 3 fuzzer blocks (all hand-written renderers), and the `ACCOUNTING_RENDERERS exposes all N utilities` count bumped 22 -> 25. Housekeeping: home count 904 -> 907, README resynced (counts, sitemap URLs 926 -> 929), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 85 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint, unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(hvac): land spec-v441..v443 -- 3 energy-recovery / hydronic / economizer tiles in calc-hvac.js; 901 -> 904 tiles, 0.152.0, 2026-07-04

The HVAC energy-recovery / hydronic / economizer trio, twenty-first batch of the v375-v474 campaign, all in the existing lazy `calc-hvac.js` (Group C); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **901 -> 904**, package **0.151.0 -> 0.152.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`erv-total-enthalpy-recovery` (spec-v441).** Q = 4.5 x CFM x effectiveness x (h_oa - h_ra), supply enthalpy from the effectiveness. Pinned: 1000 CFM, 0.75, 38 -> 28 Btu/lb -> 33,750 Btu/hr recovered, 30.5 Btu/lb supply; a winter 12 vs 28 Btu/lb recovers -54,000 Btu/hr (heating). Feeds off moist-air-enthalpy.
- **`radiant-floor-output` (spec-v442).** q = 2 x (T_surface - T_room)^1.1 Btu/hr-ft^2, solvable both ways, with the ~85 F surface comfort cap. Pinned: an 85 F floor over a 70 F room -> 39.3 Btu/hr-ft^2 (at the cap); a 30 Btu/hr-ft^2 load needs an 81.7 F surface. Distinct from radiant-loop-sizing (which sizes the tubing).
- **`economizer-enthalpy-changeover` (spec-v443).** differential-enthalpy (enable if outdoor enthalpy < return) or fixed-dry-bulb high-limit. Pinned: OA 24 vs RA 28 Btu/lb -> enable (4 margin); a humid OA 32 vs 28 locks out even when cool; fixed 70 F vs 65 F setpoint locks out. Distinct from economizer-savings-hours (which counts the free-cooling energy).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the cooling/heating recovery, both solve directions and the comfort cap, both changeover modes, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming AHRI 1060, radiant-panel practice, and ASHRAE 90.1. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks (ERV via `_rEnv`, the radiant and economizer tiles hand-written with a mode select). Module caps bumped with dated comments: calc-hvac.js 68000 -> 74000 and citations.js 320000 -> 360000 (both trending up over the campaign). ### feat(construction): land spec-v439..v440 -- 2 finish-carpentry takeoff tiles (v438 cut as dupe); 899 -> 901 tiles, 0.151.0, 2026-07-04

The finish-carpentry takeoff pair, twentieth batch of the v375-v474 campaign: the batt insulation count and the trim linear footage, in the existing lazy `calc-construction.js` (Group E). The proposed trio's first tile, v438 `flooring-plank-layout`, was cut as a duplicate of the existing `flooring-takeoff` (same boxes-of-plank + row-layout deliverable), so only two landed. Catalog **899 -> 901**, package **0.150.0 -> 0.151.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-03.

- **`insulation-batt-coverage` (spec-v439).** batts = ceil(net / coverage per batt), bags = ceil(net / coverage per bag). Pinned: 500 ft^2, R-13 batt (10.67 ft^2), 88 ft^2/bag -> 47 batts, 6 bags; a deeper R-21 bag (67 ft^2) -> 8 bags.
- **`trim-linear-footage` (spec-v440).** net = (perimeter - openings) x (1 + waste%), pieces = ceil(net/stock), with 45-deg miters (base/casing) or the crown compound cut. Pinned: 70 ft perimeter, 6 ft openings, 10% waste, 16 ft stock -> 70.4 ft, 5 sticks; a 38-deg crown corner -> ~31.6 miter / 33.9 bevel.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the counts, the crown compound cut, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the label-coverage takeoff and the finish-carpentry / compound-miter practice. No cap bump needed. v438's spec annotated CUT with the reason. This continues the campaign's pattern of pruning dupes in built-out domains: the intervening v435-v437 NEC 690/705 PV trio was also all dupes and was cut.
