### feat(hvac): land spec-v305..v307 -- 3 pump-and-fluid fundamentals tiles in calc-hvac.js; 773 -> 776 tiles, 0.108.0, 2026-07-03

The pump-and-fluid fundamentals trio, eleventh batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the pieces the friction and pump tiles use internally but never expose, all in the existing lazy `calc-hvac.js` (Group C); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **773 -> 776**, package **0.107.0 -> 0.108.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`reynolds-number-pipe` (spec-v305).** Re = V D / nu and the laminar (< 2,300) / transitional / turbulent (> 4,000) regime. Pinned: 60 F water at 6 ft/s in a 2 in line -> Re 82,600 turbulent; a crawl -> Re 275 laminar. The fuzzer pins both regime boundaries.
- **`hydronic-gpm-deltat` (spec-v306).** GPM = Q / (500 dT) with a selectable tons unit (x 12,000) and the chilled-water 24 tons/dT shortcut. Pinned: a 10-ton coil at 10 F -> 24 GPM; a 100,000 Btu/h boiler at 20 F -> 10 GPM (the wide-delta-T lever).
- **`pump-specific-speed` (spec-v307).** Ns = N sqrt(Q) / H^(3/4) and the radial (< 2,000) / mixed (< 4,500) / axial impeller bands. Pinned: 1,750 rpm, 500 gpm, 100 ft -> Ns 1,237 radial; dropping the head to 25 ft jumps it to 3,500 mixed flow (the H^3/4 lever).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the regime and class boundaries, the tons unit path and 24-tons identity, the H^3/4 lever, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the Reynolds definition, the water-side heat-transport equation, and the Hydraulic Institute specific-speed convention. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. ### feat(hydraulics): land spec-v302..v304 -- 3 site-hydraulics depth tiles in calc-plumbing.js; 770 -> 773 tiles, 0.107.0, 2026-07-03

The site-hydraulics depth trio, tenth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the pieces the rational-method and open-channel tiles need but do not compute, all in the existing lazy `calc-plumbing.js` (Group B); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **770 -> 773**, package **0.106.0 -> 0.107.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`time-of-concentration` (spec-v302).** The Kirpich tc = 0.0078 L^0.77 S^(-0.385) that sets the storm duration `stormwater-rational` reads its intensity at. Pinned: a 1,000 ft flow path at 2% slope -> 7.2 min; flattening to 0.5% stretches it to 12.3 min (a lower intensity and smaller peak).
- **`orifice-flow` (spec-v303).** The detention-outlet discharge Q = Cd A sqrt(2 g h). Pinned: a 6 in sharp-edged orifice under a 4 ft head -> 1.89 cfs (849 gpm); the fuzzer pins the exact sqrt-of-head 1.5x scaling for a 2.25x head.
- **`channel-froude-number` (spec-v304).** The open-channel regime Fr = V/sqrt(g y) and the rectangular critical depth yc = (q^2/g)^(1/3). Pinned: a 4 ft channel at 50 cfs and 2 ft deep -> Fr 0.78 subcritical (yc 1.69 ft, consistent); dropping to 1 ft crosses to Fr 2.20 supercritical, the setup for a hydraulic jump.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the slope exponent, the sqrt-of-head scaling, the regime boundary and yc consistency, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming Kirpich/TR-55, the orifice equation, and Chow's open-channel hydraulics. These three compute functions are exported (adding corpus/dims/fuzzer rows); the renderers are non-exported. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 25 collision-checked aliases (the "orifice flow" term already lived on septic-lpp-orifice and was skipped), `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. No cap bumps (calc-plumbing at 90.7%). Housekeeping: home count 770 -> 773, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 1016/1019/1016, Group B cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 39 appended. ### feat(concrete): land spec-v299..v301 -- 3 reinforced-concrete depth-2 tiles in calc-concrete.js; 767 -> 770 tiles, 0.106.0, 2026-07-03

The reinforced-concrete depth-2 trio, ninth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the ACI checks the strength tiles never make, all in the existing lazy `calc-concrete.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **767 -> 770**, package **0.105.0 -> 0.106.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`rc-slab-min-thickness` (spec-v299).** ACI 318-19 Table 7.3.1.1 / 9.3.1.1 deflection-waiving minimum thickness (l/20, l/24, l/28, l/10) times the (0.4 + fy/100,000) grade modifier and the lightweight factor. Pinned: a simply supported 12 ft one-way slab -> 7.2 in; a Grade 40 both-ends-continuous slab -> 4.11 in. The fuzzer pins all four denominators, both modifiers, and the lightweight floor at 1.09 (wc > 112 pcf).
- **`rc-doubly-reinforced` (spec-v300).** ACI 318-19 doubly-reinforced flexure a = (As - A's) fy / (0.85 f'c b), Mn = the singly couple plus A's fy (d - d'), with the compression-steel yield check and the tension-controlled phi. Pinned: a 14x24 beam (As 8, A's 3) -> Mn 771, phi Mn 694 kip-ft, both layers yielding, tension-controlled; removing the compression steel drops it to 678 and deepens the stress block, and a deep d' fires the not-yielding flag.
- **`rc-shear-friction` (spec-v301).** ACI 318-19 22.9 Vn = mu Avf fy (mu 1.4/1.0/0.6/0.7 x lambda by interface), capped by the concrete interface. Pinned: 2.0 in^2 of Grade 60 dowels across a roughened joint -> 120 kip (90 design); adding dowels to 3.0 in^2 hits the 153.6 kip cap (nothing more transfers), the reason shear friction has a ceiling. Note: the compute name uses `iface` for the interface param -- `interface` is a strict-mode reserved word.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the four support denominators, the grade/lightweight modifiers, the two flexural couples, the yield/phi checks, the mu interface map, and the interface cap, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming ACI 318-19 7.3.1.1 / doubly-reinforced flexure / 22.9 by section. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Module caps bumped with dated comments: calc-concrete 9000 -> 12500 (111.4% of the old cap) and the citations.js registry 240000 -> 260000 (100.6%). Housekeeping: home count 767 -> 770, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 1013/1016/1013, Group E cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 38 appended. ### feat(loads): land spec-v296..v298 -- 3 ASCE 7 wind-and-snow load depth tiles in calc-construction.js; 764 -> 767 tiles, 0.105.0, 2026-07-03

The ASCE 7 wind-and-snow load depth trio, eighth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the load cases the single velocity-pressure and flat-snow tiles never build, all in the existing lazy `calc-construction.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **764 -> 767**, package **0.104.0 -> 0.105.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`wind-cc-pressure` (spec-v296).** ASCE 7 Chapter 30 components-and-cladding p = qh [(GCp) - (GCpi)] with qh = 0.00256 Kz Kzt Kd Ke V^2 and both internal signs evaluated. Pinned: a 115 mph Exp C building -> qh 25.9 psf, a roof corner (Zone 3, GCp -1.8) draws -51.3 psf suction; a wall corner draws less (-40.9), the reason roof corners drive the fastening schedule.
- **`snow-drift-load` (spec-v297).** ASCE 7 Chapter 7 leeward drift hd = 0.43 (lu)^(1/3) (pg + 10)^(1/4) - 1.5, gamma = 0.13 pg + 14 (capped 30 pcf), pd = hd gamma over a 4 hd width. Pinned: a 100 ft upwind roof at pg 30 -> hd 3.52 ft, 63 psf surcharge; pg 50 drives it to 83 psf. The fuzzer pins the gamma cap, the hd-floor-at-zero for a short fetch, and the two-branch width.
- **`wind-mwfrs-pressure` (spec-v298).** ASCE 7 Chapter 27 MWFRS p = q G Cp - qi (GCpi), G = 0.85, Cp +0.8/-0.5. Pinned: a 25.9 psf building -> +22.3 windward / -15.7 leeward walls, **net 28.6 psf**. **As-landed correction:** the net cancels the internal pressure (it acts equally outward on both walls); the spec's section-2.1 pinned net of 37.9 psf double-counted GCpi, and the landed net of 28.6 (with the cross-check's 32.8) matches the spec's own stated "internal pressure cancels in the net" principle. The fuzzer pins the net's independence from GCpi.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the governing-sign selection, the V^2 scaling, the gamma cap and hd floor, the two-branch drift width, and the internal-cancelling net, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming ASCE 7-22 Ch. 30 / Ch. 7 / Ch. 27 by chapter. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 27 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. No cap bumps (calc-construction at 92.5%). Housekeeping: home count 764 -> 767, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 1010/1013/1010, Group E cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED (v298 carrying the net-cancels-internal correction note), docs/mobile-responsive.md section 37 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (**767 tiles / 56 modules / 789 sitemap URLs**), unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(steel): land spec-v293..v295 -- 3 steel connection/detailing depth tiles in calc-steel.js; 761 -> 764 tiles, 0.104.0, 2026-07-03

The steel connection/detailing depth trio, seventh batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the checks the member tiles never touch, all in the existing lazy `calc-steel.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **761 -> 764**, package **0.103.0 -> 0.104.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`steel-web-local-strength` (spec-v293).** AISC J10.2 web local yielding (Fy tw (5k + lb) interior / 2.5k end) and J10.3 web crippling with their DIFFERING factors (Omega 1.50 vs 2.00). Pinned: a W18x50 on a 4 in interior bearing -> WLY 181.9 / WC 204.2 kip, crippling governs ASD at 102.1 kip and LRFD at 153.2; at the member end yielding drops to 126.5 kip (the fixture pins the exact value, correcting the spec prose's 126.0 rounding) and takes over at 84.3 kip ASD.
- **`steel-bolt-slip-critical` (spec-v294).** AISC J3.8 friction resistance Rn = mu Du hf Tb ns (mu 0.30/0.50, Du 1.13, Table J3.1 Tb, standard-hole phi 1.00 / Omega 1.50). Pinned: a 3/4 in A325 Class A single-plane bolt -> 9.49 kip (ASD 6.33), a four-bolt group 38.0 kip LRFD; Class B in double shear lifts one bolt 3.33x to 31.6 kip (the fuzzer pins the exact mu x ns ratio).
- **`steel-fillet-weld-size` (spec-v295).** The Table J2.4 minimum leg by thinner part, the J2.2b along-an-edge maximum (t under 1/4 in; t - 1/16 at or over), te = 0.707 w, and the 4w minimum length -- the detailing window `fillet-weld-strength` assumes you already picked. Pinned: 1/2 to 3/8 in plates -> a 3/16-to-5/16 window, a compliant 1/4 in leg at 0.177 in throat and 1 in minimum; the 3/16 in sheet cross-check pins the under-1/4-in full-thickness edge branch, and out-of-window legs flag (BELOW/EXCEEDS) rather than error.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the interior/end branch, the differing-Omega governing flip, the mu/ns scaling, all four J2.4 brackets and both edge-max branches, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming AISC 360-22 J10 / J3.8 / J2.2b-Table J2.4 by section. ### feat(wood): land spec-v290..v292 -- 3 NDS wood-member depth tiles in calc-construction.js; 758 -> 761 tiles, 0.103.0, 2026-07-03

The NDS wood-member depth trio, sixth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the checks the wood bending/shear/compression/connection bench left open, all in the existing lazy `calc-construction.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **758 -> 761**, package **0.102.0 -> 0.103.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`wood-bearing-perpendicular` (spec-v290).** NDS 3.10.2 crushing at a support: fc_perp = R/(b lb) against Fc_perp x Cb, with the 3.10.4 bearing-area factor Cb = (lb + 0.375)/lb inside its under-6-in / not-near-end limits (fuzzer pins both limit branches). Pinned: an 800 lb joist on 1.5 in of DF-L plate -> Cb 1.25, DCR 0.46, 0.85 in required; a 6,000 lb beam reaction on the same footprint fails at 3.4x and demands 6.4 in or a plate.
- **`wood-tension-member` (spec-v291).** NDS 3.8.1 net-section tension: ft = T/An against Ft x CD x CF, An deducting the bolt holes a gross-area estimate misses. Pinned: a 2x6 DF-L chord at 3,000 lb through one 3/4 in bolt loses 14% of its section and runs DCR 0.56; snow duration and a clean section relax it to 0.42.
- **`wood-combined-bending-axial` (spec-v292).** NDS 3.9.2 beam-column interaction (fc/Fc')^2 + fb/[Fb'(1 - fc/FcE)] with FcE = 0.822 Emin'/(le/d)^2. Pinned: a 4x4 stud at 3,000 lb + 3,000 in-lb passes at 0.55; doubling the axial load nearly quadruples the amplified bending term to a 1.55 fail, and at fc >= FcE the tile errors (buckling) rather than dividing through zero.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the Cb limit branches, the net-area deduction, the P-delta growth and buckling seam, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming NDS 2018 3.10 / 3.8 / 3.9.2 by section with the no-CD-on-Fc_perp, single-hole-line, uniaxial, and design-aid caveats. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 22 collision-checked aliases (2 spec-listed terms already lived on other tiles and were skipped), `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. ### feat(geotech): land spec-v287..v289 -- 3 geotechnical foundation depth tiles in calc-geotech.js; 755 -> 758 tiles, 0.102.0, 2026-07-03

The geotechnical foundation depth trio, fifth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the checks the bearing/earth-pressure/wall bench left open, all in the existing lazy `calc-geotech.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **755 -> 758**, package **0.101.0 -> 0.102.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`soil-settlement-elastic` (spec-v287).** The immediate settlement `soil-bearing-capacity` calls separate: Se = q B (1 - nu^2) Is / Es (Bowles). Pinned: a 6 ft rigid footing at 3 ksf on medium sand -> 0.64 in, under the customary 1 in read; halving Es doubles it to 1.29 in and flips the verdict - the footing that passes bearing and fails settlement.
- **`pile-axial-capacity` (spec-v288).** The alpha-method pile in clay: Qult = alpha cu (pi D L) + 9 cu (pi D^2/4), FS 2-3 without a load test (FHWA / Das). Pinned: a 16 in x 40 ft pile in cu = 1 ksf -> 104.7 kip ultimate (88% skin), 34.9 kip allowable; doubling the length doubles the shaft term and leaves the tip put - why a friction pile is lengthened, not fattened.
- **`slope-stability-infinite` (spec-v289).** The infinite-slope FS = (c' + gamma H cos^2 beta tan phi') / (gamma H sin beta cos beta) with the cohesionless tan phi'/tan beta reduction (Das / NAVFAC DM-7). Pinned: a 25 degree cut in c' 200 / phi' 30 soil holds at 1.78; the cohesionless identity is depth- and weight-independent (fuzzer pins it at two different gamma/H) and stands at exactly FS = 1 at the angle of repose.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the 1/Es scaling, the shaft-vs-tip scaling, the cohesionless identity and repose point, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the Bowles / FHWA-Das / Das-NAVFAC authorities with the immediate-not-consolidation, single-layer, no-seepage, and screening-aid caveats. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 23 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Module cap bumped with a dated comment: calc-geotech 7000 -> 10000 (114% of the old cap). Housekeeping: home count 755 -> 758, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 1001/1004/1001, Group E cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 34 appended. ### feat(concrete): land spec-v284..v286 -- 3 reinforced-concrete member depth tiles in calc-concrete.js; 752 -> 755 tiles, 0.101.0, 2026-07-03

The reinforced-concrete member depth trio, fourth batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the ACI checks the beam flexure/shear/development bench left open, all in the existing lazy `calc-concrete.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **752 -> 755**, package **0.100.0 -> 0.101.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`rc-column-axial` (spec-v284).** ACI 318-19 22.4.2 tied-column concentric capacity Po = 0.85 f'c (Ag - Ast) + fy Ast with the 22.4.2.1 cap phi Pn,max = 0.80 x 0.65 x Po and the 10.6.1 1-8% rho band flag. Pinned: a 16 in square 4,000 psi column with eight #8 Grade 60 -> Po 1,228 kip, 639 kip design; the 5,000 psi cross-check lifts it 17% (749 kip), the concrete-carries-most lever.
- **`rc-punching-shear` (spec-v285).** ACI 318-19 Table 22.6.5.2 two-way shear on the d/2 perimeter: least of 4, 2 + 4/beta, 2 + alpha_s d/bo (each x lambda sqrt(f'c)), alpha_s 40/30/20 by position, phi = 0.75. Pinned: an interior 20 in column on a d = 6 in slab -> 253 psi, 118.4 kip; the 36 in column flips governing to the large-column term (163.9 kip); an elongated column pins the aspect-ratio branch.
- **`rc-hook-development` (spec-v286).** ACI 318-19 Eq. 25.4.3.1a hook development ldh = (fy psi_e psi_r psi_o psi_c / (55 lambda sqrt(f'c))) db^1.5 with psi_c = f'c/15,000 + 0.6 under 6,000 psi and the max(8 db, 6 in) floor. Pinned: a #8 Grade 60 in 4,000 psi -> 14.9 in; the #5 cross-check scales by db^1.5 to 7.4 in; a #3 pins the floor branch.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, fuzzer blocks covering the rho flag, the three-term least selection, the alpha_s map, the psi_c branch, the floor, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming ACI 318-19 22.4 / 22.6 / 25.4.3 by section with the concentric-short-tied, no-moment-transfer, standard-hook, and design-aid caveats. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. ### feat(steel): land spec-v281..v283 -- 3 steel members-and-connections depth tiles in calc-steel.js; 749 -> 752 tiles, 0.100.0, 2026-07-03

The steel members-and-connections depth trio, third batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the three checks the v254..v256 steel bench explicitly defers, all in the existing lazy `calc-steel.js` (Group E); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **749 -> 752**, package **0.99.0 -> 0.100.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`steel-beam-ltb` (spec-v281).** AISC 360 F2 lateral-torsional buckling -- the reduction the braced `steel-beam-flexure` plateau misses: Lp = 1.76 ry sqrt(E/Fy), Lr via the Eq. F2-6 closed form (computed from the entered section properties with c = 1, sharper than the spec's entered-from-Manual fallback and matching the Manual's W18x50 value 16.9 ft), the linear inelastic branch capped at Mp and scaled by Cb, and the elastic Eq. F2-4 Fcr beyond Lr. Pinned: a W18x50 braced at 10 ft -> 360.2 kip-ft (ASD 215.7, LRFD 324.2), a 14% cut from the braced 420.8; at 20 ft the elastic branch halves it to 199.9 (Fcr 27.0 ksi); the fuzzer pins all three zones and the Cb-capped-at-Mp seam.
- **`steel-block-shear` (spec-v282).** AISC 360 J4.3 block-shear rupture at a bolted/coped end: Rn = 0.6 Fu Anv + Ubs Fu Ant capped by 0.6 Fy Agv + Ubs Fu Ant, with the (n - 0.5)-hole shear-path and half-hole tension-path deductions. Pinned: three 3/4 in bolts in a 1/2 in A36 plate -> the gross-shear-yield cap governs at 111.8 kip (ASD 55.9, LRFD 83.9); shortening the end distance to 1.25 in drops it to 109.1; a higher-Fy element flips governing to the rupture path.
- **`steel-tension-member` (spec-v283).** AISC 360 D2/D3 tension member: gross yielding Fy Ag against net rupture Fu U An with the Table D3.1 Case-2 shear lag U = 1 - xbar/L (entered U overrides, capped at 1.0). Pinned: an L4x4x1/2 bolted through one leg draws U = 0.803 and rupture governs at 77.2 kip ASD; welding the full section (U = 1, no holes) flips the governing limit back to yielding at 80.8 kip -- the limit-state flip the fuzzer pins.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, a fuzzer block covering the zone branches, the governing-path min() seams, the U cap/override, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming AISC 360-22 F2 / J4.3 / D2-D3 by section with the compact-doubly-symmetric-only, single-row, single-hole-line, and design-aid caveats. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 26 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Module cap bumped with a dated comment: calc-steel 9000 -> 13000 (the three tiles took the built copy to 117% of the old cap). Housekeeping: home count 749 -> 752, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 995/998/995, Group E cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 32 appended. ### feat(electrical): land spec-v278..v280 -- 3 NEC conductor-and-overcurrent-sizing tiles; 746 -> 749 tiles, 0.99.0, 2026-07-03

The NEC conductor-and-overcurrent-sizing trio, second batch of the v275-v374 campaign: the three device-and-conductor sizing rules the catalog referenced but never computed, one tile each in the existing lazy Group A modules `calc-motor.js`, `calc-service.js`, and `calc-feeder.js`; no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **746 -> 749**, package **0.98.0 -> 0.99.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`motor-overload-sizing` (spec-v278, calc-motor.js).** The second of the two motor protection devices -- the 430.32 running overload `motor-branch-protection` explicitly defers, sized on the NAMEPLATE FLA (not the table FLC): 125% for a marked SF >= 1.15 or rise <= 40 degC, 115% otherwise, with the 430.32(C) 140%/130% will-not-start ceiling. Pinned: 26 A SF-1.15 -> 32.5 A setting, 36.4 A ceiling; the SF-1.0/55 degC cross-check takes the lower class (29.9 / 33.8 A); a blank (unmarked) SF or rise deliberately does NOT qualify for the higher class.
- **`service-conductor-sizing` (spec-v279, calc-service.js).** The NEC 310.12 dwelling 83% allowance: A_req = 0.83 x the service rating, conductor from an ORDERED Table 310.16 75 degC array (integer-like object keys would reorder). Pinned: 200 A -> 166 A -> 2/0 Cu and 100 A -> #4 Cu, both matching Table 310.12. **As-landed correction:** the spec prose's aluminum column was shifted one size high; the landed table uses the true Table 310.16 aluminum values, reproducing Table 310.12's 200 A -> 4/0 Al (the spec text would have wrongly yielded 3/0).
- **`continuous-load-ocpd` (spec-v280, calc-feeder.js).** The most-reused NEC sizing step standalone: A_min = 1.25 x continuous + noncontinuous (1.00 x for a listed 100%-rated assembly), device from the 240.6(A) standard ratings. Pinned: 40+20 A -> 70 A device; the 100%-rated cross-check drops to 60 A; 44+10 A -> 65 A rounds up to 70 A.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dims annotation, pinned example + cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the class-selection branches, the ordered-array round-ups, the blank-unmarked seam, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline (GOVERNANCE.electrical + NEC_DISCLOSURE, NEC 2023 sections by name, nfpa.org/freeaccess). Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 7 worked-example fixtures (string-equality on the conductor-size output), 24 collision-checked aliases, `related-tiles`, and 3 fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Module caps bumped with dated comments: calc-motor 6000 -> 7500, calc-feeder 7000 -> 8500 (both had crossed 96%). Housekeeping: home count 746 -> 749, README resynced (counts, correctness figures 992/995/992, Group A cheat-sheet row and exemplars, catalog-state prose), three specs marked LANDED (v279 carrying the aluminum-table correction note), docs/mobile-responsive.md section 31 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (**749 tiles / 56 modules / 771 sitemap URLs**), unit tests, build, data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(hvac): land spec-v275..v277 -- 3 ventilation-and-recovery tiles in calc-hvac.js; 743 -> 746 tiles, 0.98.0, 2026-07-03

The HVAC ventilation-and-recovery trio, the opening batch of the v275-v374 proposed expansion campaign: recover / temper / modulate -- the three ways the trade meets a ventilation requirement without oversizing the equipment behind it. All three in the existing lazy `calc-hvac.js` (Group C); no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **743 -> 746**, package **0.97.0 -> 0.98.0** (a minor). Proposed 2026-07-02.

- **`erv-sensible-recovery` (spec-v275).** The ASHRAE Standard 84 / AHRI 1060 sensible effectiveness: the outdoor air leaves the core at T_oa + eps_s x (T_ra - T_oa) and the plant is relieved of Q_s = 1.08 x CFM x eps_s x dT, reported beside the no-recovery load and the residual. Pinned: 200 cfm at 75% on a 10 F design day -> 55 F leaving, 9,720 of 12,960 Btu/h recovered (the load cut to a quarter); the 95 F summer cross-check carries the sign (-3,240 Btu/h of cooling relieved); equal temperatures recover zero without erroring.
- **`mua-tempering-load` (spec-v276).** The makeup-air unit behind an IMC 508 exhaust hood: sensible 1.08 x CFM x dT, optional latent 0.68 x CFM x dW (gr/lb, zero when omitted), total, and the burner input at the thermal efficiency. Pinned: a 2,000 cfm kitchen MUA lifting 20 F air to 65 F -> 97,200 Btu/h out, 121,500 Btu/h input at 80%; the 0 F cross-check adds a 13,600 Btu/h latent term (154,000 total).
- **`dcv-co2-ventilation` (spec-v277).** The steady-state single-zone CO2 mass balance C_in = C_oa + N/Q solved as Q = N/(C_set - C_oa), with the total for the occupancy and the equilibrium back-check that closes the loop. Pinned: 20 people at a 1,100 ppm setpoint vs 400 ppm outdoor -> the classic 15.1 cfm/person, 303 cfm total, back-check 1,100 ppm; the 800 ppm cross-check demands 1.75x the air (26.5 cfm/person, 530 cfm).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the examples, the leaving-temperature cross-form, the sign carry, the latent-optional and zero-dT seams, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the ASHRAE 84 / AHRI 1060 / ASHRAE Fundamentals / IMC 508 / ASHRAE 62.1 authority with the balanced-flow, rated-effectiveness, sensible-only, sea-level, equilibrium-only, and design-aid caveats. Per-tile wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 25 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Housekeeping: home count 743 -> 746 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, sitemap 765 -> 768, correctness-phase counts to the live 989/992/989, unit-test count, and the cheat-sheet Tiles column -- including a pre-existing 33-tile drift on the Group E/F rows left by the structural-trio landings), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 30 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **746 tiles / 56 modules / 768 sitemap URLs**), **5,039** unit tests, build (746 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(lateral): land spec-v272..v274 -- 3 SDPWS wood lateral-system tiles in a new calc-lateral.js; 740 -> 743 tiles, 0.97.0, 2026-07-02

The SDPWS wood lateral-force-resisting-system trio: three tiles in a **new lazy Group E module, `calc-lateral.js`** -- the catalog's **56th `calc-*` module**, closing the load path from the `seismic-base-shear` / `wind-pressure` demand into the wood: the diaphragm that collects the story force, the shear wall that takes it to the foundation with its holdown, and the drift that wall deflects. Catalog **740 -> 743**, package **0.96.0 -> 0.97.0** (a minor). **This is the last of the proposed new-module trios -- the PROPOSED backlog is now empty and the spec tree is fully landed through v274.** Proposed 2026-07-02 (commit 47f6d84).

- **`diaphragm-shear` (spec-v272).** The flexible diaphragm as a deep horizontal beam: end reaction wL/2, maximum unit shear v = wL/(2b) for the nailing schedule, moment wL^2/8, and the chord force T = C = M/b the boundary members resolve it into. Pinned: a 192 x 120 ft diaphragm at 516 plf -> 413 plf (the published WoodWorks value) and a 19.8 kip chord; the b = 60 ft cross-check exactly doubles both, pinning the 1/b law.
- **`shearwall-overturning` (spec-v273).** The segmented shear wall's two detailing numbers: unit shear v = V/b, and the net holdown tension T = (V h - 0.6 W b/2)/b under the ASD 0.6D + 0.7E combination, clamped to zero when gravity stabilizes the wall. Pinned: an 8 ft wall taking 8 kip at 10 ft with 3,000 lb dead -> 1,000 plf and a 9.1 kip holdown; the 20,000 lb cross-check cuts the holdown to 4.0 kip; a 34,000 lb case pins the clamp-and-flag seam (no negative holdown ever reported).
- **`shearwall-deflection` (spec-v274).** SDPWS Equation 4.3-1's three terms in series -- end-post bending 8vh^3/(EAb), panel shear + nail slip vh/(1000 Ga), anchorage rotation h da/b -- in the equation's own calibrated (unit-specific) convention, plus the drift ratio checked against ASCE 7. Pinned: a 10 x 8 ft wall at 400 plf on 4x4 DF-L posts with Ga 15 and da 0.15 -> 0.020 + 0.267 + 0.188 = 0.47 in (0.40% drift); the 20 ft cross-check pins the h^3-vs-h scaling laws term by term (bending x8, the others x2).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the examples, the 1/b and h/h^3 scaling laws, the clamp-to-zero holdown seam, the da = 0 rigid-anchorage path, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the AWC SDPWS / AWC-APA design-guide / ASCE 7 authority with the flexible-distribution, segmented-wall, 0.6D-combination, unit-specific-equation, and design-aid caveats. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` LATERAL_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap, and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles` (substituting the live `joist-deflection` for the spec's never-landed `beam-deflection`), dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. One platform bump rode along (dated comment): the `check-shells` group-shell gzip cap 20 -> 24 KB -- the three all-Group-E trios of the day took `groups/construction/index.html` to ~20.5 KB gz, past the cap set only hours earlier. Housekeeping: home count 740 -> 743 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 55 -> 56, sitemap 762 -> 765, catalog-state line and gates-green tail, unit-test count), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 29 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **743 tiles / 56 modules / 765 sitemap URLs**), **5,036** unit tests, build (743 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:home-payload` / `check:shell-mobile`, the full-catalog 320 px no-horizontal-scroll sweep, the 743-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(masonry): land spec-v269..v271 -- 3 TMS 402-16 reinforced-masonry member tiles in a new calc-masonry.js; 737 -> 740 tiles, 0.96.0, 2026-07-02

The TMS 402-16 reinforced-masonry member trio: three tiles in a **new lazy Group E module, `calc-masonry.js`** -- the catalog's **55th `calc-*` module**, tracing the flexure / shear / axial triad for a reinforced CMU wall exactly as the AISC batch (v254..v256) traced it for a wide-flange and the ACI batch (v257..v259) for an RC beam. Catalog **737 -> 740**, package **0.95.0 -> 0.96.0** (a minor). Every masonry tile to date was a *material takeoff* (`cmu-grout-volume`, `masonry-coursing`, `masonry-count`); none resolved a structural limit state -- masonry now joins steel and concrete as a trade with both a takeoff and a design presence. Fifth of the proposed new-module trios to land; only the wood-lateral trio remains PROPOSED. Proposed 2026-07-02 (commit 6844d82).

- **`cmu-wall-flexure` (spec-v269).** The ASD cracked transformed-section out-of-plane moment: n = Es/(900 f'm), rho, k = sqrt(2 rho n + (rho n)^2) - rho n, j = 1 - k/3, the steel-governed Ms = As Fs j d against the masonry-governed Mm = 0.5 Fb k j b d^2 (Fb = 0.45 f'm), the lesser governing. Pinned: an 8 in fully grouted wall, #5 at 24 in, f'm 2,000 psi -> 1,428 lb-ft per foot, steel-governed (the textbook lightly-reinforced outcome); the #5-at-16-in cross-check flips the governing mode to masonry at 1,924 lb-ft, pinning both branches of the min(Ms, Mm) seam.
- **`cmu-shear-wall` (spec-v270).** The ASD in-plane shear pair Fvm = 0.5((4.0 - 1.75 M/(Vdv)) sqrt(f'm)) + 0.25 P/An (the shear-span ratio clamped at 1.0 inside the masonry term) plus Fvs = 0.5 Av Fs dv/(An s), against the M/(Vdv)-graded 3-to-2 sqrt(f'm) cap -- the masonry capacity the `seismic-base-shear` demand finally lands on. Pinned: an 8 in x 8 ft wall, f'm 1,500, 20 kip axial, #4 horizontals at 48 in at M/(Vdv) = 0.5 -> 67.3 + 8.7 = 76.1 psi and 55.7 kip; the zero-axial slender cross-check falls to 38.3 kip; a heavy-Av case pins the cap-governs branch.
- **`cmu-wall-axial` (spec-v271).** The slenderness-reduced allowable axial Pa = (0.25 f'm An + 0.65 Ast Fs) x R, with R = 1 - (h/140r)^2 up to h/r = 99 and the Euler-type (70r/h)^2 beyond, the branches meeting continuously (pinned by the fuzzer). Pinned: a 12 ft, 8 in grouted wall with #5 verticals at 24 in -> h/r 65.4, R 0.782, 38.3 kip per foot; the 24 ft cross-check flips to the slender branch and more than halves it to 14.0 kip.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the examples, the governing-mode switch, the mvd clamp, the capped branch, the h/r = 99 continuity, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the TMS 402-16 / Masonry Designers' Guide / CMHA TEK 14-07C authority with the cracked-section, sustained-axial, tied-bars, pure-axial, and design-aid caveats. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` MASONRY_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap, and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 23 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Two registry-cap bumps rode along (dated comments): `tools-data.js` 84000 -> 92000 and `tile-meta.js` 10000 -> 11000, both tipped past 100% by the twelve rows the four new-module trios added. Housekeeping: home count 737 -> 740 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 54 -> 55, sitemap 759 -> 762, catalog-state line and gates-green tail, unit-test count), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 28 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **740 tiles / 55 modules / 762 sitemap URLs**), **5,033** unit tests, build (740 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:home-payload` / `check:shell-mobile`, the full-catalog 320 px no-horizontal-scroll sweep, the 740-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(geotech): land spec-v260..v262 -- 3 geotechnical foundation / earth-retaining tiles in a new calc-geotech.js; 734 -> 737 tiles, 0.95.0, 2026-07-02

The geotechnical foundation-and-earth-retaining trio: three tiles in a **new lazy Group E module, `calc-geotech.js`** -- the catalog's **54th `calc-*` module**, where the load path the steel (v254..v256) and reinforced-concrete (v257..v259) member benches trace finally meets the ground. Catalog **734 -> 737**, package **0.94.0 -> 0.95.0** (a minor). The catalog divided a service load by an allowable pressure keyed to a soil *class* (`footing-area`) and counted a segmental wall's blocks (`retaining-wall-block`), but nothing computed the allowable pressure from the soil's own strength, the lateral push of retained soil, or the tip / slide / bearing stability of the wall that resists it. Fourth of the proposed new-module trios to land; the masonry and wood-lateral trios remain PROPOSED. Proposed 2026-07-02 (commit 6226c11).

- **`soil-bearing-capacity` (spec-v260).** The general bearing-capacity equation qu = c Nc sc + q Nq sq + 0.5 gamma B Ngamma sgamma with the Vesic (1973) factors and the De Beer / Vesic shape factors for square and circular footings, plus the customary FS = 3 allowable. Pinned: a 6 ft strip footing 4 ft deep on phi = 32 sand at 120 pcf -> Nq 23.18 / Nc 35.49 / Ngamma 30.21, 22.0 ksf ultimate, 7.3 ksf allowable (the textbook value); the phi = 0 stiff-clay cross-check pins the Prandtl Nc = 5.14 branch that keeps (Nq - 1)/tan(phi) from dividing by zero.
- **`lateral-earth-pressure` (spec-v261).** Rankine (1857) active and passive earth pressure: Ka = (1 - sin phi)/(1 + sin phi), Kp = 1/Ka, the triangular soil thrust at H/3, the rectangular surcharge thrust at H/2, the combined resultant height, and the passive thrust. Pinned: a 10 ft wall of phi = 30 sand -> Ka exactly 1/3, Pa exactly 2,000 lb/ft, Pp 18,000 lb/ft (the 9:1 ratio); a 250 psf surcharge grows the thrust 42% and lifts its arm to 3.82 ft.
- **`retaining-wall-stability` (spec-v262).** The three cantilever-wall global-stability checks against the IBC 1807.2.3 minimum FS of 1.5: overturning Mr/Mo from the stem / base / heel-soil rectangles, sliding mu sum(V)/Pa, and the eccentricity-based toe / heel pressures with the middle-third check. Pinned: a 10 ft wall on a 6 ft base passes dry at FS 3.37 / 1.69 with 1,731 psf toe pressure (comfortably under the v260 allowable); the 300 psf surcharge cross-check drops sliding to 1.31 -- **failing** -- and pushes the resultant outside the middle third, the case that most often governs.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the examples, the phi = 0 factor identities, the Ka x Kp = 1 reciprocal, the no-heel geometry guard, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the Das / FHWA GEC 6 / NAVFAC DM-7.02 / IBC 1807.2.3 authorities with the general-shear-only, no-groundwater/settlement, Rankine-conditions, no-passive-credit, and design-aid caveats. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` GEOTECH_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap (7000 gzip; built ~5.4 KB), and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. One registry-cap bump rode along: `citations.js` 224000 -> 240000 (the nine new-module-trio citation entries pushed it to 100.2% of the old cap). Housekeeping: home count 734 -> 737 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 53 -> 54, sitemap 756 -> 759, catalog-state line and gates-green tail, unit-test count), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 27 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **737 tiles / 54 modules / 759 sitemap URLs**), **5,030** unit tests, build (737 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:home-payload` / `check:shell-mobile`, the full-catalog 320 px no-horizontal-scroll sweep, the 737-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(concrete): land spec-v257..v259 -- 3 ACI 318-19 reinforced-concrete member tiles in a new calc-concrete.js; 731 -> 734 tiles, 0.94.0, 2026-07-02

The ACI 318-19 reinforced-concrete member trio: three tiles in a **new lazy Group E module, `calc-concrete.js`** -- the catalog's **53rd `calc-*` module** and the reinforced-concrete companion to `calc-steel.js` (v254..v256) one material over. Catalog **731 -> 734**, package **0.93.0 -> 0.94.0** (a minor). The catalog already placed and cured the concrete (`concrete`, `concrete-mix-design`, `concrete-evaporation-rate`, `concrete-strength-gain`) and counted the rebar (`rebar`, `rebar-schedule`, `rebar-lap-splice`), but nothing checked the reinforced-concrete *member itself*: does the beam carry the moment, does it carry the shear, and does the bar develop its force. Third of the proposed new-module trios to land; the geotechnical, masonry, and wood-lateral trios remain PROPOSED. Proposed 2026-07-02 (commit ad13e20).

- **`rc-beam-flexure` (spec-v257).** The §22.2 singly-reinforced, tension-controlled flexural capacity: the equivalent stress-block depth a = As x fy / (0.85 x f'c x b) (§22.2.2.4.1), Mn = As x fy x (d - a/2), phi Mn at the §21.2.2 tension-controlled phi = 0.90, and the demand/capacity ratio against an entered Mu. Pinned: a 12 x 24 in beam, three #9 Grade 60 bars on 4,000 psi concrete -> a = 4.41 in, phi Mn = 260 kip-ft (the textbook value), 0.77 utilization at Mu = 200; the 10 x 18 two-#9 cross-check -> 128 kip-ft, showing depth, not steel, is the dominant lever.
- **`rc-beam-shear` (spec-v258).** The §22.5 shear pair: the simplified concrete contribution Vc = 2 x lambda x sqrt(f'c) x bw x d (§22.5.5.1), phi Vc at phi = 0.75 (§21.2.1), the stirrup demand Vs = Vu/phi - Vc when Vu exceeds phi Vc, the required vertical-stirrup spacing s = Av x fyt x d / Vs (§22.5.10.5.3), and the §9.7.6.2.2 d/2 maximum. Pinned: a 12 x 21.5 in beam on 4,000 psi -> phi Vc = 24.5 kip; a 40 kip demand computes #3 stirrups at 13.7 in but the d/2 = 10.75 in cap governs; the 20 kip cross-check pins the no-stirrups-by-strength boundary.
- **`rc-development-length` (spec-v259).** The §25.4.2.3 general straight-bar tension development length with the Table 25.4.2.5 psi factors (casting position / coating / size / grade, the psi_t x psi_e product capped at 1.7), the §25.4.2.4 confinement term capped at 2.5, and the §25.4.2.1 12 in floor -- the actual code length the `rebar-lap-splice` 40-bar-diameter rule of thumb papers over. Pinned: a #8 Grade 60 bottom bar in 4,000 psi, well confined -> 28.5 in; cast as a top bar the 1.3 casting factor stretches it to 37.0 in.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the examples, the 1.7 / 2.5 cap boundaries, the 12 in floor, the capacity-only null paths, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the ACI 318-19 chapter-and-section authority with the tension-controlled-only, simplified-Vc, user-selected-psi, and design-aid caveats. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` CONCRETE_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap (6000 gzip; built ~4.5 KB), and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 24 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. One platform bump: the `check-shells` group-shell gzip cap 17 -> 20 KB (dated comment) -- the building-code / steel / concrete batches have grown the Group E (Construction) hub past Group A (Electrical) as the catalog's largest, and the trio took `groups/construction/index.html` to ~17.8 KB gz. Housekeeping: home count 731 -> 734 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 52 -> 53, sitemap 753 -> 756, catalog-state line and its gates-green tail, unit-test count; also repaired a batch-(11) historical arrow the 0.93.0 global count-replace had corrupted), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 26 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **734 tiles / 53 modules / 756 sitemap URLs**), **5,027** unit tests, build (734 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:home-payload` / `check:shell-mobile`, the full-catalog 320 px no-horizontal-scroll sweep, the 734-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y + output-to-the-value pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(fire): land spec-v248..v250 -- 3 NFPA 13/20 fire-sprinkler system-design tiles in a new calc-firesprinkler.js; 728 -> 731 tiles, 0.93.0, 2026-07-02

The fire-sprinkler system-design trio: three tiles in a **new lazy Group F module, `calc-firesprinkler.js`** -- the catalog's **52nd `calc-*` module**, a sibling split off beside `calc-fire.js` exactly as `calc-rescue.js` was (the fire module sits near its size cap). Catalog **728 -> 731**, package **0.92.0 -> 0.93.0** (a minor). The catalog carried deep fire-*service* hydraulics (`pdp`, `hydrant-flow`, `standpipe-pdp`, `iso-nff`) and the sprinkler discharge side (`sprinkler-density`, `sprinkler-k-factor`); this bench adds the three numbers a fire-protection designer sets before a single head goes up -- the pump that feeds the system, the water demand and stored supply that pump must sustain, and the head layout that puts the density on the floor -- closing the pump / supply / layout design loop. Second of the proposed new-module trios to land; the reinforced-concrete, geotechnical, masonry, and wood-lateral trios remain PROPOSED. Proposed 2026-07-01.

- **`fire-pump-curve` (spec-v248).** The NFPA 20 three-point envelope for a listed centrifugal fire pump: the churn (no-flow) ceiling at 140% of rated pressure, the overload point at 150% of rated flow, and the 65%-of-rated overload floor, with pass/fail flags and margins as a percent of rated pressure (either field-test point can be left blank and its check returns a null "not entered"). Pinned: a 500 gpm at 100 psi pump at 128 psi churn / 72 psi at 750 gpm passes both ends (12% / 7% margins); a 148 psi churn fails by 8 psi (driving a pressure-relief-valve requirement) and a 60 psi overload fails the floor by 5 psi.
- **`sprinkler-system-demand` (spec-v249).** The NFPA 13 area/density screening demand: sprinkler flow = density x design area, plus the inside/outside hose-stream allowance, held for the required duration to give the stored volume -- the numbers that size the fire pump and the tank. Pinned: Ordinary Hazard Group 2 at 0.20 gpm/ft^2 over 1,500 ft^2 + 250 gpm hose over 90 min -> 550 gpm and 49,500 gal; the Light Hazard cross-check (0.10 / 100 gpm / 30 min) -> 250 gpm and 7,500 gal, a six-fold drop that shows the hazard class, not the room, dominates the water supply.
- **`sprinkler-head-layout` (spec-v250).** The NFPA 13 protection-area / linear-spacing head layout: governing spacing = min(max spacing, sqrt(area per head)), then heads per line, lines, total heads, achieved area per head with a coverage flag, and the spacing/2 wall distance. Pinned: a 40 x 30 ft ordinary-hazard room (130 ft^2 / 15 ft caps) -> the area cap binds at 11.40 ft, 4 x 3 = 12 heads at 100 ft^2 each; the light-hazard cross-check (225 ft^2) -> the 15 ft linear cap binds, 6 heads at 200 ft^2 each.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus cross-checks verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the example, cross-checks, the optional-input null-flag path, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the NFPA 20 (2022) / NFPA 13 (2022) authority with the acceptance-test, relief-valve, hydraulic-calc-separate, hazard-class-editable, and not-a-submittal caveats. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` FIRESPRINKLER_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap (6000 gzip; built ~4.5 KB), and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 7 worked-example fixtures, 28 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Housekeeping: home count 728 -> 731 (index.html title/meta/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/lede), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 51 -> 52, sitemap 750 -> 753, catalog-state line, unit-test count), three specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 25 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all checks agreeing at **731 tiles / 52 modules / 753 sitemap URLs**), **5,024** unit tests, build (731 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:home-payload` / `check:shell-mobile`, the full-catalog 320 px no-horizontal-scroll sweep, the 731-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted render-no-nan + a11y pass on the three new tiles.

### feat(steel): land spec-v254..v256 / v266..v268 -- 6 AISC 360 steel tiles in a new calc-steel.js; 722 -> 728 tiles, 0.92.0, 2026-07-02

The AISC 360 structural-steel batch: six tiles in two trios in a **new lazy Group E module, `calc-steel.js`** -- the catalog's **51st `calc-*` module** and the first to check the steel *member itself* (it already sized steel welds and wood members, never the rolled shape). Catalog **722 -> 728**, package **0.91.0 -> 0.92.0** (a minor). This is the first of the seven proposed new-module trios to land; the fire-sprinkler, reinforced-concrete, geotechnical, masonry, and wood-lateral trios remain PROPOSED. Proposed 2026-07-01 (members, commit a2e7f76) and 2026-07-02 (connections, commit a3fc348).

- **The steel-member trio (spec-v254..v256).** **`steel-beam-flexure`** is the AISC 360 Chapter F plastic-moment plateau Mn = Mp = Fy x Zx (compact + braced), with the ASD Mn/1.67 and LRFD 0.90 Mn -- a W18x50 in A992 gives 421 kip-ft nominal, 252 allowable, 379 design, matching Manual Table 3-2. **`steel-beam-shear`** is the Chapter G web shear Vn = 0.6 Fy Aw Cv1 with the paired Omega_v 1.50 / phi_v 1.00 rolled-I-shape factors (the general 1.67 / 0.90 for slender webs), the end check a moment-passing beam can still fail. **`steel-column-capacity`** is the Chapter E flexural-buckling Fcr from KL/r, the elastic Fe = pi^2 E/(KL/r)^2, and the 4.71 sqrt(E/Fy) inelastic-elastic transition -- a W10x45 stretched from 14 to 24 ft drops from 239 to 97 kips on slenderness alone. Pinned: W18x50 -> Mn 421 / Ma 252 kip-ft; W18x50 -> Vn 192 / Va 128 kips; W10x45 14 ft -> Pn 399 / Pa 239 kips (inelastic), 24 ft -> Pa 97 kips (elastic).
- **The bolted-connection trio (spec-v266..v268).** **`bolt-group-eccentric`** is the elastic vector method, superposing the direct shear P/n with the torsional M r / Ip on the worst corner bolt (the bolted counterpart to `weld-group-eccentric`); a 2x3 group at 3 in under 30 kip at 6 in puts 15.1 kip on the corner bolt, 27.0 kip at 12 in. **`bolt-shear-bearing`** is the AISC J3.6 shear rupture Fnv Ab against the J3.10 bearing/tearout min(1.2 lc t Fu, 2.4 d t Fu), catching the flip from bolt-shear- to tearout-governed as the plate thins (17.9 kip at 1/2 in, 14.3 kip tearout at 1/4 in). **`column-base-plate`** is the Design Guide 1 bearing area A1_req, the m/n/n' cantilevers, and tp = l sqrt(2 Pu/(0.90 Fy B N)), flagging an undersized plate in bearing (400 kip -> 1.06 in plate on a 14 in square; 700 kip -> plate flagged too small).

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the example, cross-check, and every error seam -- including the column inelastic/elastic branch switch, the bolt-shear/tearout governing flip, and the base-plate negative-cantilever geometry error), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the AISC 360-22 Chapter E/F/G/J and Design Guide 1 authority. Full new-module scaffolding: a new `app.js` STEEL_RENDERERS declare, `sw.js` precache entry, `scripts/build.mjs` FILES entry, `check-module-sizes` cap (9000 gzip; built ~7.1 KB), and `check-dimensions` graduated-module registration; plus the per-tile `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations`, `compute-map`, 12 worked-example fixtures, 52 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, dims annotations, and 6 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Housekeeping: home count 722 -> 728 (index.html), README figures resynced (catalog total, module count 50 -> 51, spec range v265 -> v268, package 0.92.0, sitemap 750, batch-(11) narrative), six specs marked LANDED, docs/mobile-responsive.md section 24 appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all 27 checks agreeing at **728 tiles / 51 modules / 750 sitemap URLs**), **5,021** unit tests, build (728 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, the 728-tile render-no-nan sweep, and the responsive-stress sweep on Chromium + WebKit.

### feat(construction): land spec-v263..v265 -- 3 NDS sawn-lumber design tiles; 719 -> 722 tiles, 0.91.0, 2026-07-02

The NDS sawn-lumber design trio, all in the existing Group E / `calc-construction.js` beside the existing `column-buckling-wood` (compression) tile, with **no new module, group, or dependency**. Catalog **719 -> 722**, package **0.90.0 -> 0.91.0** (a minor). This adds wood as the fourth structural material in Group E beside steel, reinforced concrete, and geotechnics, and completes the four member-level limit states for sawn lumber -- bending, shear, compression, and connection. Proposed 2026-07-02 (commit 30c255e).

- **`wood-beam-bending` (spec-v263).** The NDS 3.3.3 beam stability factor `CL` from the beam slenderness `RB = sqrt(le d / b^2)` (capped at 50) and the critical buckling value `FbE = 1.20 Emin' / RB^2`, then the adjusted bending value `Fb' = Fb* CL` and allowable moment `M' = Fb' S` with `S = b d^2 / 6`. The flexural twin the catalog had been missing since it shipped the wood *column*: a stocky, nearly-braced 4x12 header holds `CL` near 1.0, and the same section left tall and unbraced drops off a cliff. Pinned: `Fb* = 1,350`, `Emin' = 620,000`, 3.5 x 11.25 in over `le = 144` in -> `RB` 11.50, `FbE` 5,626 psi, `CL` 0.985, `Fb'` 1,329 psi, `M'` 8,179 ft-lb; cross-check at `le = 600` in -> `CL` 0.817 (the buckling seam).
- **`wood-beam-shear` (spec-v264).** The rectangular-section shear stress `fv = 3V / (2 b d)` (NDS 3.4.2) and the tension-side end-notch reduction `V' = (2/3) Fv' b dn (dn/d)^2` (NDS 3.4.3.2). A single 2 in tension-side notch in a 4x12 cuts the allowable end reaction nearly in half through the squared depth ratio. Pinned: `Fv' = 180`, 3.5 x 11.25 in notched to `dn = 9.25` in, `V = 2,000` lb -> `Vr` 4,725 lb, `Vr'` 2,626 lb, `fv` 92.7 psi, dcr 0.76; cross-check `dn = d` -> `Vr'` equals `Vr` (the no-notch continuity seam).
- **`wood-bolt-connection` (spec-v265).** The full six-mode single-shear NDS European yield model `Z` from the dowel bearing strengths `Fe|| = 11,200 G` / `Fe_perp = 6,100 G^1.45 / sqrt(D)` (NDS 12.3.3) Hankinson-blended to the angle to grain, the bolt bending yield `Fyb`, and the geometry (NDS Table 12.3.1A/B), with the governing value the smallest of the six modes. Pinned: 1/2 in bolt, 4x main into 2x side, DF-L, parallel -> modes 2,450 / 1,050 / 913 / 1,103 / 615 / 716 lb, governing `Z` 615 lb in mode IIIs; cross-check perpendicular to grain (theta 90) -> `Fem` 3,158 psi, `Ktheta` 1.25, `Z` 331 lb.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the example, cross-check, and every error seam including the `RB > 50` slenderness and `dn > d` notch-depth boundaries), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the NDS 3.3.3 / 3.4 / Chapter 12 authority that governs. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), `compute-map`, 6 worked-example fixtures, 28 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, the `app.js` construction declare, dims annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated (the `calc-construction.js` 95000 gzip cap held with headroom, no bump needed). Housekeeping: home count moved 719 -> 722 (index.html title/meta/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/lede); README figures resynced (catalog total, spec range v253 -> v265, package 0.91.0, sitemap 744, per-axis mobile-sweep counts, batch-(10) narrative); three specs marked LANDED; docs/mobile-responsive.md running record appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all 27 checks agreeing at **722 tiles / 50 modules / 744 sitemap URLs**), **5,015** unit tests, build (722 tile + 21 group shells, sitemap 744 URLs), data:verify, `check-shell-mobile` (zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait / 568x320 landscape / 200% text zoom), the 722-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted live-SPA sweep of the three new tiles at the 320px floor (zero horizontal scroll, zero NaN / Infinity / undefined leak, each rendering its pinned worked example to the value).

### feat(construction): land spec-v242..v247 / v251..v253 -- 9 building-code tiles; 710 -> 719 tiles, 0.90.0, 2026-07-02

Nine building-code tiles in three trios, all in the existing Group E / `calc-construction.js` with **no new module, group, or dependency**. Catalog **710 -> 719**, package **0.89.0 -> 0.90.0** (a minor). These were three of the five trios proposed on 2026-07-01 (commits f028fdc, a47f22a, 044eb54); the two that require a **new lazy module** stay PROPOSED (the NFPA 20/13 fire-sprinkler trio spec-v248-v250 -> `calc-firesprinkler.js`, and the AISC 360 steel-member trio spec-v254-v256 -> `calc-steel.js`), and are the clear next unit of work.

- **The IBC/IPC occupancy trio (spec-v242..v244).** **`occupant-load`** sums `ceil(area / occupant-load factor)` across spaces (a repeatable 3-space UI), the one number the whole life-safety chain hangs on -- a 3,000 ft^2 office at 150 gross is 20 people, the same floor as a standing bar at 5 net is 600. **`egress-capacity`** turns that load into the exit count (1 up to 49, 2 to 500, 3 to 1,000, 4 beyond) and the required width (load x the 0.15/0.2/0.3 in-per-occupant capacity factor), floored at the 32 in door-leaf minimum. **`plumbing-fixture-count`** gives the minimum water closets, lavatories, drinking fountains, and service sink from the per-sex two-tier ratios of Table 2902.1. Pinned: 3,000 + 600 ft^2 -> 60 occupants; 160-occupant sprinklered level egress -> 2 exits, 32 in per door; 100-occupant office -> 4 WC, 4 lav, 1 fountain.
- **The cast-in-place placing-and-curing trio (spec-v245..v247).** **`shore-post-load`** is the ACI 347 `max(slab/12 x unit weight + form + live, 100 psf)` design pressure times the shore tributary area (the vertical companion to `formwork-pressure`). **`concrete-evaporation-rate`** is the ACI 305 / Menzel plastic-shrinkage go/no-go, where the concrete temperature, not the air, drives the vapor term. **`concrete-strength-gain`** is the ACI 209R `t/(a+bt)` developed fraction plus the inverse solve for the age to a strip/shore-removal target. Pinned: 8 in slab on a 4 ft grid -> 2,560 lb/shore; 90 degF / 40% / 15 mph -> 0.31 lb/ft^2/hr (precautions); 4,000 psi at 7 days -> 2,814 psi (70%), 8.3 days to 75%.
- **The IBC plan-review trio (spec-v251..v253).** **`allowable-area`** is the Chapter 5 `Aa = At + NS x If` per-story limit with the `[F/P - 0.25] x W/30` frontage increase and the sprinkler-column choice that can triple the area. **`egress-travel-distance`** is the Chapter 10 travel / common-path / dead-end three-check where a single fail fails the floor. **`exterior-opening-protection`** is the Table 705.8 opening-area limit banded by fire separation distance and protection status (with a "no limit" band at 30 ft or a sprinklered wall at 20 ft). Pinned: VB / B corner-lot office -> 29,250 ft^2 allowable, 25,000 passes; 240/68/18 vs 300/100/50 -> all pass; 8 ft sprinklered wall, 1,200 ft^2 -> 25%, 300 ft^2 allowed.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the example, cross-check, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the IBC 2021 / IPC / ACI 347 / ACI 305 / ACI 209R authority that governs. One correctness note: the v244 spec cross-check prose states `ceil(80/75) = 1` water closet per sex for the 160-occupant restaurant; the code-correct value is 2 (four total), consistent with the round-up the primary office example relies on, so the implementation and its pinned fixture use the correct four. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), `compute-map`, 18 worked-example fixtures, 71 collision-checked search aliases, `related-tiles`, the `app.js` construction declare, dims annotations, and 9 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. The `calc-construction.js` module cap was raised 75500 -> 95000 (gzip) for the nine tiles. Housekeeping: home count moved 710 -> 719 (index.html title/meta/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/lede); README figures resynced (catalog total, spec range v241 -> v253, package 0.90.0, tools-data raw/gz size, sitemap 741, per-axis mobile-sweep counts); nine specs marked LANDED; docs/mobile-responsive.md running record appended. All gates green at the new state: lint (all 27 checks agreeing at **719 tiles / 50 modules / 741 sitemap URLs**), **5,012** unit tests, build (719 tile + 21 group shells, sitemap 741 URLs), data:verify, `check-shell-mobile` (zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait / 568x320 landscape / 200% text zoom), the 719-tile render-no-nan sweep, and a targeted live-SPA sweep of the nine new tiles at the 320px floor (zero horizontal scroll, zero NaN / Infinity / undefined leak, each rendering its pinned worked example to the value).

### feat(restoration): land spec-v143 / v150 / v155 / v156 -- 4 restoration novelty tiles; 706 -> 710 tiles, 0.89.0, 2026-07-01

Four of the nine long-deferred water/mold restoration specs (PROPOSED 2026-06-23, DEFERRED 2026-06-29 as conceptually adjacent to the already-saturated live water/mold set), re-vetted one last time against the now-42-tile Group D catalog and landed **only** where they answer a question no live tile already does. Catalog **706 -> 710**, package **0.88.0 -> 0.89.0** (a minor), with **no new module, group, or dependency** (all four land in the existing Group D / `calc-restoration.js`).

The dupe vet is the substance of this batch. Comparing all nine deferred specs against the live catalog found five genuine near-duplicates that stayed unbuilt: **v145** (`antimicrobial-coverage`) duplicates the live `antimicrobial-dilution`, whose description is literally "Antimicrobial Mix and Coverage" and which already sizes gallons from area and label coverage; **v151** (`dry-standard-mc`) duplicates `moisture-dry-goal` ("Dry Standard vs Affected Reading"), which already returns the delta above the dry standard and the points still to remove; **v149** duplicates `air-sample-volume`, which already computes total pump time; **v142** overlaps `evaporation-load` plus `dry-time-projection`; and **v144** overlaps `flood-cut-quantity` plus `carpet-restore-replace` plus `demo-debris`. The four that landed each fill a real gap:

- **`surface-condensation-risk`** (v143, Group D): the Magnus dew point of the chamber air compared to the coldest IR-read surface, flagging where warm humid drying air will condense on a cold window or slab edge and make secondary water. The surface screen `psychrometric` (which reports a dew point but takes no surface temperature) never made. Pinned: 80 degF / 50% RH vs a 50 degF window -> 59.7 degF dew point, -9.7 degF margin, condensing.
- **`spore-io-ratio`** (v150, Group D): the indoor/outdoor airborne-spore ratio and the water-damage marker-genera (Stachybotrys / Chaetomium) check, the everyday mold clearance read no tile computed. A screen, not a certificate. Pinned: 800 vs 1,500 spores/m^3, no marker -> 0.53 ratio, supportable.
- **`hardwood-floor-drying-mat`** (v155, Group D): the Class 4 mat-and-suction-unit count for saving a wet wood floor in place, the specialty equipment the class screen routes to but never sized. Pinned: 120 ft^2 at 6 ft^2/mat, 16 mats/unit -> 20 mats, 2 units.
- **`mold-cleaning-labor`** (v156, Group D): the multi-pass HEPA-vacuum-and-damp-wipe labor hours and crew calendar time, the S520 source-removal cost driver between the scope tier and the chemistry. Pinned: 500 ft^2 at 100 ft^2/hr, 2 passes, crew of 2 -> 10.0 labor-hr, 5.0 hr on the clock.

Each carries the full v14 discipline (dimensional-analysis annotation, a pinned worked example plus a cross-check verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block covering the example, the cross-check, and every error seam), the v18/v21 `{error}` output contract, and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the ANSI/IICRC S500 / S520 authority that governs. The `calc-restoration.js` module cap was raised 44000 -> 52000 (gzip) for the four tiles; the `tools-data.js`, `citations.js`, and `tile-meta.js` registries stayed within cap. All gates green at the new state: lint (all 27 checks agreeing at **710 tiles / 50 modules / 732 sitemap URLs**), **5,003** unit tests, build (710 tile + 21 group shells, sitemap 732 URLs), data:verify, `check-shell-mobile` (826 checks across 732 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait / 568x320 landscape / 200% text zoom), and a targeted live-SPA sweep of the four new tiles at the 320px floor (zero horizontal scroll, zero NaN / Infinity / undefined leak, each rendering its pinned worked example to the value).

### feat: land spec-v230..v241 -- 12 energy-cost-savings / equipment-sizing tiles; 694 -> 706 tiles, 0.88.0, 2026-06-30

Twelve tiles in four trios (PROPOSED 2026-06-30, landed same day) that turn physics the catalog already carried into the retrofit and operating-cost numbers a trade actually quotes. Catalog **694 -> 706**, package **0.87.0 -> 0.88.0** (a minor), with **no new module, group, or dependency** (all twelve land in existing modules and groups).

**Electrical energy-cost-savings trio (`calc-service.js`, Group A):**
- **`vfd-energy-savings`** (v230) -- the annual kWh and dollars a variable-frequency drive saves on a centrifugal pump or fan, integrating the affinity cube law `P/P_full = (Q/Q_full)^3` over a three-bin duty cycle. The load profile, not the horsepower, is the business case. A screening estimate, not a metered M&V.
- **`lighting-retrofit-savings`** (v231) -- the energy `fixtures x (W_old - W_new)/1000 x hours x rate` plus demand-charge savings `kW x $/kW-mo x 12` and simple payback of an LED fixture swap. The demand term often shortens the payback by a third. A simple payback, not a life-cycle analysis.
- **`power-factor-billing-savings`** (v232) -- the kVA demand a capacitor bank frees (`kVA = kW/pf`), the correcting `kVAR = kW x (tan(acos pf_old) - tan(acos pf_new))`, and the payback against a $/kVA-month tariff. Sharply diminishing returns above 0.9. A billing estimate, not a rate-tariff analysis.

**Heat-pump heating-mode trio (`calc-hvac.js`, Group C):**
- **`heat-pump-seasonal-energy`** (v233) -- the seasonal operating cost of a heat pump (`load x 1000 / HSPF x rate`) set beside gas (`load / AFUE`) and resistance (COP 1) at the local rates. The AHRI 210/240 HSPF; the same unit is a bargain in one utility territory and a premium in another. An operating-cost estimate, not a metered bill.
- **`dual-fuel-balance-point`** (v234) -- the economic switchover COP where a dual-fuel thermostat should hand off to gas (`hp $/MMBtu = 293.07/COP x rate_kwh` vs `gas $/MMBtu = 10/AFUE x rate_therm`). An economic setpoint aid, not a controls-commissioning procedure.
- **`heat-pump-cold-capacity`** (v235) -- the AHRI 47 F / 17 F capacity interpolation to the outdoor design temperature and the auxiliary strip heat (`shortfall / 3412`) a cold-climate conversion needs. A unit sized on its 47 F nameplate is badly undersized at 5 F. A sizing check, not a performance guarantee.

**Grid-tied battery-economics trio (`calc-solar.js`, Group A):**
- **`battery-tou-arbitrage`** (v236) -- the time-of-use spread value `usable x peak - usable/RTE x offpeak` net of the round-trip loss, and the break-even ratio `1/RTE` the peak must beat. A gross spread-value aid, not a financed payback.
- **`battery-peak-shaving`** (v237) -- the demand-charge savings and the energy-limited actual shave `min(target, usable/duration)` a battery can hold across a peak. A wide plateau demands far more storage per kW than a spike. A demand-savings estimate, not a metered bill.
- **`battery-c-rate`** (v238) -- the deliverable power bounded by the C-rate (`nameplate x C`) and the inverter, and the discharge duration `usable / deliverable`. A pack's power is not its energy. A nameplate power check, not a cell-level thermal model.

**Compressed-air energy trio (`calc-hvac.js`, Group C):**
- **`air-leak-cost`** (v239) -- the US DOE Compressed Air Challenge load/unload leak-test flow (`t_load / (t_load + t_unload) x cfm`) and annual cost at the system specific power. A neglected system loses 20-30% of its air. An estimate from a stopwatch test, not a metered audit.
- **`compressed-air-power`** (v240) -- the single-stage adiabatic (isentropic) compression horsepower `0.004364 x P1 x Q x (k/(k-1)) x [(P2/P1)^((k-1)/k) - 1]` and running cost. Multi-staging beats single stage above ~100 psig. A sizing and cost estimate, not a compressor selection.
- **`air-pressure-setpoint-savings`** (v241) -- the isentropic power-ratio savings from dropping the discharge pressure, reproducing the DOE rule of ~0.5% of compressor energy per psi. An energy-savings estimate, not a metered result.

Each compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit and carries the v14 `// dims:` annotation, the v18/v21 `{error}` contract (non-finite and out-of-domain inputs return `{ error }`; the two payback fields return `null` rather than a divide-by-zero when annual savings are zero), and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the US DOE motor/pump-system / Compressed Air Challenge / AHRI 210/240 HSPF / NREL battery round-trip / power-triangle authority that governs. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), `compute-map`, 24 worked-example rows, 96 collision-checked search aliases, related-tiles, `app.js` declares, dims annotations, 12 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated.

Housekeeping: raised five module-size caps with dated notes (`calc-service` 13k->18.5k, `calc-hvac` 43.5k->55k, `calc-solar` 14k->18k, `tools-data` 77k->84k, `citations` 212k->224k) and the group-shell cap (14k->17k, the Group A electrical hub grew with six tiles); bumped the live home-page count (`694 -> 706`, index.html title/meta/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/lede) and resynced the README figures (tile / sitemap / route counts, the unit-test count, and the catalog-state narrative with a new batch entry). All gates green: lint (27 checks at **706 tiles / 50 modules / 728 sitemap URLs**), **4,999** unit tests, build (706 tile + 21 group shells, 728 sitemap URLs), data:verify (117 hashes), `check-shells`, `check-dist`, `check-shell-mobile` (822 checks across 728 shells), render-no-nan (12/12 new tiles), and the full-catalog responsive-stress sweep (706 live tile views at 320px on Chromium + WebKit, zero horizontal scroll).

### feat: land spec-v218..v229 -- 12 energy / structural / solar building-science tiles; 682 -> 694 tiles, 0.87.0, 2026-06-30

Twelve tiles in four trios (PROPOSED 2026-06-30, landed same day) that deepen the residential-energy, structural-load, PV-design, and cooling-load corners of the catalog. Catalog **682 -> 694**, package **0.86.0 -> 0.87.0** (a minor), with **no new module, group, or dependency** (all twelve land in existing modules and groups).

**Air-tightness and ventilation trio (`calc-hvacservice.js`, Group C):**
- **`blower-door-ach50`** (v218) -- turns a blower-door reading into ACH50 = CFM50 x 60 / conditioned volume, checked against the IECC R402.4.1.2 limit (<= 3 ACH50 in CZ 3-8, <= 5 in CZ 1-2), plus the LBL natural infiltration (ACH and cfm) the next two tiles consume. ASTM E779 / E1827 are the test methods; the N-factor varies with climate, height, and shielding. A field normalization, not a rater's signed report.
- **`ashrae-622-ventilation`** (v219) -- the ASHRAE 62.2-2019 whole-house ventilation `Qtot = 0.03 x CFA + 7.5 x (Nbr + 1)` and the continuous fan `Qfan = max(0, Qtot - Qinf)` a tightened house then needs. The conservative default is zero infiltration credit. A sizing aid, not a compliance certificate.
- **`infiltration-load`** (v220) -- the sensible `1.08 x cfm x dT` and latent `0.68 x cfm x grains` heating/cooling load the leaked air drives, from the blower-door natural-infiltration cfm. The infiltration component only, not a stamped Manual J.

**PV system-design trio (`calc-solar.js`, Group A):**
- **`pv-energy-yield`** (v221) -- the NREL PVWatts annual energy `Pdc x PSH x 365 x PR`, plus specific yield (kWh/kWp) and capacity factor. The performance ratio (default 0.77) is the single biggest lever. A pre-design estimate, not a bankable model.
- **`pv-row-spacing`** (v222) -- the inter-row pitch `L cos(tilt) + L sin(tilt)/tan(profile_angle)` and ground-coverage ratio `L / pitch` from tilt and the winter-sun profile angle. A layout geometry, not an annual shading-loss model.
- **`pv-inverter-ratio`** (v223) -- the inverter loading ratio (DC:AC), the clipping onset as a fraction of nameplate, and the cost-optimal 1.1-1.3 band verdict. A sizing sanity check, not a clipping-loss model.

**ASCE 7 structural design-loads trio (`calc-construction.js`, Group E):**
- **`rain-load-ponding`** (v224) -- the ASCE 7 Ch. 8 rain load `R = 5.2 x (ds + dh)` at the blocked-primary case plus the optional IPC secondary-drainage flow `Q = 0.0104 x A x i`. A flat roof must also pass the §8.4 ponding-instability check. A load and flow aid, not a stamped design.
- **`asce7-load-combinations`** (v225) -- the seven §2.4.1 basic ASD combinations reduced to the governing gravity demand (sizes the member) and the net uplift (`0.6D + 0.6W < 0`, sizes the hold-down). Wind is signed. A load-combination aid, not a member design.
- **`seismic-base-shear`** (v226) -- the §12.8 equivalent-lateral-force `Cs = SDS / (R/Ie)`, capped at `SD1 / (T (R/Ie))` for T <= TL and floored at the code minimum, times the seismic weight. SDS / SD1 from the USGS maps; R from Table 12.2-1. A lateral-demand estimate, not a stamped seismic design.

**Cooling-load-components trio (`calc-hvacsystems.js`, Group C):**
- **`window-solar-heat-gain`** (v227) -- the fenestration solar `A x SHGC x PSF` and conduction `A x U x CLTD` cooling load; on a west wall the solar term dwarfs conduction by an order of magnitude. One Manual J component.
- **`internal-heat-gains`** (v228) -- the people (sensible + latent), lighting, and equipment (`3.412 x W x use factor`) gains and the sensible/latent split that flips a packed room latent-heavy. One Manual J component.
- **`envelope-conduction-load`** (v229) -- the opaque `U x A x sol-air CLTD` conduction load that makes the cool-roof case (cutting solar absorptance drops the load far more than the air temperature would suggest). One Manual J component.

Each compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit and carries the v14 `// dims:` annotation, the v18/v21 `{error}` contract (non-finite and out-of-domain inputs return `{ error }`), and v19/v22 citation discipline naming the ASHRAE 62.2 / IECC R402.4.1.2 / ASTM E779 / NREL PVWatts / NREL-Sandia / ASCE 7 Ch. 8 / §2.4.1 / §12.8 / ASHRAE-ACCA Manual J authority that governs. Full wiring: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), `compute-map`, 24 worked-example rows, 96 collision-checked search aliases, related-tiles, `app.js` declares, dims annotations, 12 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated.

Housekeeping: bumped the live home-page count (`682 -> 694`, index.html title/meta/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/lede) and resynced the README figures (tile / sitemap / route counts, the unit-test count, and the catalog-state narrative with a new batch entry). All gates green: lint (27 checks at **694 tiles / 50 modules / 716 sitemap URLs**), **4,987** unit tests, build (694 tile + 21 group shells, 716 sitemap URLs), data:verify (117 hashes), `check-shells`, `check-dist`, `check-shell-mobile` (810 checks across 716 shells), render-no-nan (12/12 new tiles), and the full-catalog responsive-stress sweep (694 live tile views at 320px on Chromium + WebKit, zero horizontal scroll).

No bug was found.
