### feat(roofing): land spec-v215..v217 -- 3 roofing material-takeoff tiles; 679 -> 682 tiles, 0.86.0, 2026-06-30

Three install-side roofing tiles (PROPOSED 2026-06-30, landed same day) that close the gaps the shingle-only `roofing-squares` left: the eave ice barrier, the metal-panel alternative, and the ridge-cap-and-fastener accessories. Catalog **679 -> 682**, package **0.85.0 -> 0.86.0** (a minor), with **no new module, group, or dependency** (all three land in the existing Group E / `calc-construction.js`).

- **`ice-barrier-coverage`** (v215) -- the self-adhering ice-and-water membrane the eave actually needs: the up-slope coverage from the overhang and pitch (`coverage = (overhang + 24 in) x sqrt(rise^2 + 144)/12`, running the IRC R905.1.2 extent 24 in inside the exterior wall line measured on the slope), the courses when that exceeds one roll width, and the rolls. A deep overhang or a low pitch quietly pushes the coverage past a single 36 in course, so a one-roll-per-eave guess shorts the order. Required only where the AHJ has adopted it; valley and low-slope coverage is a separate add. IRC R905.1.2 + ASTM D1970 govern.
- **`metal-roof-panels`** (v216) -- panels, linear feet, and through-fasteners (or clips) for one metal roof plane: the panel count from the product's net coverage width (`panels = ceil(eave width in / net coverage in)`, not the sheet width), the total linear feet, the slope-area squares, and the fasteners from the wind-zone pattern. A 36 in exposed-fastener panel and a 16 in standing-seam panel over the same plane differ by nearly a factor of two in panel count, which is why net coverage width, not square footage, sets a metal order. Per roof plane (double for a symmetric gable). MCA / MRA references + manufacturer charts govern.
- **`ridge-cap-fasteners`** (v217) -- the two accessories the shingle field takeoff leaves open: the hip-and-ridge cap bundles by the linear foot of ridge and hip, and the roofing nails by the pound. IRC R905.2.6 steps the field pattern from four nails to six in the high-wind / steep rows (`field nails = squares x shingles/sq x nails/shingle`), moving the order by half again, and a pre-formed hip/ridge product covers far less per bundle than field-cut 3-tab caps. A material takeoff, not a nailing schedule. IRC R905.2.6 + manufacturer instructions govern.

Each compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit, carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, the v18/v21 output contract (any non-finite or out-of-domain input -- non-positive eave/roll/panel dimensions, negative overhang/ridge/hip, pitch outside 0-24, a side lap that meets or exceeds the roll width, nails-per-shingle outside 2-8, or nothing to order -- returns `{error}`), and the v19/v22 citation discipline (the IRC / ASTM / MCA-MRA references named, never reproduced). Full wiring landed: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), compute-map, 6 worked-example rows, 21 collision-checked search aliases, related-tiles, the `app.js` declare, dimensional annotations, and 3 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated, and the ungated home-page count moved off "600+" to the live **682**. All gates green: lint (all checks agreeing at **682 tiles / 50 modules / 704 sitemap URLs**), **4,975** unit tests, build (682 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify (117 hashes), render-no-nan **682/682**, responsive-stress (Chromium + WebKit), a11y, and `check:shell-mobile` (zero horizontal scroll at the 320px floor across 704 shells). This closes the spec-v215..v217 roofing material-takeoff batch. See specs [v215](specs/spec-v215.md) - [v217](specs/spec-v217.md).

### feat(restoration): land spec-v141/v146-v148/v152-v154 -- 7 fire & smoke restoration tiles; 672 -> 679 tiles, 0.85.0, 2026-06-29

Seven long-deferred, in-charter restoration specs (PROPOSED 2026-06-23, never landed) that the spec-v189..v198 water pass left on the shelf. Per the vetted-novel review this is the cohesive **fire & smoke** family -- a capability area the catalog never carried, so there is zero duplication risk against the already-saturated water/mold set. Catalog **672 -> 679**, package **0.84.0 -> 0.85.0** (a minor), with **no new module, group, or dependency** (all seven land in the existing Group D / `calc-restoration.js`).

- **`equipment-heat-load`** (v141) -- the sensible heat air movers, dehumidifiers, and scrubbers dump into a drying chamber (`3.412 BTU/hr per watt`) and the makeup ventilation to hold a target temperature rise via the 1.08 standard-air relation (`60 min x 0.075 lb/ft^3 x 0.24 BTU/lb-degF`): the heat load, the cfm to cap the rise, and the rise an entered exhaust airflow actually delivers. An overheated chamber leaves the efficient-evaporation band. ANSI/IICRC S500 names the band.
- **`char-depth-capacity`** (v146) -- whether a fire-charred wood beam or post kept enough sound section to carry load: the AWC/NDS one-dimensional char depth (~1.5 in/hr) plus the heat-degraded zero-strength layer, the residual cross-section, and the bending-capacity (section-modulus) fraction that drives the keep-or-replace call. Screens the bending section only; a structural engineer governs connections, splitting, and the load path.
- **`soot-cleaning-takeoff`** (v147) -- soot-affected wall and ceiling area turned into the chemical sponges, labor hours, and odor-seal primer the dry-smoke cleaning phase consumes (sponges from a per-sponge coverage, labor from a production rate, primer gallons from a coverage rate). The residue type governs the method, so dry sponging is for dry smoke only. IICRC S700 governs.
- **`ozone-shock-treatment`** (v148) -- an ozone shock-deodorization sized by treatable volume against each unit's rated volume, with the safety lockout front and center: the generators needed, the shock duration, and the sealed-unoccupied-aerate sequence. The space must air below the 0.1 ppm OSHA limit before reentry; a hydroxyl generator is the occupied-space alternative. Sizes equipment, not authority to run ozone occupied.
- **`smoke-residue-method`** (v152) -- a reference screen mapping an observed residue (dry, wet, protein, fuel-oil/puffback, or synthetic) to its cleaning method, the decision every fire job turns on before a sponge or solvent is chosen: source, appearance, recommended method, deodorization note, and the trap to avoid (protein looks invisible but must not be dry-sponged). A test-clean of an inconspicuous area governs the final method. IICRC S700 governs.
- **`thermal-fog-deodorization`** (v153) -- the deodorant a thermal or ULV fogging pass consumes, dosed by structure volume against a label rate in ounces per 1,000 cubic feet across one or more passes: the ounces to mix and the gallons. Fogging penetrates the porous paths the smoke took. A thermal fogger produces a hot fog from an open flame, so the space is unoccupied and a fire watch applies.
- **`contents-packout-inventory`** (v154) -- an affected room's contents turned into the boxes, climate storage, and truck loads a pack-out requires: contents volume from a density per floor area, the box count, the storage volume with a warehouse stacking allowance, and the truck loads. The actual inventory and box mix govern; the densities are starting rules of thumb.

The remaining nine restoration specs (v142/v143/v144/v145/v149/v150/v151/v155/v156) stay annotated **DEFERRED** as conceptually adjacent to live water/mold tiles. Full wiring landed for the seven: `tools-data`, `tile-meta`, `citations` (editionNote + assumptions), compute-map, 14 worked-example rows, 42 collision-checked search aliases, related-tiles, the `app.js` declare, dimensional annotations, and 7 bounds-fuzzer blocks; corpus + tile-index regenerated. Every compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit, carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, the v18/v21 output contract (any non-finite or out-of-domain input returns `{error}`; six render stacked single-column output lines and `smoke-residue-method` renders one `makeSelect` plus wrapping lines), and the v19/v22 citation discipline (the IICRC/AWC/OSHA references named, never reproduced). All gates green: lint (all checks agreeing at **679 tiles / 50 modules / 701 sitemap URLs**), **4,972** unit tests, build (679 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify (117 hashes), render-no-nan **679/679**, responsive-stress **71/71** (Chromium + WebKit), a11y **680/680**, and `check:shell-mobile` (**795 checks across 701 shells**, zero horizontal scroll at the 320px floor). A follow-up docs pass resynced the ungated README figures (home-payload %, `tools-data.js` raw size) and added `docs/mobile-responsive.md` sec 17. This closes the last in-charter `PROPOSED` specs for the fire & smoke family. See specs [v141](specs/spec-v141.md), [v146](specs/spec-v146.md) - [v148](specs/spec-v148.md), and [v152](specs/spec-v152.md) - [v154](specs/spec-v154.md).

### feat(landscape, masonry): land spec-v207..v214 -- 8 landscape-irrigation and masonry/finish tiles; 664 -> 672 tiles, 0.84.0, 2026-06-29

Two adjacent install benches the trades-only catalog had never carried, landed under the spec-v106 trades-only charter with **no new module, group, or dependency**. Catalog **664 -> 672**, package **0.83.2 -> 0.84.0** (a minor). The landscape-irrigation and planting install cluster (Group L / `calc-agriculture.js`):

- **`sprinkler-precip-rate`** (v207) -- the precipitation rate a valve zone applies, `PR = 96.3 x zone gpm / zone area`, in in/hr: the number that sets the runtime and the reason spray heads (~1.5-2 in/hr) and rotors (~0.4-0.8 in/hr) never share a valve. The existing three irrigation tiles were all aimed elsewhere (crop ET demand, catch-can audit, chemical spray rigs); nothing gave a landscape installer the in/hr the zone is designed around.
- **`irrigation-zone-runtime`** (v208) -- the controller program: net (depth / rate x 60), gross (net / lower-quarter distribution uniformity, so the dry corners get the target), and a cycle-and-soak split into runs short enough to beat runoff on the soil and slope.
- **`drip-zone-flow`** (v209) -- total emitter flow (gph and gpm) vs the valve / lateral flow limit, with the emitter count given directly (point-source) or derived from dripline length and emitter spacing (inline). Keep utilization under 100% or the far emitters starve.
- **`plant-spacing-count`** (v210) -- square (area / spacing^2) and triangular (area / (0.866 x spacing^2), the staggered 60-degree grid that packs ~15% more plants) plant counts from bed area and on-center spacing.
- **`sod-takeoff`** (v211) -- slabs and pallets from a lawn area plus a cut/edge waste allowance (in ft^2 and square yards); slab and pallet coverage vary by farm (editable defaults ~10 / ~450 ft^2).

The masonry/finish takeoffs the two-tile masonry shelf left open (Group E / `calc-construction.js`):

- **`cmu-grout-volume`** (v212) -- grouted-core (cores = floor(len x 12 / spacing) + 1) plus bond-beam grout in ft^3 and cubic yards for a reinforced CMU wall; cites TMS 602 / ACI 530.1 and NCMA TEK, and the engineer of record governs the reinforcement.
- **`masonry-coursing`** (v213) -- courses to a height (course = unit + bed joint) and the course-out check that flags a height off the 8 in CMU / three-course-brick module; cites BIA Technical Notes and NCMA TEK.
- **`wallpaper-rolls`** (v214) -- the strip-method roll takeoff where one pattern repeat is wasted per strip, so a large repeat can nearly double the order for the same wall area; door/window openings are a manual strip credit.

Every compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit (machine-verified in `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js`, +16 worked-example fixtures), carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, the v18/v21 output contract (any non-finite or out-of-domain input returns `{error}`), and the v19/v22 citation discipline (`GOVERNANCE.general`; the design references named, never reproduced). All eight render with zero horizontal scroll at the 320px floor. No module or data cap moved (both modules had headroom). All gates green: lint (all 27 checks agreeing at **672 tiles / 50 modules / 694 sitemap URLs**), **4,965** unit tests, build (672 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, the worked-examples runner (802 fixtures), the 672-tile render-no-nan sweep, and the responsive-stress sweep on Chromium and WebKit. This closes the last `PROPOSED` specs in the tree.

### docs: close the README catalog-narrative gap (608 -> 664) and correct one ungated figure, 2026-06-26

The README "The catalog (cheat sheet)" prose log chronicled spec landings only through v128 (608 tiles, package 0.73.0), so a reader reaching the end of the log saw a 608-tile catalog that contradicted the gate-anchored 664-tile headline and cheat-sheet table. The log also never documented its single most significant inflection: the **spec-v106/v107 trades-only refocus** that retired the four liability-bearing non-trade groups (S Legal, U Veterinary, V EMS, W Aviation), cutting the catalog 688 -> 600. Added one consolidated current-state lead paragraph that documents the v107 cut and the v121-v206 trades-only second pass (600 -> 664, including the `calc-motor.js` 50th-module split and the restoration / electrician / pipefit deepening) and lands on the live totals (664 tiles / 50 modules / 686 sitemap URLs / 4,957 tests, all gates green), with a forward pointer to the CHANGELOG as the exhaustive per-release record. Also corrected the lone drifted ungated figure in the repository map (`tools-data.js` gzip ~65 -> ~64 KB; live 63.5 KB). Docs-only; no version bump, no source or behavior change. Verified: full lint chain green (check-readme-counts and the em-dash / grep guards), and the README narrative tail now agrees with the headline.

### fix(catalog): retire five dead input controls and add the check-dead-inputs gate; 0.83.2, 2026-06-26

A full-tree sweep for the trench-slope failure mode (a control the user can set that the math silently ignores) found five live tiles whose renderer drew an input field that the compute function never read. Unlike trench-slope (an inert `surcharge ? 1.0 : 1.0` factor), these were destructured and then never referenced at all, so the displayed answer stayed finite and dimensionally valid and the render-leak, dimensions, and worked-example gates all passed it. **No calculator output changes**: every compute already ignored these inputs, so removing them leaves all pinned worked examples correct to the digit; package **0.83.1 -> 0.83.2** (a patch). The retired controls:

- `water-meter-sizing` (calc-plumbing.js) -- `available_loss_psi`. The citation told the user "the available pressure loss must be the drop across the meter, not the static pressure," implying the field governed the verdict, but the AWWA M22 flow-percent sizing here has no meter head-loss curve to compare it against, so the input dangled. The flow-based adequate / headroom / above-peak-rating outputs are unchanged.
- `srt-fm-ratio` (calc-water.js) -- `ras_flow_mgd` and `ras_tss_mg_l`. The Metcalf & Eddy / WEF MOP 11 solids-retention-time and F:M identities are a mass balance over the solids that *leave* the system (wasting plus effluent); return activated sludge recirculates internally and by construction never appears in either formula. The two RAS fields collected data the math correctly excludes.
- `wind-pressure` (calc-construction.js) -- `roof_type`. The tile reports ASCE 7 wall velocity pressure with fixed windward/leeward `Cp`; the roof-type select drives roof pressures, which this tile does not compute, so gable/hip/flat changed nothing.
- `heat-stress` (calc-cross.js) -- `wind_mph`. The NWS Rothfusz heat index does not use wind, and the bundled WBGT approximation estimates the natural wet-bulb without it, so the wind field was inert. (The neighboring `wind-chill` tile keeps its `wind_mph` -- it genuinely uses it.)
- `ev-charger-load` (calc-solar.js) -- `charger_voltage`. The NEC 625 panel-impact math runs entirely in amperes (nameplate amps in; breaker / conductor / panel-total / headroom in amps out); the 240 vs 208 V select never entered the result.

Four further vestigial signature/example params with no rendered control were removed in the same pass for hygiene (economizer `changeover_db_f` / `supply_temp_f`, evaporation-load `ceiling_ft`, slope-stake `convention`). A new lint gate, `scripts/check-dead-inputs.mjs` (27th in the chain), sweeps every `calc-*.js` and `pure-math.js` and fails CI if any exported compute function destructures an input name it never references, so the class cannot recur. The v14 corpus was regenerated and the five worked-example fixtures pruned of the retired keys. All gates green: lint (including the new gate), **4,957** unit tests, build (664 tile + 21 group shells), data:verify, the 664-tile render-no-nan sweep, the 780-check 320px shell-mobile audit, and the responsive-stress sweep on Chromium and WebKit.

### fix(cross): trench-slope honors the surcharge input -- defers to a PE when a surcharge load is present; 0.83.1, 2026-06-26

The `trench-slope` tile (OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P) exposed a "Surcharge near trench" checkbox whose value was silently discarded: `computeTrenchSlope` multiplied the setback by `(surcharge ? 1.0 : 1.0)`, an inert no-op, so toggling the control changed nothing in the displayed slope, setback, top width, or bench height. That is the worst failure mode for a safety aid: a control that implies it governs the answer but does not. OSHA Appendix B's simple maximum-allowable-slope option assumes no surcharge load adjacent to the trench; a surcharge voids the simple tables and requires an engineered protective system. The fix replaces the no-op with a guard that, when surcharge is checked, returns the engineered-design notice (mirroring the existing depth > 20 ft PE guard) instead of a possibly-unsafe simple-slope number. No safety factor is invented. A regression test in `test/unit/calc-cross-v3.test.js` pins the new guard; package **0.83.0 -> 0.83.1** (a patch). Found during a full-tree QA sweep (4,957 unit tests, every lint gate, the 664-tile render-no-nan sweep, and the 320px responsive sweep all green).

### feat(restoration): land spec-v189..v198 -- 8 water-damage restoration tiles; 656 -> 664 tiles, 0.83.0, 2026-06-26

The deferred water-damage restoration second/third pass under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: **eight** evidence-driven, in-scope tiles in `calc-restoration.js`, **no new module, group, or dependency**. These were the last `PROPOSED` specs in the tree; the catalog landed v199-v206 ahead of them, so this batch clears the backlog. Each tile id and renderer was verified absent from the live catalog before writing (no duplicates this pass), and the three spec-named related-tile ids that pointed at never-landed tiles (`thermal-fog-deodorization`, `hardwood-floor-drying-mat`, `dry-standard-mc`) were substituted with real ids (`smoke-ejector-cfm` / `chamber-turnover`, `air-movers`, `moisture-dry-goal`). Catalog **656 -> 664**, package **0.82.0 -> 0.83.0** (a minor). Every compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit (machine-verified in `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js`), and all eight render with zero horizontal scroll and zero axe-core violations at the 320px floor. Spec-v198 §6 notes this batch leaves the restoration trade effectively saturated for everyday IICRC S500/S520/S700 work.

- `drying-balance` (v189) -- closes the loop between `evaporation-load` and `dehumidifier`: balance (installed minus evaporation load, pints/day), the capacity-to-load ratio against a target margin, and a balanced / no-margin / deficit verdict with the capacity to add. 200 ppd load vs 260 ppd installed at a 1.2 target -> **+60 ppd, ratio 1.30, balanced**; vs 150 ppd installed -> **-50 ppd, ratio 0.75, deficit, add 90 ppd**. Field capacity falls below the AHAM nameplate as the air dries; the restorer's daily monitoring and S500 govern.
- `bound-water` (v190) -- the pounds and gallons of water held in wet materials that drying must evaporate, from material volume, oven-dry density, and the moisture-content drop to goal. 10 ft^3 softwood at 32 lb/ft^3, 40% -> 12% -> **320 lb dry mass, 89.6 lb (10.7 gal) of water**. Dry-weight basis (consistent with `wood-emc`); meter readings and S500 govern. A planning estimate, not a gravimetric measurement.
- `disinfectant-dwell` (v193) -- the label-typical wet contact (dwell) time by EPA-registered antimicrobial class plus the keep-wet and pre-clean rules. Quat -> **~10 min** wet contact; accelerated hydrogen peroxide -> **~1-5 min**. Antimicrobials are EPA-registered pesticides whose label is the legal authority (FIFRA); the surface must stay visibly wet and be pre-cleaned. S500/S520 govern the context.
- `carpet-restore-replace` (v194) -- the S500 carpet/cushion restore-vs-replace matrix from water category, component, and delamination. Category 1 carpet not delaminated -> **dry in place or float and restore** (cushion typically out); Category 3 -> **remove and dispose**; delaminated carpet -> **replace**. A professional determination case by case; the restorer's judgment, customer agreement, and S500 govern.
- `category-deterioration` (v195) -- the S500 principle that water category is set at the time of remediation, not by the source: origin category + elapsed wet time + warm flag + contaminant contact -> the likely current category. Category 1 at 72 h warm -> **Category 2**; contaminant contact -> **Category 3** regardless of the clock. When in doubt the higher category is assumed; the restorer and S500 govern.
- `hydroxyl-sizing` (v196) -- occupied-space hydroxyl deodorization sized by structure volume against a per-unit coverage rating, with the continuous run-time expectation; the occupied-safe counterpart to ozone shock and thermal fogging. 12,000 ft^3 at 6,000 ft^3/unit -> **2 generators**. Coverage and run times are manufacturer-specific; remove the source and clean first. The manufacturer and IICRC S700 govern.
- `cavity-drying-system` (v197) -- injection / wall-cavity drying sized by the stud-bay count from wall length and stud spacing, the injection port count, and the air-system count; the minimally invasive alternative to a flood cut. A 32 ft wall on 16 in centers, 1 port/bay, 12 ports/system -> **24 bays, 24 ports, 2 systems**. Port counts are manufacturer-specific; respect cavity contamination and pressure direction. S500 governs.
- `dry-time-projection` (v198) -- turns the drying log into a schedule: the remaining points to goal and a constant-rate days-to-goal estimate from the moisture-content drop between readings, or a not-progressing flag when the daily drop stalls. 28% to a 12% goal at 4 points/day -> **4 days** (8 days at 2/day; not-progressing at 0). Drying is non-linear and slows near the goal; the drying-log readings and S500 govern the real pull date.

### feat(pipefitting): land spec-v204..v206 -- 3 process-piping / med-gas tiles; 653 -> 656 tiles, 0.82.0, 2026-06-24

The deferred process/specialized plumbing-pipefitting cluster under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: **three** evidence-driven, in-scope tiles, **no new module, group, or dependency**. Each closes a daily-math gap that the existing run-and-flange and fuel-gas coverage left open, and each was verified against the live catalog before writing (no duplicates this pass). Catalog **653 -> 656**, package **0.81.0 -> 0.82.0** (a minor). Group B (Plumbing & Process Piping) is now **73** tiles. Every compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit (machine-verified in `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js`). Spec-v206 §6 notes this batch clears the last cluster of everyday plumbing/pipefitting hand-tools with a defensible, cleanly pinnable worked example; the trade is, for practical purposes, saturated.

- `branch-reinforcement` (v204, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- the area-replacement check of ASME B31.1 para 104.3.1 (and B31.3 304.3 for process): when a fitter cuts a branch opening into a pressurized run, the metal removed (`A_required = t_rh x d1 x (2 - sin beta)`) must be made up by the excess wall in the run (`A1`) and branch (`A2`) within the reinforcement zone, or a reinforcing pad makes up the deficit. An NPS 6 Sch 40 run with an NPS 2 Sch 40 branch at `t_rh` 0.10 is adequate on run excess alone (**A1 0.372 >= A_req 0.207, no pad**); raise the required wall to 0.25 and the same opening flips to needing a **~0.362 in^2** pad. The required walls come from the pressure design (`pipe-pressure-rating`); the engineer of record and the AHJ govern -- the area balance, not a stamped branch-connection design.
- `expansion-guide-spacing` (v205, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- completes the thermal-expansion family (`pipe-expansion`, `pipe-expansion-loop`, `pipe-cold-spring`) with the guide placement that keeps a pipe a straight column feeding into the joint instead of buckling sideways. The EJMA rule is fixed: first guide within four pipe diameters of the joint, second within fourteen diameters of the first. A 4 in line (OD 4.5) guides at **18 in then 63 in past it (81 in from the joint)**; the distances scale directly with the diameter. Beyond guide 2 the intermediate spacing comes from the EJMA table; the anchor and joint selection govern.
- `medgas-demand` (v206, `calc-gas.js`) -- opens medical gas as a plumbing/pipefitting service (installed and certified by ASSE 6010 brazing-qualified fitters) alongside the fuel-gas family. The first sizing step for an oxygen, medical-air, nitrous, or vacuum main is the demand: connected flow = station count x per-station design flow, system design flow = connected x a diversity (simultaneous-use) factor that falls as the station count rises. 20 oxygen outlets at 1.0 scfm, diversity 0.25 -> **5.0 scfm** design flow; 30 medical-surgical vacuum inlets at 0.50 diversity -> 15 scfm. The per-station flows and diversity factors are read from the adopted NFPA 99 edition and the facility equipment list (user-supplied); a medical-gas verifier and the AHJ govern. Feeds `gas-pipe-sizing`, not the system design itself.

### feat(plumbing): land spec-v199..v203 -- 1 hydronic + 4 pipefitting tiles; 648 -> 653 tiles, 0.81.0, 2026-06-24

The plumbing/pipefitting batch under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: **five** evidence-driven, in-scope tiles, **no new module, group, or dependency**. Each closes a daily-math gap on the hydronic and steam-and-condensate side of Group B, and each was verified against the live catalog before writing (no duplicates this pass). Catalog **648 -> 653**, package **0.80.0 -> 0.81.0** (a minor). Group B (Plumbing & Process Piping) is now **70** tiles. Every compute matches its pinned worked example to the digit (machine-verified in `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js`).

- `radiant-loop-sizing` (v199, `calc-plumbing.js`) -- the residential radiant-floor calc the catalog was missing between the boiler (`boiler-pipe-sizing`) and the expansion tank: tube footage = area x 12 / spacing, loops = ceil(footage / max-loop), design flow GPM = load / (500 x delta-T), split across the loops. 300 ft^2 at 6 in o.c., 9,000 Btu/hr, 1/2 in PEX, 20 F delta-T -> **600 ft, 2 loops, 0.45 GPM per loop**; the same room at 12 in o.c. -> 300 ft, one loop. The manufacturer's tubing tables and the room-by-room heat loss govern the final layout (ASHRAE radiant-panel chapter / Radiant Panel Association).
- `condensate-return-sizing` (v200, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- closes the steam-and-condensate cluster (v157 flash, v158 supply main, v159 trap): the return line is sized for the **flash steam** that re-boils at the lower return pressure, not the liquid. flash = load x flash fraction, volumetric flow = flash x specific volume / 60, required area = flow / a return-velocity ceiling, then the smallest Sch 40 nominal. 800 lb/hr at 13% flash to a 0 psig return (26.8 ft^3/lb), 4,000 ft/min -> **1-1/2 in**; a cooler 7% flash -> 1-1/4 in. The flash, not the gallons, drives the size; a return sized for the water floods and water-hammers.
- `branch-saddle-cutback` (v201, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- the saddle contour ordinates for a branch pipe notched onto a run pipe: cutback(theta) = R - sqrt(R^2 - (r sin theta)^2), zero at the heel/toe and maximum at the sides. A 2 in branch on a 6 in run -> **0.22 in** max cutback; the same branch on a 12 in run -> 0.11 in (a larger run is flatter, the saddle shallower). The geometric contour for a 90-degree same-centerline branch (Pipe Fabrication Institute layout); the weld bevel, gap, and root face are added per the WPS. Completes the fabrication-layout trio (miter, wrap, saddle).
- `reducer-offset` (v202, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- the centerline offset = (large OD - small OD) / 2 and which surface stays continuous through a size change: concentric holds the centerline (an invert dam on a drain, an air pocket on a pump suction), eccentric flat-on-bottom holds the invert (correct on a sewer), flat-on-top holds the crown (correct on a pump suction). A 6 x 4 reducer offsets **1.0625 in**; a 4 x 3, 0.50 in. Standard lay lengths per ASME B16.9 (user-entered).
- `flange-rating` (v203, `calc-pipefit.js`) -- the ASME B16.5 pressure-temperature rating of a flange class (Material Group 1.1, carbon steel), linearly interpolated between table temperatures. A Class 150 A105 flange is 285 psig cold but **200 psig at 400 F**; a Class 300 follows its own higher curve (635 psig at 400 F); Class 150 at 350 F interpolates to 215 psig. The value is the flange's rating; the weakest component (gasket, bolting, mating pipe) can still govern the joint. Completes the flanged-joint trio (rate the flange, rate the pipe at `pipe-pressure-rating`, torque the bolts at `flange-bolt-torque`).

### feat(electrical): land spec-v179..v187 -- 9 electrician second-pass tiles; 639 -> 648 tiles, 0.80.0, 2026-06-24

The second-pass electrician batch under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: **nine** evidence-driven, in-scope tiles, **no new module, group, or dependency**. Each closes a daily-math gap a concept-check of the live electrical group surfaced, and each was verified against the live catalog before writing (no duplicates this pass). Catalog **639 -> 648**, package **0.79.0 -> 0.80.0** (a minor). Group A (Electrical) is now **90** tiles.

- `motor-branch-protection` (v179, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the NEC 430.52 / Table 430.52 maximum branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault device from the table FLC and device type (250% inverse-time breaker, 175% dual-element fuse, 300% nontime-delay fuse, 800% instantaneous-trip), the 430.52(C)(1) Exception 1 round-up to the next standard size (240.6), and the 430.110 disconnect at 115% of FLC. A 10 HP / 230 V / 3ph motor (FLC 28 A) on an inverse-time breaker -> **70 A** (standard), disconnect **32.2 A**; on a dual-element fuse -> 49 A -> next standard **50 A**. Overload is sized separately (430.32, the existing `motor-branch-from-nameplate`).
- `commercial-lighting-load` (v180, `calc-service.js`) -- the commercial general-lighting load (area x the Table 220.12 unit load) plus the 180 VA-per-strap receptacle load (220.14(I)) with the 220.44 demand factor (100% of the first 10 kVA, 50% of the remainder). A 5,000 ft^2 store at 3 VA/ft^2 with 60 straps -> 15,000 VA lighting + 10,400 VA receptacle demand = **25,400 VA**, 122 A at 208 V; 40 straps stay under the 10 kVA breakpoint (all at 100%).
- `noncoincident-load` (v181, `calc-service.js`) -- NEC 220.60: where two loads are unlikely to run together (electric heat vs A/C), the calc carries only the larger; the exception adds both where they are coincident. 9,000 VA heat vs 6,000 VA A/C -> count **9,000**, omit 6,000; a heat-pump compressor + supplemental strip heat -> add to **15,000**. Distinct from the residential `service-load` aggregate, which applies 220.60 inline.
- `pv-circuit-ampacity` (v182, `calc-solar.js`) -- NEC 690.8(A)(1) maximum current = Isc x strings x 125% and 690.8(B)(1) minimum ampacity = that x another 125%, the two factors stacking to **156%** of Isc. Two strings of Isc 10 A -> 25 A max, **31.25 A** minimum ampacity (10 x 2 x 1.5625). The conductor must also satisfy 690.8(B)(2) after conditions of use, and the greater governs.
- `transformer-k-factor` (v183, `calc-powerquality.js`) -- the transformer K-factor from a measured harmonic spectrum, K = sum(Ih^2 x h^2) / sum(Ih^2) in per-unit (UL 1561 / IEEE C57.110), with the standard K-rating round-up. A receptacle-heavy spectrum -> K = **4.61** -> **K-9**. **Note:** the spec draft's near-linear cross-check called K = 1.04 "K-1 (standard)"; the landed tile applies the same strict round-up that yields the K-9 result, so 1.04 rounds up to the smallest tabulated K-rated unit, **K-4** (a near-linear load needs no K-rated transformer in practice; the deviation is recorded in the spec).
- `motor-capacitor-max` (v184, `calc-powerquality.js`) -- the largest power-factor capacitor that can be switched with a motor before self-excitation overvoltage: magnetizing kVAR = sqrt(3) x V x no-load current / 1000 (NEMA MG-1 / IEEE 18), times a safety factor (default 0.90). A 480 V motor at 8 A no-load -> 6.65 kVAR magnetizing -> **5.99 kVAR** maximum. Pair with `pf-correction` and keep the smaller value.
- `bends-between-pulls` (v185, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the cumulative bend total vs the NEC 358.26 (and matching Chapter 3 .26) 360-degree / four-quarter-bend limit between pull points, with the equivalent quarter-bend count and the add-a-pull-point verdict. 90 + 90 + 45 + 45 = 270 deg -> 3.0 quarter bends, **within**; adding 90 + 45 -> 405 deg -> 4.5, **over**. Offsets count both bends, saddles all three.
- `shock-approach-boundary` (v186, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the NFPA 70E Table 130.4 AC shock approach boundaries by nominal voltage: the limited approach (separate distances for an exposed movable conductor vs a fixed circuit part) and the restricted approach. A 480 V panel (151-750 V) -> limited **3 ft 6 in** fixed / 10 ft 0 in movable, restricted **1 ft 0 in**; below 50 V no boundary is specified. Shock boundaries only; the arc-flash boundary is separate (`arc-flash-screen`).
- `pool-bonding-680-26` (v187, `calc-references.js`) -- the NEC 680.26(B) equipotential-bonding component checklist by pool type (pool shell/rebar, the 3 ft perimeter, underwater forming shells, metal fittings 1 in and larger, the pump motor, metal piping within 5 ft, and the listed water bond), with the #8 AWG solid copper minimum and irreversible connections. A permanent pool returns the full **8-component** list; a storable pool narrows to listed cord-and-plug equipment (no 680.26(B) grid).

Each carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, two pinned worked-example rows (18 fixtures, verified to the digit), a bounds-fuzzer block (rule branches, boundary cases, and error seams), and the v18/v21 output contract. Citation discipline (v19/v22): `GOVERNANCE.electrical`; the six NEC tiles cite NEC 2023 by section with the `NEC_DISCLOSURE` edition note, while the three non-NEC tiles (transformer-k-factor UL 1561/IEEE C57.110, motor-capacitor-max NEMA MG-1/IEEE 18, shock-approach-boundary NFPA 70E-2024) carry single-edition notes per CF-01 (NFPA 70E does not match the NEC_DISCLOSURE relevance regex, so it is treated like the v172/v175 non-NEC tiles); no licensed table is reproduced. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, five `app.js` declares (ELECTRICAL +3, SERVICE +2, SOLAR +1, POWERQUALITY +2, REFERENCE +1), `citations.js` (+9), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs` (+9), `data/search/aliases.json` (+59 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (+9), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+18 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+9 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module caps raised with dated notes: `calc-electrical.js` 63000 -> 67000 (built 64.1 KB, 95.7%), `calc-service.js` 10500 -> 13000 (11.4 KB), `calc-powerquality.js` 6500 -> 9500 (8.2 KB), `calc-references.js` 15500 -> 17500 (16.1 KB), `calc-solar.js` 10500 -> 11500 (9.9 KB); the group-shell gzip cap 12 -> 14 KB (Group A's 90-tile hub reached ~12.6 KB). The `REFERENCE_RENDERERS` count test bumped 17 -> 18. README + CHANGELOG resynced (648 tiles / 671 sitemap URLs, Group A 81 -> 90; the silently-drifted corpus 882 -> 892, dimensions 885 -> 894, fuzzer 882 -> 892, and unit-test 4,931 -> 4,940 figures re-pinned in the same pass); specs v179-v187 marked as-landed. Verified green: `npm run lint` (every gate), 4,940 unit tests, `npm run build` (648 tile + 21 group shells), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + 568x320 landscape + 200% zoom), and the chromium + webkit render-no-nan + responsive-stress 320px sweeps across all 648 live tile views.

### feat(electrical): land spec-v165..v178 -- 11 electrician tiles (3 proposals cut as duplicates); 628 -> 639 tiles, 0.79.0, 2026-06-24

Continues the spec-v164..v178 electrician batch under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: **eleven** evidence-driven, in-scope tiles, **no new module, group, or dependency**. Critically, the landing pass found that **three of the fourteen proposed tiles already exist in the catalog** and cut them rather than ship duplicates (the v24/v27 dropped-not-renamed discipline): spec-v166 `egc-upsize` duplicates the existing `egc-upsize-proportional` (NEC 250.122(B), `computeEgcUpsizeProportional`); spec-v171 `conductor-withstand` duplicates `conductor-short-circuit-withstand` (Onderdonk/ICEA, same K/B constants, already returns the minimum cmil); and spec-v173 `triplen-neutral` is covered by `neutral-current-3ph`, which already takes a per-phase triplen percentage and computes `3 x (fraction x phase)` with the same neutral-as-CCC verdict. Those three specs are marked **CUT** with the evidence; the eleven genuinely-new tiles landed. Catalog **628 -> 639**, package **0.78.0 -> 0.79.0** (a minor).

- `buck-boost-sizing` (v165, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the single-phase autotransformer kVA to correct a line voltage up or down: boost/buck = desired minus supply, and the unit is rated only for that boost times the load current, a fraction of the full load kVA. 208 V to 230 V at 50 A -> a **1.10 kVA** unit supports an 11.5 kVA load; the buck path 240 V to 208 V at 20 A -> 0.64 kVA. NEC Article 450; the unit, connection, and protection are the manufacturer's and the AHJ's.
- `wireway-fill` (v170, `calc-electrical.js`) -- metal wireway / auxiliary gutter 20% fill (NEC 376.22 / 366.22) and the 30 current-carrying-conductor ampacity-adjustment trigger. 4x4 in carrying 2.5 in^2, 18 CCC -> **15.6%**, within; 3.5 in^2 is over the 3.2 in^2 (20%) allowance; 34 CCC flags the 310.15(C)(1) adjustment.
- `rooftop-temp-adder` (v174, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the NEC 310.15(B)(2) rooftop sunlight adder (33 C / 60 F where the raceway is < 7/8 in above the roof) applied to a measured ambient, then the 90 C-column correction (reusing the module's `_AMBIENT_FACTORS[90]` table). A #8 at 55 A in a 95 F ambient on the roof -> 155 F design ambient -> ~0.58 -> **31.9 A**; on standoffs (>= 7/8 in) the adder drops and it recovers to ~52.8 A.
- `working-space-110-26` (v176, `calc-electrical.js`) -- the NEC 110.26(A) working-space lookup: depth by voltage-to-ground and Condition 1/2/3 (0-150 V is 3 ft; 151-600 V is 3 / 3.5 / 4 ft), width = max(30 in, equipment width), 6.5 ft headroom with a 90-degree door swing. A 480Y/277 V Condition-2 panel -> **3.5 ft / 30 in / 6.5 ft**.
- `range-demand-220-55` (v167, `calc-service.js`) -- NEC Table 220.55 Column C with the Note 1 over-12 kW increase. One 12 kW range demands **8 kW** (33.3 A at 240 V), not 12; a 16 kW range adds 5% per kW over 12 (20%) to 9.6 kW / 40 A; four ranges read Column C directly (17 kW).
- `dryer-demand-220-54` (v168, `calc-service.js`) -- NEC 220.54: each dryer at the larger of 5,000 W or nameplate, then the Table 220.54 demand factor at five or more. Four 4.5 kW dryers -> 20 kW / **83.3 A**; five 5 kW dryers -> 85% -> 21.25 kW / 88.5 A.
- `neutral-demand-220-61` (v169, `calc-service.js`) -- NEC 220.61: the neutral carries the maximum unbalanced load, the portion over 200 A at 70%. 250 A -> 200 + 0.70x50 = **235 A**; a 250 A nonlinear load (220.61(C)) stays at the full 250 A.
- `motor-unbalance-derate` (v172, `calc-powerquality.js`) -- turns a measured three-phase voltage unbalance into a NEMA MG-1 derating factor and the do-not-operate flag above 5%. 460/455/450 V -> 1.10% -> derate **~0.98**; 480/456/456 V -> 3.45% -> ~0.85; over 5% raises the do-not-operate flag. Builds on the existing `voltage-imbalance` measurement.
- `point-illuminance` (v175, `calc-elecdesign.js`) -- IES point method: horizontal illuminance at a point, E = candela x cos^3(angle) / mounting-height^2. A 1,000 cd source 10 ft up gives **10 fc** directly below (107.6 lux), 6.5 fc at 30 deg off nadir. Distinct from the stagecraft `lighting-beam` tile (on-axis center-beam only): adds the cosine-law off-axis term for general lighting layout.
- `burial-depth-300-5` (v177, `calc-references.js`) -- NEC Table 300.5 minimum cover by method and location: direct burial 24 in, PVC 18 in, RMC/IMC 6 in in general earth; all methods 24 in under public streets; 0 in (in raceway) under a building.
- `support-spacing` (v178, `calc-references.js`) -- the NEC Chapter 3 .30 securing rules: EMT within 3 ft and every 10 ft, NM within 12 in and every 4.5 ft, MC within 12 in and every 6 ft, AC within 12 in and every 4.5 ft; RMC/PVC intervals vary with trade size.

Each carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, two pinned worked-example rows (22 fixtures, verified to the digit), a bounds-fuzzer block (the rule branches, boundary cases, and error seams), and the v18/v21 output contract (the working-space `_finiteGuard` was added after the contract sweep caught an `equipment_width_in = Infinity` leak). Citation discipline (v19/v22): `GOVERNANCE.electrical` with the cited edition naming the NEC section / Table (or NEMA MG-1 / IES point method for the two non-NEC tiles, which carry a single-edition note rather than `NEC_DISCLOSURE` per CF-01); no licensed table is reproduced. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, five `app.js` declares, `citations.js` (+11), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs` (+11), `data/search/aliases.json` (+66 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (+11), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+22 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+11 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module caps raised with dated notes: `calc-electrical.js` 60000 -> 63000 (built 61.0 KB, 96.9%) and `calc-service.js` 8000 -> 10500 (built 9.7 KB); lazy-loaded, absent from the home payload. The `REFERENCE_RENDERERS` count test bumped 15 -> 17. README + CHANGELOG resynced (639 tiles / 662 sitemap URLs, Group A 70 -> 81; the silently-drifted corpus 871 -> 882, dimensions 874 -> 885, fuzzer 871 -> 882, sources 162 -> 163, and unit-test 4,920 -> 4,931 figures re-pinned in the same pass); specs v165-v178 marked as-landed or CUT. Verified green: `npm run lint` (every gate), 4,931 unit tests, `npm run build` (639 tile + 21 group shells), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + 568x320 landscape + 200% zoom), a live-SPA render-no-nan sweep of all 11 new tiles, and the chromium + webkit responsive-stress 320px sweep across all 639 live tile views.

### feat(electrical): land spec-v164 -- feeder tap conductor 10-ft / 25-ft rule (NEC 240.21(B)); 627 -> 628 tiles, 0.78.0, 2026-06-23

Opens the spec-v164..v178 electrician batch with one evidence-driven, in-scope tile under the spec-v106 trades-only charter: `feeder-tap-rule` in `calc-feeder.js` (Group A), no new module, group, or dependency. The catalog sized the transformer secondary tap (`transformer-conductor-protection`, 240.21(C)) but never the everyday *feeder* tap -- the unprotected conductor run from a large feeder OCPD to a smaller panel or device that NEC 240.21(B)(1) and (B)(2) permit only when the tap ampacity clears 1/10 (10-ft rule) or 1/3 (25-ft rule) of the feeder OCPD rating. Electricians make these taps constantly (gutter taps, sub-panel feeds, equipment taps); undersizing them is a classic violation. Catalog **627 -> 628**, package **0.77.0 -> 0.78.0** (a minor).

- `feeder-tap-rule` (v164) -- classifies the tap by length (<= 10 ft -> 240.21(B)(1), 1/10; <= 25 ft -> 240.21(B)(2), 1/3; > 25 ft -> neither short-tap rule applies), computes the minimum tap ampacity = feeder OCPD x the fraction, and returns a verdict against the proposed conductor's 75 C ampacity with the margin. Pinned example: a 400 A feeder with a 22 ft tap falls under the 25-ft rule -> minimum **133.3 A**; a 1/0 Cu THWN tap (150 A) clears it (margin 16.7 A). Cross-check: the same feeder with a 9 ft tap falls under the 10-ft rule -> minimum **40 A**; a 35 ft run is flagged as outside both short-tap rules. A non-finite input or a non-positive OCPD / length / ampacity returns `{ error }`; the only divisions are by the fixed rule denominators (10 and 3), never by a user value.

Carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, two pinned worked-example rows (example + cross-check, verified to the digit), a bounds-fuzzer block exercising both rule branches, the boundary cases (exactly 10 ft and 25 ft), the out-of-rule branch, the undersized verdict, and the four error seams, and the v18/v21 output contract. Citation discipline (v19/v22): `GOVERNANCE.electrical`, edition naming **NEC 2023 240.21(B)(1) and (B)(2)** with the `NEC_DISCLOSURE` edition note, stating that the supplemental 240.21(B) conditions (physical protection, single-OCPD termination for the 25-ft rule, the rating of the supplied device) apply and are not modeled, and that the AHJ-adopted NEC edition and the design engineer govern; no licensed table is reproduced (the 1/10 and 1/3 fractions and the 10 ft / 25 ft boundaries are the exact code values). Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` FEEDER_RENDERERS declare, `citations.js` (+1), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs` (-> transformer-conductor-protection / wire-ampacity / breaker-sizing), `data/search/aliases.json` (+7 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+1 block), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module cap raised with a dated note: `calc-feeder.js` 6000 -> 7000 (built 5721 B, 95.3%, +~17% headroom for the v164..v178 batch); lazy-loaded, absent from the home payload. README + CHANGELOG resynced (628 tiles / 651 sitemap URLs; the silently-drifted corpus 842 -> 871, dimensions 845 -> 874, fuzzer 842 -> 871, and unit-test 4,892/4,919 -> 4,920 figures re-pinned in the same pass); spec-v164 marked as-landed.

### feat(plumbing): land spec-v163 -- drainage invert elevation, drop, and cover; 626 -> 627 tiles, 0.77.0, 2026-06-23

Closes the spec-v157..v163 plumbing/pipefitting batch with the elevation companion to the drainage-slope tiles: one evidence-driven, in-scope tile in `calc-plumbing.js` (Group B), no new module, group, or dependency. `slope` and `manning-slope` give the pitch; neither carries the run to its elevations. A plumber laying a building sewer or storm drain works in inverts -- the flow-line elevation at each end -- and needs the total fall over the run, the downstream invert, and the resulting cover to confirm the pipe clears the minimum bury. Catalog **626 -> 627**, package **0.76.0 -> 0.77.0** (a minor).

- `drainage-invert` (v163) -- total fall = slope x run; invert-out = invert-in - fall; top of pipe = invert-out + OD/12; cover = surface - top of pipe. Slope is entered as in/ft or percent and normalized to ft/ft. Pinned example: invert-in 100.00 ft, 1/4 in/ft, 80 ft run, 4.5 in OD, downstream surface 102.00 ft, 2.0 ft minimum cover -> fall 1.67 ft, invert-out **98.33 ft**, top 98.71 ft, cover **3.29 ft** (above the minimum, no flag). Cross-check at 1/8 in/ft halves the fall to 0.83 ft (invert-out 99.17 ft); the fall scales linearly with slope and the cover follows the invert. A non-finite input, a non-positive run, or a negative slope returns `{ error }`; a negative cover (pipe crown above grade) is a flag, not an error.

Carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, two pinned worked-example rows (example + cross-check, verified to the digit), a bounds-fuzzer block exercising the percent-units path, the under-cover and above-grade flags, and the three error seams, and the v18/v21 output contract. Citation discipline (v19/v22): `GOVERNANCE.general`, `editionNote` naming **IPC 2021 Section 704** for the drainage-slope minimums and stating that the minimum-cover and maximum-depth limits, the local frost depth, and the live-load bury requirement are set by the adopted code and the engineer/survey of record -- the minimum cover compared against is the user-supplied governing value; no licensed table is reproduced. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` PLUMBING_RENDERERS declare, `citations.js` (+1), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs` (-> slope / manning-slope / trench-slope), `data/search/aliases.json` (+7 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+2 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+1 block), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. `calc-plumbing.js` stays within its 60000 B cap (no cap change). README + CHANGELOG resynced (627 tiles); spec-v163 marked as-landed.

### feat(pipefitting): land spec-v157..v162 -- 6 steamfitting / pressure-piping / pipe-support tiles; 620 -> 626 tiles, 0.76.0, 2026-06-23

The steamfitting / pressure-piping / pipe-support bench: six evidence-driven, in-scope tiles under the spec-v106 trades-only charter, all in `calc-pipefit.js` (Group B), no new module, group, or dependency. Steamfitting is half of "pipefitting" but the catalog had no steam tile at all (the water and gas sides of Group B were both deep); this batch gives live steam and its condensate a home, adds the pressure-piping core, and completes the pipe-support chain (size -> spacing -> load -> rod). Catalog **620 -> 626**, package **0.75.0 -> 0.76.0** (a minor).

- `flash-steam-pct` (v157) -- the fraction of condensate that re-boils to steam across a pressure drop, `flash = (hf_high - hf_low) / hfg_low` from the ASME steam-table enthalpies. 100-to-0 psig (309/180/970) -> 13.3%; the smaller 50-to-0 psig drop -> 9.0%. The thermodynamic-ideal fraction; trap subcooling and line losses move the field value.
- `steam-pipe-velocity` (v158) -- steam main size by continuity: required area = (lb/hr x specific volume) / (velocity x 60), then the smallest Sch 40 nominal whose ID clears it and the actual velocity. 1,000 lb/hr of 15 psig steam (13.7 ft3/lb) at 6,000 ft/min -> 3 in at ~4,450 ft/min; double the flow -> 4 in. The velocity band is a recommendation, not a code limit.
- `steam-trap-sizing` (v159) -- the running condensate load (heat duty / latent heat) and the required trap capacity (load x a 2x-3x safety factor). 400,000 Btu/hr on 15 psig steam (hfg 945) at 2x -> 423 lb/hr load, 847 lb/hr capacity; a 3x warm-up factor -> 1,270 lb/hr. The trap is selected from the manufacturer's chart at the actual differential.
- `pipe-pressure-rating` (v160) -- the ASME B31.1 internal-pressure relation both directions: maximum allowable pressure from a wall, or minimum wall from a design pressure. 4 in Sch 40 A106-B (OD 4.5, wall 0.237) at S 17,100 / E 1.0 / y 0.4 -> ~1,636 psi allowable; the inverse at 300 psi design -> 0.0392 in (Sch 40 ample). The allowable stress, joint factor, and y-coefficient are user-entered from the code tables; a design screen, not a stamped calc.
- `pipe-filled-support-load` (v161) -- the operating load per hanger: (empty pipe + contained fluid + insulation) weight per foot from the cross-section and densities, times the spacing. 4 in Sch 40 water-filled at 14 ft -> 16.3 lb/ft, 228 lb/hanger. **Note:** the spec draft's insulation cross-check (~+2.0 lb/ft -> 256 lb) was arithmetically wrong for its own 6 lb/ft3 default; the landed tile and fixtures use the correct computed +0.72 lb/ft -> 239 lb, with the deviation recorded in the spec.
- `hanger-rod-sizing` (v162) -- the smallest carbon-steel threaded rod whose MSS SP-58 maximum safe load (<= 650F) clears the per-hanger load after the temperature derate, with utilization. 228 lb -> 3/8 in (610 lb) at 37%; 1,200 lb steps up two sizes -> 5/8 in (1,810 lb) at 66%; a load above the largest tabulated rod returns an engineer-the-support error.

All six carry the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example verified to the digit with a cross-check, a bounds-fuzzer block exercising the error seams (non-finite, non-positive, out-of-domain, and the exceeds-table / degenerate-geometry paths), and the v18/v21 output contract. Citation discipline (v19/v22): each names its standard (ASME steam tables, ASHRAE, ASME B31.1, MSS SP-58) by reference, with the steam tables, the code allowable-stress tables, and the engineer of record as the governing authority -- no licensed table is reproduced (the allowable stress, joint factor, and y-coefficient are user inputs; the bundled Sch 40 IDs and rod loads are widely-published standard values). Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` PIPEFIT_RENDERERS declare, `citations.js` (+6), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (+36 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+12 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+6 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module cap raised with a dated note: `calc-pipefit.js` 5000 -> 12700 (built ~10.3 KB gz, +6 tiles, +~20% headroom); lazy-loaded, absent from the home payload. README + CHANGELOG resynced (626 tiles / 50 modules / 649 sitemap URLs); specs v157-v162 marked as-landed.

Verified green: full lint (every gate, README-count agreeing at 626 tiles; citation-coverage 626/626; derivation + fuzzer coverage complete), 4,918 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), build (626 tile + 21 group shells, 649 sitemap URLs), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile (zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + landscape + 200% zoom), render-no-nan (626/626), responsive-stress (full-catalog 320px sweep, chromium), and a11y axe-core (6/6 new). The remaining proposed specs v163, v164-v206 (NEC/NFPA/ASME demand-load and reference tiles, restoration, plumbing) stay as tracked backlog. See specs [v157](specs/spec-v157.md) through [v162](specs/spec-v162.md).

### feat(restoration): land spec-v136..v140 -- 5 on-arrival water-loss tiles (flood-cut takeoff, ceiling collapse screen, dehumidifier derate, class-of-loss screen, desiccant sizing); 615 -> 620 tiles, 0.75.0, 2026-06-23

The on-arrival water-loss bench: five evidence-driven, in-scope tiles under the spec-v106 trades-only charter, all in `calc-restoration.js` (Group D), no new module, group, or dependency. They cover what a restoration tech meets before any drying starts -- the demolition takeoff, the overhead-collapse safety read, and the class screen that feeds the existing drying-load tiles -- plus the two cases the catalog's refrigerant sizing never reached: the field derate that explains the drying plateau, and the desiccant path for deep / specialty drying. Catalog **615 -> 620**, package **0.74.0 -> 0.75.0** (a minor).

- `flood-cut-takeoff` (v136) -- turns a measured wall run and the flood-cut line into the demolition takeoff: score-cut linear feet, board removed, replacement 4x8 sheet count, cavity insulation area, baseboard run. 60 ft wall, 24 in cut, 1 face -> 120 ft^2 board, 4 sheets, 120 ft^2 insulation, 60 lf base; the 12 in line halves the board (60 ft^2, 2 sheets). The moisture meter sets the cut line, per IICRC S500 practice (by name).
- `ceiling-water-load` (v137) -- the distributed load, weight, and volume of water trapped overhead in a sagging ceiling, with a drain-before-entry screen. 20 ft^2 bulge, 2 in deep -> 10.4 psf, 208 lb, drain-first true; the gallons path (24.9 gal x 8.34) closes on the 208 lb. The threshold is an editable screen, not a code capacity -- the fastening, span, and a structural engineer govern.
- `dehumidifier-derate` (v138) -- derates a nameplate AHAM rating to its field output at the chamber grain depression and shows the honest unit count, which rises as the chamber dries. 130 ppd at derate 0.5, 300 ppd demand -> 65 ppd field, 3 nameplate vs 5 field units, shortfall 2; at derate 0.3 the field count rises to 8 (the drying plateau). The operator reads the derate off the unit's performance curve.
- `class-of-loss-screen` (v139) -- turns the wetted-surface field read (floor / wall fractions, wick height, low-evaporation materials) into a candidate IICRC S500 Class and the matching per-class evaporation factor that feeds `evaporation-load`. Whole floor, 30% wall, 1.5 ft wick, ordinary materials -> Class 2; raise the wick past 24 in -> Class 3; flag a wet hardwood floor -> Class 4. A deterministic screen that states its rationale; the inspector and a moisture map govern.
- `desiccant-airflow-sizing` (v140) -- sizes a desiccant dehumidifier by the process airflow needed to carry the water-removal rate across the wheel at the design grain depression, from the same psychrometric mass balance `grains-removed` uses in reverse. 300 ppd at a 60-grain depression, 2000 cfm unit -> 13.04 lb/hr, 338 cfm, 1 unit; a shallower 30-grain depression doubles the air to 676 cfm. The manufacturer's performance map governs; reactivation air ducts outside.

All five carry the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example verified to the digit with a cross-check, a bounds-fuzzer block exercising the error seams (non-finite, non-positive, and out-of-domain inputs return `{error}`; divisions are guarded-positive only), and the v18/v21 output contract. Citation discipline (v19/v22): each names ANSI/IICRC S500 by section/name with the meter, inspector, or manufacturer's performance map as the governing authority; no standard text is reproduced. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` RESTORATION_RENDERERS declare, `citations.js` (+5), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (+30 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+10 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+5 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module cap raised with a dated note: `calc-restoration.js` 30500 -> 36000 (built ~29.3 KB gz, +5 tiles, +~20% headroom); lazy-loaded, absent from the home-view payload.

Verified green: full lint (every gate, README-count agreeing at 620 tiles / 50 modules / 643 sitemap URLs; citation-coverage 620/620; derivation + fuzzer coverage complete), 4,912 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), build (620 tile + 21 group shells, 643 sitemap URLs), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile (739 checks / 643 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + landscape + 200% zoom), render-no-nan, and responsive-stress (Chromium + WebKit). The remaining proposed specs v141-v206 (66 tiles across restoration, pipefitting, plumbing, solar, gas) stay as tracked backlog. See specs [v136](specs/spec-v136.md) through [v140](specs/spec-v140.md).

### feat(fabrication): land spec-v129..v135 -- 7 metal-trades tiles (weld estimating, plate forming, shrink fit, cutting power); 608 -> 615 tiles, 0.74.0, 2026-06-23

The metal-trades batch: seven evidence-driven, in-scope tiles under the spec-v106 trades-only charter -- six in `calc-fab.js` (Groups E and G) and one in `calc-machining.js` (Group K), no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **608 -> 615**, package **0.73.0 -> 0.74.0** (a minor).

- `weld-metal-volume` (v129) -- weld cross-section, deposit weight, filler purchased after deposition efficiency, and pass count from first-principles joint geometry and steel density (0.2836 lb/in3). 5/16 in fillet, 120 in, 0.90 eff -> 0.0488 in2, 1.66 lb deposit, 1.85 lb filler, 1 pass.
- `wire-feed-deposition` (v130) -- melt-off rate from wire feed speed and electrode diameter, deposition rate after spatter loss. 0.035 in wire, 300 in/min, 0.92 eff -> 4.91 lb/hr melt, 4.52 lb/hr deposit.
- `weld-transverse-shrinkage` (v131) -- Blodgett transverse shrinkage (0.2 x weld area / thickness) per weld, total, and recommended pre-set. 0.10 in2 weld in 1/2 in plate, 3 welds -> 0.040 in per weld, 0.120 in total. A screen; longitudinal/angular distortion out of scope.
- `weld-group-eccentric` (v132) -- AISC elastic (vector) method for two vertical fillet welds under in-plane eccentric load: line polar moment, direct shear, torsional components, resultant, required leg. 12 kip at 6 in, two 10 in welds 4 in apart -> 1,879 lb/in, 3/16 in fillet. Conservative elastic method, a screen; engineer of record governs.
- `min-bend-radius` (v133) -- minimum inside bend radius from the published forming-limit relation `R_min = T x (50 / %elongation - 1)`. 1/4 in A36 at 20% elongation -> 1.5 T, 0.375 in. A screen; mill cert, grain direction, and a test bend govern.
- `shrink-fit` (v134) -- interference-fit heating/chilling temperature from `delta_dia = alpha x dia x delta_T` (steel alpha 6.5e-6/degF). 4 in fit, 0.004 in interference, 0.002 in clearance -> 231 degF rise, heat to 301 degF (or chill to -161 degF, below dry ice -> liquid nitrogen). Sizes only the assembly temperature; a separate Lame contact-pressure check governs holding capacity.
- `spindle-power-torque` (v135) -- cutting horsepower, motor horsepower after drive efficiency, and spindle torque from material removal rate and specific cutting energy. 3.0 in3/min steel, 80% eff, 800 rpm -> 3.0 cutting hp, 3.75 motor hp, 19.7 lb-ft. The stall / motor-size check before a heavy cut.

All seven carry the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example verified to the digit with a cross-check, a bounds-fuzzer block exercising the error seams, and the v18/v21 output contract (non-finite or out-of-domain inputs return `{error}`; divisions are guarded-positive only). Citation discipline (v19/v22): v132 cites AISC 360 / Manual Part 8 (engineer-of-record governance); v131 names Blodgett, Design of Welded Structures; the rest carry single-edition first-principles / published-relation notes. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` FAB_RENDERERS + MACHINING_RENDERERS declares, `citations.js` (+7), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (+42 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+14 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+7 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. Module caps raised with dated notes: `calc-fab.js` 16000 -> 24000 (built ~19.8 KB gz, +6 tiles), `calc-machining.js` 5000 -> 6500 (built ~5.2 KB gz, +1 tile); both lazy-loaded, absent from the home-view payload.

Verified green: full lint (every gate, README-count agreeing at 615 tiles / 50 modules / 638 sitemap URLs; citation-coverage 615/615; derivation + fuzzer coverage complete), 4,907 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), build (615 tile + 21 group shells, 638 sitemap URLs), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile (734 checks / 638 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + landscape + 200% zoom), render-no-nan, and responsive-stress (Chromium + WebKit). The remaining proposed specs v136-v206 (71 tiles across restoration, pipefitting, plumbing, solar, gas) stay as tracked backlog. See specs [v129](specs/spec-v129.md) through [v135](specs/spec-v135.md).

### fix(build): split the motor bench into calc-motor.js -- calc-electrical.js was 0.1% over its gzip cap after v121..v128; 49 -> 50 modules, 2026-06-23

The spec-v121..v128 batch landed eight tiles in `calc-electrical.js` and pushed its built gzip size to **60031 B against the 60000 B cap (100.1%, over)** -- `check-module-sizes.mjs` FAILs on that, so push CI would have gone red. (The v121..v128 entry's "no module cap moved, ~53.4 KB gz / 89%" line was measured before the batch's own additions; the as-built module was over.)

Fix follows the module's own established remediation -- the same per-bench split that produced `calc-feeder.js` (v72), `calc-powerquality.js` (v79), `calc-solar.js` (v88), and `calc-elecdesign.js` (v101). The cohesive four-tile motor bench -- `motor-synchronous-speed-slip`, `motor-shaft-torque`, `motor-operating-cost`, `multi-motor-feeder` -- moves verbatim out of `calc-electrical.js` into a new `calc-motor.js` (`MOTOR_RENDERERS`). All four **keep `group: "A"`** (a tile's group letter is independent of its module, the v79/v88/v101 precedent); their ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and rendered DOM are byte-for-byte unchanged, so no catalog, sitemap, or visual output changes (608 tiles, 631 sitemap URLs hold). The non-exported `_finiteGuard` and the `multi-motor-feeder`-only `_OCPD_STANDARD_SIZES` / `_standardOcpdAtOrBelow` helpers move with the bench. After the split `calc-electrical.js` builds to **~56.9 KB gz (94.8%)** and `calc-motor.js` to **~4.7 KB gz** (cap 6000, lazy-loaded, not in the home-view payload).

Every touchpoint repointed and gated: the `app.js` declare lists (new `calc-motor.js` MOTOR_RENDERERS block), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (new cap + dated note), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (the four motor imports + their `calc-motor` test labels), the README module count (49 -> 50), and the regenerated v14 corpus (the four functions re-attributed to `calc-motor.js`).

Verified green: full lint (every gate; wiring lint reporting 50 renderer modules / 608 tile-id entries; README-count agreeing at 608 tiles / 50 modules / 631 sitemap URLs), 4,900 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), build (608 tile + 21 group shells, 631 sitemap URLs), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile (727 checks / 631 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + landscape + 200% zoom).

### feat(electrical): land spec-v121..v128 -- 8 Group A tiles (motors, feeders, fault, raceway, grounding, three-phase); 600 -> 608 tiles, 0.73.0, 2026-06-23

The electrician second-pass batch: eight evidence-driven, in-scope tiles under the spec-v106 trades-only charter, all added to Group A / `calc-electrical.js` with no new module, group, or dependency. Catalog **600 -> 608**, package **0.72.0 -> 0.73.0** (a minor).

- `motor-synchronous-speed-slip` (v121) -- `Ns = 120 f / P`, slip from nameplate rpm, rotor frequency. 60 Hz / 4-pole / 1750 rpm -> 1800 rpm sync, 2.78% slip, 1.67 Hz rotor.
- `motor-shaft-torque` (v122) -- two-way `T = 5252 x HP / RPM`. 10 HP at 1750 rpm -> 30.0 lb-ft; inverse 30 lb-ft at 3500 rpm -> 20.0 HP.
- `motor-operating-cost` (v123) -- `input_kW = HP x 0.746 x load / efficiency`, annual kWh, energy cost. 25 HP / 93% / 4000 hr / $0.12 -> 20.05 kW, 80,215 kWh, $9,626; premium-motor delta.
- `multi-motor-feeder` (v124) -- NEC 430.24 feeder ampacity (1.25 x largest FLC + sum of others) and 430.62 OCPD ceiling rounded down to a standard size. 28 / 26 / 70 -> 61 A conductor, 90 A OCPD.
- `conductor-short-circuit-withstand` (v125) -- public-domain ICEA P-32-382 / Onderdonk adiabatic `(I/A)^2 t = K log10((T2+B)/(T1+B))`. Cu #6 at 0.1 s -> 6,313 A withstand (inadequate vs 10,000 A), 41,560 cmil minimum. A screen, not an engineered study.
- `conduit-thermal-expansion` (v126) -- NEC 352.44 PVC length change vs the 1/4-inch fitting trigger. 100 ft / 50 F -> 2.03 in, fitting required (PVC coefficient 3.38e-5 in/in/F, editable).
- `egc-upsize-proportional` (v127) -- NEC 250.122(B) circular-mil proportion when ungrounded conductors are upsized. 26,240 cmil EGC, 167,800 -> 250,000 cmil phase -> ratio 1.49, 39,094 cmil (#4 Cu); clamped so the EGC never drops below table size.
- `delta-wye-line-phase` (v128) -- wye/delta line-to-phase voltage and current with `S = root-3 x V_line x I_line`. 208 V wye -> 120.1 V phase, 3,602 VA; 240 V delta / 30 A -> 17.3 A winding current.

All eight carry the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example verified to the digit with a cross-check, a bounds-fuzzer block exercising the error seams, and the v18/v21 output contract (non-finite or out-of-domain inputs return `{error}`; divisions are guarded-positive only). Citation discipline (v19/v22): the three NEC tiles cite NEC 2023 by section with the `NEC_DISCLOSURE` note and nfpa.org/freeaccess; the Onderdonk tile cites ICEA P-32-382 as a public-domain relation; the four first-principles tiles carry single-edition notes. Wired across `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, the `app.js` ELECTRICAL_RENDERERS declare, `citations.js` (+8), `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (+45 terms), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+16 rows), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (+8 blocks), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.

Verified green: full lint (every gate, README-count agreeing at 608 tiles / 49 modules / 631 sitemap URLs; citation-coverage 608/608; derivation + fuzzer coverage complete), 4,900 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), build (608 tile + 21 group shells, 631 sitemap URLs), check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile (727 checks / 631 shells, zero horizontal scroll at 320px portrait + landscape + 200% zoom), render-no-nan, and responsive-stress (Chromium + WebKit). No module cap moved (`calc-electrical.js` ~53.4 KB gz, 89% of 60 KB; `tools-data.js` ~52.5 KB gz, 85% of 62 KB). The remaining proposed specs v129-v206 (78 tiles across fabrication, restoration, pipefitting, plumbing, solar, gas) stay as tracked backlog. See specs [v121](specs/spec-v121.md) through [v128](specs/spec-v128.md).

### fix(ci): repair the data-refresh GITHUB_OUTPUT heredoc; add a regression guard; 4,889 -> 4,892 tests, 2026-06-23

The weekly Data Refresh workflow (and its monthly twin) died at the "Generate data change analysis" step with "Invalid value. Matching delimiter not found 'EOF'" on the 2026-06-22 scheduled run. Root cause: `scripts/analyze-data-changes.mjs` wrote its markdown body with `out.join("\n")` and no trailing newline. The workflows pipe that body into a `body<<EOF ... EOF` heredoc for `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`, so without the trailing newline the last content line is glued onto the delimiter (`...rate.EOF`), Actions never finds a standalone `EOF` line, and the step fails before `create-pull-request` can read `steps.analysis.outputs.body`. The push CI does not run the data pipeline, so it stayed green; only the cron broke.

Fix: terminate the body with a newline (one line at the source), which repairs both workflows and makes the output a well-formed text file. Added a regression guard in `test/unit/analyze-data-changes.test.js` asserting the body is non-empty, ends with a newline, and contains no standalone `EOF` line (the two ways the heredoc can break), and resynced the README's ungated unit-test count 4,889 -> 4,892 for the three new tests (headline + commands table).

Verified green: full 26-gate lint, 4,892 unit tests, data:verify, build, check:dist / check:shells / check:shell-mobile, render-no-nan (600/600 tiles), and responsive-stress (66/66, Chromium + WebKit, full 600-tile 320px sweep).

Sweeps green at HEAD: lint (26 gates), 4,889 unit tests, data:verify (117 hashes), responsive-stress 66/66 on Chromium + WebKit, check:shell-mobile (717 checks / 623 shells), render-no-nan 600/600. No horizontal scroll on any view at 320 px.

### fix(test): repair CI red since v107 -- drop 2 dead cut-tile test routes, 2026-06-21

The spec-v107 cut (405f084) retired groups S/U/V/W but missed two hardcoded tile-id routes in the integration tests, leaving CI red on main for every commit since. `a11y.test.js` routed `#holding-fuel` (W.5, Aviation, cut); the dead route renders the home view, so `waitForSelector('.input-region button')` timed out, a deterministic failure (3 CI retries, all timeout) that failed the integration job's full-suite step (1317 passed, 1 failed). `responsive-stress.test.js` listed `#parkland-formula` (V, EMS, cut) in `SPA_ROUTES`; a dead route renders the scroll-free home view, so the 200%-zoom assertion passed silently, a latent false-pass giving false coverage confidence. Dropped both routes (their surviving siblings rent-vs-buy and duct-sizing already cover the same long-output / select-heavy surfaces) and resynced the two current-tense gate descriptions in README.md and docs/mobile-responsive.md that still named `#holding-fuel`. Historical changelog/spec references left intact.

Verified: a11y 320px h-scroll block 5/5, responsive 200%-zoom 16/16 (both engines), full render-no-nan + responsive-stress catalog sweep 668/668, lint + 4,889 unit tests + data:verify all green.

### fix(data): complete the spec-v107 legal cut -- remove 6 orphaned shards, repair the broken build-data import, drop dead notice code; 4,895 -> 4,889 tests, 2026-06-21

Follow-up that finishes what the spec-v107 cut left half-done. Retiring Group S deleted `calc-legal.js` but did **not** update the data pipeline or the runtime that referenced it, leaving three latent defects:

- **Broken `data:refresh`.** `scripts/build-data.mjs` still imported five constants (`JUDGMENT_INTEREST_RATES`, `STATUTE_OF_LIMITATIONS`, `LANDLORD_TENANT_NOTICE`, `STATE_MINIMUM_WAGE`, `SMALL_CLAIMS_THRESHOLDS`) from the deleted `../calc-legal.js`, so `node scripts/build-data.mjs` threw `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` on the import. The push CI does not run the data pipeline, so it stayed green; the weekly `data-refresh` cron would have failed on Monday.
- **6 orphaned shards.** Of the seven `data/legal/` shards, only `sales-tax-nexus.json` still has a runtime consumer (its tile moved to the reference group). The other six (`judgment-interest-rates`, `court-holidays`, `statute-of-limitations`, `landlord-tenant-notice`, `state-minimum-wage`, `small-claims`) had no tile, no reference view, and an unbuildable source -- dead weight still shipped to every client and precached.
- **Dead notice code.** `app.js` kept the `tool.group === "S" | "U" | "V" | "W"` notice branches and the now-unreachable `NOTICE_VETERINARY` / `NOTICE_EMS` / `NOTICE_AVIATION` constants for the retired groups.

Removed: the 6 orphaned shards + their builders + `COURT_HOLIDAYS_V5` + the broken `calc-legal.js` import in `build-data.mjs`; the 6 matching `v5-shards.test.js` cases; the 6 entries in `scripts/expected-hashes.json` and the per-shard entries in `data/legal/manifest.json` (legal manifest hash refreshed in both `expected-hashes.json` and the `data/integrity.json` runtime sidecar). The surviving `sales-tax-nexus` shard keeps its **2026-05-08** verification date -- the manifest was hand-trimmed rather than re-stamped via `build-data`, so no shard falsely claims re-verification today. Also dropped the four dead `app.js` notice branches + three orphaned constants, and the four deleted-module entries (`calc-legal.js`, `calc-vet.js`, `calc-ems.js`, `calc-aviation.js`) in the `check-dimensions.mjs` graduated allowlist.

Also resynced the README's ungated post-cut audit figures against live gate output: formula corpus / bounds fuzzer **992 -> 842**, dimensional annotations **995 -> 845**, Phase F cross-tile-invariant suite **390 -> 341 tests**, and the headline unit-test total **4,895 -> 4,889** (the 6 removed shard tests).

Verified green: full lint (every gate, incl. check-readme-counts: 600 tiles / 49 modules / 623 sitemap URLs, em-dash / n-gram / grep guards), **4,889** unit tests, build (600 tile + 21 group shells, 623 sitemap URLs, 823 dist files), `data:refresh` (97 shards across 15 datasets, clean) + `data:verify` (117 integrity entries), and `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile` (623 routes, 717 checks, zero page-level horizontal scroll at 320px portrait / 568x320 landscape / 200% text zoom).

### feat(catalog)!: land spec-v107 -- retire four non-trade groups (CUT); -88 tiles (688 -> 600); stamps 0.72.0, 2026-06-21

**Breaking change to public URLs.** Executes the spec-v106 trades-only charter's first *subtractive* disposition: removes the four liability-bearing non-trade groups recorded as NOT DOING -- **S (Legal)**, **U (Veterinary)**, **V (EMS and Pre-hospital)**, and **W (Pilots and General Aviation)** -- plus one stray flight-ops tile (`weight-balance`, Aircraft Weight and Balance) that had been filed under Group K (Mechanic). Each fails the inclusion test on gate 1 (not a trade) and gate 4 (failure mode is patient / animal harm, safety-of-life, or unauthorized practice of law). Adds nothing; changes no surviving tile's output.

Catalog: **688 -> 600** (-88: 87 group tiles + 1 stray), **25 -> 21 groups**, **53 -> 49 modules**, **715 -> 623 sitemap URLs**, **206 -> 162 tracked sources**. The modules `calc-legal.js`, `calc-vet.js`, `calc-ems.js`, and `calc-aviation.js` are deleted; `calc-mechanic.js` survives with `weight-balance` removed tile-level. Retired group letters leave gaps in A..Z (never renumbered, spec-v106 §5); aircraft *maintenance* content stays under K.

URL retirement: 88 tile deep-links + 4 group-hub URLs stop resolving; they drop from `sitemap.xml` on rebuild and the SPA router lands an unknown `#<id>` on home.

Verified green: full lint (every gate, check-readme-counts now 600 tiles / 49 modules / 623 sitemap URLs, em-dash / n-gram / grep guards), **4,895** unit tests (from 5,563), build (600 tile + 21 group shells, 623 sitemap URLs), data:verify, the worked-examples runner (657 fixtures), and the Chromium + WebKit render-no-nan + responsive-stress sweeps (zero NaN / Infinity / undefined outputs; zero page-level horizontal scroll at 320px on all 600 live tile views). ### docs: backfill CHANGELOG for spec-v106..v120 + resync 4 README ungated current-state figures

Housekeeping after the v109..v120 catalog growth (688 tiles). Two parts, no code or catalog change:

- **CHANGELOG backfill.** The four feat / docs commits landed since the v105 entry (spec-v109..v111, the v106..v108 scope-charter backlog, spec-v112..v119, spec-v120) had updated the catalog and shipped green but were never recorded here. Added their entries above in reverse-chronological order, each derived from the landing commit and its spec.

### feat(catalog): land spec-v120 -- room acoustics (RT60 + axial modes); +1 tile (687 -> 688); stamps 0.71.0, 2026-06-20

One live-production tile added to the existing `calc-stage.js` bench (Group N; no new module, no new group, no new dependency), closing the acoustics family's room gap -- Group N already covered SPL, inverse-square distance loss, surface absorption, and speaker time-alignment, but never the room itself.

- **`room-acoustics` -- Room Acoustics (RT60 + Axial Modes).** Sabine reverberation time `RT60 = 0.049 x V / A` (volume over total absorption in sabins) plus the three first axial room modes `f = c / (2 x dimension)` with `c = 1130 ft/s`. The `0.049` Sabine constant and the `1130 ft/s` speed of sound are bundled editable fields so the tile carries no hidden magic numbers. `GOVERNANCE.general` over the public-domain Sabine equation and the standing-wave relation.

Full wiring: tools-data row, tile-meta, citations (public-domain Sabine + `f = c/(2L)`), compute-map, related-tiles, search aliases, the `STAGE_RENDERERS` declare in app.js, +2 worked-example fixtures (worked example + cross-check), one bounds-fuzzer block, and a regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. The `calc-stage.js` gzip cap and the `citations.js` registry cap absorb the new rows; all still lazy-loaded and absent from the home-view payload. All gates green: lint (README-count gate re-pinned to 688 tiles / 53 modules / 715 sitemap URLs), 5,563 unit tests, build (688 tile + 25 group shells), data:verify, `check:shell-mobile` (715/715 clean at 320px), and the Chromium render-no-nan + full-catalog 320px no-horizontal-scroll sweep. See [specs/spec-v120.md](specs/spec-v120.md).
