### feat(catalog): land spec-v105 -- HVAC evacuation & leak-check diagnostics; +2 tiles (667 -> 669); stamps 0.68.0, 2026-06-20

Two evidence-driven tiles added to the existing `calc-hvacservice.js` bench (no new module, no new group; both `group: "C"`), the two go/no-go numbers a tech reads off the gauges before charging a system -- named in the spec-v104 §5 roadmap. A concept-check of the post-v104 live ids found no micron decay test and no temperature-corrected standing pressure test.

- **`vacuum-decay-test` -- Vacuum Decay (Blank-Off) Test.** The evacuation standing-decay verdict: from the micron level at valve-off, the level after a timed hold, and the hold time, the total rise (`end - start`) and the rise rate (`rise / hold`), with a tight-and-dry / moisture / leak reading against an editable 500-micron pass ceiling (the ACCA Standard 4 / AHRI field convention). Pull to 300 microns, 450 after 15 min -> 150-micron rise, 10 microns/min, below the 500 ceiling -> tight and dry, ok to charge. `GOVERNANCE.general` over public rise/time arithmetic.
- **`nitrogen-pressure-test` -- Nitrogen Pressure Test (Temperature-Corrected).** Separates a real leak from the ambient thermal swing in a standing nitrogen pressure test using Gay-Lussac's law (at constant volume `P/T` is constant in *absolute* units): `expected_psig = (start_psig + atm) x (T2_R / T1_R) - atm` with `R = F + 459.67`, and `leak_drop = expected_psig - end_psig` is the pressure lost below the temperature-corrected value. 150 psig at 70 F cooling to 50 F is expected to read 143.8 psig; a gauge reading 144 holds (the 6 psi drop is thermal), while 138 flags a 5.8 psi leak. `GOVERNANCE.general` over the public ideal-gas law.

Both carry the full v14 dimensional annotation (microns and psi as pressure `M L^-1 T^-2`, temperatures as `T`) and the v18/v21 output contract (a non-finite, non-positive, or below-absolute-zero input returns `{error}`). The `calc-hvacservice.js` cap in `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` is raised 6,500 -> 9,000 B gz (the bench is now six tiles at ~7.5 KB gz, still lazy-loaded and absent from the home-view payload), and the `citations.js` registry cap is bumped 192,000 -> 200,000 B gz for the two new citation entries; no other cap is touched. All gates green: lint (README-count gate at 669 tiles / 53 modules / 696 sitemap URLs), 5,551 unit tests, build (669 tile + 25 group shells), data:verify, worked-examples runner (+4 fixtures), render-no-nan, a11y, and the 320 px shell-mobile + responsive-stress sweeps on Chromium and WebKit. See [specs/spec-v105.md](specs/spec-v105.md).

### feat(catalog): land spec-v104 -- HVAC field-service electrical diagnostics; +2 tiles (665 -> 667); stamps 0.67.0, 2026-06-20

Two evidence-driven tiles added to the existing `calc-hvacservice.js` bench (no new module, no new group; both `group: "C"`), covering the electrical side of an HVAC service call that the v102 refrigerant/drain pair left open. A concept-check of the post-v103 live ids for `mca`, `mocp`, `440`, and `capacitor` returned nothing.

- **`hvac-equipment-circuit` -- HVAC Equipment Circuit (MCA / MOCP).** The NEC 440 nameplate branch-circuit math: `MCA = 1.25 x largest-motor RLA + sum of the other loads` (440.33) and `MOCP = 1.75 x largest RLA + others` taken to the next NEC 240.6(A) standard size *down* (440.22(A) is a ceiling), with the 225% start allowance reported separately and an optional check of the installed breaker. A compressor at RLA 20 A + fan 1.5 A -> MCA 26.5 A, MOCP 35 A, ceiling 45 A (matching a real split-system nameplate). `GOVERNANCE.electrical`; cites NEC 2023 440.33 / 440.22(A) / 240.6(A).
- **`run-capacitor-microfarad` -- Run Capacitor Microfarad Check.** The in-circuit capacitance test from the capacitive reactance `Xc = 1/(2 x pi x f x C)`: `C[uF] = 1e6 x I / (2 x pi x 60 x V) ~= 2652 x amps / volts` at 60 Hz, compared to the nameplate rating and an editable +/-6% tolerance band with a good / weak / replace verdict. A 45 uF cap drawing 6.2 A at 370 V -> 44.4 uF (within band); the same cap at 3.0 A -> 21.5 uF (replace). `GOVERNANCE.general` over public capacitive-reactance physics.

Both carry the full v14 dimensional annotation (current `I`, capacitance `M^-1 L^-2 T^4 I^2`) and the v18/v21 output contract (a non-finite, non-positive, or out-of-domain input returns `{error}`). The `calc-hvacservice.js` cap in `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` is raised 4,000 -> 6,500 B gz (the bench is now four tiles at ~5.4 KB gz, still lazy-loaded and absent from the home-view payload); no other cap is touched. All gates green: lint (README-count gate at 667 tiles / 53 modules / 694 sitemap URLs), 5,550 unit tests, build (667 tile + 25 group shells), data:verify, worked-examples runner (+4 fixtures), render-no-nan, a11y, and the 320 px shell-mobile + responsive-stress sweeps on Chromium and WebKit. See [specs/spec-v104.md](specs/spec-v104.md).

### feat(catalog): land spec-v101 + v102 + v103 -- the field-service design bench pass; +6 tiles (659 -> 665); stamps 0.66.0, 2026-06-19

Three evidence-driven growth specs landed together, adding the everyday *design and field-service* numbers each trade reaches for off the print or at the unit -- numbers the deep rough-in benches did not cover. Each was concept-checked against the post-v100 live ids and machine-verified to the digit. Three new lazy-loaded modules open (50 -> 53 `calc-*` modules), each relieving a brushing source-module cap instead of bumping it.

- **v101 -- `calc-elecdesign.js` (Group A, electrician design/layout bench, off `calc-electrical.js`'s ~93.7% cap).** `pull-box-sizing` -- the NEC 314.28 pull/junction box minimum: 8x the largest raceway for a straight pull, 6x plus the same-row others for an angle/U pull, and the 6x between-same-conductor-entries rule (distinct from the 314.16 `box-fill` conductor-volume). A 3 in straight pull -> 24 in; a 3 in angle pull with two 2 in others -> 22 in, between 18 in. `lumen-method` -- the IES luminaire count to hit a target maintained footcandle: `ceil(target_fc x area / (lumens x CU x LLF))`, with the achieved footcandles at the integer count and the total installed lumens (the inverse of `lux-to-footcandle`'s forward illuminance). A 1200 ft^2 office at 50 fc / 5000 lm / 0.7 / 0.8 -> 22 luminaires, 51.3 fc.
- **v102 -- `calc-hvacservice.js` (Group C, HVAC field-service bench, off `calc-hvac.js`'s ~93% cap).** `condensate-drain` -- the gallons-per-hour a coil sheds, the IMC 307.2.2 minimum drain size by tonnage, and the fall over a run at the 1/8-in-per-foot code slope (the per-ton rate an editable field estimate since condensate tracks the latent load). A 3-ton split at 3 pints/ton-hr -> 1.125 gph, 3/4 in drain, 2.5 in fall over 20 ft. `recovery-cylinder` -- the maximum net refrigerant under the DOT/EPA-608 80% fill rule from the stamped water capacity and the refrigerant liquid density, plus the headroom and the fill / do-not-fill action. A 50 lb WC cylinder of 9.0 lb/gal R-410A with 30 lb in -> 43.2 lb max net, 13.2 lb remaining, ok to fill.
- **v103 -- `calc-disinfect.js` (Group B, pipe/well disinfection bench, off `calc-plumbing.js`'s ~93.5% cap).** `main-disinfection-chlorine` -- the AWWA C651 new-main chlorination: pipe volume (`0.0408 x dia^2 x length`), pounds of available chlorine for the dose, and the product weight at its strength. An 8 in x 1000 ft main at 25 mg/L with 65% cal-hypo -> 2611 gal, 0.544 lb chlorine, 0.837 lb product. `well-shock-chlorination` -- the household-bleach shock for a private well from the casing diameter and standing water column, with a quart/ounce echo for small amounts. A 6 in x 100 ft well at 100 ppm / 6% bleach -> 147 gal, 0.245 gal bleach (about 1 quart).
- **Contract + sources.** All six carry `GOVERNANCE.general` design/dosing governance (the code, the standard, and the AHJ govern; NEC 314.28, IES lumen method, IMC 307.2, DOT/AHRI 700/EPA 608, AWWA C651, AWWA A100 named, never reproduced), the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example with cross-checks, a bounds-fuzzer block, and the v18/v21 output contract (a non-finite or out-of-domain input returns `{error}`; the do-not-fill branch returns an exact zero headroom, not an error).
- **Wiring.** Per tile: `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+13 fixtures), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (5 each), the `app.js` declares, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. The three new modules register in `scripts/build.mjs`, `sw.js`, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (elecdesign 5000, hvacservice 4000, disinfect 4000), and the `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` graduated set.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate, including module-size, wiring, sw-precache, dimensions, corpus, tile-contract, and README-count gates agreeing at 665 tiles / 53 modules / 692 sitemap URLs), `npm test` (5,549 unit, +3 bounds-fuzzer blocks), `npm run build` (665 tile + 25 group shells, 692 sitemap URLs), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (ran 696), render-no-nan, and the 320 px shell-mobile sweep.

### fix: responsive-stress 320 px sweep gets an explicit timeout (was crossing the test.slow() 90 s ceiling); stamps 0.65.3, 2026-06-19

A CI-reliability fix. The `responsive-stress.test.js` "every live tile view: no page-level horizontal scroll at 320 px" sweep drives all 659 live tile views on the 320 px phone floor in one test body, on both Chromium and WebKit. It is not a product regression -- the test was timing out before it could finish, not finding a sideways scrollbar.

- **Cause.** The test's own comment documented a "~2-3 min" runtime but it relied on `test.slow()`, which only triples the 30 s Playwright base timeout to 90 s -- below its real runtime. Runtime scales with the catalog (~70 ms of settle per tile), so as tiles were added the WebKit pass on the shared 2-core CI runner crept past 90 s. It had been surviving on the `retries: 2` budget; this push it exhausted all three attempts (initial + 2 retries) and reddened the integration job, while every other gate stayed green. The previous commit's run passed integration in 13m59s on a lucky retry, confirming a flake at the ceiling rather than a new defect.
- **Fix.** Replace `test.slow()` with an explicit `test.setTimeout(300_000)` (5 min) -- ~4x the observed single-engine runtime, with headroom for further catalog growth. The two engine projects run in parallel workers, so this does not serialize the rest of the integration suite. The stale "all 531 live views" count in the adjacent comment (the catalog is 659) was corrected in the same block.
- **Verified.** The exact failing case run in isolation on WebKit -- `--project=webkit-responsive --grep "every live tile view"` -- now passes first try in 1.2m (no retry), plus `npm run lint` and `npm test` (5,546 unit, unchanged; this is a test-file-only change).

### fix: dev-server CSP now mirrors production (carries the boot-script hash); stamps 0.65.2, 2026-06-19

A dev/prod CSP-parity bug found by a runtime console-error sweep over all 659 SPA tile views (the path the gates cover only as a binary no-horizontal-scroll pass, not a console-clean pass). On every cold document load the browser logged a Content-Security-Policy violation -- `Executing inline script violates ... 'script-src 'self''` -- blocking the inline theme-boot script in `index.html` (the flash-of-un-themed-paint guard).

- **Cause.** The local dev server (`scripts/dev.mjs`) sends its own `Content-Security-Policy` header, documented to mirror the production `_headers` file, but its `script-src` had drifted to a bare `'self'` -- missing the `'sha256-0qFLOnMo4ZqgtC+YMO+1763cr/ZxTi2v7KEhzLIIz4I='` boot-script hash that both `_headers` and the `index.html` `<meta>` CSP carry. When a header CSP and a `<meta>` CSP are both present the browser enforces the intersection, so the dev header's hash-less `script-src 'self'` blocked the boot script even though the `<meta>` allowed it. Production was always correct; only the dev surface (and any Playwright suite it serves) was stricter than prod, masking nothing but flashing un-themed paint on first load.
- **Fix.** `scripts/dev.mjs` `script-src` now carries the boot-script sha256, making the dev CSP byte-identical in posture to `_headers`.
- **Gate.** `scripts/check-csp.mjs` -- which already recomputes the boot hash and asserts it appears in the `index.html` `<meta>` and `_headers` -- now also reads and validates `scripts/dev.mjs`, so the dev CSP can never silently drift from production again. The gate runs the same self-only / no-external-origin posture checks against all three surfaces.
- **Verified.** A before/after console-error sweep over all 659 tile routes (1 CSP violation -> 0), `node scripts/check-csp.mjs` (OK across all three CSP surfaces), `npm run lint` (every gate), `npm test` (5,546 unit), `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, the dist 320 px shell-mobile audit (790 checks, zero horizontal scroll), and the Playwright responsive-stress + a11y SPA sweeps (zero horizontal scroll on every live tile view, Chromium + WebKit).

### fix: keyboard-shortcut overlay now closes on navigation; stamps 0.65.1, 2026-06-19

A behavior fix found by a 320 px visual-QA sweep (the path the gates cover only as a binary no-horizontal-scroll pass). The `?` keyboard-shortcut panel is a `role="dialog"` `aria-modal="true"` overlay with a focus trap. It advertises the `G`-leader navigation shortcuts (e.g. `G V` -> Voltage Drop), so the natural flow is "open the overlay, read a shortcut, use it" -- but using one routed the view underneath while leaving the modal open over the destination, dimming and covering the calculator the user had just navigated to until they pressed Escape. Browser back/forward while the overlay was open had the same effect.

- **Cause.** No navigation path closed the overlay. `runShortcut` called `navigateTo` but never dismissed the dialog, and `applyRoute` -- the single entry point every navigation funnels through (G-leader shortcut, `hashchange` / back-forward, `navigateTo`, boot) -- did not touch it either.
- **Fix.** `applyRoute()` now calls `closeShortcutOverlay(false)` at the top, dismissing the modal on every navigation. The new `restoreFocus` parameter (default `true`, preserving the Escape / Close-button WCAG 2.4.3 focus-restore) is passed `false` here: the pre-overlay element belongs to the view just left, so the destination view receives focus naturally instead. No-op when no overlay is open (the boot route included).
- **Regression test.** `test/integration/a11y.test.js` gains a case: open the overlay with `?`, press the `G V` leader shortcut, and assert the route became `#voltage-drop` *and* the overlay is gone. The existing aria-modal / focus-trap / Esc-restores-focus test is unchanged and still green.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate, incl. home-payload after the +287 B gz app.js tick, resynced in README + docs/performance.md), `npm test` (5,546 unit), `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, and the full Playwright integration suite (1,435 passed: render-no-nan + a11y over every tile, responsive-stress 320 px Chromium + WebKit, perf, print, CSV).

### feat: spec-v90 through v100 (field-trades depth pass) -- +25 evidence-driven tiles, catalog 634 -> 659, new calc-finish.js module (49 -> 50); stamps 0.65.0, 2026-06-18

Eleven evidence-driven growth specs that finish benches the catalog named but only half-equipped. Each gap was concept-checked against the live catalog, each formula machine-verified to the digit with cross-checks. The catalog goes **634 -> 659** (+25 tiles) across the same 25 groups; one new lazy-loaded module is opened.

- **The benches.** **v90** food-service cost control in Group O / `calc-kitchen.js` (`food-cost-percentage`, `prime-cost`, `pour-cost`). **v91** owner-operator load economics in Group J / `calc-trucking.js` (`load-profitability`, `fuel-surcharge`, `maintenance-reserve`). **v92** LED video wall + projection in Group N / `calc-stage.js` (`led-video-wall`, `projector-brightness`). **v93** pool / spa chemical balance in Group M / `calc-treatment.js` (`pool-alkalinity-adjust`, `pool-cya-dose`, `pool-salt-dose`). **v94** fencing in Group E / `calc-construction.js` (`fence-estimate`, `post-hole-concrete`). **v95** tile + resilient-flooring take-off in the **new `calc-finish.js`** module (`thinset-coverage`, `flooring-takeoff`). **v96** concrete contraction joints + rebar lap splices in `calc-construction.js` (`control-joint-spacing`, `rebar-lap-splice`). **v97** hardscape take-off in `calc-finish.js` (`paver-patio`, `retaining-wall-block`). **v98** attic ventilation + residential gutter sizing in `calc-finish.js` (`attic-ventilation`, `gutter-downspout`). **v99** building-envelope insulation in Group C / `calc-hvac.js` (`assembly-r-value`, `blown-insulation-coverage`). **v100** shop fluid mixing in Group K (`paint-mix-ratio` in `calc-mechanic.js`, `cutting-fluid-concentration` in `calc-machining.js`).
- **New module.** `calc-finish.js` (`FINISH_RENDERERS`), the finish-and-site-carpentry take-off bench named in the spec-v94 module note, holds the six v95/v97/v98 tiles (all `group: "E"`); it relieves the standing `calc-construction.js` cap watch instead of bumping that module again. Wired through `app.js` declares, `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (cap 8500), and `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` `GRADUATED_MODULES`.
- **Contract.** Every tile carries the full v14 dimensional annotation, a pinned worked example verified to the digit, a bounds-fuzzer block, and the v18/v21 output contract: a non-finite or out-of-domain input returns `{error}`; optional outputs are `null` when their trigger input is zero; the three-way pool and cutting-fluid action branches return an exact zero dose at target (not an error).
- **Caps bumped (dated).** kitchen 13000->16000, trucking 19500->22500, stage 18500->21500, treatment 6000->8500, construction 62000->65000, hvac 41000->43500, mechanic 18000->19500, machining 4000->5000, new calc-finish.js 8500, tools-data 54000->58000, citations 187000->192000.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate green; README count gate agrees at 659 tiles / 50 modules / 686 sitemap URLs), `npm test` (5,546 unit tests), `npm run build` (659 tile + 25 group shells, 686-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+25 fixtures), and a focused 320 px render + horizontal-scroll sweep over all 25 new tiles (clean, including every select option). See specs [v90](specs/spec-v90.md) - [v100](specs/spec-v100.md).

### chore: spec-v88 (calc-electrical.js cap-relief split) -- solar-PV / battery-storage / EV-charging bench -> new calc-solar.js; catalog holds 634; stamps 0.64.3, 2026-06-17

The sixteenth cap-relief cut in the v70-v87 lineage, taking the next-named candidate: a platform-only / housekeeping spec that **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **634 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (47 -> 48 `calc-*` modules).

- **Why.** After the v79 power-quality split and the v87 agriculture split, `calc-electrical.js` was the tightest renderer module in the repo at **94.7% of its 58 KB gzip cap** (54943 B) -- the named next renderer-split candidate in the spec-v87 §5 roadmap.
- **What moved.** The cohesive solar-PV / battery-storage / EV-charging electrification bench -- five tiles spanning two eras (`pv-string-sizing`, cold-Voc / warm-Vmp series-count limits for an inverter MPPT window, spec-v2; `battery-runtime`, `Ah x V x DoD / load_W` with the Peukert form for k > 1, spec-v2; `pv-interconnection-busbar`, the NEC 705.12 120% busbar allowance and supply-side 705.11 path, spec-v15; `off-grid-battery`, the IEEE 1013 / 1561 bank sizing, spec-v15; `ev-charger-load`, the NEC 625 continuous-load 125% rule and panel-headroom check, spec-v15) -- into a new module `calc-solar.js` (`SOLAR_RENDERERS`). The generation / storage / EV-charging side of the trade is a distinct domain from the branch-circuit, conductor, motor, transformer, and service-load tiles that stay in `calc-electrical.js`. **All five keep `group: "A"`** (group letter independent of module, the v28/v30/v36/v39/v42/v70..v87 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cut is clean: the bench reaches nothing outside the `ui-fields.js` helpers it imports, the single `ampacityFromPhysics` primitive from `pure-math.js` (used only by `ev-charger-load`), its own module-local `_EV_BREAKER_SIZES` / `_EV_CU_AWG` tables (which move with it), and the per-module `_finiteGuard` (copied verbatim, non-exported, no corpus row) -- leaving no cross-module import.
- **Sizes / caps.** `calc-electrical.js` 54943 -> 48734 B gz (cap lowered 58000 -> 52000 to lock in the freed space, ~93.7%); `calc-solar.js` ~8.8 KB gz (8843 B, cap 10500; lazy-loaded, not in the home-view first-paint payload; declare tick 38388 -> 38581 B / 37.7%).
- **Wiring (all gated).** `app.js` declares (five ids move to a new `SOLAR_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (lower source cap + add new), `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` (`GRADUATED_MODULES` gains `calc-solar.js`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (five ids repointed), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (the five compute fns + five render fns split into a new `calc-solar.js` import), `test/unit/calc-electrical-v2.test.js` and `test/unit/calc-electrical-v15.test.js` (the moved compute fns + examples repointed), `test/unit/first-principles.test.js` and `test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js` (the focused imports repointed), and the regenerated v14 corpus. `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `worked-examples.json`, `audit-trail.md`, and `related-tiles.mjs` reference tiles by id and need no change. README catalog-count gate agrees at 48 modules; wiring lint reports 48 renderer modules / 634 tile-id entries. README spec-history + module diagram + file-tree, docs (architecture, deployment, performance), and `specs/spec-v88.md` added/updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate), `npm test` (5,538 unit tests, unchanged), `npm run build` (634 tile + 25 group shells, 661-URL sitemap, 866 dist files), `npm run data:verify`, and the integration sweep -- render-no-nan over every TOOL_ID and responsive-stress at the 320 px floor (zero horizontal scroll on every live tile view, both Chromium and WebKit) -- with the five moved tiles rendering identically from the new module. See [specs/spec-v88.md](specs/spec-v88.md).

### chore: spec-v86 (calc-plumbing.js cap-relief split) -- onsite-wastewater / septic bench -> new calc-septic.js; catalog holds 634; stamps 0.64.1, 2026-06-16

The fourteenth cap-relief cut in the v70-v82 lineage, resumed after the v83-v85 expansion: a platform-only / housekeeping spec that **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **634 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (45 -> 46 `calc-*` modules).

- **Why.** v83 added its three onsite-septic pressure-distribution tiles to `calc-plumbing.js`, pushing it to **98.9% of its 53 KB gzip cap** (52412 B) -- the tightest calculator module in the repo, where the next Group B tile would break the build.
- **What moved.** The cohesive onsite-wastewater / septic bench -- five tiles spanning three eras (`septic-tank`, EPA 150-gpd-per-bedroom static-tank sizing with a 1000-gal floor, spec-v2; `septic-drainfield`, soil-absorption area and trench length from the design flow and code application rate, spec-v7; and the v83 pressure-distribution trio `septic-dose-tank` / `septic-pumpout-interval` / `septic-lpp-orifice`) -- into a new module `calc-septic.js` (`SEPTIC_RENDERERS`). The septic side of a building's plumbing is a distinct domain from the supply, DWV, drainage, and hydronic tiles that stay in `calc-plumbing.js`. **All five keep `group: "B"`** (group letter independent of module, the v28/v30/v36/v39/v42/v70..v82 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cut is clean: the four standard renderers use the plain `ui-fields.js` names, the v7-era `septic-drainfield` renderer keeps its aliased `_v7p_` set verbatim, the per-module `_finiteGuard` is copied (non-exported, no corpus row), and the now-orphaned `limitation-banner.js` import (used only by the drainfield renderer) is dropped from `calc-plumbing.js` -- leaving no cross-module import.
- **Sizes / caps.** `calc-plumbing.js` 52412 -> 48645 B gz (cap lowered 53000 -> 52000 to lock in the freed space, ~93.5%); `calc-septic.js` ~5.7 KB gz (5741 B, cap 7000; lazy-loaded, not in the home-view first-paint payload).

### feat: spec-v83 + spec-v84 + spec-v85 (field-trades depth pass) -- +10 tiles, catalog 624 -> 634; stamps 0.64.0, 2026-06-16

Three evidence-driven tile-adding specs landed together in one commit. Each finishes a bench the catalog named but only half-equipped, and -- after the thirteen-spec v70-v82 cap-relief run restored headroom in every module -- each tile drops into the cohesive home its siblings already occupy, so **no new module is created**. The three specs targeted 0.64.0 / 0.65.0 / 0.66.0 individually; landed as one commit they stamp a single minor, **0.64.0** (a discrete release per spec would be a phantom; one bump per commit is the convention).

- **spec-v83 -- onsite septic pressure distribution (Group B, `calc-plumbing.js`, +3).** `septic-dose-tank` (timed-dose working volume and daily cycle count: net dose = daily flow / doses, pumped-per-cycle = net dose + drainback, and the dose-to-void ratio with a sub-5 flag; ratio is `null` not infinity when drainback is zero), `septic-pumpout-interval` (years between pump-outs = tank x fill fraction / (occupants x per-capita accumulation); loud that a sludge-judge measurement and any mandatory state interval govern), and `septic-lpp-orifice` (the orifice-discharge `Q = 19.63 x Cd x d^2 x sqrt(h)` per-orifice flow, total orifice count, and system flow for a low-pressure-pipe or mound lateral). Sources named, not reproduced: USEPA Onsite Wastewater Treatment Systems Manual (EPA/625/R-00/008), university onsite-wastewater extension guidance, the orifice-discharge equation; all `GOVERNANCE.plumbing`.
- **spec-v84 -- sprayer nozzles, drift, and field capacity (Group L, `calc-agriculture.js`, +3, new `landscaping` cross-trade).** `nozzle-flow-pressure` (the square-root flow law `Q proportional to sqrt(pressure)` plus a target-flow solve that flags the out-of-band pressure, proving you change tips not pressure to change the rate), `spray-drift-buffer` (a relative downwind buffer scaling an editable droplet-class base by wind and release height; explicit that the label's mandatory buffer is the law under FIFRA -- `GOVERNANCE.pesticide`), and `sprayer-field-capacity` (theoretical and effective acres/hr = boom x speed / 8.25 x efficiency, spray time, and tank count). Sources: the nozzle-flow relation, USDA / land-grant extension calibration and drift guidance, EPA label / WPS 40 CFR 170.
- **spec-v85 -- welding gas, cutting, and consumable cost (Group E, `calc-fab.js`, +4).** `shielding-gas-runtime` (cylinder runtime, bottle count via `ceil`, and prorated gas cost; cost is `null` when no rate is entered), `oxyfuel-cutting-gas` (oxygen/fuel consumed and runtime per cylinder for a cut), `weld-preheat-fuel` (sensible-heat fuel `mass x specific heat x rise / efficiency` to reach the preheat temperature `carbon-equivalent` recommends; preheat at-or-below start returns `{error}`), and `weld-cost-per-foot` (all-in filler + labor + gas per foot, where the operating factor moves the number more than the filler). `calc-fab.js` was the chosen home to keep the rest of Group E's modules from growing; it was already declared since spec-v36. Sources: torch-maker tip charts, AWS welding cost references, carbon-steel specific heat (0.11 Btu/lb-degF) and propane heating values (21,600 Btu/lb, 91,500 Btu/gal); gas tiles `GOVERNANCE.worker_safety`, cost tile `GOVERNANCE.general`.
- **Wiring (all gated, per tile).** `tools-data.js` row, `tile-meta.js` `_TILES`, `citations.js` entry, `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+10 pinned fixtures with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (>=5 each), the `app.js` declare lists, the `// dims:` annotations, a `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block per spec, and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index.
- **Sizes / caps bumped** (all lazy-loaded, dated comments in `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs`): `tools-data.js` 52000 -> 54000, `tile-meta.js` 8000 -> 8800, `citations.js` 182000 -> 187000, `calc-agriculture.js` 34000 -> 37000, `calc-fab.js` 13000 -> 16000. `calc-plumbing.js` (+3 septic) stayed within its 53000 cap.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate; `check-readme-counts` agrees at 634 tiles / 45 modules / 661 sitemap URLs), `npm test` (5,537 unit tests), `npm run build` (634 tile + 25 group shells, 661-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+10 fixtures), and the integration sweep -- render-no-nan over every TOOL_ID and responsive-stress at the 320 px floor (zero horizontal scroll on every live tile view). Correctness phases move with the catalog: corpus 928 -> 938 rows, dimensions 931 -> 941, bounds-fuzzer 928 -> 938, derivation-index and source-map 624 -> 634. See specs [v83](specs/spec-v83.md), [v84](specs/spec-v84.md), [v85](specs/spec-v85.md).

### chore: spec-v82 (calc-fire.js cap-relief split) -- technical-rescue bench -> new calc-rescue.js; catalog holds 624; stamps 0.63.13, 2026-06-16

The thirteenth consecutive cut on the v70..v81 module-cap watch-list, same shape: a platform-only / housekeeping spec that **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **624 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (44 -> 45 `calc-*` modules).

- **Why.** After v81 relieved `calc-hvac.js`, the standing module-cap watch (spec-v81 §5) named `calc-fire.js` as the next split candidate. It was the tightest remaining calculator module at **94.9% of its 27 KB gzip cap** (25622 B).
- **What moved.** The cohesive spec-v3 technical-rescue bench -- three contiguous, self-contained tiles (`confined-space-purge`, the air-change purge time `t = V*N/CFM` per OSHA 1910.146; `rope-ma`, rope-rescue mechanical advantage across rig types, theoretical and actual after `efficiency^pulleys` losses, per NFA/NFPA training literature; `sling-angle`, the per-leg tension multiplier `L = W/(n*sin(theta/2))` with the choker 0.75 derate and the spec-v27 D/d bend-efficiency curve, per ASME B30.9) -- into a new module `calc-rescue.js` (`RESCUE_RENDERERS`). These are life-safety / rescue-rigging decisions, distinct from the fire-suppression hydraulics that stay in `calc-fire.js` (hose friction, pump discharge, hydrant flow, required fire flow, master stream, foam, sprinkler/standpipe). **All three keep `group: "F"`** (a tile's group letter is independent of its module, the v28/v30/v36/v39/v42/v70..v81 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cut was clean: the bench carries its own scoped `ui-fields.js` import block (the aliased `_DF`/`_mnF`/`_msF`/`_moF`/`_aeF`/`_fmtF` set, used by nothing else in `calc-fire.js`, so it moves out whole with no orphan) and the per-module `_finiteGuard` (copied verbatim, non-exported so it adds no corpus row); its only tables (`ROPE_RIGS`, the `_V27_DD_EFFICIENCY` curve) are local to the moved tiles, leaving no cross-module import.
- **Sizes / caps.** `calc-fire.js` 25622 -> ~22767 B gz (cap lowered 27000 -> 24500 to lock in the freed space, ~92.9%); `calc-rescue.js` ~4.4 KB gz (4370 B, cap 5500; lazy-loaded, not in the home-view first-paint payload).
- **Wiring (all gated).** `app.js` declares (three ids move to a new `RESCUE_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (lower source cap + add new), `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` (`GRADUATED_MODULES` gains `calc-rescue.js`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (three ids repointed), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (the focused three-compute-fn import split into a new `calc-rescue.js` import), `test/unit/calc-fire-v3.test.js` (the whole import block repointed), `test/unit/calc-v27.test.js` and `test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js` (focused imports repointed), and the regenerated v14 corpus (the tile-index is id-keyed and unchanged). `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `worked-examples.json`, `audit-trail.md`, and `related-tiles.mjs` reference tiles by id and need no change. README catalog-count gate agrees at 45 modules; wiring lint reports 45 renderer modules / 624 tile-id entries. ### chore: spec-v81 (calc-hvac.js cap-relief split) -- building-systems HVAC engineering bench -> new calc-hvacsystems.js; catalog holds 624; stamps 0.63.12, 2026-06-16

The twelfth consecutive cut on the v70/v71/v72/v73/v74/v75/v76/v77/v78/v79/v80 module-cap watch-list, same shape: a platform-only / housekeeping spec that **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **624 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (43 -> 44 `calc-*` modules).

- **Why.** After v80 relieved `calc-construction.js`, the standing module-cap watch (spec-v80 §5) named `calc-hvac.js` as the next split candidate. It was the tightest remaining calculator module at **94.9% of its 60 KB gzip cap** (56914 B).
- **What moved.** The cohesive spec-v16 "Group C expansion" batch -- seven contiguous, self-contained first-principles HVAC engineering tiles (`chiller-tons`, cooling tonnage from chilled-water flow and the entering/leaving split per ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch. 31; `hx-lmtd-ntu`, counter/parallel-flow LMTD, duty, UA, and effectiveness-NTU per TEMA / Incropera; `air-changes-hour`, ACH = CFM*60/volume with pressurization and the ASHRAE 62.1 / 170 bands; `boiler-pipe-sizing`, hydronic GPM, the velocity-limited copper/steel/PEX size, and Hazen-Williams head per ASHRAE Systems Ch. 13; `compressor-short-cycle`, the ASHRAE/AHRI part-load cycling parabola per Copeland AE 17-1226; `humidifier-capacity`, moisture addition and latent load from the entering-to-target RH rise; `filter-pressure-drop`, velocity-scaled drop by MERV/HEPA class and the annual fan-energy penalty per ASHRAE 52.2) -- into a new module `calc-hvacsystems.js` (`HVACSYSTEMS_RENDERERS`). These are engineered building-systems decisions (central plant, hydronics, air handling), distinct from the residential split-system core that stays in `calc-hvac.js` (Manual J loads, duct sizing, refrigerant charging and superheat/subcool service). **All seven keep `group: "C"`** (a tile's group letter is independent of its module, the v28/v30/v36/v39/v42/v70..v80 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cut was clean: the bench owns its module-local `_v16h_*` helpers and reaches nothing outside its own scoped `ui-fields.js` import block (`DEBOUNCE_MS`, `debounce`, `makeNumber`, `makeSelect`, `makeOutputLine`, `attachExampleButton`, `fmt`), one `pure-math.js` import (`hazenWilliamsFrictionLoss`), the per-module `_finiteGuard`, and two shared spec-v9 psychrometric helpers (`_v9_satPressure_kPa`, `_v9_pressureAtAltitude_kPa`) -- all copied verbatim (non-exported, so they add no corpus row), leaving no cross-module import.
- **Sizes / caps.** `calc-hvac.js` 56914 -> 44005 B gz (cap lowered 60000 -> 47000 to lock in the freed space, ~93.6%); `calc-hvacsystems.js` ~14.7 KB gz (14683 B, cap 16500; lazy-loaded, not in the home-view first-paint payload).
- **Wiring (all gated).** `app.js` declares (seven ids move to a new `HVACSYSTEMS_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (lower source cap + add new), `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` (`GRADUATED_MODULES` gains `calc-hvacsystems.js`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (seven ids repointed), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (the focused seven-compute-fn import split into a new `calc-hvacsystems.js` import), `test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js` (the whole import block -- compute fns, worked-example constants, lookup tables, and the renderer registry renamed `HVAC_RENDERERS` -> `HVACSYSTEMS_RENDERERS` -- repointed), and the regenerated v14 corpus (the tile-index is id-keyed and unchanged). `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `worked-examples.json`, `audit-trail.md`, and `related-tiles.mjs` reference tiles by id and need no change; the moved tiles use no `makeTextarea`, so `check-multiline-inputs.mjs` needs no update; the remaining `calc-hvac.js` keeps every import (no orphan). ### chore: spec-v80 (calc-construction.js cap-relief split) -- site-civil bench -> new calc-civil.js; catalog holds 624; stamps 0.63.11, 2026-06-16

The eleventh consecutive cut on the v70/v71/v72/v73/v74/v75/v76/v77/v78/v79 module-cap watch-list, same shape: a platform-only / housekeeping spec that **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **624 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (42 -> 43 `calc-*` modules).

- **Why.** After v79 relieved `calc-electrical.js`, the standing module-cap watch (spec-v79 §5) named `calc-construction.js` as the next split candidate. It was the tightest remaining calculator module at **95.0% of its 66 KB gzip cap** (62719 B).
- **What moved.** The cohesive spec-v25 site-civil / roadway-geometry quartet (`horizontal-curve`, the AASHTO/FM 5-233 circular-curve geometry with the arc definition D = 5729.58 / R; `vertical-curve`, the equal-tangent parabola measured from the BVC with the crest/sag turning point; `earthwork-end-area`, the average-end-area volume with the optional prismoidal pair and swell/shrink factor; `slope-stake-cut-fill`, the cut/fill and planar catch-offset from the hinge) -- into a new module `calc-civil.js` (`CIVIL_RENDERERS`). These are roadway and site-grading decisions, distinct from the building-construction core (framing, concrete, rebar, beams). **All four keep `group: "E"`** (a tile's group letter is independent of its module, the v28/v30/v36/v39/v42/v70..v79 precedent); ids, citations, worked examples, dimensional annotations, and behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged. The cut was clean: the bench reaches nothing outside its own scoped `ui-fields.js` import block (`DEBOUNCE_MS`, `debounce`, `makeNumber`, `makeSelect`, `makeOutputLine`, `attachExampleButton`, `fmt`) and the per-module `_finiteGuard` (copied verbatim; non-exported, so it adds no corpus row).
- **Sizes / caps.** `calc-construction.js` 62719 -> 57861 B gz (cap lowered 66000 -> 62000 to lock in the freed space, ~93.3%); `calc-civil.js` ~6.6 KB gz (6621 B, cap 8000; lazy-loaded, not in the home-view first-paint payload).
- **Wiring (all gated).** `app.js` declares (four ids move to a new `CIVIL_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (lower source cap + add new), `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` (`GRADUATED_MODULES` gains `calc-civil.js`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (four ids repointed), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (the focused four-compute-fn import repointed), `test/unit/calc-v24-v25.test.js` (the four civil compute fns split into a new `calc-civil.js` import), and the regenerated v14 corpus (the tile-index is id-keyed and unchanged). `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `worked-examples.json`, `audit-trail.md`, and `related-tiles.mjs` reference tiles by id and need no change; the moved tiles use no `makeTextarea`, so `check-multiline-inputs.mjs` needs no update; the remaining `calc-construction.js` keeps every import (no orphan). README catalog-count gate agrees at 43 modules; wiring lint reports 43 renderer modules / 624 tile-id entries. README module diagram + file-tree + spec-history and `specs/spec-v80.md` added/updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate), `npm test` (5,534 unit tests, unchanged), `npm run build` (624 tile + 25 group shells, 651-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, and render-no-nan + a11y + responsive-stress (320 px, Chromium + WebKit) on the four moved tiles -- which render identically from the new module. See [specs/spec-v80.md](specs/spec-v80.md).

- **Wiring (all gated).** `app.js` declares (two ids move to a new `DRAINAGE_RENDERERS` declare), `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES`, `sw.js` precache, `scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs` (lower source cap + add new), `scripts/check-dimensions.mjs` (`GRADUATED_MODULES` gains `calc-drainage.js`), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js` (two ids repointed), `test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js` (two compute-fn imports repointed), and the regenerated v14 corpus (the tile-index is id-keyed and unchanged). `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js`, `worked-examples.json`, and `related-tiles.mjs` reference tiles by id and need no change; the moved tiles use no `makeTextarea`, so `check-multiline-inputs.mjs` needs no update. README catalog-count gate agrees at 36 modules; wiring lint reports 36 renderer modules / 624 tile-id entries. README module diagram + file-tree + spec-history and `specs/spec-v73.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (every gate), `npm test` (5,534 unit tests, unchanged), `npm run build` (624 tile + 25 group shells, 651-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, and render-no-nan + a11y + responsive-stress (320 px, Chromium + WebKit) on the two moved tiles -- which render identically from the new module. ### docs: refresh stale home-view payload figures (gate-invisible drift), 2026-06-15

The `check-home-payload` gate enforces the 100 KB ceiling but does not compare against the prose figures in README / docs, so they had drifted as `app.js` grew. Refreshed the live numbers: total home-view payload **37,236 B (36.4% of budget)**, JS sub-budget **23,971 B (47.8% of the 50,176 B cap)** -- in `README.md` (the lazy-catalog note) and `docs/performance.md` (two stanzas, dated 2026-06-15). Docs-only; no shipped code or calculator output changed.

### fix(test): de-flake the example-button 48px touch-target assertion (sub-pixel snapping), 2026-06-15

The integration check `calculator 'Test with example' button meets the 48px touch-target floor` (test/integration/a11y.test.js) failed intermittently: `.example-btn` carries `min-height: var(--touch-min)` (48px, border-box), so the CSS box is exactly 48px, but `boundingBox()` returns device-pixel-snapped geometry and reported `47.999969...` (= 48 - 1/32768) on the runs where the button landed on a fractional sub-pixel y-offset, and `48` on others -- a flake, not a regression. The assertion now rounds the measured height before the `>= 48` compare, which absorbs sub-pixel snapping noise while still failing on any real shortfall (a bare ~22px `<button>`, or a 44px min-height). Test-only; no shipped code, version, or calculator output changed.

### chore: spec-v71 (calc-field.js cap-relief split) -- surveying bench -> new calc-survey.js; catalog holds 624; stamps 0.63.2, 2026-06-15

A platform-only / housekeeping spec in the spirit of v70 / v10 / v36 / v39: **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **624 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (33 -> 34 `calc-*` modules).

### chore: spec-v70 (calc-construction.js cap-relief split) -- earthwork bench -> new calc-earthwork.js; catalog holds 624; stamps 0.63.1, 2026-06-15

A platform-only / housekeeping spec in the spirit of v10 / v36 / v39: **adds no tiles, removes no tiles, and changes no calculator output.** The catalog holds at **624 tiles** across 25 groups; only the on-disk module layout changes (32 -> 33 `calc-*` modules).

### feat: spec-v69 (Surface Prep, Coatings, and Abatement) -- 3 tiles across Groups E and D, catalog 621 -> 624; stamps 0.63.0, 2026-06-13

Closes the dirty-jobs expansion (v65 -> v69) with the surface-prep and hazardous-material work between demolition and finish. A new `coatings` trade label is added. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. The two surface-prep tiles land in `calc-construction.js` (Group E); the abatement tile lands in `calc-restoration.js` (Group D).

- **E (Construction) +2, D (Restoration) +1.** `coating-coverage-dft` (industrial coating coverage from volume-solids and DFT: 60% solids, 5 mil DFT, 2,000 ft^2, 35% loss -> 192.5 / 125.1 ft^2/gal, 16.0 gal, 8.33 mil WFT; 1604 constant). `abrasive-blast` (blast air and abrasive consumption by nozzle: 3/8 in at 100 psi, 3,000 ft^2 -> 283 cfm, 70.8 hp, 768 lb/hr, 12.0 tons). `abatement-containment` (asbestos / lead containment take-off: 20 x 15 x 9 room, 4 ACH, 1,500 cfm machines, 3 cy debris -> 1,353 ft^2 poly, 180 cfm, 1 machine, 19 bags).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `paint-coverage` is a residential ft^2-per-gallon estimate with no volume-solids, no dry-film thickness, and no loss factor - it cannot size an industrial coat to a spec. No tile sized blasting air or abrasive. The restoration ventilation bench (`confined-space-vent`, `smoke-ejector-cfm`, `hepa-filter-life`) sizes airflow for drying or purge; `abatement-containment` reuses the air-change idea but adds the poly take-off, the negative-air machine count, and the regulated-waste bags none of them computes.
- **Honesty.** All three `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming SSPC / AMPP PA 2 and the SP specs, the 1604 coverage constant, the nozzle manufacturer's chart, EPA NESHAP 40 CFR 61 Subpart M, EPA RRP 40 CFR 745, and OSHA 1926.1101 / 1926.62 without reproducing them. The product data sheet, the nozzle chart, and a licensed / certified abatement contractor govern; blasting and abatement are regulated work with mandatory protections.
- **Module / caps.** `calc-construction.js` cap raised 67000 -> 70000 B gz (+2 coatings tiles, built ~68.3 KB); `calc-restoration.js` cap raised 27000 -> 29000 B gz (+1 abatement tile, built ~27.6 KB).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js` (new `coatings` trade), `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (3), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+3 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (20 terms), the `app.js` `CONSTRUCTION_RENDERERS` and `RESTORATION_RENDERERS` declares, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the volume-solids-over-100 seam, the pressure-scaled nozzle branch, the two-machine abatement branch, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group E 66 -> 68, Group D 25 -> 26; totals 624 tiles / 651 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 928 / dims 931 / fuzzer 928 / derivation 624), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v69.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,534), `npm run build` (624 tile + 25 group shells, 651-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+3 -> 629), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all three new tiles. **Closes the v65 -> v69 dirty-jobs expansion** (a new Group Z + earthwork + tree care + surface prep, 25 tiles across five specs). See [specs/spec-v69.md](specs/spec-v69.md).

### feat: spec-v68 (Tree Care and Arborist Rigging) -- 5 tiles in calc-agriculture.js, catalog 616 -> 621; stamps 0.62.0, 2026-06-13

Equips the production tree crew Group L had only gestured at: weigh the piece, predict the shock when it is dropped, cut the hinge that steers a fell, pick the wraps that let one person lower it, and size the chip haul. A new `arboriculture` trade label is added. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. All five land in `calc-agriculture.js` next to `timber-cruise`.

- **L (Forestry) +5.** `log-limb-weight` (green log / limb weight from a frustum volume and species green density: 16 in x 8 ft red oak -> 11.17 ft^3, 715 lb). `tree-rigging-shock` (dynamic load when a piece is dropped and caught: 500 lb, 3 ft drop, 30 ft rope at 5% -> 1,618 lb peak, 3.24x). `felling-notch-hinge` (open-face notch and hinge geometry: 20 in cut, 22% notch -> 4.4 in notch, 2.0 in hinge, 16 in width). `porta-wrap-friction` (capstan hold force by wrap count: 800 lb load side at 0.20 friction -> 227.7 / 64.8 / 18.5 / 5.2 lb for 1-4 wraps). `chipper-debris` (loose chip volume and haul loads: 4,400 lb green wood at 550 lb/lcy -> 8.0 lcy, 1 load).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `timber-cruise` is a stand-level board-foot estimate, not a single-piece rigging weight. The Group Z rigging tiles size crane and hardware systems; the arborist tiles size a climbing-and-lowering system. `tree-rigging-shock` computes the dynamic load no Group Z tile models (the crane tiles assume a controlled, non-shock pick), and `porta-wrap-friction` is the capstan equation for a lowering device. The single most lethal misjudgment in tree rigging -- a heavy piece dropped onto a static rope -- now has a tile that is loud about it.
- **Honesty.** The rigging-load and felling tiles carry `GOVERNANCE.worker_safety`; `log-limb-weight` and `chipper-debris` carry `GOVERNANCE.general`. ANSI Z133-2017, the FPL Wood Handbook, the arborist rigging research literature, and capstan mechanics are named, never reproduced. The shock-load tile is explicit that it UNDERESTIMATES a hard catch (a floor, not a ceiling) and that a static catch is unbounded.
- **Module / cap.** `calc-agriculture.js` cap raised 28000 -> 34000 B gz (+5 tiles, built ~32.2 KB).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js` (new `arboriculture` trade), `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (5), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+5 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (27 terms), the `app.js` `AGRICULTURE_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the frustum-vs-cylinder branch, the fall-factor sensitivity, the zero-elongation error seam, the wrap-count table, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group L 19 -> 24; totals 621 tiles / 648 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 925 / dims 928 / fuzzer 925 / derivation 621), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v68.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,533), `npm run build` (621 tile + 25 group shells, 648-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+5 -> 626), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all five new tiles (the 4-row wrap table stacks legibly at 320 px). See [specs/spec-v68.md](specs/spec-v68.md).

### feat: spec-v67 (Earthwork and Excavation Deepening) -- 5 tiles in calc-construction.js, catalog 611 -> 616; stamps 0.61.0, 2026-06-13

Turns Group E's earthwork from a single volume number into a production bench: convert dirt among bank / loose / compacted, balance the haul fleet, pump the pit, keep the spoil off the wall, and bed the pipe. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI. All five land in `calc-construction.js` next to `excavation`.

- **E (Construction) +5.** `soil-swell-shrink` (bank / loose / compacted conversion: 100 bank cy at 25% swell / 15% shrink -> 125 loose, 0.80 load factor, 85 compacted). `haul-cycle-production` (truck/loader cycle and fleet match: 18.0 min cycle -> 2.78 loads/hr, 33.3 lcy/hr, 9 trucks, 300 lcy/hr fleet). `dewatering-rate` (excavation pump rate: 20x12 pit drawn 3 ft in 30 min, 40 gpm inflow, 25% margin -> 5,386 gal, 219.5 gpm, 274.4 gpm sized). `spoil-setback` (OSHA spoil setback and surcharge: 10 ft trench, 4 ft pile at 34 deg -> 5.93 ft toe spread, 10 ft setback, 15.93 ft clear). `pipe-bedding-backfill` (three-zone pipe trench take-off: 100 ft, 24 in trench, 12 in OD -> 2.47 cy / 3.46 tons bedding, 4.50 cy embedment, 22.2 cy backfill).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `excavation` returns bank cubic yards and stops; nothing converted that to trucks or placed fill, balanced a fleet, handled water, or set the spoil off the wall. `aggregate` is a flat pad volume, not the three-zone pipe-trench take-off. `pump-tdh` / `pump-sizing` give head; `dewatering-rate` gives the flow an open pit needs and defers head to `pump-tdh`.
- **Honesty.** Earthmoving tiles `GOVERNANCE.general`, naming the Caterpillar Performance Handbook soil-conversion and cycle-time methods, OSHA 1926.651(j) / Subpart P, and ASTM D2321 without reproducing them. Notes: swell / shrinkage are geotech properties; the 50-minute hour is a planning default; the inflow must come from a pumping test; 2 ft is the absolute code minimum setback; the embedment excludes the pipe's own volume.
- **Module / caps.** `calc-construction.js` cap raised 64000 -> 67000 B gz (+5 earthwork tiles, built ~65.8 KB); `citations.js` cap raised 178000 -> 182000 B gz (the v62-v67 citation entries crossed the cap).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (5, `GOVERNANCE.general`), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+5 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (25 terms), the `app.js` `CONSTRUCTION_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the fleet-match count, the hold-only dewatering branch, the code-floor-vs-surcharge setback, the pipe-exceeds-trench seam, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group E 61 -> 66; totals 616 tiles / 643 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 920 / dims 923 / fuzzer 920 / derivation 616), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v67.md` updated.
- **Verified.** `npm run lint` (26 gates), `npm test` (5,532), `npm run build` (616 tile + 25 group shells, 643-URL sitemap), `npm run data:verify`, the worked-examples runner (+5 -> 621), `check:dist` / `check:shells` / `check:shell-mobile`, and render-no-nan + a11y on all five new tiles. See [specs/spec-v67.md](specs/spec-v67.md).

### feat: spec-v66 (Group Z, Rigging and Heavy Lift -- hardware and below-the-hook) -- 6 tiles in calc-rigging.js, catalog 605 -> 611; stamps 0.60.0, 2026-06-13

Completes the **Group Z (Rigging and Heavy Lift)** founding batch at 13 tiles, adding the iron between the hook and the load to the existing `calc-rigging.js`. All carry `GOVERNANCE.rigging`. No new group, no new module, no new dependencies, no telemetry, no AI.

- **Group Z +6.** `shackle-eyebolt-wll` (hardware WLL and angular derate: shoulder eye bolt 7,000 lb at 45 deg -> 0.30, 2,100 lb, FAIL; shackle side-load and shoulder-eye-bolt derate curves). `spreader-beam` (spreader bar vs lifting beam below the hook: 10,000 lb on a 10 ft bar, top 6 ft -> 50.2 deg sling, 6,509 lb top sling, 4,167 lb bar compression, 25,000 ft-lb beam moment). `forklift-capacity-derate` (data-plate load-center derate: 5,000 lb @ 24 in handling a load at 36 in -> 3,333 lb net, 10% margin). `roller-jack-force` (push / breakaway force on rollers and skates with a grade term: 12,000 lb on skates -> 360 lb steady, 540 lb breakaway, 3 skates). `chain-lever-hoist` (hand effort and chain travel: 2,000 lb at MA 32, eff 0.85 -> 73.5 lb hand pull, 128 ft chain travel). `block-redirect-load` (resultant on a rigging block: 3,000 lb line at 90 deg -> 4,243 lb; doubled back -> 6,000 lb).
- **Why they earn tiles.** `rigging-check` (Group N) is a stage-hardware WLL pass/fail with no eye-bolt angular derate, no shackle side-load chart, and no below-the-hook member; `sling-angle` / `sling-d-d-efficiency` reduce a sling, not the hardware; `pulley-ma-gen` / `rope-ma` give mechanical advantage, not hand effort or the resultant on a block. No tile sized a spreader bar / lifting beam, derated a forklift to its load center, or found roller / jacking force.
- **Honesty.** Every tile carries `GOVERNANCE.rigging`; ASME B30.26 / B18.15 / BTH-1 / B30.20 / B56.1 / B30.16 / B30.21 are named, never reproduced. The manufacturer's rating plate and chart govern.
- **Module / caps.** `calc-rigging.js` cap raised 9000 -> 15000 B gz (Group Z complete at 13 tiles, built ~13.3 KB gz; the authorized `calc-heavylift.js` split lands if it grows further). `tools-data.js` cap raised 50000 -> 52000 B gz (the registry crossed its cap after the v62-v66 rows; lazy-loaded, not on the home view).
- **Per-tile wiring (all gated).** `tools-data.js`, `tile-meta.js`, `citations.js` (6, `GOVERNANCE.rigging`), `test/fixtures/worked-examples.json` (+6 with cross-checks), `test/fixtures/compute-map.js`, `scripts/related-tiles.mjs`, `data/search/aliases.json` (28 terms), the `app.js` `RIGGING_RENDERERS` declare, the `// dims:` annotations, a `bounds-fuzzer.test.js` block (every worked example, the eye-bolt FAIL path, the lifting-beam moment, the forklift overload, the on-grade roller case, and every error seam), and the regenerated v14 corpus + tile-index. README catalog table + counts (Group Z 7 -> 13; totals 611 tiles / 638 sitemap URLs; correctness pipeline refreshed to corpus 915 / dims 918 / fuzzer 915 / derivation 611), `docs/audit-trail.md`, and `specs/spec-v66.md` updated.
