  - `macrs` ([calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js)): `year_of_interest = NaN` indexed the schedule with `NaN` and threw on `.year` of `undefined` -> finite guard.
  - `serial-dilution` ([calc-lab.js](calc-lab.js)): `number_of_steps = Infinity` built an unbounded tube array (OOM) -> finite + 1,000-step cap.
  - `hip-valley-rafter` ([calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js)): `run_ft = Infinity` or a non-positive jack spacing looped the jack-rafter generator forever -> finite run + positive-spacing guards.
  - `court-deadline` ([calc-legal.js](calc-legal.js)): `days = Infinity` threw "Invalid time value" on the calendar path and looped on the court-day path -> finite + 100,000-day cap.
  - `solar-times` ([calc-field.js](calc-field.js)): `tz_offset_hours = Infinity` spun the 1440-minute wrap loop forever -> finite-offset guard.
- **Mobile: real horizontal-scroll defect fixed.** Promoting `check-shell-mobile` to a standing gate (below) and making its measurement wait for `networkidle` (so it measures rendered content, not the empty "Loading..." shell) surfaced a genuine page-level overflow: the client-rendered **changelog** overflowed 320px on long file-path / URL tokens (rendered as raw text, up to ~97 chars). Fixed in [styles.css](styles.css) with `overflow-wrap: anywhere; word-break: break-word` on `#changelog-content` blocks. All **463 / 463** shells now clean at 320px.
- **`check-shell-mobile` promoted to a CI gate (spec-v18 §6).** Wired into the `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `integration` job (which already has Playwright + chromium): it builds `dist/` and fails the build on any page-level horizontal scroll at 320px. The script now tries `playwright` then `@playwright/test` so it actually runs in CI instead of skipping.
- **Housekeeping.** The 442-entry `COMPUTE_MAP` (tile id -> compute function) was extracted from [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js) into a shared [test/fixtures/compute-map.js](test/fixtures/compute-map.js), now the single source of truth for the worked-example runner and the contract sweep.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,953 -> **4,961** (the contract Tier-1 assertion + 7 regression tests). `npm run lint` (now **23 gates**), `npm test`, `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, and `npm run check:shell-mobile` (463/463) all green. Tier-1 contract backlog **0**; Tier-2 backlog **889** (baselined, ratcheting down). See [specs/spec-v18.md](specs/spec-v18.md).

### spec-v17 OPEN: X.5 GRM income-method output on rental-worksheet + prerendered-shell mobile guard, 2026-06-09

This stanza lands the X.5 income-method valuation as a **net-new output extension** on the existing `rental-worksheet` tile (the v16 B.3 / C.4 precedent: extend the existing tile, never ship a duplicate), and adds a permanent mobile guard for the prerendered static shells. v17 stays **open**; package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog count is unchanged at **437** (an output extension, not a new tile).

- **Audit refinement.** The earlier Group X batch recorded X.5 as covered by `cap-rate-dscr`. That holds for the cap-rate / DSCR core, but the **gross rent multiplier (GRM)** the income approach leads with was genuinely absent from the catalog. Rather than ship a near-duplicate tile, the genuinely-new metric lands on the tile that already owns the inputs.
- **[calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js) `rental-worksheet`** now reports two more outputs: the **GRM** = `property value / annual gross rent` (the appraisal income-approach quick-screen; computed on annual scheduled gross rent, with the citation flagging the annual-vs-monthly basis), and the **value at a market GRM** = `market_grm x annual gross rent` from a new optional "market / comparable GRM" input. Both are `null` until their inputs are supplied, so the existing price-free / market-free worksheet behavior is unchanged. Worked example pinned: $320,000 value / $26,400 annual gross rent -> **GRM 12.121**; at a market GRM of 10 -> **implied value $264,000**. Per the Appraisal Institute, The Appraisal of Real Estate (15th ed.).
- **Wiring.** Updated the `// dims:` out-annotation, the inline + [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (GRM basis + Appraisal Institute), the [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) description, three search aliases (`gross rent multiplier`, `grm`, `income approach valuation`) in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json), the worked-example fixture in [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json), two dedicated unit tests in [test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js](test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js), and GRM assertions in the existing [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row (no new corpus / bounds row -- the same exported function). With this, every genuinely-new v17 tile and tile-output the first-principles / extension discipline can deliver has shipped; only the Z.5 state-keyed shards remain (deferred).
- **Mobile: prerendered-shell guard ([scripts/check-shell-mobile.mjs](scripts/check-shell-mobile.mjs), `npm run check:shell-mobile`).** The SPA 320px sweep guards the interactive `#hash` tile views, but the prerendered static shells (`dist/tools/<id>/`, `dist/groups/<slug>/`, `changelog.html`) are a separate zero-JS surface the always-on integration job (which serves the repo root, not `dist/`) never reaches -- they had been verified only by manual spot-check. The new on-demand script serves `dist/` and asserts no page-level horizontal scroll on **all 463** shells at 320px; the 2026-06-09 run reports **463 / 463 clean**. It skips cleanly (exit 0) when Playwright or `dist/` is absent, mirroring the dist-absent skip in `check-module-sizes`. Documented as §10 in [docs/mobile-responsive.md](docs/mobile-responsive.md).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,951 -> **4,953** (two GRM tests). Build emits **437 tile shells / 24 group shells / 463 sitemap URLs** (unchanged). Corpus / dims / bounds coverage unchanged at 734 / 737 / 734 (output extension, not a new function). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, the 480-test integration suite, and `npm run check:shell-mobile` (463/463) all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: W.5 holding-fuel + X.2 rent-vs-buy landed 2026-06-08

This stanza lands two genuinely-new no-shard tiles: W.5 holding-pattern fuel/endurance (Aviation) and X.2 rent-vs-buy NPV (Real Estate). v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **435 -> 437 tiles** (Group W 18 -> 19, Group X 18 -> 19). Both are pure first-principles with no bundled data shard.

- **Audit note.** The rest of Phase W is already covered (W.1 `top-of-descent`, W.2 `weight-balance`, W.3 `pressure-altitude`, W.4 Mach-in-`true-airspeed`); W.5 holding fuel was the genuinely-new gap. Phase X X.5 income-method GRM stays covered by the existing `cap-rate-dscr`, leaving X.2 rent-vs-buy as the genuinely-new gap.
- **[calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js) W.5 `holding-fuel`** (Holding Pattern Fuel and Time, 14 CFR 91.151 / 91.167). `computeHoldingFuel` answers the in-hold endurance question the pre-flight `fuel-planning` tile does not: fuel burned over the hold (`burn_gph x hold_min / 60`), fuel remaining at release, endurance left in hr:min, the reserve floor converted to gallons, and the maximum hold before busting reserve (`(tank - reserve) / burn x 60`). It reports fuel weight at 6.0 lb/gal avgas / 6.7 lb/gal jet-A and a no-wind holding-leg distance from the optional airspeed, flagging a reserve bust, an insufficient-fuel divert, and an unusually long (>60 min) hold. Worked example pinned: 12 gph, 30-min hold, 40 gal, 45-min reserve -> **6 gal burned, 34 gal remaining, 155-min max hold**.
- **[calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js) X.2 `rent-vs-buy`** (Rent vs Buy NPV Comparison, NYT methodology). `computeRentVsBuy` runs the present-value comparison: each path is a PV of out-of-pocket cost discounted at the investment-return rate. `PV_buy = down_payment + PV(P&I + property tax + insurance + HOA + maintenance) - PV(net sale proceeds)` (net sale = `price x (1+appr)^N - selling cost - loan balance at year N`) against `PV_rent = PV(rent x 12 x (1+rent_infl)^(t-1))`. It returns the break-even holding year (smallest year buying's PV cost first drops to or below renting's) and a buy-vs-rent verdict; the renter's retained down payment correctly carries zero NPV because it is discounted at the same rate it earns. Tax treatment (mortgage-interest deduction, the Section 121 exclusion) is out of scope and noted. Worked example pinned: $400k / $80k down / 6.5% 30y / tax 1.2% / ins $1,800 / maint 1% / appr 3% / rent $2,200 infl 3% / return 5% / 7-yr hold / sell 6% -> **PV_buy $158,759.65 vs PV_rent $166,256.12, buying cheaper by ~$7,496, break-even at year 6**.
- **Full pipeline.** Both land with: renderers in `AVIATION_RENDERERS` / `REALESTATE_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` list in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations (regenerated at 100%); [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (aviation / real-estate governance); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; dedicated tests in [test/unit/calc-aviation.test.js](test/unit/calc-aviation.test.js) (AVIATION_RENDERERS count guard 18 -> 19) and [test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js](test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js) (REALESTATE_RENDERERS count guard 18 -> 19); [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entries; [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) rows; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json). The 320px no-horizontal-scroll mobile sweep in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js) was extended to cover `#holding-fuel` and `#rent-vs-buy`.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,939 -> **4,951**. Build emits **437 tile shells / 24 group shells / 463 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 437/437; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 730 -> **734 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 733 -> **737** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (435 -> 437) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy refreshed across [README.md](README.md) (including the stale Group L / R / S per-group counts) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, `npm run data:verify`, and the 479-test integration suite all green.
- **Housekeeping: module-size caps in [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs).** Bumped `calc-aviation.js` **29000 -> 33000** and `calc-realestate.js` **31000 -> 35000** (current + ~20% headroom rule) to absorb the two tiles.

### spec-v17 OPEN: Group U (Veterinary) tiles - U.1 vet-cri, U.3 vet-transfusion, U.4 equine-weight landed 2026-06-07

This stanza lands the three genuinely-new Phase U (Veterinary) tiles. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **432 -> 435 tiles** (Group U 18 -> 21). All three live in [calc-vet.js](calc-vet.js) and carry the spec-v12 "veterinarian governs" limitation banner.

- **Audit note.** The remaining Phase U drafts are covered or out of genuinely-new scope: **U.2** anesthesia induction is a formulary range-lookup already served by `vet-weight-based-dose` + `vet-anesthesia-vitals`, and **U.5** caloric-need-with-illness-factor is the existing `vet-energy-requirement` (RER / MER). U.1, U.3, and U.4 are the genuinely-new tiles.
- **[calc-vet.js](calc-vet.js) U.1 `vet-cri`** (Constant-Rate Infusion Drip Rate, Plumb's 10th ed. / AVECCT). `computeVetCRI` runs the CRI bag method: `mg/hr = dose x weight` (dose as mcg/kg/min or mg/kg/hr), drug to add = `mg/hr x duration`, infusion rate = `bag / duration`, drug volume = `total mg / stock concentration`, and drops/min = `rate x gtt/mL / 60`, with flags when the drug volume exceeds the bag or the rate exceeds 500 mL/hr. Worked example pinned: 0.5 mcg/kg/min fentanyl, 15 kg dog, 24 hr, 250 mL bag -> **0.45 mg/hr, 10.8 mg total, 10.4167 mL/hr**.
- **[calc-vet.js](calc-vet.js) U.3 `vet-transfusion`** (Blood Transfusion Volume, ACVIM 2021 consensus). `computeVetTransfusion` computes `volume = blood_volume_per_kg x weight x (PCV_target - PCV_current) / PCV_donor` (dog 90, cat 60, horse 80 mL/kg), the infusion rate, and the duration, rejecting a donor PCV below 35 and a non-increasing PCV gap, and flagging over-transfusion (target > 35%) and the 4-hr single-unit limit. Worked example pinned: dog 20 kg, PCV 15 -> 25, donor 40, at 5 mL/kg/hr -> **450 mL over 4.5 hr**.
- **[calc-vet.js](calc-vet.js) U.4 `equine-weight`** (Body Weight from Heart-Girth Tape, Carroll & Huntington 1988). `computeEquineWeight` computes `BW_lb = girth_in^2 x length_in / 330` (horse) or `/ 299` (pony), the kg conversion, and a 1.5-2.5% hay-feeding band, flagging a girth outside the 50-95 in validation range. Worked example pinned: horse, girth 75 in, length 65 in -> **1107.95 lb (502.56 kg)**.
- **Full pipeline.** All three land with: renderers in `VET_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` list in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations (regenerated at 100%); [limitation-banner.js](limitation-banner.js) canonical copy + [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) `SIMPLIFIED` rows; [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (veterinary governance); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; 19 dedicated tests in [test/unit/calc-vet.test.js](test/unit/calc-vet.test.js) (with the VET_RENDERERS count guard bumped 18 -> 21); [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entries; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,920 -> **4,939**. Build emits **435 tile shells / 24 group shells / 461 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 435/435; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 724 -> **730 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 727 -> **733** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (432 -> 435) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy refreshed across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md).
- **Housekeeping: module-size cap in [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs).** Bumped `calc-vet.js` **30000 -> 36000** (current + ~20% headroom rule) to absorb the three tiles and their banner copy.

### spec-v17 OPEN: Group X (Real Estate) financing tiles - X.1 mortgage-point-breakeven, X.3 per-diem-interest, X.4 mortgage-reserves landed 2026-06-06

This stanza lands the three no-shard Phase X (Real Estate) financing tiles. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **429 -> 432 tiles** (Group X 15 -> 18). All three live in [calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js), reuse the existing closed-form amortization, and bundle no data shard.

- **[calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js) X.1 `mortgage-point-breakeven`** (Mortgage Discount-Point Break-Even). `computeMortgagePointBreakeven` prices a buy-down: the monthly payment at the base rate and at the bought-down rate (P&I = `(P x r) / (1 - (1 + r)^-n)`), the monthly savings, the up-front point cost (`loan x point_cost_pct`), and the break-even month (`point cost / monthly savings`), with a worth-it verdict against the holding period and a rejection when the rate-with-points is not below the base rate. Per first-principles amortization + the CFPB Loan Estimate (12 CFR 1026.37). Worked example pinned: $300k, 7.0% vs 6.5% with 2 points ($6,000), 30y -> **$99.70/mo savings, 60.18-mo (5.0 yr) break-even, worth it for a 7-yr hold**.
- **[calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js) X.3 `per-diem-interest`** (Per-Diem Prorated Interest at Closing). `computePerDiemInterest` computes the prepaid (odd-days) interest line on the CFPB Closing Disclosure: daily interest (`loan x rate / basis`, basis 365 / 360 / 30-360) times the days from the closing date through the end of the month (counting the closing day; the first regular payment is due the 1st of the following month and pays in arrears). Per 12 CFR 1026.38 Appendix H. Worked example pinned: $300k at 6.0%, close 2026-06-15, Actual/365 -> **$49.3151/day x 16 days = $789.04**.
- **[calc-realestate.js](calc-realestate.js) X.4 `mortgage-reserves`** (Reserves Requirement, Months PITI). `computeMortgageReserves` sizes the post-closing reserve test: `required = PITI x months` against eligible liquid assets plus an allowable fraction (default 60%) of vested retirement, returning surplus/shortfall and the months of PITI covered, with an above-24-month flag. Per Fannie Mae Selling Guide B3-4.1-01 / B3-4.3-03. Worked example pinned: $2,500 PITI x 6 mo = $15,000 required; $20k liquid + 60% of $30k = $38,000 eligible -> **$23,000 surplus**.
- **Audit note.** X.2 rent-vs-buy NPV and X.5 income-method GRM remain for a later batch (X.5's cap-rate / DSCR core already ships as `cap-rate-dscr`).
- **Full pipeline.** All three land with: renderers in `REALESTATE_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` list in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations (regenerated at 100%); [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (real-estate governance); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; 19 dedicated tests in [test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js](test/unit/calc-realestate.test.js); [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entries; [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) rows; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,899 -> **4,920**. Build emits **432 tile shells / 24 group shells / 458 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 432/432; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 718 -> **724 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 721 -> **727** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (429 -> 432) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy refreshed across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md).

### spec-v17 OPEN: Group V (EMS) completed - V.1 ideal-body-weight + V.3 corrected-qt landed 2026-06-05

This stanza lands the two genuinely-new Phase V (EMS) tiles, completing the Group V deepening. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **427 -> 429 tiles** (Group V 20 -> 22). Both live in [calc-ems.js](calc-ems.js) and carry the spec-v12 §13 medical limitation banner.

- **Audit note (the v16/v17 pattern again).** Most of the Phase V drafts already exist and are documented as covered, not duplicated: **V.2** anion gap is `anion-gap`, **V.4** NIHSS is `nihss`, **V.5** Wells is `wells-dvt` / `wells-pe`, **V.6** PERC is `perc-rule`. The two genuinely-new tiles are V.1 and V.3.
- **[calc-ems.js](calc-ems.js) V.1 `ideal-body-weight`** (Ideal / Lean / Adjusted Body Weight, Devine 1974 + Hume 1966). `computeIdealBodyWeight` reports the **Devine IBW** (`50 + 2.3*(height_in - 60)` male, `45.5 + ...` female), the **Hume lean body weight**, and the **ICU adjusted body weight** (`IBW + 0.4*(ABW - IBW)`) once actual weight exceeds 130 % of IBW. Accepts height in inches or centimeters, flags short stature below 60 in (Devine under-estimates; Robinson / Miller alternatives), and rejects out-of-range height / weight. Worked example pinned: **male, 70 in -> IBW 73.0 kg; ABW 100 kg -> adjusted 83.8 kg**.
- **[calc-ems.js](calc-ems.js) V.3 `corrected-qt`** (Corrected QT, Bazett 1920 / Fridericia 1920 / Framingham 1992). `computeCorrectedQT` computes `RR = 60/HR` then **QTcB** (`QT/sqrt(RR)`), **QTcF** (`QT/cbrt(RR)`), and **QTcFram** (`QT + 154*(1 - RR)`), flags that Fridericia / Framingham is preferred outside 60-100 bpm (Bazett mis-corrects at rate extremes), and bands each correction (>= 450 / 460 borderline, >= 500 ms high torsades risk). Worked example pinned: **QT 400 ms at HR 75 (RR 0.8 s) -> QTcB 447.21 ms, QTcF 430.89 ms, QTcFram 430.8 ms**.
- **Full pipeline.** Both land with: renderers in `EMS_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` list in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations (regenerated at 100%); [limitation-banner.js](limitation-banner.js) canonical copy + [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) `SIMPLIFIED` rows; [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (EMS-prehospital governance); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; 13 dedicated tests in [test/unit/calc-ems.test.js](test/unit/calc-ems.test.js); and four search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,885 -> **4,899**. Build emits **429 tile shells / 24 group shells / 455 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 429/429; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 714 -> **718 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 717 -> **721** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (427 -> 429) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy refreshed across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.
- **Housekeeping: module-size caps in [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs).** Bumped `calc-ems.js` **28500 -> 34000** and `limitation-banner.js` **9500 -> 11500** (current + ~20% headroom rule) to absorb the two tiles and their banner copy.

### spec-v17 OPEN: R.3 home-office deduction + S.1 federal wage-garnishment cap landed 2026-06-05

This stanza lands the two genuinely-new finance/legal tiles that need no bundled state shard, after auditing the rest of the Phase R / S drafts against the live catalog. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **425 -> 427 tiles** (Group R 12 -> 13, Group S 9 -> 10).

- **Audit note (the v16/v17 pattern again).** Most of the Phase R / S drafts already exist and are documented as covered, not duplicated: **R.1** quarterly estimated tax is `estimated-tax`, **R.2** mileage deduction is `mileage-rollup` (and `mileage-cost`), **R.4** Schedule SE is `se-tax`; **S.2** the FRCP Rule 6 deadline is `court-deadline`, **S.3** prejudgment interest is `judgment-interest`. The two genuinely-new, no-state-shard tiles are R.3 and S.1.
- **[calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) R.3 `home-office`** (Home-Office Deduction, IRS Pub 587 / Form 8829). `computeHomeOffice` reports the **simplified** method (`min(office_ft2, 300) x $5`, capped at $1,500 per Rev. Proc. 2013-13), the **actual** method (`(office_ft2 / home_ft2) x total_home_expenses`), and the higher of the two, with flags for the 300 ft^2 cap, an office-use percent above 50%, and the Form 8829 / depreciation-recapture consequence of the actual method. Worked example pinned: 200 ft^2 office in a 2,000 ft^2 home, $24,000 expenses -> **simplified $1,000, actual $2,400, recommended $2,400 (actual)**.
- **[calc-legal.js](calc-legal.js) S.1 `wage-garnishment`** (Wage Garnishment Cap, federal CCPA Title III, 15 USC 1673 / DOL Fact Sheet 30). `computeWageGarnishment` computes the maximum garnishment per pay period: **consumer debt** = lesser of 25% of disposable or the amount above 30x the federal minimum wage; **federal student loan** = 15% under the same floor; **child support / alimony** = 50% / 55% / 60% / 65% of disposable (supporting-another and >12-week-arrears flags), exempt from the 30x floor. The 30x floor scales by pay period (weekly 30, biweekly 60, semimonthly 65, monthly 130 x $7.25). An optional **state cap** percent applies the stricter limit rather than bundling a 50-state shard; federal tax levies (IRS Pub 1494) are noted as out of scope. Uses the legal "information, not advice" governance. Worked example pinned: $600 weekly disposable, consumer debt -> **$150 max garnishment, $450 protected** (floor $217.50).
- **Full pipeline.** Both land with: renderers in `ACCOUNTING_RENDERERS` / `LEGAL_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` lists in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations (regenerated at 100%); [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (tax for home-office, legal for wage-garnishment); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; 16 dedicated tests (8 each) in [test/unit/calc-accounting.test.js](test/unit/calc-accounting.test.js) and [test/unit/calc-legal.test.js](test/unit/calc-legal.test.js); [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entries; [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) rows; and four search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,867 -> **4,885**. Build emits **427 tile shells / 24 group shells / 453 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 427/427; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 712 -> **714 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 715 -> **717** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (425 -> 427) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js) and the renderer-registry counts (ACCOUNTING 12 -> 13, LEGAL 9 -> 10). Tile-count copy updated to 427 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified both new tiles at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.
- **Housekeeping: module-size caps in [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs).** Bumped `calc-accounting.js` **15000 -> 18000** for the R.3 tile (built ~15.1 KB gzipped). Also bumped `calc-agriculture.js` **19000 -> 24000**: the prior L.2 / L.5 batch grew the built module to ~19.7 KB (over its 19000 cap) but its local lint measured a stale `dist/` and missed it. CI runs `lint` before `build`, so on a fresh checkout `dist/` is absent and this gate no-ops (`check-module-sizes` reads `dist/`) -- main was not red -- but the cap is now honest. Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile split stays the preferred long-term remediation for both modules.

### spec-v17 OPEN: Group L agriculture completed its genuinely-new surface (L.2 npk-blend, L.5 tank-mix) 2026-06-05

This stanza lands the two remaining genuinely-new Phase L tiles, completing the Group L deepening. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **423 -> 425 tiles** (Group L 12 -> 14). Both live in [calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline. The §H.2 extraction landed earlier today restored the home-view payload headroom these two TOOLS rows consume.

- **[calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) L.2 `npk-blend`** (NPK Fertilizer Blend from Soil Test, USDA NRCS Agronomy Technical Note ranges + Cooperative Extension nutrient-budget method). `computeNpkBlend` computes the per-nutrient recommendation `max(0, crop demand - soil-test credit)` for N / P2O5 / K2O, then solves a three-straight blend the standard Extension way: potash (0-0-60) for K, DAP (18-46-0) for P, and urea (46-0-0) for the N balance **after crediting the nitrogen DAP carries**. It reports each product's lb/acre and field total, the nutrients delivered, and over-application flags (N above 250 lb/acre, P2O5 above 100 lb/acre buildup/runoff, the legume zero-N case, and the "soil meets demand -> no fertilizer" case). Crop nutrient demand is a representative inline default; the certified soil-test lab report and state Extension recommendation govern. Worked example pinned: corn (150-60-40 demand), soil credit 20-10-15 lb/acre, 80 acres -> **rec 130 N / 50 P2O5 / 25 K2O; urea 240.1, DAP 108.7, potash 41.7 lb/acre; blend delivers exactly the recommendation**.
- **[calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) L.5 `tank-mix`** (Pesticide Tank-Mix and Acres per Tank, EPA label / FIFRA + NRCS Agronomy Technical Note 5). `computeTankMix` computes acres treated per tank (`tank gal / GPA`), product per tank (`acres/tank x rate`) in the entered unit with gallon / millilitre (liquid) or pound / gram (dry) conversions, and -- given a field area -- the tanks needed (ceil), total product, and total carrier water. **Audit note:** the nozzle-output GPA calibration (`GPA = 5940 x GPM / (speed x spacing)`) is the *existing* `gpa-rate` tile, and `sprayer-calibration` covers the 1/128-acre method; L.5 lands only the genuinely-new tank-loading accounting those two do not cover, rather than duplicating the calibration math. Uses the pesticide / "the label is the law" governance variant. Worked example pinned: 300 gal tank, 15 GPA, 1.5 pt/acre, 80-acre field -> **20 acres/tank, 30 pt (3.75 gal) per tank, 4 tanks, 120 pt total, 1,200 gal carrier water**.
- **Full pipeline.** Both land with: renderers registered into `AGRICULTURE_RENDERERS` + TOOLS registry entries in [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) + the precache `declare()` list in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations on the new exports (regenerated at 100%); [citations.js](citations.js) source stamps (general for the fertilizer blend, pesticide for the tank-mix); [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixtures registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows; 16 dedicated tests (8 each) appended to [test/unit/calc-agriculture-v17.test.js](test/unit/calc-agriculture-v17.test.js); [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entries; [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) rows; and four search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,849 -> **4,867**. Build emits **425 tile shells / 24 group shells / 451 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 425/425; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 710 -> **712 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 713 -> **715** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (423 -> 425) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js) and the Group L citation-count guard (12 -> 14). Tile-count copy updated to 425 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified both new tiles at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: §H.2 TOOLS catalog extracted into a lazy-loaded tools-data.js (home-payload headroom restored) 2026-06-05

This stanza lands the **§H.2 TOOLS-metadata extraction** the prior three stanzas flagged as the mandatory next change. It is a pure architecture move -- **no tile added, no behavior changed** -- so the catalog stays **423 tiles** and the package version stays **0.16.0**. After Y.2 the home-view JS sub-budget (spec-v10 §H.2) sat at **99.3%** (49,839 / 50,176 B, ~337 B of headroom), with no room for another tile; this change restores it to **39.9%** (20,025 / 50,176 B) and the total home payload to **31.9%** (32,659 / 102,400 B).

- **New file [tools-data.js](tools-data.js).** The 423-entry `TOOLS` array (formerly the bulk of `app.js`, ~30 KB gzipped) moved here verbatim, preserving the exact `{ id, name, group, trades, desc }` text shape and the inline `// Group X:` comments so every regex parser keeps matching. It exports `export const TOOLS = [...]` and nothing else. `GROUP_NAMES` / `GROUPS` and the `declare()` renderer-wiring table stay inline in [app.js](app.js) (small, and used only on interaction paths).
- **Lazy load at runtime ([app.js](app.js)), mirroring `ensureAliases`.** A module-level `let TOOLS = null` plus an `ensureTools()` that dynamic-imports `tools-data.js` once and caches it. The home `#tools` view is static HTML (`applyRoute` only unhides it), and `parseHashRoute` consults the id list only for a *tile* hash -- empty / `#home` / `#b=` route home without it -- so the new single `route()` resolves the home case synchronously and only `ensureTools()`-then-re-routes when the hash names a tile. `bindSearch` defers building its `nameToId` / `ALL` indexes (and the alias fold) to the first focus / keystroke via `loadAndRender()`, behind the same `ensureTools()` gate; `searchTools` returns `[]` until the indexes are ready. Net effect: the bare home view's first paint loads neither the catalog nor the aliases.
- **Why lazy, not a static import.** A static `import { TOOLS }` would shrink `app.js` *on disk* -- exactly what `check-home-payload`'s fixed `HOME_FILES` list measures -- without reducing the bytes the browser loads on first paint. That would game the gate. Only the lazy form is honest, and `HOME_FILES` keeps measuring `app.js` (now small) while honestly excluding the genuinely-deferred `tools-data.js`.
- **Repointed ~15 TOOLS parsers.** Build / lint scripts that `readFile` + regex the `{ id: ... }` shape now read `tools-data.js`: [build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) (its `loadGroupNames` still reads `app.js`), [build-tile-index.mjs](scripts/build-tile-index.mjs), [check-audit-trail.mjs](scripts/check-audit-trail.mjs), [check-citation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs) (its `loadCitations` still reads `citations.js`), [check-cross-validation.mjs](scripts/check-cross-validation.mjs), [check-derivation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-derivation-coverage.mjs), [check-discoverability.mjs](scripts/check-discoverability.mjs), [check-related-tiles.mjs](scripts/check-related-tiles.mjs), [check-shells.mjs](scripts/check-shells.mjs), [check-tile-meta.mjs](scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs), [check-worked-examples.mjs](scripts/check-worked-examples.mjs); and the test-side parsers [test/unit/tile-meta.test.js](test/unit/tile-meta.test.js), [test/unit/search-discovery.test.js](test/unit/search-discovery.test.js), [test/unit/citation-strings.test.js](test/unit/citation-strings.test.js), [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js) (group-comment splits), and [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js) (per-tile a11y route enumeration). Scripts that read `app.js` for *other* content (`check-wiring`'s `declare()` table, `spa-canonical-emission`'s head-helper assertions, `alias-autocomplete-wiring`'s bindSearch shape) keep reading `app.js`.
- **Plumbing.** [tools-data.js](tools-data.js) added to the [build.mjs](scripts/build.mjs) `FILES` copy list, the [sw.js](sw.js) `SHELL_ASSETS` precache, the [grep-checks.mjs](scripts/grep-checks.mjs) forbidden-pattern scan, and given an explicit **40 KB** cap in [check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) (the catalog registry, lazy-loaded once on first interaction -- not a per-tile module; built ~30 KB gzipped). Updated the `alias-autocomplete-wiring` focus-handler assertion to the new `loadAndRender` wiring.
- **Counts.** Unit tests **4,849** (unchanged; one alias-wiring assertion rewritten, not added). Build emits **423 tile shells / 24 group shells / 449 sitemap URLs**. The full Playwright integration suite (**463 tests**) confirms deep-link routing, Enter-to-route, the empty-catalog dropdown (still 423 results), result-click routing, and 320 px no-horizontal-scroll all resolve with the async catalog load. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: Y.2 linear regression landed (Educators statistics set complete) 2026-06-05

This stanza lands Y.2 `linear-regression`, completing the Educators statistics trio (Pearson Y.4, chi-square Y.3, regression Y.2). v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **422 -> 423 tiles** (Group Y +1).

- **[calc-edu.js](calc-edu.js) Y.2 `linear-regression`** (Least-Squares Linear Regression, OpenIntro Statistics Ch. 8). `computeLinearRegression` returns the fitted line (`slope = Sxy/Sxx`, `intercept = ybar - slope*xbar`), R^2 and Pearson r, the residual sum of squares and residual standard error (`sqrt(RSS/(n-2))`), the slope t-test for slope = 0 (`t = slope / (RSE/sqrt(Sxx))` on n-2 df, two-tailed p from `tcdf`), and an optional prediction (`y = intercept + slope*x`). It reuses the same Sxx/Sxy/Syy sums as Y.4 Pearson but reports the predictive *line* the correlation tile does not, and the slope t-test is identical to Y.4's correlation t-test on the same data (a cross-tile test pins this). Rejects a constant x series (undefined slope), mismatched lengths, and fewer than 3 pairs. Worked example pinned: x = 1..5, y = 2,4,5,4,5, predict at x = 6 -> **slope 0.6, intercept 2.2, R^2 0.6, RSE 0.894, y(6) = 5.8**.
- **Audit note.** With Pearson, chi-square, and regression landed, the genuinely-new Educators statistics surface is complete; Y.1 z-percentile remains covered by the existing `bell-curve-zscore`. The rest of what v17 drafts in the Aviation / EMS / Lab sections is already in the catalog and documented as covered, not duplicated.
- **Platform note: the §H.2 TOOLS extraction is now the mandatory next change.** After Y.2 the home-view JS sub-budget (spec-v10 §H.2) sits at **99.3%** (49,839 / 50,176 B, ~337 B of headroom) -- there is no room for another tile without it. The [spec-v17](specs/spec-v17.md) status header now carries the **fully-scoped, code-verified extraction design**: move the `TOOLS` array (~30 KB gzipped, the bulk of `app.js`) verbatim into a new `tools-data.js`; lazy-load it at runtime via an `ensureTools()` that mirrors the existing `ensureAliases` pattern (the home `#tools` view is static HTML and `parseHashRoute` consults the id list only for a *tile* hash, so the bare home view excludes the shard); and repoint the ~14 build / lint scripts that regex-parse the `{ id: ... }` shape from `app.js` to `tools-data.js`. A static import would shrink `app.js` on disk -- what `check-home-payload`'s fixed `HOME_FILES` list measures -- without reducing the bytes the browser loads, so only the lazy form is honest. It lands as its own dedicated commit because it is broad (one new file + async-routing surgery + ~14 script repoints) and the integration suite must confirm deep-link routing and search still resolve.
- **Full pipeline.** Y.2 lands with: a renderer registered into `EDU_RENDERERS` + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations on the new exports (713/713 at 100%); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (education governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 6 dedicated Y.2 tests appended to [test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js](test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,842 -> **4,849**. Build emits **423 tile shells / 24 group shells / 449 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 423/423; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 708 -> **710 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 711 -> **713** at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (422 -> 423) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 423 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: Y.3 chi-square goodness-of-fit landed + stale home-page tool-count SEO fixed 2026-06-05

This stanza lands Y.3 `chi-square-gof` (the second consumer of the §Z.4 special functions, using `chi2Cdf`) and fixes a stale, inconsistent tool count in the home page's crawler-facing metadata. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **421 -> 422 tiles** (Group Y +1).

- **[calc-edu.js](calc-edu.js) Y.3 `chi-square-gof`** (Chi-Square Goodness-of-Fit, OpenIntro Statistics Ch. 6 / Numerical Recipes 6.2). `computeChiSquareGof` returns the chi-square statistic (`sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)` with per-cell contributions), the degrees of freedom (k - 1), the p-value (`1 - chi2Cdf(chi2, df)`), and a reject / fail-to-reject verdict at alpha. The expected distribution is entered as counts or as proportions (scaled to the observed total); an expected count below 5 is flagged (Cochran's rule), and proportions not summing to 1 are normalized with a note. Rejects fewer than 2 categories, mismatched lengths, negative observed counts, and a non-positive expected. Worked example pinned: observed 10/20/30/40 against a uniform expectation -> **chi-square 20 on 3 df, p ~ 0.00017** (reject the uniform fit at 0.05).
- **Stale-SEO fix (issue found while reading the home bootstrapping).** The home page's tool count had drifted and gone inconsistent: [index.html](index.html) read **"404"** across the `<title>`, meta description, Open Graph, Twitter Card, and JSON-LD; the SPA's runtime `HOME_TITLE` / `HOME_DESC` in [app.js](app.js) read **"400"**. The live catalog is 422. Both were corrected to a durable **"420+ deterministic field-math tools"** / **"420+ Field-Math Tools"** that stays accurate as the catalog grows past 420, so this surface no longer needs touching every tile. (The runtime `HOME_TITLE` change is covered by the existing [test/unit/spa-canonical-emission.test.js](test/unit/spa-canonical-emission.test.js) shape assertion, which still passes.)
- **Audit note.** Y.3 is genuinely new (no chi-square tile existed). The remaining Educators statistics tile that would consume the helpers, Y.2 linear regression, and the Y.1 z-percentile (partly covered by the existing `bell-curve-zscore`) land in later batches. As in v16, most of what v17 drafts in the Aviation / EMS / Lab sections is already in the catalog and documented as covered, not duplicated.
- **Platform note (home-view payload).** The home-view JS sub-budget (spec-v10 §H.2) now sits at **99.1%** (49,743 / 50,176 B, ~433 B of headroom, roughly three more concise tiles). The §H.2 TOOLS-metadata extraction is the documented remediation and is now the near-term blocker for the next multi-tile batch. It must land as a **dedicated change**, not in-batch: the savings are honest only if `tools-data.js` is *lazy-loaded* (the bare home view routes / searches against TOOLS only on interaction, so it can be excluded) and the ~14 build / lint scripts that regex-parse the `{ id: ... }` shape are repointed from `app.js` to `tools-data.js`. A static import would shrink `app.js` on disk (which is what `check-home-payload`'s fixed `HOME_FILES` list measures) without reducing the bytes the browser actually loads -- i.e. it would game the gate -- so only the lazy form is acceptable.
- **Full pipeline.** Y.3 lands with: a renderer registered into `EDU_RENDERERS` + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations on the new exports (709/709 -> they regenerate at 100%); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (education governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 8 dedicated Y.3 tests appended to [test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js](test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,833 -> **4,842**. Build emits **422 tile shells / 24 group shells / 448 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 422/422; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 706 -> **708 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (421 -> 422) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 422 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: §Z.4 statistical special functions + Y.4 Pearson correlation landed 2026-06-05

This stanza lands the spec-v17 §Z.4 special-function platform work (shared numeric `erf` / incomplete-gamma / incomplete-beta and the normal / chi-square / Student-t CDFs in `pure-math.js`) and the first tile to consume it, Y.4 `pearson-correlation`. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0**. Catalog: **420 -> 421 tiles** (Group Y +1).

- **[pure-math.js](pure-math.js) §Z.4 statistical special functions.** Added `erf` (Abramowitz & Stegun 7.1.26, max abs error 1.5e-7), `normCdf` (= 0.5(1 + erf(z/sqrt2))), `gammaln` (Lanczos), `gammainc` (regularized lower incomplete gamma, Numerical Recipes 6.2 series / continued-fraction split), `chi2Cdf` (= gammainc(df/2, x/2)), `betainc` (regularized incomplete beta, Numerical Recipes 6.4), and `tcdf` (Student-t CDF from `betainc`). Each is verified against a published table value (erf(1) = 0.8427008; chi2Cdf at the 0.05 critical points 3.8415 / 5.9915 / 11.0705 = 0.95; tcdf at t_0.025 critical values 12.706 / 2.776 / 2.228 = 0.975) per the v14 §C.3 tolerance discipline. These are internal helpers, lazy-loaded with `pure-math.js`, so they add zero bytes to the home-view payload.
- **[calc-edu.js](calc-edu.js) Y.4 `pearson-correlation`** (Pearson Correlation Coefficient, OpenIntro Statistics Ch. 8 / Numerical Recipes 6.4). `computePearson` returns the Pearson r (`sum((x-xbar)(y-ybar)) / sqrt(sum(x-xbar)^2 sum(y-ybar)^2)`, clamped to [-1, 1]), R^2, the direction and a strength band, and a significance test for rho = 0: `t = r sqrt(n-2) / sqrt(1-r^2)` on n-2 df with a two-tailed p-value from the new `tcdf`. Paired x / y series are entered as comma/whitespace lists. Rejects fewer than 3 pairs, mismatched lengths, and a constant series. Worked example pinned: x = 1..5, y = 2,4,5,4,5 -> **r = 0.7746, R^2 = 0.6, t = 2.121 on 3 df, p ~ 0.124** (not significant at 0.05).
- **Audit note.** Y.1 (z-percentile) is partly covered by the existing `bell-curve-zscore` tile (which has its own local A&S 26.2.17 normal CDF); Y.4 is genuinely new (no correlation/regression tile existed). The remaining statistics tiles that consume the new helpers (Y.3 chi-square goodness-of-fit via `chi2Cdf`; Y.2 regression) land in later batches. As in v16, most of what v17 drafts in the Aviation / EMS / Lab sections is already in the catalog and documented as covered, not duplicated.
- **Platform note (home-view payload).** The home-view JS sub-budget (spec-v10 §H.2) is at ~99% (49,601 / 50,176 B) after this batch's single new TOOLS row. The §H.2 TOOLS-metadata extraction into a lazy-loaded shard is the documented remediation and must precede the next batch that adds several TOOLS rows; the §Z.4 helpers were deliberately chosen for this batch because they add capability without touching that budget.
- **Full pipeline.** Y.4 lands with: a renderer registered into `EDU_RENDERERS` + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); `// dims:` annotations on the new exports (all eight balance and parse, 709/709); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (education governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) rows for all eight new functions; published-table tests in [test/unit/pure-math.test.js](test/unit/pure-math.test.js); 11 dedicated Y.4 tests in the new [test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js](test/unit/calc-edu-v17.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) gave `pure-math.js` an explicit cap (was the 6 KB default; now **8.5 KB**, built ~7.0 KB) for the §Z.4 functions, and raised the `calc-edu.js` cap **26 -> 30 KB** (built ~25.1 KB) for the Pearson tile, both per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,813 -> **4,833**. Build emits **421 tile shells / 24 group shells / 447 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 421/421; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 697 -> **706 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 709/709. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (420 -> 421) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 421 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v17 OPEN: first Phase L (Agriculture) batch landed (L.1 irrigation requirement; L.3 cattle stocking rate; L.4 grain bin capacity) 2026-06-05

This stanza **opens spec-v17** (Allied-Profession Deepening, Part III of III) now that v16 has closed, and lands the first three genuinely-new agriculture tiles. v17 stays **open**, so the package version stays **0.16.0** (it stamps 0.17.0 at the v17 close). Catalog: **417 -> 420 tiles** (Group L 9 -> 12). All three live in [calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline. The tiles are pure first-principles math that bundles no new reference dataset and overlaps no existing tile.

- **[calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) L.1 `irrigation-requirement`** (Irrigation Requirement, ET-based, FAO 56 / USDA NRCS Irrigation Guide). `computeIrrigationRequirement` returns the crop ET demand (`ET_crop = Kc x ET0 x days`), the net depth after effective rainfall (`max(0, ET_crop - rain)`), the gross depth after application efficiency (`net / eff`), and the total acre-feet / gallons over the field (`gross_in x acres / 12`, `x 325,851 gal`). The reference ET0 is user-supplied from the local CIMIS / Mesonet / NOAA station; the FAO 56 Table 12 mid-season Kc values are inline (representative single values, the same inline-defaults pattern as the v16 C.6 pipe and D.5 nameplate tables -- no state-keyed ET shard is bundled in this batch). Worked example pinned: corn (Kc 1.20), 0.25 in/day ET0 over 30 days, 80 acres, 90% drip, 1 in rain -> **9.0 in ET, 8.889 in gross, 59.26 acre-ft**.
- **[calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) L.3 `cattle-stocking-rate`** (Cattle Stocking Rate / AUM, USDA NRCS National Range and Pasture Handbook Ch. 6). `computeStockingRate` returns the available forage (`production x area x utilization`), the animal-unit-months of carrying capacity (`available / 780 lb`), the head of the entered animal class supported for 30 days (`AUMs / AU-equivalent`), and the grazing days for a herd (`available / (herd x AU x 26 lb/day)`). AU equivalents: cow-calf 1.0, yearling 0.7, sheep 0.2, horse 1.25. Flags utilization above 60% (overgrazing). Worked example pinned: 160 acres, 1,500 lb/acre, 40% utilization, cow-calf, 30 head -> **96,000 lb, 123.08 AUMs, 123 grazing days**.
- **[calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js) L.4 `grain-bin-capacity`** (Grain Bin Capacity, bin geometry + USDA FGIS test weights). `computeGrainBin` returns the cylinder volume (`pi (d/2)^2 x eave`), the cone volume (`(1/3) pi (d/2)^2 x peak`), the total bushels (`ft^3 x 0.8036`, with a packing factor), and the weight by grain test weight (corn 56, wheat 60, soybeans 60, oats 32 lb/bu). Worked example pinned: 30 ft diameter, 20 ft eave, 8 ft cone, corn, free-flow -> **14,137 + 1,885 ft^3, ~12,875 bushels, 721,022 lb**.
- **Audit note (the same finding the v16 batches surfaced).** Much of what v17 drafts already exists in the live catalog and is documented as covered, not duplicated: Aviation W.1 (`top-of-descent`), W.2 (`weight-balance`), W.3 (`pressure-altitude`), W.4 (Mach via `true-airspeed`); EMS V.2 (anion gap), V.4 (`nihss`), V.5 (`wells-pe`), V.6 (`perc-rule`); Lab T.1 (`henderson-hasselbalch`), T.2 (`molarity-dilution`), T.3 (`beer-lambert`). The genuinely-new v17 surface concentrates in Group L (this batch plus L.2 / L.5), the Educators statistics tiles needing the §Z.4 special functions (Y.2 regression, Y.3 chi-square, Y.4 Pearson), and a subset of finance / tax / legal tiles (several needing §Z.5 state-keyed shards). These land in later v17 batches. Documented in the [spec-v17](specs/spec-v17.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a renderer registered into `AGRICULTURE_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no rendered data tables, so all three clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute; a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (general governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 21 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-agriculture-v17.test.js](test/unit/calc-agriculture-v17.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-agriculture.js` cap **12.5 -> 19 KB** (built module ~15.7 KB gzipped after the three tiles + the FAO 56 Kc table), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. The home-view JS sub-budget sits at 98.6% of its 49 KB ceiling after the three TOOLS rows; the spec-v10 §H.2 TOOLS-extraction into a lazy-loaded shard is the documented remediation before the next large batch.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,789 -> **4,813**. Build emits **420 tile shells / 24 group shells / 446 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 420/420; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 694 -> **697 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. Updated the Group L citation-id assertion (9 -> 12) in [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js) and the whole-catalog search assertion (417 -> 420) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 420 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the three new tiles at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 CLOSED: mechanical / plumbing / HVAC / water deep-dive complete; version stamped 0.16.0 2026-06-05

This stanza **closes spec-v16** (Mechanical / Plumbing / HVAC / Water deep-dive, Part II of III) and stamps the package version **0.15.0 -> 0.16.0** per the spec's close convention. Catalog at close: **417 tiles** (up from 400 at the v15 close). No code changes land in this stanza -- it is the version stamp plus the status finalization across [package.json](package.json), the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header, [README.md](README.md), and this changelog.

- **What shipped (16 genuinely-new tiles + 2 output extensions), 400 -> 417.** Group B: `water-heater-recovery` (B.1), `wh-expansion-tank` (B.2), `sanitary-dfu` (B.5), `trap-primer` (B.6), `backflow-sizing` (B.8), plus the B.3 annual-cost extension of `recirc-loop-sizing`. Group C: `chiller-tons` (C.3), `hx-lmtd-ntu` (C.5), `boiler-pipe-sizing` (C.6), `filter-pressure-drop` (C.7), `compressor-short-cycle` (C.8), `air-changes-hour` (C.9), `humidifier-capacity` (C.10), plus the C.4 efficiency-ratio extension of `cooling-tower`. Group D: `equipment-power-draw` (D.5). Group M: `pool-turnover` (N.1), `well-drawdown` (N.3), `cooling-water-makeup` (N.4), `chlorine-decay` (N.5). Each landed with full v14 discipline (dimensional annotation, bounds fuzzer, a worked example cross-checked against the cited reference, citation + tile-meta + related-tiles + search aliases, and a prerendered shell).
- **What was covered by existing tiles (documented, not duplicated).** B.4 storm-drain by `computeStormwaterRational` + `computeManningSlope`; C.1 duct fittings by `duct-friction-static`'s `C_o` data; D.1-D.4 by `air-movers` / `dehumidifier` / `nam-sizing` / `drying-times`; N.2 / N.7 by `filter-loading`; N.6 WSFU by the Hunter's-curve `pipe-sizing` tile.
- **What is deferred to its own reviewed change (per spec-v12 §H), not blocking the close.** Two tiles each bundle a genuinely external dataset that cannot be reconstructed first-principles without risk: **C.2 refrigerant line-set** (NIST REFPROP property tables) and **B.7 LP-gas vaporization** (NPGA wetted-area curve, safety-sensitive). They will land later as 0.16.x reviewed data changes. The §Z.4 per-group reviewer signoffs remain open and gate the "audited" announcement, not the close -- the same convention v15 closed under.
- **Version stamp rationale.** Per the [maintainer-quickstart](docs/maintainer-quickstart.md) semver policy ("minor for new tiles or new platform features") and the spec-v16 header ("package version stamps at 0.16.0 at the close of v16"), the v16 close is the minor-version stamp for the +17-tile mechanical expansion (400 -> 417). v17 (Allied-Profession Deepening, Part III of III) is the next frontier and stamps 0.17.0 at its own close. Nothing on the site displays the package version at runtime; the bump is a release marker only.
- **Verification.** No catalog-count, shell-count, test-count, or corpus change in this stanza (status + version only). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test` (4,789 unit tests), `npm run build` (417 tile shells / 24 group shells / 443 sitemap URLs), and `npm run data:verify` all green; the full Playwright suite stays green with no horizontal scroll at 320 px.

### spec-v16 OPEN: B.8 cross-connection backflow assembly sizing screen landed (reuses bundled curves, no new dataset) 2026-06-05

This stanza lands B.8 as a new Group B tile that **reuses the head-loss curves already bundled for the `backflow-loss` tile** rather than bundling the USC FCCCHR dataset the draft anticipated, and adds the genuinely-net-new hazard-override + residual-pressure + compliance screening. v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**. Catalog: **416 -> 417 tiles** (Group B 32 -> 33).

- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.8 `backflow-sizing`** (Backflow Assembly Sizing Screen, IPC 2021 §312 / AWWA M14 / EPA 40 CFR 141.85). `computeBackflowSizing` selects the required assembly from the hazard category -- a high (health) hazard forces a reduced-pressure principle (RP) assembly regardless of the user's selection (the tile overrides and records it; PVB / AVB are back-siphonage-only and never valid for a high hazard) -- interpolates the head loss at the design flow from the bundled Watts curves (reusing `computeBackflowLoss`), computes the downstream residual pressure (`upstream − head loss`) and flags it against a minimum (default 20 psi), and surfaces the EPA 40 CFR 141.85 / AWWA M14 annual-test reminder. The Watts curves are representative; the specific assembly's cut sheet and the USC FCCCHR approved-assembly list govern the actual loss. Worked example pinned: a high-hazard cross-connection, user picked a double-check, 2 in service at 100 GPM and 70 psi upstream -> **overridden to RP, 7.0 psi head loss, 63 psi downstream**.
- **Why this is honest first-principles work, not a new dataset.** The `backflow-loss` tile already bundles the assembly head-loss curves (`data/plumbing/backflow-curves.json`); B.8 reuses them and contributes the screening logic `backflow-loss` lacks (hazard-driven assembly selection, the RP override, the downstream-residual check, and the compliance reminder). No new reference dataset was added.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** With B.3 (recirc annual cost) and B.8 landed, every genuinely-new v16 tile the first-principles / extension / representative-defaults discipline can deliver has shipped. Only two external-dataset tiles remain, each landing as its own reviewed change per spec-v12 §H: C.2 refrigerant line-set (NIST REFPROP property tables) and B.7 LP-gas vaporization (NPGA wetted-area curve, held because it is a safety-sensitive dataset that cannot be reconstructed first-principles without risk). v16 is ready to close once a maintainer stamps 0.16.0. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** B.8 lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no rendered data tables, so it clears the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (plumbing corpus 58 -> **59**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (plumbing governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 10 dedicated unit tests in [test/unit/calc-plumbing-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-plumbing-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-plumbing.js` cap **42 -> 46 KB** (built module ~37.8 KB gzipped after B.8 + the B.3 extension), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,778 -> **4,789**. Build emits **417 tile shells / 24 group shells / 443 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 417/417; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 693 -> **694 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (416 -> 417) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 417 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: B.3 recirculation annual-cost extension landed (final genuinely-new v16 contribution) 2026-06-04

This stanza lands B.3 as an **extension of the existing `recirc-loop-sizing` tile** (not a new tile, the same pattern as the C.4 cooling-tower efficiency output), and records that v16's first-principles / extension / representative-defaults work is now complete. v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**. Catalog count unchanged at **416** (B.3 adds an output to an existing tile).

- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.3 recirculation annual energy cost (extension).** `computeRecircLoopSizing` now also returns the annual standing-loss energy and cost: a continuously-recirculated hot-water loop loses `Q_total` BTU/hr, which the heater replaces at its efficiency. `annual_loss_btu = Q_total x runtime_hr`; `annual_energy_units = annual_loss_btu / (100,000 BTU/therm gas OR 3,412 BTU/kWh electric) / heater_efficiency`; `annual_cost = units x fuel_price`. The fuel price is optional, so the cost renders only when supplied (and is `null` otherwise -- a `Number(null) === 0` guard bug was caught and fixed during the build so a missing price reports no cost rather than `$0`). Defaults: natural gas, 0.80 efficiency, 8,760 hr continuous. Worked example: the spec-v9 200 ft / 3/4 in / 1 in-insulation loop (1,870 BTU/hr) at continuous gas and $1.50/therm -> **205 therms/yr, ~$307/yr**; the same loop on electric resistance at $0.15/kWh -> **4,801 kWh/yr, ~$720/yr**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** With B.3 landed, every genuinely-new v16 tile the first-principles / extension / representative-defaults discipline can deliver has shipped. C.1 duct-fitting equivalent-length is covered two ways already (the pipe-oriented `equivalent-length` tile carries elbows / tees / gate-ball-check valves; duct fittings live in `duct-friction-static`'s `C_o` data), so there is no clean extension to make. B.4 storm-drain is covered by `computeStormwaterRational` + `computeManningSlope`; N.6 WSFU by the Hunter's-curve `pipe-sizing` tile; B.8's hydraulic piece by `backflow-loss`. Only the external-dataset tiles remain (C.2 line-set / NIST REFPROP; B.7 LP-gas / NPGA; B.8 backflow / USC FCCCHR), which land as their own reviewed changes per spec-v12 §H. v16 is ready to close once a maintainer stamps 0.16.0. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full discipline.** The extension updated the renderer (fuel / efficiency / runtime / price inputs + an annual-cost output line), the inline citation, the [docs/citation-discipline.md](docs/citation-discipline.md) markdown stamp and its regenerated [docs/citation-strings.generated.json](docs/citation-strings.generated.json) artifact (the citation-runtime-alignment audit holds these verbatim-equal), 4 new unit tests in [test/unit/calc-plumbing-v9.test.js](test/unit/calc-plumbing-v9.test.js), and the [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) recirc row. The `// dims:` annotation and the worked-example fixture were unaffected (new outputs are additive).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,775 -> **4,778**. No catalog-count, shell-count, or corpus-count change (B.3 is an output on an existing tile). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green; the full Playwright suite stays green with no horizontal scroll at 320 px.

### spec-v16 OPEN: C.7 filter pressure-drop schedule landed + recurring print-test flake fixed 2026-06-04

This stanza lands C.7, the HVAC filter pressure-drop and fan-energy tile, as a first-principles calculator (the site's "compute the physics, never reproduce the licensed table" rule is what makes that honest), and fixes a recurring flaky integration test. v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**. Catalog: **415 -> 416 tiles** (Group C 39 -> 40).

- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.7 `filter-pressure-drop`** (Filter Pressure Drop and Fan-Energy Penalty, ASHRAE 52.2-2017 + manufacturer cut sheets). `computeFilterPressureDrop` returns the airflow (`face area x face velocity`), the clean and change-out pressure drop by MERV 8 / 11 / 13 / 16 / HEPA class (velocity-scaled from a 300 fpm reference, `dp(V) = dp_ref x V / 300`, with user-overridable cut-sheet defaults inline), the fan power at each drop via the brake-HP identity (`kW = CFM x dp_in_wc / 6356 / fan total efficiency x 0.7457`), and the annual fan energy (average drop over a linear loading cycle) plus the loading penalty over a clean filter and an optional dollar cost. The clean/change-out drops are representative cut-sheet values (the MERV rating is defined by ASHRAE 52.2, but the cut sheet, not the test method, publishes the drop) -- the same representative-defaults pattern as the C.6 pipe-dimension table and the D.5 nameplate-amps table. The dust-loading change *interval* (which depends on the site dust load) is honestly omitted. Worked example pinned: MERV 13, 4 ft^2 at 300 fpm -> **1,200 CFM, 0.35 -> 0.70 in WC, 0.082 kW clean fan power**.
- **CI reliability: fixed the recurring print-emulation flake.** The spec-v10 print-parity audit ([test/integration/print.test.js](test/integration/print.test.js)) loaded each tile, clicked "Test with example", switched the page to `print` media, then read `#view-region .citation` via `innerText`. Because `innerText` is layout-aware, reading it on the same tick that `emulateMedia('print')` triggers a style/layout recompute was an intermittent empty read (observed twice: `fire-friction`, then `ohms-law`, each retried green). The four DOM-content invariants now use Playwright's retrying web-first assertions (`toHaveText(/\S/)` and `not.toHaveCount(0)`), which match normalized `textContent` and auto-retry until stable -- removing the print-emulation race while keeping the same invariants.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** This closes every genuinely-new v16 tile the first-principles / representative-defaults discipline can land without reproducing a licensed table. Every remaining v16 tile either bundles a genuinely external dataset that lands as its own reviewed change per spec-v12 §H (C.2 refrigerant line-set / NIST REFPROP; B.7 LP-gas / NPGA wetted-area curve; B.8 backflow / USC FCCCHR assembly head-loss) or substantially overlaps an existing tile (B.4 storm-drain by `computeStormwaterRational` + `computeManningSlope`; N.6 WSFU by the Hunter's-curve `pipe-sizing` tile; B.3 / C.1 are extensions of `recirc-loop-sizing` / `equivalent-length`). Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** C.7 lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `HVAC_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no rendered data tables, so it clears the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (HVAC corpus 60 -> **61**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (mechanical governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 10 dedicated unit tests appended to [test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,764 -> **4,775**. Build emits **416 tile shells / 24 group shells / 442 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 416/416; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 692 -> **693 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. `calc-hvac.js` built ~50.2 KB gzipped (88% of the 57 KB cap; no bump needed). Updated the whole-catalog search assertion (415 -> 416) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 416 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: Group D / C.4 loose-ends batch landed (D.5 equipment power draw vs circuit capacity; C.4 cooling-tower efficiency ratio) 2026-06-04

This stanza closes every genuinely-new no-new-dataset v16 tile by landing D.5, the one genuinely-new Group D tile, and the C.4 cooling-tower efficiency output (the spec's stated "minor extension" of the existing `cooling-tower` tile). v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**. Catalog: **414 -> 415 tiles** (Group D 15 -> 16; C.4 is an output added to an existing tile, not a new tile).

- **[calc-restoration.js](calc-restoration.js) D.5 `equipment-power-draw`** (Equipment Power Draw vs Circuit Capacity, NEC 2023 210.20(A)). `computeEquipmentCircuitLoad` sums the continuous amperage from LGR-dehumidifier / air-mover / HEPA-scrubber / heat-dryer counts using representative cut-sheet nameplate defaults (8.5 / 2.5 / 3.5 / 12 A at 120 V; inline and user-overridable, not a fixed reference table), applies the NEC 210.20(A) continuous-load limit (a continuous load must not exceed 80% of the breaker rating), and reports the number of 15 / 20 / 30 A circuits required (`ceil(total / (0.8 x breaker))`), the total VA, and a flag for any single unit larger than its own circuit's continuous limit. Optional other-load amperage covers gear not in the list. Worked example pinned: 4 air movers + 1 LGR dehumidifier on 20 A / 120 V -> **18.5 A, 16 A continuous limit, 2 circuits, 2,220 VA**.
- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.4 cooling-tower efficiency (extension, not a new tile).** `computeCoolingTower` now also returns `efficiency = range / (range + approach) = range / (T_in - T_wb)`, the thermal efficiency toward the wet-bulb floor (CTI ATC-105). The renderer surfaces it as a percentage and the citation, worked-example fixture, and v7 unit tests were updated. Per the spec-v16 audit, C.4 is "substantially covered by the existing cooling-tower tile; the only net-new output is the efficiency ratio," so it extends that tile rather than shipping a duplicate. The example (95 / 85 / 78 F) reads **58.8%**; the fixture pins the 95 / 85 / 75 F case at exactly **0.5**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** This closes every genuinely-new no-new-dataset v16 tile. Every remaining v16 tile either bundles an external reference dataset that lands as its own reviewed change per spec-v12 §H (B.4 storm-drain, B.7 LP-gas, B.8 backflow, C.2 refrigerant line-set, C.7 filter pressure-drop, N.6 WSFU service sizing) or extends an existing tile (B.3 into `recirc-loop-sizing`, C.1 into `equivalent-length`); N.6 also substantially overlaps the existing Hunter's-curve `pipe-sizing` tile. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** D.5 lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `RESTORATION_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no rendered data tables, so it clears the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (restoration corpus 27 -> **28**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (electrical governance, since it is an NEC check); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 11 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-restoration-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-restoration-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json). The C.4 efficiency output added one v7 unit test and an `efficiency` key to the cooling-tower worked-example fixture.
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-restoration.js` cap **15.5 -> 19 KB** (built module ~16.0 KB gzipped after D.5), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. The home-view JS sub-budget held at 49 KB (97.3% after the D.5 TOOLS row). Per spec-v10 §H.1 / §H.2 the per-tile module split and the TOOLS-extraction shard stay the preferred long-term remediations.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,751 -> **4,764**. Build emits **415 tile shells / 24 group shells / 441 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 415/415; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 691 -> **692 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. Updated the Group D citation-id assertion (15 -> 16) in [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js) and the whole-catalog search assertion (414 -> 415) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 415 across [README.md](README.md) and the regenerated [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md). Verified the new tile at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: second Group C (HVAC) first-principles batch landed (C.6 boiler distribution pipe sizing; C.8 compressor short-cycle protection; C.10 humidifier capacity) 2026-06-04

This stanza continues spec-v16 (Mechanical / Plumbing / HVAC / Water deep-dive, Part II of III) by landing the remaining three genuinely-new Group C tiles: the first-principles HVAC math that bundles no new reference dataset. This **closes the Group C first-principles set** (the only undelivered v16 HVAC tiles, C.2 refrigerant line-set and C.7 filter pressure-drop, each bundle a new reference dataset and land as their own reviewed change). v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**; it stamps 0.16.0 only at the v16 close. Catalog: **411 -> 414 tiles** (Group C 36 -> 39). All three live in [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline.

- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.6 `boiler-pipe-sizing`** (Hot Water Boiler Distribution Pipe Sizing, ASHRAE Systems and Equipment 2020 Ch. 13 / Hazen-Williams). `computeBoilerPipeSizing` derives the hydronic flow (`GPM = Q / (500 x delta-T)`), then picks the smallest standard inner diameter whose velocity (`v = GPM / (2.448 x d^2)`) is at or below the material ceiling (copper Type L 4 ft/s, steel Sch 40 6 ft/s, PEX SDR-9 3 ft/s; ASTM B88 / A53 / F876 dimensions bundled inline), and reports the Hazen-Williams head loss per 100 ft (C = 150 copper/PEX, 130 steel) and the pump head at the run length. **Audit refinement (documented, not a silent skip):** the §C.6 draft's "1.25 in copper" worked example sizes to **1-1/2 in** under the spec's own stated 4 ft/s copper ceiling (the 1.265 in ID runs 5.11 ft/s at 20 GPM), so the implementation follows the velocity-limited method and the 1-1/2 in result. Worked example pinned: 200,000 BTU/hr, 20 F delta-T, copper, 4 ft/s -> **20 GPM, 1-1/2 in Type L at 3.61 ft/s, 1.48 ft head/100 ft**.
- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.8 `compressor-short-cycle`** (Compressor Short-Cycle Protection Minimum Runtime, Copeland AE Bulletin 17-1226 / ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021). `computeCompressorShortCycle` applies the ASHRAE/AHRI part-load cycling parabola (`N = N_max x 4 x X x (1 - X)`, X = runtime fraction, peaking at 50% load) to estimate cycles/hr and on/off time, and checks them against the minimum oil-return runtime and pressure-equalization off-time by system type (single-stage 10/5 min, 6 cph; two-stage 8/5 min, 8 cph; VRF/inverter modulates with no fixed ceiling).
