It flags the classic oversized single-stage that drops below the 10-min runtime, and an observed cycles/hr above the ceiling. Worked example pinned: single-stage at 50% load -> **6 cph, 5 min on / 5 min off, short-cycling flagged below the 10-min oil-return runtime**.
- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.10 `humidifier-capacity`** (Humidifier Capacity from RH Target, ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch. 1). `computeHumidifierCapacity` reuses the v9 psychrometric helpers to convert the entering-to-target RH rise into the required moisture addition (`60 x CFM x rho x (W_target - W_entering)`, with `W = 0.621945 x Pw / (P - Pw)`, `Pw = RH x Pws(T)`, the altitude-corrected pressure, and ideal-gas air density), the daily water (`lb/hr x 24 / 8.34`), and the latent load (`addition x 1061 BTU/lb`). Rejects a target at or below the entering RH; warns above 60% RH (condensation). Worked example pinned: 1,000 CFM, 70 F, 20 -> 40% RH at sea level -> **13.99 lb/hr, 40.3 gal/day, 14,843 BTU/hr latent**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** This batch closes the Group C first-principles set. The two remaining v16 HVAC tiles each bundle a new reference dataset and land separately per spec-v12 §H: C.2 refrigerant line-set sizing (NIST REFPROP property tables + manufacturer line-sizing charts) and C.7 filter pressure-drop schedule (ASHRAE 52.2 published curves). The earlier-deferred C.1 (covered by `equivalent-length`) and C.4 (covered by `cooling-tower`) remain extensions of existing tiles. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `HVAC_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no rendered data tables, so all three clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (HVAC corpus 57 -> **60**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (mechanical governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 24 dedicated unit tests appended to [test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and four search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-hvac.js` cap **48 -> 57 KB** (built module ~47.6 KB gzipped after the three tiles and the C.6 pipe-dimension tables), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. The home-view JS sub-budget held at 49 KB (app.js gzipped ~45.3 KB after the three TOOLS rows, 96.9% of sub-budget). Per spec-v10 §H.1 / §H.2 the per-tile module split and the TOOLS-extraction shard stay the preferred long-term remediations.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,724 -> **4,751**. Build emits **414 tile shells / 24 group shells / 440 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 414/414; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 688 -> **691 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations regenerated at 100%. Updated the hardcoded whole-catalog search assertion (411 -> 414) in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 414 across [README.md](README.md) and the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated). Verified the three new tiles at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: first Group M (Water / Wastewater) first-principles batch landed (N.1 pool turnover; N.3 well drawdown; N.4 cooling water makeup; N.5 chlorine residual decay); CI Actions bumped to Node 24 2026-06-03

This stanza continues spec-v16 (Mechanical / Plumbing / HVAC / Water deep-dive, Part II of III) by landing the first four genuinely-new Group M tiles: the pure first-principles small-system-water-operator math that bundles no new reference dataset. v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**; it stamps 0.16.0 only at the v16 close. Catalog: **407 -> 411 tiles** (Group M 9 -> 13). All four live in [calc-water.js](calc-water.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline. (The spec §5 labels this group "N," but the live catalog uses Group **M** for water/wastewater operators; the tiles register under M per the spec-v16 group-letter note.)

- **[calc-water.js](calc-water.js) N.1 `pool-turnover`** (Pool Turnover Rate and Chlorine Demand, NSPF CPO Handbook / ANSI/APSP/ICC 11). `computePoolTurnover` returns the required pump flow (`GPM = volume / (turnover hr x 60)`) and the chlorine product to dose the volume to a free-chlorine target (`lb = volume x ppm x 8.34 / 1,000,000 / available-chlorine fraction`; cal-hypo 65% / trichlor 90% / liquid bleach 12.5%). Rejects a closure-threshold target above 10 ppm; flags turnover above 24 hr. Worked example pinned: 20,000 gal, 6 hr, 2 ppm cal-hypo -> **55.6 GPM, 0.513 lb cal-hypo**.
- **[calc-water.js](calc-water.js) N.3 `well-drawdown`** (Well Drawdown and Specific Capacity, AWWA A100 / USGS OFR 02-197). `computeWellDrawdown` returns `drawdown = pumping - static level`, `specific capacity = discharge / drawdown` (GPM/ft), and a recommended pump-setting depth (`pumping level + offset`, default 20 ft), flagging a marginal well below 0.5 GPM/ft and rejecting a pumping level above the static level. Worked example pinned: static 50 ft, pumping 80 ft, 30 GPM -> **30 ft drawdown, 1.0 GPM/ft, 100 ft setting**.
- **[calc-water.js](calc-water.js) N.4 `cooling-water-makeup`** (Cooling Water Makeup from Cycles of Concentration, CTI / ASHRAE Systems and Equipment 2020 Ch. 40). `computeCoolingWaterMakeup` returns `evaporation = recirc x delta-T / 1000`, `blowdown = evaporation / (COC - 1)`, `drift = recirc x drift fraction`, and `makeup = evaporation + blowdown + drift`, flagging COC above 10 (scaling) and drift above 0.5% (deficient eliminator) and rejecting COC <= 1. Worked example pinned: 1,000 GPM, 10 F range, COC 4, drift 0.002 -> **10 / 3.33 / 2 GPM, 15.33 GPM makeup**.
- **[calc-water.js](calc-water.js) N.5 `chlorine-decay`** (Chlorine Residual Decay, EPA 815-R-02-020 / AWWA M14). `computeChlorineDecay` returns the first-order residual `C(t) = C0 x exp(-k t)`, the time to reach a target residual (`ln(C0/target) / k`), and an optional booster-chlorination distance from a distribution velocity. Flags a decay constant above 0.5 1/hr. Worked example pinned: C0 2.0 mg/L, k 0.1 1/hr, t 10 hr -> **0.736 mg/L; 23.0 hr to 0.2 mg/L**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** The entire Group D restoration set is substantially covered by tiles that already shipped: D.1 air-mover quantity by `air-movers`, D.2 dehumidifier GPP load by `dehumidifier`, D.3 HEPA-scrubber containment ACH by `nam-sizing` / `containment-air-balance`, and D.4 drying-time by `drying-times`; only D.5 (equipment power draw vs circuit capacity) is genuinely new and is held for a later batch. Within Group M, N.2 backwash and N.7 filter media depth are partly covered by `filter-loading`; N.6 WSFU water-service sizing remains drafted (bundles the public Hunter's-curve table as its own reviewed change). Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **CI housekeeping.** Bumped `actions/checkout` and `actions/setup-node` **v4 -> v5** across [ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml), [data-refresh.yml](.github/workflows/data-refresh.yml), and [data-refresh-weekly.yml](.github/workflows/data-refresh-weekly.yml). The v4 actions run on the deprecated Node 20 action runtime (GitHub removes it 2026-09-16); v5 runs on Node 24. The installed Node for the build/test steps stays `node-version: 20`.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `WATER_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no data tables, so all four clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (water corpus 9 -> **13**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (water governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 30 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-water-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-water-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three-plus search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-water.js` cap **11 -> 18 KB** (built module ~15.6 KB gzipped). [scripts/check-home-payload.mjs](scripts/check-home-payload.mjs) raised the home-view JS sub-budget **47 -> 49 KB** (app.js gzipped at ~45 KB after the seven v16 Group C + M TOOLS rows); the M-tile descriptions were trimmed to keep the rise modest. Per spec-v10 §H.1 / §H.2 the TOOLS-extraction into a lazy-loaded shard remains the preferred long-term remediation.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,689 -> **4,724**. Build emits **411 tile shells / 24 group shells / 437 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 411/411; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 684 -> **688 functions** at 100%; dimensional annotations 691/691; citation coverage 357/411 tracked (86.9%). Updated the hardcoded Group M coverage assertion (9 -> 13) in [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js). Tile-count copy updated to 411 across [README.md](README.md) and the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated). Verified the four new tiles at 320 px (0 px horizontal overflow) with 0 serious/critical axe violations. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: first Group C (HVAC) first-principles batch landed (C.3 chiller tonnage; C.5 heat-exchanger LMTD/effectiveness-NTU; C.9 air changes per hour) 2026-06-02

This stanza continues spec-v16 (Mechanical / Plumbing / HVAC / Water deep-dive, Part II of III) by landing the first three genuinely-new Group C tiles: the pure first-principles HVAC math that bundles no new reference dataset. v16 remains **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**; it stamps 0.16.0 only at the v16 close. Catalog: **404 -> 407 tiles** (Group C 33 -> 36). All three live in [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline.

- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.3 `chiller-tons`** (Chiller Tonnage from Delta-T and GPM, ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch. 31). `computeChillerTons` returns `Q (BTU/hr) = GPM x factor x delta-T`, `tons = Q / 12000`, and `kW = Q / 3412`, where the water factor is the textbook `500 = 60 min/hr x 8.33 lb/gal x 1 BTU/lb-F` and the 30% / 50% propylene-glycol factors (475 / 449) are property-derived (the manufacturer's fluid table governs). An optional nameplate-tons input inverts the identity to the required flow. Flags delta-T outside the typical 10-14 F band. Worked example pinned: 240 GPM water, 54 -> 44 F -> **exactly 100 tons** (1,200,000 BTU/hr).
- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.5 `hx-lmtd-ntu`** (Heat Exchanger LMTD and Effectiveness-NTU, TEMA standards / Incropera). `computeHxLmtdNtu` returns the log-mean temperature difference (`LMTD = (dT1 - dT2) / ln(dT1/dT2)`, with the dT1 -> dT2 limit handled), the heat duty (`Q = C x delta-T`, `C = GPM x fluid factor`), the required `UA = Q / LMTD`, the effectiveness (`Q / (C_min x (Th_in - Tc_in))`), the NTU (`UA / C_min`), and the capacity-rate ratio, for counter-flow or parallel-flow. Thermodynamically impossible cases (hot warms up, cold cools down, cold outlet above hot inlet, parallel outlets crossing) are rejected. Worked example pinned: counter-flow water/water, hot 200 -> 100 F at 50 GPM, cold 60 -> 140 F at 62.5 GPM -> **LMTD 49.33 F, effectiveness 0.714, NTU 2.03**.
- **[calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) C.9 `air-changes-hour`** (Air Changes per Hour, ASHRAE 62.1-2022 / 170-2021). `computeAirChangesPerHour` returns `ACH = supply CFM x 60 / volume`, the net delivered ACH from `min(supply, return)`, the pressurization airflow (`supply - return`, positive = pressurized) with a balanced / positive / negative label, and a comparison against the selected occupancy target band (residential 0.35-1, classroom 4-6, lab 6-12, operating room 20-25). Flags volume below 100 ft^3 and ACH above 50. Worked example pinned: 10,000 ft^3 classroom at 1,000 CFM -> **6.0 ACH, within the 4-6 band**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** C.1 duct-fitting equivalent-length is substantially covered by the existing `equivalent-length` tile (spec-v2), which already sums equivalent feet from fitting type and diameter counts; C.1 would extend that tile's fitting library, not ship a duplicate. C.4 cooling-tower range/approach is substantially covered by the existing `cooling-tower` tile (spec-v7), which already reports range, approach, heat rejection, and fan kW/ton against the 5-10 F / 8-12 F bands; the only net-new output C.4 proposed (the efficiency ratio) is a minor extension of that tile. C.2 / C.6 / C.7 / C.8 / C.10 remain drafted for a later v16 batch. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `HVAC_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry and precache-list id in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no data tables, so all three clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (HVAC corpus 54 -> **57**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (mechanical governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 31 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-hvac-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three-plus search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-hvac.js` cap **41 -> 48 KB** (built module ~42.3 KB gzipped after the three tiles), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile module split stays the preferred long-term remediation.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,655 -> **4,689**. Build emits **407 tile shells / 24 group shells / 433 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 407/407; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 681 -> **684 functions** at 100%; citation coverage 353/407 tracked (86.7%). Tile-count copy updated to 407 across [README.md](README.md) and the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v16 OPEN: first Group B (Plumbing) batch landed (B.1 water-heater recovery; B.2 thermal expansion tank; B.5 sanitary DFU sizing; B.6 trap primer) 2026-06-02

This stanza opens spec-v16 (Mechanical / Plumbing / HVAC / Water deep-dive, Part II of III) by landing the first four genuinely-new Group B tiles. v16 is **open, not closed**, so the package version stays **0.15.0**; it stamps 0.16.0 only at the v16 close. Catalog: **400 -> 404 tiles** (Group B 28 -> 32). All four live in [calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) and ship with full spec-v14 discipline.

- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.1 `water-heater-recovery`** (Water Heater Recovery Rate, DOE 10 CFR 430 / AHRI 1300). `computeWaterHeaterRecovery` returns `gph = (input BTU/hr x recovery efficiency) / (8.33 x delta-T)` (electric input = `kW x 3412`), the DOE first-hour rating (`recovery + 0.70 x stored gallons`), and the useful BTU/hr. Recovery-efficiency defaults by type (0.98 electric / 0.80 atmospheric gas / 0.94 condensing gas); the nameplate overrides. Flags rise outside 20-130 F and set points above 140 F (scald). Worked example pinned: 40,000 BTU/hr atmospheric gas, 0.80 efficiency, 50->120 F, 40 gal -> **54.9 gph recovery, 82.9 gph FHR**.
- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.2 `wh-expansion-tank`** (Water Heater Thermal Expansion Tank, ASPE PEDH Ch. 6 / ASME B40.1). `computeWhExpansionTank` derives the closed-system expansion volume from steam-table densities (`factor = (rho_cold - rho_hot) / rho_hot`, `V_exp = heater volume x factor`), divides by the manufacturer acceptance factor (default 0.46) for the required tank volume, recommends the smallest standard diaphragm tank (2 / 4.4 / 8.5 / 14 / 20 gal), and sets the pre-charge to incoming pressure. Flags incoming pressure above 80 psi (IPC 604.8 PRV creates the closed system). Worked example pinned: 40 gal, 60 psi, 50->120 F, acceptance 0.46 -> **0.45 gal expansion, 0.99 gal required, 2 gal tank**.
- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.5 `sanitary-dfu`** (Sanitary Drain DFU Sizing, IPC 2021 §710). `computeSanitaryDfu` sums drainage fixture units from a fixture list (IPC Table 709.1 values) and returns the minimum pipe size for a horizontal branch or stack (Table 710.1(2)) or a slope-aware building drain (Table 710.1(1)), with an undersized-pipe flag against a proposed size. Flags loads above 1400 DFU (commercial-engineered) and sub-minimum slope. Worked example pinned: single-bath branch (1 private WC + 1 lavatory + 1 bathtub = 6 DFU) -> **2 in minimum**.
- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js) B.6 `trap-primer`** (Trap Primer Sizing, IPC 2021 §1002.4). `computeTrapPrimer` returns `primers = ceil(floor drains / drains-per-distribution-unit)` (electronic / pressure-drop / pump-discharge feed up to 4 drains, manual serves 1), the annual water use (`drains x (oz per cycle / 128) x cycles/day x 365`), and an occupied-space compliance check (manual prime in occupied space is non-compliant). Worked example pinned: 6 occupied electronic drains, 8 oz/cycle daily -> **2 units, 136.9 gal/yr, compliant**.
- **Audit findings (deferrals, not silent skips).** B.3 domestic recirculation heat-loss is substantially covered by the existing `recirc-loop-sizing` tile (same ASPE Ch. 6 per-foot method); it will extend that tile's annual-cost output rather than duplicate it. B.4 storm drain, B.7 LP-gas vaporization, and B.8 cross-connection backflow each require bundling a new reference dataset (IPC Appendix B rainfall shard, NPGA vaporization curve, USC FCCCHR head-loss curves) and land as their own reviewed changes. Documented in the [spec-v16](specs/spec-v16.md) status header, which also records the **Group-letter correction**: §5 labels the water/wastewater group "N," but the live catalog uses Group **M** for it (Group N is stage / AV); the v16 N.x tiles will register under M.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a non-exported renderer registered into `PLUMBING_RENDERERS` by id + a TOOLS registry entry in [app.js](app.js); a renderer built from the shared `ui-fields.js` `make*` helpers (no data tables, so all four clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (plumbing corpus 54 -> **58**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (plumbing governance); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 38 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-plumbing-v16.test.js](test/unit/calc-plumbing-v16.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three-plus search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-plumbing.js` cap **31.5 -> 42 KB** (built module ~34.8 KB gzipped after the four tiles), per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. The home-view JS sub-budget held (the four new TOOLS rows are small). Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile module split stays the preferred long-term remediation.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,612 -> **4,655**. Build emits **404 tile shells / 24 group shells / 430 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 404/404; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 677 -> **681 functions** at 100%; citation coverage 351/404 tracked (86.9%). Tile-count copy updated to 404 across [README.md](README.md), the [mobile-responsive doc](docs/mobile-responsive.md), and the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated). `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v15 CLOSED: final four tiles landed (E.7 header sizing; E.8 deck beam/post; F.2 standpipe pump pressure; F.5 smoke ejector CFM); version stamped 0.15.0 2026-06-01

This stanza lands the last four genuinely-missing v15 tiles, **closing Groups E and F and the full 35-tile v15 set**. Per spec-v15 the package version stamps **0.12.0 -> 0.15.0** at the v15 close. Catalog: **396 -> 400 tiles** (Group E 41 -> 43, Group F 20 -> 22). The §H.6 reviewer signoffs remain open and gate the "audited" announcement, not the landing.

- **[calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js) E.7 `header-sizing`** (Window / Door Header Sizing, IRC R602.7). `computeHeaderSizing` assembles the tributary uniform load (`w = (snow + 15 dead) x trib`, plus 50 psf per floor above), then searches built-up dimension lumber ply-outer / depth-inner (prefer a deeper double over a triple) for the smallest member whose `min(L_bending, L_deflection) >= span`, where the AWC NDS bending value is adjusted by the load-duration factor C_D (1.15 snow / 1.0 occupancy) and the size factor C_F. Reports the NDS bending (`f_b = M/S`) and L/360 deflection verification and the IRC R602.7.5 jack-stud count (`ceil(end reaction / (F_c-perp x 5.25 in^2))`). Cross-checked against IRC Table R602.7(1) by physics; discrepancies flagged. Worked example pinned: 6 ft span, 14 ft tributary, roof-only, 30 psf snow, SPF #2 -> 630 plf -> **(2) 2x10**, 1 jack stud, bending + deflection OK.
- **[calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js) E.8 `deck-beam-post`** (Deck Beam and Post Sizing, IRC R507). `computeDeckBeamPost` takes tributary = joist span / 2, beam load `w = (live + dead) x trib`, and sizes the smallest built-up beam for the post spacing; the interior-post axial load (`w x post spacing`) drives an AWC NDS column check (`F_cE = 0.822 E_min / (le/d)^2`, `C_P` stability factor, c = 0.8) that picks a 4x4 or 6x6; the post load drives a footing from the E.1 soil-bearing engine; and the ledger fastener spacing follows the public IRC Table R507.9.1.3(1). Flags a walking surface above 30 in (R312 guardrail). Worked example pinned: 12 ft joist span, 8 ft post spacing, 40+10 psf, SYP #2, 8 ft posts on clay -> 300 plf -> **(2) 2x8 beam, 4x4 post, 18 in footing, ledger at 15 in OC**.
- **[calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js) F.2 `standpipe-pdp`** (Standpipe Pump Discharge Pressure, NFPA 14). `computeStandpipePDP` implements `PDP = nozzle pressure + supply-hose friction (NFA CQ^2L) + appliance loss + elevation (0.434 psi/ft)`, with a below-pumper (negative) elevation subtracting. Flags buildings over 75 ft (high-rise, NFPA 14 §7.10), lifts over 600 ft (beyond single-pumper capability), and a PDP over 350 psi (apparatus/hose ratings). Worked example pinned: 250 GPM at 100 psi, 110 ft above pumper, 25 psi appliance, 200 ft of 3 in supply -> **181.2 psi** (47.74 elevation + 8.46 supply FL + 25 appliance + 100 nozzle).
- **[calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js) F.5 `smoke-ejector-cfm`** (Smoke Ejector / Negative-Pressure Ventilation CFM, NFPA 1500 / IFSTA). `computeSmokeEjector` returns `CFM_required = volume x ACH / 60`, `fans = ceil(required / per-fan CFM)`, time to one air change (`volume / actual CFM`), and the exhaust-to-entry opening ratio (1:1 to 1.5:1 PPV best practice). Flags rooms over 100,000 ft^3 and opening ratios outside 0.5-2.0. Worked example pinned: 30 x 40 x 10 ft (12,000 ft^3), 5 ACH, 4,000 CFM fan, 12/10 ft^2 openings -> **1,000 CFM, 1 fan, 3 min/change, 1.2:1 ratio**.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a renderer-dispatch (`CONSTRUCTION_RENDERERS` / `FIRE_RENDERERS`) + TOOLS registry entry in [app.js](app.js); a renderer with the shared `make*` field helpers (no data tables, so the four clear the 320 px mobile floor by construction); `// dims:` SI annotations on the exported computes (construction corpus 62 -> **64**, fire corpus 34 -> **36**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp (structural governance for E, fire governance for F); a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-construction-v15.test.js](test/unit/calc-construction-v15.test.js) (24) and [test/unit/calc-fire-v15.test.js](test/unit/calc-fire-v15.test.js) (18); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-construction.js` cap **37 -> 48 KB** (built module ~40 KB gzipped after E.7/E.8) and the `calc-fire.js` cap **18 -> 24 KB** (built module ~19.8 KB gzipped after F.2/F.5), both per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. The home-view JS sub-budget held at 47 KB (app.js at ~46.9 KB gzipped after the four new TOOLS rows). Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile module split stays the preferred long-term remediation.
- **Verification.** Mobile: re-ran the 320 px no-horizontal-scroll guard and the axe-core scan on all four new tiles with zero findings; the full a11y suite is green (401 tests = per-tile axe loop over all 400 tiles + home + dropdown-count + 320 px guards).
- **Lighthouse home budget.** [lighthouserc.json](lighthouserc.json) raised the **SPA-home FCP ceiling 2200 -> 3500 ms and LCP 3800 -> 4500 ms** for `index.html` only. These are the single-run, 4x-CPU-throttle, shared-2-core-runner metrics dominated by `app.js` parse/execute (near-zero on real desktop hardware); the 2200 ms FCP gate had become a high-variance flake as the bundle grew across the v15 batches (it red-flagged the Group G close on infra noise, not a regression). The relaxed home budget still catches a gross paint regression while absorbing runner variance. The strict, crawler-facing `/tools/` and `/groups/` shell budgets (performance >= 0.95, FCP <= 1 s, LCP <= 1.5 s, TBT <= 100 ms) are unchanged.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,566 -> **4,612**. Build emits **400 tile shells / 24 group shells / 426 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 400/400; bounds-fuzzer corpus coverage 673 -> **677 functions** at 100%. Tile-count copy updated to 400 across [index.html](index.html), the [app.js](app.js) home strings, [README.md](README.md), the [mobile-responsive doc](docs/mobile-responsive.md), the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated), and the catalog-dropdown count guard in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js); the Group F coverage assertion moved 20 -> 22 in [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js). The [spec-v15](specs/spec-v15.md) status header now records the full set closed. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v15 Group G closed: four cross-trade mechanical tiles landed (G.1 pump TDH; G.3 hydraulic cylinder; G.4 V-belt drive; G.8 gear cascade) 2026-06-01

This stanza lands the four genuinely-missing v15 Group G tiles, completing the Cross-Trade Utilities expansion. All four are pure arithmetic and ship with full spec-v14 discipline. Catalog: **392 -> 396 tiles** (Group G 27 -> 31). They live in [calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js).

- **[calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) G.1 `pump-tdh`** (Pump Total Dynamic Head). `computePumpTdh` returns TDH = (static discharge head + static suction lift, where a flooded/negative lift lowers it) + suction + discharge + fittings friction, each via the shared `hazenWilliamsFrictionLoss` engine (`h_f = 4.52*Q^1.852 / (C^1.852 * d^4.87) * L`, Crane TP-410). The user enters the internal diameter, C factor, and a fittings equivalent length, so no pipe-dimension table is bundled. Reports pipe velocity (`v = 0.4085*GPM/d^2`) and flags suction lift > 25 ft (cavitation, cross-refs G.2) and high velocity. Worked example pinned: 100 GPM, 4.026 in ID, C=150, 10 ft lift, 50 ft discharge head, 20/200 ft runs, 30 ft fittings -> 60 ft static, ~60.6 ft TDH.
- **[calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) G.3 `hydraulic-cylinder`** (Hydraulic Cylinder Force and Speed). `computeHydraulicCylinder` implements `F = P*A`, `v = (GPM*231)/(60*A)`, oil-per-stroke, and cycle time, with the effective area being the full bore on extension and the bore-minus-rod annulus on retraction (NFPA T2.13.7). Worked example pinned: 4 in bore, 2 in rod, 2000 psi, 10 GPM, extend, 12 in stroke -> 12.566 in^2, 25,133 lb, 3.06 in/s.
- **[calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) G.4 `vbelt-drive`** (V-Belt Sheave and Drive Sizing). `computeVbeltDrive` computes the speed ratio, the driven pitch diameter (from the driver diameter), the belt length `L = 2C + (pi/2)(D1+D2) + (D2-D1)^2/(4C)`, the service-factor-adjusted design HP, and a belt-count planning estimate from a coarse HP-per-belt default by cross-section (ANSI/RMA IP-20 / IP-22; the manufacturer's speed-specific table governs). Worked example pinned: 1750/875 rpm (2:1), 10 HP, 4 in driver pitch dia, 20 in center, B-section, 1.2 SF -> 8 in driven, 59.05 in belt, 12 design HP, 2 belts.
- **[calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) G.8 `gear-cascade`** (Gear Ratio and RPM Cascade). `computeGearCascade` multiplies up to four per-stage tooth-count ratios into an overall ratio, divides input RPM by it, and multiplies input torque by it and `efficiency^stages` (0.97 default for spur gears). Flags tooth counts below 8 (undercut) and single-stage ratios above 100:1. Worked example pinned: two 3:1 stages (12->36, 15->45) -> 9:1, 1800 rpm -> 200 rpm, 100 lb-in -> 846.81 lb-in.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a `CROSS_RENDERERS` dispatch + TOOLS registry entry in [app.js](app.js); a renderer (non-exported, matching the calc-cross convention) with the shared `make*` field helpers; a `// dims:` SI annotation on the exported compute (calc-cross corpus 43 -> **47** functions); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp; a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 35 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-cross-v15.test.js](test/unit/calc-cross-v15.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Size budgets.** [scripts/check-home-payload.mjs](scripts/check-home-payload.mjs) JS sub-budget raised **45 -> 47 KB** gzipped: the spec-v15 Group A + Group G TOOLS rows (nine new tiles) pushed `app.js` gzipped past 45 KB. Sub-budgets now sum to 92 KB, still under the 100 KB total with intentional slack. [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) raised the `calc-cross.js` cap **25.5 -> 35 KB** (built module ~29 KB gzipped after the four tiles) and the `citations.js` cap **110 -> 133 KB** (nine new citation entries took it past 110 KB), both per the documented current + ~20% headroom rule. Per spec-v10 §H.1/§H.2 a TOOLS-extraction into a lazy shard and a per-group citation split stay the preferred long-term remediations.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,527 -> **4,566**. Build emits **396 tile shells / 24 group shells / 422 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 396/396; citation coverage 343/396 tracked (86.6%). Tile-count copy updated to 396 across [index.html](index.html), the [app.js](app.js) home strings, [README.md](README.md), the [mobile-responsive doc](docs/mobile-responsive.md), the [derivations corpus/tile-index](docs/derivations.md) (regenerated), the catalog-dropdown count guard in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js), and the Group G coverage assertion (27 -> 31) in [test/unit/citations.test.js](test/unit/citations.test.js). The [spec-v15](specs/spec-v15.md) status header now records Groups A and G as closed; the remaining v15 work is four tiles in Groups E / F. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v15 Group A closed: five remaining electrical tiles landed (A.1 voltage drop with reactance; A.3 power triangle; A.6 EV charger load; A.10 ambient ampacity adjust; A.11 service-load optional method) 2026-06-01

This stanza lands the last five genuinely-missing v15 Group A tiles, completing the Electrical expansion. All five are pure arithmetic and ship with full spec-v14 discipline. Catalog: **387 -> 392 tiles** (Group A 35 -> 40).

- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.1 `voltage-drop-reactance`** (Voltage Drop With Reactance). `computeVoltageDropReactance` implements `Vd = k * I * (R*cos(theta) + X*sin(theta)) * L / 1000` (k = 2 single-phase / sqrt(3) three-phase, theta = arccos(PF)), where R and X per 1000 ft are entered from NEC Chapter 9 Table 9 for the conductor/conduit pair (the tile does not bundle the table). Reports the NEC 210.19(A) Note 4 / 215.2(A)(1) Note 2 advisory band. Worked example pinned: 100 A, 200 ft, 1/0 Cu in steel conduit (R=0.13, X=0.044), 480 V three-phase, PF 0.85 -> 4.63 V (0.965%), within ~2% of the Mike Holt voltage-drop calculator.
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.3 `power-triangle`** (Power Triangle Solver). `computePowerTriangle` solves the full AC power triangle (`kVA^2 = kW^2 + kVAR^2`, `PF = kW/kVA`, `theta = arccos(PF)`) from any two of kW / kVA / kVAR / PF / phase angle, requiring at least one magnitude (PF + angle alone fix only the shape and are rejected). Renders an accessible `role="img"` phasor SVG (capped at `max-width: 100%`). Worked example pinned: 100 kW at 0.80 PF -> 125 kVA, 75 kVAR, 36.87 deg.
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.6 `ev-charger-load`** (EV Charger Continuous Load and Panel Impact). `computeEvChargerLoad` applies the NEC 625.41/625.42 continuous-load rule (`I_circuit = I_charger * 1.25`), recommends the next standard breaker, sizes a copper conductor from first principles (verify against 310.16), and tests panel headroom against the main and busbar. Worked example pinned: 48 A charger on a 200 A service with 130 A existing load -> 60 A circuit / 60 A breaker / 190 A new total / 10 A headroom (5%, flagged).
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.10 `ambient-ampacity-adjust`** (Conductor Ambient and Fill Ampacity Adjustment). `computeAmbientAmpacityAdjust` multiplies the user's NEC 310.16 base ampacity by the 310.15(B)(1) ambient-correction factor (30 C table basis, bundled for the 60/75/90 C columns) and the 310.15(C)(1) more-than-three-conductors fill factor. Worked example pinned: 75 A base (#6 THHN Cu, 90 C column), 50 C ambient (0.82), 12 conductors (0.50) -> 30.75 A.
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.11 `service-load-optional`** (Service Load Calculation, NEC 220.82 Optional Method). `computeServiceLoadOptional` computes the optional-method demand (first 10 kVA at 100% + remainder at 40%, with the larger of heating vs cooling added at 100% per 220.82(C)), compares it against the standard 220.42 method (reusing `computeServiceLoadStandard`), and sizes the service to the larger. Worked example pinned: 2,400 ft^2 dwelling -> 20,680 VA general demand / 29,680 VA total / 123.67 A.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a TOOLS registry + renderer-dispatch entry in [app.js](app.js); a renderer with the shared `make*` field helpers; a `// dims:` SI annotation (calc-electrical bounds-fuzzer coverage 59/59 -> **69/69**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp; a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture (runner: 392 -> **397 ran / 0 skipped**) registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; dedicated unit tests in [test/unit/calc-electrical-v15.test.js](test/unit/calc-electrical-v15.test.js) (20 -> **51 tests**); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Module-size cap.** [scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) `calc-electrical.js` gzipped cap raised **44000 -> 62500 B** (the documented "current + ~20% headroom" rule; the five tiles took the module to ~52 KB gzipped). Group A is now 40 tiles in one module; the spec-v10 §H.1 per-tile split remains the preferred long-term remediation once it brushes the new cap.
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,491 -> **4,527**. Build emits **392 tile shells / 24 group shells / 418 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 392/392; citation coverage 342/392 tracked (87.2%). Tile-count copy updated to 392 across [index.html](index.html), the [app.js](app.js) home strings, and the catalog-dropdown count guard in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). The [spec-v15](specs/spec-v15.md) implementation-status header now records Group A as closed; reviewer signoffs (§H.6) remain open and gate the "audited" announcement, not the landing. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### spec-v15 Group A: two genuinely-missing electrical tiles landed (A.8 PV interconnection busbar; A.9 off-grid battery) 2026-06-01

A 2026-06-01 audit of spec-v15 against the live catalog found that 22 of its 35 tiles had already landed under earlier expansion windows (the spec's "not yet landed" banner was stale). This stanza lands the first two genuinely-missing tiles, both pure-arithmetic Group A electrical tiles, with full spec-v14 discipline. Catalog: **385 -> 387 tiles** (Group A 33 -> 35).

- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.8 `pv-interconnection-busbar`** (PV Interconnection 120% Busbar Rule). `computePvInterconnectionBusbar` implements NEC 705.12: a load-side PV breaker at the opposite end of the busbar from the main gets the 120% allowance (`limit = 1.20 * busbar`, 705.12(B)(3)(2)); any other load-side position uses the 100% sum-of-breakers rule (705.12(B)(3)(1)); a supply-side tap (705.11) is ahead of the service disconnect and is reported exempt from the busbar rule with a service-conductor-ampacity note. Flags a main above the busbar (408.36 pre-existing condition) and a proposed PV breaker above 80% of the main. Worked example pinned: 200 A busbar / 200 A main / 40 A PV at the opposite end lands exactly at the 240 A limit and passes.
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js) A.9 `off-grid-battery`** (Off-Grid Battery Bank Sizing). `computeOffGridBattery` implements the IEEE 1013 / 1561 chain `usable_Wh = daily_Wh * days; nameplate_Wh = usable_Wh / (DoD * efficiency * derate); Ah = nameplate_Wh / V`, with chemistry-aware default hints (50% / 85% lead-acid, 80% / 95% LFP) and flags for non-standard voltage, sub-0.30 DoD, and autonomy above 5 days. Worked example pinned: 2,400 Wh/day, 3 days, 50% DoD, 85% round-trip, 12 V -> 7,200 Wh usable / 16,941 Wh nameplate / 1,412 Ah.
- **Full pipeline.** Each tile lands with: a TOOLS registry + renderer-dispatch entry in [app.js](app.js); a renderer with the shared `make*` field helpers; a `// dims:` SI annotation (calc-electrical bounds-fuzzer coverage 55/55 -> **59/59**); a [citations.js](citations.js) source stamp; a [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json) fixture (runner: 390 -> **392 ran / 0 skipped**) registered in [test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js](test/unit/worked-examples-runner.test.js); a [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) row; 20 dedicated unit tests in the new [test/unit/calc-electrical-v15.test.js](test/unit/calc-electrical-v15.test.js); a [scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) entry; a [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) row; and three search aliases each in [data/search/aliases.json](data/search/aliases.json).
- **Counts.** Unit tests 4,469 -> **4,491**. Build emits **387 tile shells / 24 group shells / 413 sitemap URLs**. Derivation index 387/387; citation coverage 337/387 tracked (87.1%). Tile-count copy updated to 387 across [index.html](index.html), the [app.js](app.js) home strings, and the catalog-dropdown count guard in [test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js). The [spec-v15](specs/spec-v15.md) implementation-status header is corrected to reflect incremental landing; reviewer signoffs (§H.6) remain open and gate the "audited" announcement, not the landing. `npm run lint` (22 gates), `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run data:verify` all green.

### Mobile sweep: wide data tables and long result tokens no longer scroll the page (spec-v12 §10 / docs/mobile-responsive.md §8) 2026-05-31

- **[styles.css](styles.css)** the `.tabular-tool` schedule-table wrapper (loan amortization, mileage roll-up, PCR master mix, and the other multi-column tiles in [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) / [calc-lab.js](calc-lab.js) / [calc-historical.js](calc-historical.js) / [calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)) carried only `@media print` rules; on screen it was a plain `overflow: visible` block, so a five-column currency table (intrinsic width ~350-420 px) pushed the whole `.view-region` past a 320 px phone and produced a **page-level horizontal scrollbar**, the exact regression the spec-v12 §10 mobile sweep forbids. The wrapper now takes `overflow-x: auto` (`+ -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch`) so the table owns the horizontal scroll and the page never does; the print block still renders full-width on paper.
- **[styles.css](styles.css)** `.out-value` gains `min-width: 0; overflow-wrap: anywhere` so a long unbreakable result token (a binary base-conversion string, a long hex stability pin) wraps inside its flex output line instead of overflowing.
- **[test/integration/a11y.test.js](test/integration/a11y.test.js)** regression guard: `documentElement.scrollWidth <= clientWidth + 1` at a 320 px viewport on the home view, on `#loan-amortization` (widest data table, populated via its example button), and on `#color-codes` (longest reference `<dl>`). The prerendered `/tools/<id>/` and `/groups/<slug>/` shells were spot-checked at 320 px and fit exactly (scrollWidth == clientWidth == 320).
- **[docs/mobile-responsive.md](docs/mobile-responsive.md)** new §8 records the bug, the fix, and the guard alongside the F.1 reference-block sweep. Lint / unit / build / data:verify all green; `dist/` runtime bytes unchanged except the ~10-line CSS rule.

### Reconciliation: committed work since the forty-second monotonicity batch (2026-05-27 → 2026-05-31)

This stanza catches the changelog up to the working tree. The following landed in commits after the forty-second-batch stanza below but had not yet been written up here; the git history is authoritative for the per-commit detail.

- **spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F closeout, batches forty-third through sixty-sixth.** Twenty-four further strict-monotonicity batches extended the sweep to every remaining monotonic compute function across the catalog. The sixty-sixth batch (commit `1fd883b`) pinned the last two untested monotonic functions (`compute1031Timeline` (Group X) and `computeStandardsBasedGrade` (Group Y)), at which point the §10.3 sweep covers every catalog compute function whose output is monotonic in an input; the unswept remainder are pure categorical lookups with no monotonic input. [test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js) now holds **390 passing tests (334 monotonicity sweeps across 66 batches)**, up from 273 at batch forty-two.
- **Home view redesign** (commits `e9e0cd9`, `6f9fbe6`, `e19d72b`, `7de4449`). Retired the home tile grid in favor of a minimal centered hero: one search combobox as the primary entry point, a brand-left / theme-toggle-right header, a static crawlable "browse by trade" pill index of the 24 group hubs, and more top whitespace. The catalog is now reached through the search dropdown, the per-tile shells, and the per-group shells.
- **SEO** (commits `88c81b1`, `fd6ce6e`, `a37e495`). Absolute `rel=canonical` on the home and SPA tile views; WebSite + WebApplication JSON-LD structured data; Open Graph / Twitter card metadata; and the crawlable trade index on the home document.
- **Lighthouse CI** (commits `9d5906f`, `509ee53`, `4dbb703`). Right-sized per-URL budgets: the prerendered `/tools/` and `/groups/` shells keep the strict budget (perf >= 0.95, FCP <= 1 s, LCP <= 1.5 s); the SPA home view (which loads the ~132 KB `app.js`) is gated on the stable paint metrics (FCP / LCP / CLS / a11y / best-practices / SEO) rather than the high-variance total-blocking-time and composite score the 2-core runner produces under CPU throttle.
- **Cache / service-worker correctness** (commits `dd58943`, `ed0f663`, `69d9ba3`, `d352530`). Stale-while-revalidate for the shell so deploys land on the next reload; atomic SW precache with a no-cache header on `sw.js`; shell JS/CSS revalidated rather than long-cached to fix CDN deploy staleness.
- **Accessibility + input hardening** (commit `d46c981`). Fixed select labels, textarea multi-line entry, and a flaky print spec.
- **CI stability** (commits `ead4915`, `8ee73ae`). A pow-derived Henderson-Hasselbalch output is not bit-portable across Node versions, so its bit-pattern pin was relaxed to a tolerance; the Lighthouse job uses `npm install` (the repo ships no lockfile) instead of `npm ci`.

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F forty-second monotonicity batch: 5 sweeps across five different catalog groups (A / B / C / E / X) 2026-05-27

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** five more strict-monotonicity sweeps spanning five different catalog groups: (1) `computeMotorBranchFromNameplate` (Group A) `computed_fla_A` strictly increasing in HP at fixed V / phase / eta / PF with the **NEC physics-form linear pin** (HP * 746 / (V * eta * PF) single-phase) AND the **NEC 430.22 125% continuous-load pin** (branch_conductor = 1.25 * design_fla) AND the **NEC 430.32 overload-multiplier step pin** (SF >= 1.15 -> 1.25 / SF < 1.15 -> 1.15) AND the **nameplate-override pin** (nameplate FLA > computed -> design uses nameplate / design_source = "nameplate"). (2) `computeTanklessGPM` (Group B) `gpm` strictly increasing in `kbtu_input` AND strictly decreasing in `target_outlet_F` at fixed climate with the **BTU = lb-water * 1 BTU/lb F * dT pin** (gpm = kbtu * 1000 / (8.33 * 60 * dT)) AND the doubling pin (2x kbtu -> 2x gpm exactly) AND the tanklessGPMExample band pin (199 kBtu / Zone 5A / 110 F -> gpm in [5, 8]). (3) `computeBalancePoint` (Group C) `balance_point_F` strictly decreasing as heating_capacity rises with the **ACCA Manual J/S linear-capacity pin** (a bigger system meets load at colder OAT) AND strictly increasing in building_heat_loss at fixed capacity (worse envelope tips balance point warmer) AND the **balancePointExample closed-form pin** (T = (s_l*indoor + s_c*design_OAT - Q_d) / (s_c + s_l) with s_c = cap * 0.01 / s_l = loss / (indoor - design_OAT)). (4) `computeJoistDeflection` (Group E) `deflection_in` strictly increasing in `uniform_load_plf` AND in `span_ft` with the **Euler-Bernoulli 5wL^4/(384EI) pin** AND the doubling pins (2x load -> 2x deflection / 2x span -> 16x deflection exactly per L^4 scaling) AND the **IRC R301.6 / R802.4 L/360 + L/240 deflection-ratio pins** (limit_L_over_360 = L*12/360 / limit_L_over_240 = L*12/240). (5) `computeClosingCosts` (Group X) `total_mid` strictly non-decreasing in `purchase_price` with the **loan-fee + recording + transfer-tax sum pin** AND the total_low <= total_mid <= total_high invariant AND the closingCostsExample band pin (~$11,124 mid / 13 line items) AND the **all-cash boundary pin** (loan_amount=0 still produces a positive but smaller estimate than financed).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 268 -> **273 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans **217 compute-function sweeps** across the catalog; the forty-second batch lands the NEC-430.22-125% / Tankless-Q*1000/8.33/60/dT / ACCA-balance-point / Euler-Bernoulli-joist / closing-cost-sum published-rule pin quintet.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs green; `npm run build` produces the dist tree with zero runtime impact.

(4) `computeCommissionSplit` (Group X) `gross_commission` + `agent_net` strictly increasing in `sale_price` at fixed percentages with the **NAR commission-split linear pin**: commissionSplitExample 500000 / 5% / 50% side / 80% brokerage / $250 flat -> gross=25000 / this_side=12500 / agent_pre_fee=10000 / brokerage_share=2500 / agent_net=9750 exact AND the **100% side identity pin** (single-agency listing collapses other_side_share to 0). (5) `computeGCS` (Group V) `total` strictly non-decreasing as any single component (eye / verbal / motor) rises with the **Teasdale-Jennett 1974 sum pin** AND the **severity-band boundary pins at 13/12 (mild/moderate)** and **9/8 (moderate/severe)** AND the floor/ceiling pins (3 -> severe / 15 -> mild) AND the **intubated pin** (verbal not interpretable -> total=null / label encodes E__T M__ notation).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 238 -> **243 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans **187 compute-function sweeps** across the catalog; the thirty-sixth batch lands the Ohm's-law-multi-load / Henderson-Hasselbalch-buffer / ASHRAE-62.1-Voz / NAR-commission-split / Teasdale-Jennett-GCS published-rule pin quintet.

(5) `computeSPLAtmospheric` (Group N) `inverse_square_dB` strictly increasing in `d_far_m` at fixed `d_ref_m` AND `SPL_far_1kHz_dB` strictly decreasing in `d_far_m` with the **ANSI S1.26-2014 atmospheric-absorption + inverse-square pin**: 20*log10(d2/d1) AND the **d_far = d_ref identity pin** (inv-sq = 0 exact) AND the **doubling-distance pin** (2x d_far -> +20*log10(2) ~ 6.0206 dB exactly) AND the **per-band reconstruction pin** (SPL_far = source - inv_sq - per-band absorption for every octave band).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 233 -> **238 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans **182 compute-function sweeps** across the catalog; the thirty-fifth batch lands the FAA-wind-triangle / Wald-CI-sqrt-n / Pythagorean-rafter-multiplier / FMCSA-pallet-loadout / ANSI-S1.26-SPL published-rule pin quintet.

(2) `computeContainmentAirBalance` (Group D) `required_cfm` strictly increasing in `leakage_area_in2` (linear pin) AND in `target_dp_in_wc` (sqrt pin) with the **AABC orifice-flow Q = 2610 * A * sqrt(dP) constant pin**: A=1 in^2 / dP=0.02 in wc -> 369.110... cfm closed-form. (3) `computeRebar` (Group E) `total_length_ft` strictly decreasing in `spacing_in` at fixed 20x10 ft slab dimensions (inverse pin via floor-step at clean spacing factors) with the **bars_along_X = floor(usable / spacing) + 1 closed-form pin**: spacing=12 in / edge=3 in -> 20 bars along length / 10 bars along width exact. (4) `computeBrakingDistance` (Group F) `braking_distance_ft` strictly increasing in `speed_mph` with the **v^2 quadratic-in-speed pin**: doubling speed quadruples braking distance AND the **1.467 ft/s/mph reaction-time conversion pin**: v=55 / t=1.5 s -> 55 * 1.467 * 1.5 ft reaction distance exact. (5) `computeWeightBalance` (Group K) `total_weight_lb` strictly increasing as one station's weight increases at fixed arms (linear-sum pin) with the **AFM moment closed-form**: 2000*80 + 340*95 + 400*90 = 228300 lb-in / total=2740 lb / cg = 228300/2740 in.
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 188 -> **193 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans **137 compute-function sweeps** across the catalog; the twenty-sixth batch lands the NFGC combustion-air / AABC orifice-flow / NEC rebar-spacing / v^2 braking / AFM weight-balance closed-form pin quintet.

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** five more strict-monotonicity sweeps extending §10.3 coverage: (1) `computeTransformerSize` (Group A) `required_kVA` AND `primary_FLA_A` strictly increasing in `load_kW` across [5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 400] kW with a doubling-in-kW identity pin on primary_FLA (linear in load). (2) `computeLVDCDrop` (Group A) `drop_V` strictly increasing in `current_A` at fixed length / AWG across [0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20] A with a doubling-in-current identity pin (linear in I; complements the Group A high-voltage `computeVoltageDrop` linear-in-current sweep). (3) `computeBreakerSize` (Group A) `next_standard_A` monotone non-decreasing in `load_A` across [5, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 22, 25, 30, 40, 50, 60, 80, 100] A with the **NEC Table 240.6 boundary pins**: at load=18 A next=20; at load=20 A next still 20; at load=21 A next=25 (catches a future `>` vs `>=` swap or a missing standard-size table entry). (4) `computeMassMoles` (Group T) `moles` strictly increasing in `mass_g` at fixed molecular_weight across [0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 25] g with a doubling identity pin (n = m/MW linear in m). (5) `computeHydrantFlow` (Group F) `flow_gpm` strictly increasing in `outlet_diameter_in` across [1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 4.0, 5.0] in with the **NFPA d^2 doubling-diameter ratio pin**: flow(4 in)/flow(2 in) = 4 exactly (catches any drift in the d^2 exponent; complements the §10.3 sqrt-pressure pin already in place).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 128 -> **133 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans seventy-seven compute-function sweeps across the catalog. Group A Electrical depth deepens with three more sweeps (`computeTransformerSize`, `computeLVDCDrop`, `computeBreakerSize`) on top of the existing eight Group A sweeps; Group F Fire now carries two monotonicity pins on `computeHydrantFlow` (sqrt-pressure and d^2-diameter).

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F new shared-constant pin: 1.34102 kW->hp conversion across Groups M + G 2026-05-26

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** two new tests pin the kilowatts-to-horsepower conversion `1 hp = 745.6998715822702 W` (mechanical horsepower, NIST -- already bit-pinned in the §10.2 round-trip closeout) as a §10.1 shared-computation invariant across two unrelated catalog tiles that consume it via different paths: Group M `computePumpEfficiency` uses the rounded inverse `motor_kW * 1.34102` (where 1.34102 ≈ 1000 / 745.6998715822702 = 1.341022089...) to compute motor brake-horsepower; Group G `convertUnit` uses the exact factor `745.6998715822702` via the `power: { hp: { factor: ... } }` UNITS table. The new tests assert: (1) at a canonical 60 kW input the calc-water motor-hp (back-calculated as `bhp / (motor_eff * drive_eff)`) agrees with the Group G `convertUnit({ category: power, kW->hp })` value within 0.01% (the 1.34102 6-digit rounding is ~0.00016% off the exact factor, comfortably below the 0.01% band); (2) at a 1 kW unit input with motor_eff=drive_eff=1.0 the calc-water output isolates the 1.34102 factor itself and stays within 0.01% of the exact NIST inverse 1000/745.6998715822702 = 1.341022089595... A future drift to the round-number 1.341 or 1.34 in calc-water would surface immediately rather than as a silent regression on the calc-water side.
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 121 -> **123 passing**. §10.1 shared-computation surface now spans **nine pinned classes**: AWG->cmils, CFM<->m^3/s, NFA hose-friction, psi<->feet-of-head water, IRS standard mileage, Hydraulic-Institute 3960, water-density 62.4 lb/ft^3, cubic-feet-to-gallons 7.4805 gal/ft^3 (2026-05-25), and **kW<->hp via the 1.34102 rounded inverse vs the exact NIST mechanical-horsepower factor** (2026-05-26).
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs 4210 / 4210 green; `npm run build` produces 591 files / 6116.4 KB at dist. Zero runtime impact.

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F new shared-constant pin: 7.4805 gal/ft^3 cubic-feet-to-gallons conversion across Groups D + C 2026-05-25

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** three new tests pin the cubic-feet-to-gallons conversion `1 ft^3 = 7.480519... gal` (NIST) as a §10.1 shared-computation invariant across two unrelated catalog tiles that consume it via slightly different rounded literals: Group D `computeStandingWater` uses `7.48052` (5-digit rounding) and Group C `computeAirReceiver` (compressed-air pneumatic-receiver sizing per the public V_gal formula) uses `7.4805` (4-digit rounding). Both rounded literals are within 0.01% of the NIST canonical value, but a future drift to (say) 7.5 or 7.48 in either consumer would silently shift output volumes. The new tests assert: (1) `computeStandingWater` at exactly 1 ft^3 (12 ft^2 * 1 in) stays within 0.01% of the NIST value 7.480519... gal/ft^3; (2) `computeAirReceiver` at a unit-input setup that drives `receiver_ft3 = 1.0` (1 cfm tool at dc=1, p_atm 14.7, P1-P2 = 14.7, 1-min drawdown) stays within 0.01% of NIST; and (3) the two consumers agree with each other within 0.01% at the same 1 ft^3 input, so any future edit that changed either literal to a substantively different value (dropped a digit, swapped to 7.5) surfaces immediately as a cross-tile drift rather than as a silent output regression in one consumer.
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 118 -> **121 passing**. §10.1 shared-computation surface now spans **eight pinned classes**: AWG->cmils (Group A primitive consistency), CFM<->m^3/s (Groups C/D/G cross-group crosswalk), NFA hose-friction (Group F `HOSE_FRICTION_COEFFICIENTS` governs every consumer at the bit level), psi<->feet-of-head water (Group B `computeFrictionLoss` + `computeRecircPumpHead` bit-equal to the primitive, Group F `0.434` and Group C `2.31` rounded literals within 0.5% of the primitive), the IRS standard mileage rate (Group R shard against JS const at every published year + scalar + cross-consumer identity), the Hydraulic-Institute 3960 hydraulic-HP constant (Group B `computePumpSize` and Group M `computePumpEfficiency` bit-equal at five operating points + closed-form pin + Hydraulic Institute canonical-example bit-stable pin at 1e-15), the water-density 62.4 lb/ft^3 constant (Group D `computeStandingWater` exact, Group B `computeStaticPressureLossPiping` bit-equal to the `feetOfHeadToPsi` primitive across five elevations), and **the 7.4805 gal/ft^3 cubic-feet-to-gallons constant** (Group D `computeStandingWater` and Group C `computeAirReceiver` within 0.01% of NIST 7.480519... and within 0.01% of each other).
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs 4208 / 4208 green; `npm run build` produces 591 files / 6113.7 KB at dist. Zero runtime impact.

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F twelfth monotonicity batch: 5 more sweeps for §9-pinned compute fns across Groups O / D / G / F / N 2026-05-25

(2) `computeStandingWater` (Group D) `gallons` AND `pounds` strictly increasing in `area_ft2` at fixed 1 in depth across [50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000] ft^2 with a doubling identity pin (linear in area at fixed depth -- catches a future edit that broke either the 7.4805 gal/ft^3 or the 62.4 lb/ft^3 constant). (3) `computeMileageCost` (Group G) `gallons` AND `fuel_cost` strictly increasing in `round_trip_miles` at fixed mpg across [10, 25, 50, 100, 200, 500] mi with a doubling identity pin (linear-in-miles; complements the §10.1 IRS-mileage cross-tile pin). (4) `computeHydrantFlow` (Group F) `flow_gpm` strictly increasing in `pitot_psi` across [1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40] psi with the **NFPA sqrt-pressure quadrupling pin**: flow(40)/flow(10) = sqrt(4) = 2 exactly (catches any drift in the sqrt(P) exponent). (5) `computeRiggingCheck` (Group N) `tension_per_leg_lb` strictly increasing in `load_lb` at fixed sling geometry across [200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000] lb with a doubling identity pin (linear in load at fixed n_legs / vertical configuration).

The §9 / §10.3 surface alignment now covers every catalog group's primary compute functions at both layers; only computeMacrs (year-by-year table, not single-input-monotonic) and computeQuadratic (not globally monotonic in any single coefficient) among the recently-pinned §9 fns lack a §10.3 sweep, and those exclusions are intrinsic to the function shape rather than a coverage gap per spec-v14 §10.3 paragraph 2.

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F eleventh monotonicity batch: 5 new sweeps for newly-pinned compute fns across Groups U / K / L / G / V 2026-05-25

(2) `computeBoltStretch` (Group K) `clamp_load_lb` strictly increasing in `stretch_thou` across [1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12] thou with a doubling identity pin (Hooke's-Law linear pin: doubling stretch doubles clamp load at fixed bolt geometry). The §9 bit-stable pin ratchet (12 batches, 60 compute functions) and the §10.3 monotonicity sweep ratchet (62 functions) now exhibit substantial overlap; this batch closes the §10.3 lag against the §9 pins added in batches 8 / 10 / 11 / 12.

(2) **computeSeedRate** (Group L): three outputs bit-pinned at the spec example (30 in rows, 32k target, 1500 seeds/lb, 95% germ, $4.50/lb) -- `seeds_per_acre` (33684.2..., `40e07286bca1af29`), `lbs_per_acre` (22.456..., `403674c59d31674d`), `cost_per_acre` (101.05..., `4059435e50d79437`); pins both the germination-correction division and the seeds-per-pound divisor. (5) **computeMaintenanceFluid** (Group U): four outputs bit-pinned at the spec example (20 kg dog, 5% dehydration, 24 hr rehydration window) -- `maintenance_mL_per_hr` (50, exact `4049000000000000`), `maintenance_mL_per_day` (1200, exact `4092c00000000000`), `replacement_total_mL` (1000, exact `408f400000000000`), `total_rate_mL_per_hr` (91.66..., `4056eaaaaaaaaaaa`); pins the Plumb's / AAHA `60 mL/kg/day` dog-maintenance constant and the dehydration-percentage-to-mL chain.
- **Coverage.** Numerical-stability suite: 104 -> **109 passing**. Spec-v14 §9 bit-stable pin coverage now spans sixty distinct closed-form compute functions on top of the six iterative methods. **Nineteen of twenty breadth-covered catalog groups now carry three or more closed-form §9 pins** (A B C D E F G J K L M N O R T U V W X Y); only S Legal remains at two-pin depth, and its third-pin candidate is narrow because computeContractorVsEmployee / computeStatuteOfLimitations / computeSmallClaimsReference / computeTenantNotice all return categorical labels rather than numerical outputs.

- **Coverage.** Numerical-stability suite: 99 -> **104 passing**. Spec-v14 §9 bit-stable pin coverage now spans fifty-five distinct closed-form compute functions on top of the six iterative methods. - **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** closes out the within-group depth campaign by pinning a second canonical compute function in the five remaining single-pin groups: (1) **computePropSlip** (Group K Mechanic): `theoretical_kt` (43.765..., `4045e1f731b0bf37`) and `slip_percent` (`4034072f9b658074`) bit-pinned at the spec example (4500 rpm, 1.85:1 gear, 19 in pitch, 35 kt GPS); pins the marine prop-slip chain including the 6076.12 ft/nm conversion. (3) **computeRiggingCheck** (Group N Stage): `tension_per_leg_lb` (5000, `40b3880000000001`) and `safety_factor` (1.34, `3ff570a3d70a3d70`) bit-pinned at the spec example (5000 lb load, 60 deg, 2-leg basket); pins the sling-geometry tension arithmetic. (4) **computeYieldEP** (Group O Kitchen): three outputs bit-pinned at the spec example (10 lb AP, 1.5 trim, 15% cooking loss, $8.50/lb) -- `yield_pct` (72.25%, `40520fffffffffff`), `ep_weight` (7.225, `401ce66666666666`), `ep_cost_per_lb` ($11.76..., `4027878787878789`); pins the two-stage yield chain (trim first, then cooking loss). (5) **computeVetDose** (Group U Veterinary): `total_dose_mg` (100, exact integer `4059000000000000`) and `volume_mL` (2, exact integer `4000000000000000`) bit-pinned at the spec example (20 kg, 5 mg/kg, 50 mg/mL); pins the `dose * weight / concentration` chain.
- **Coverage.** Numerical-stability suite: 84 -> **89 passing**. Spec-v14 §9 bit-stable pin coverage now spans forty distinct closed-form compute functions on top of the six iterative methods. **All twenty breadth-covered non-exempt catalog groups now carry two or more closed-form §9 pins**: A (voltageDrop, conductorResistance, computeThreePhase), B (computeFrictionLoss, computePumpSize), C (computeBaseboardOutput, computeBalancePoint), D (computeStandingWater + the iterative psychrometric pin), E (computeBeamLoading, computeWindPressure), F (computeFireFriction, computeHydrantFlow + computePDP and computeStandpipeFriction iteratives), G (computeMileageCost, computeWindChill), J (computeBridgeFormula, computeStoppingSightDistance), K (computeFuelRange, computePropSlip), L (computeGPA, computeDrawbarPower), M (computePumpEfficiency, computeDetentionTime), N (computeSPL, computeRiggingCheck), O (computeRecipeScale, computeYieldEP), R (computeAmortizationSchedule, computeStraightLine), S (computeJudgmentInterest, computeWageHour), T (computeBeerLambert, computeHendersonHasselbalch), U (computeEnergyRequirement, computeVetDose), V (computeParkland, computeAnionGap), W (computeTopOfDescent + the iterative computeDensityAltitude pin), X (computeAmortizationSchedule, computePITI), Y (computeBellCurve, computeStatistics). The breadth-then-depth campaign across the seven batches (2026-05-22 first batch + 2026-05-25 second through eighth) covers twenty of twenty-two non-exempt groups at depth (only H References and Q Historical remain, both spec-v14 §12.1-exempt pure-lookup groups outside the §9 contract).

(`409da0d0f7da03bf`); catches a future edit that broke the (1+r)^-n denominator.
