### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F tenth monotonicity batch: 5 new sweeps across Groups S / A / E / X 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** five more strict-monotonicity sweeps close the §10.3 coverage hole at Group S Legal and extend Group A / E / X coverage: (1) `computeJudgmentInterest` (Group S) `accrued_interest` strictly increasing in principal at fixed dates and state with a doubling identity pin (linear-in-P at fixed daily-rate * days); (2) `computeWageHour` (Group S) `gross_pay` strictly increasing in `hours_worked` with the **FLSA 40 hr OT-threshold pin**: at $20/hr, 40 hrs -> $800 (no OT), 41 hrs -> $830 (1 hr of OT at 1.5x). Catches a future edit that swapped `>` for `>=` at the 40 hr boundary or broke the 1.5 multiplier. (3) `computeMotorFLA` (Group A) `fla_A` strictly increasing in HP at fixed voltage and phase (pins the NEC Table 430 motor-FLA table monotonicity in a one-line test). (4) `computeBoardFootage` (Group E) `total_board_feet` strictly increasing in each of thickness / width / length with a doubling-in-length identity pin (pins the `(t * w * L) / 12` volume identity across all three dimensions). (5) `computePropertyTax` (Group X) `annual_tax` strictly increasing in assessed_value at fixed mill rate, plus the closed-form pin that $400k at 25 mills with no exemption = exactly $10,000 (mill-rate definition pin: 1 mill = $1 per $1,000 of assessed value).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 103 -> **108 passing**. §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans twenty-three catalog groups (the original twenty-two plus S Legal); the only remaining catalog groups without a direct §10.3 sweep are H References and Q Historical, which are pure-lookup tiles outside the §10.3 contract per spec-v14 §12.1 paragraph 2.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4135 / 4135 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact.

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F ninth batch: hydraulic-HP / water-density shared-constant pins across Groups B / D / M 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** five new tests pin two more shared physical constants that surface in multiple consumers across calc modules: (1) **Hydraulic-Institute 3960 constant**: at SG=1.0 (water), Group B `computePumpSize.hydraulic_hp` is bit-equal to Group M `computePumpEfficiency.whp` across five operating points covering (Q, H) = (50,50) (100,80) (250,100) (500,150) (1000,200); both consumers also match the closed-form `(Q*H*SG)/3960` identity to 1e-12; (2) the SG factor in `computePumpSize` is linear (doubling SG doubles hydraulic_hp - catches a future edit that dropped SG or squared it); (3) `computePumpSize` at the Hydraulic Institute canonical example (Q=100, H=80, SG=1) lands at exactly `100*80/3960 = 2.020202...` to 1e-15 (bit-stable pin). (4) **62.4 lb/ft^3 water-density constant**: 1 ft^3 of water in `computeStandingWater` weighs exactly 62.4 lb, and `computeStaticPressureLossPiping` at the default density produces an `elevation_loss_psi` bit-equal to `feetOfHeadToPsi` at the same elevation. (5) Across five elevation values (1, 5, 10, 50, 100 ft), `computeStaticPressureLossPiping` matches `feetOfHeadToPsi` to 1e-12. The pin guarantees that a future edit that drifted the calc-plumbing default density (or dropped the 144 conversion) would surface immediately at CI.
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 98 -> **103 passing**. §10.1 shared-computation classes now pinned: AWG->cmils (A), CFM<->m^3/s (C/D/G), NFA hose-friction (F), psi<->feet-of-head water (B/C/F), IRS standard mileage (G/P/R), Hydraulic-Institute 3960 constant (B/M), and water-density 62.4 lb/ft^3 (B/D/pure-math). Every magic number that surfaces in more than one consumer is now sourced from a single ground-truth pin rather than re-typed at each call site.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4130 / 4130 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact.

### spec-v14 §10.2 / §10.3 Phase F eighth batch: parameterized UNITS round-trip (456 pairs) + 4 new monotonicity sweeps across Groups E / M 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** one new parameterized round-trip test plus four monotonicity sweeps: (1) **456-pair parameterized UNITS round-trip** (§10.2): iterates every (category, from, to) pair in `calc-cross.UNITS` (13 categories × pairs-per-category = 456 ordered pairs at this writing) and asserts `convertUnit(convertUnit(VALUE, from, to), to, from) === VALUE` to 1e-9 relative; a single typo in any factor (missing zero, wrong digit, wrong sign) now surfaces as a failing pair name with the category and direction rather than as a downstream calculator regression that leaves the maintainer hunting; the test also pins the pair-count floor at 100 so a future deletion of a category surfaces. (2) `computeFootingArea` (Group E Construction) `required_area_ft2` strictly increasing in `column_load_lb` at fixed soil class with a doubling identity pin (`area(2P) = 2 * area(P)`). (3) `computeDetentionTime` (Group M Water) `minutes` strictly increasing in `tank_volume_gal` at fixed flow. (4) `computeDetentionTime` strictly decreasing in `flow_gpm` at fixed volume (inverse pin). (5) `computePumpEfficiency` (Group M Water) `whp` strictly increasing in `flow_gpm` and `tdh_ft` independently with a doubling-in-flow identity pin (water-hp = flow * tdh / 3960 linear in each).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 93 -> **98 passing**. The new parameterized round-trip alone exercises 456 distinct unit factor pairs in a single test; the four monotonicity additions extend the §10.3 sweep to include two more compute functions in Group M Water (detention time, pump water-hp) and one more in Group E Construction (footing area). The 456-pair sweep covers length / area / volume / mass / force / pressure / energy / power / flow / electrical_voltage / electrical_current / electrical_resistance / electrical_capacitance.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4125 / 4125 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact.

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F seventh monotonicity batch: 6 new sweeps across Groups A / W / X / P 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** six more strict-monotonicity sweeps extend §10.3 consumer-level coverage: (1) `computeThreePhase` (Group A) `kW` strictly increasing in each of V_LL, I_L, and pf with a doubling-in-V_LL identity pin (`kW(2V) = 2 * kW(V)` at fixed I and pf - sqrt(3)*V*I*pf linear pin), (2) `computeFuelPlanning` (Group W) `required_fuel_gal` strictly increasing in `flight_time_hr` and `burn_gph` independently with a doubling-in-burn identity pin (`required(2 gph) = 2 * required(1 gph)` at fixed flight time and reserve), (3) `computeLTV` (Group X) `ltv_percent` strictly increasing in `loan_amount` at fixed value plus the PMI-flag threshold pin (LTV = 80% does NOT require PMI, LTV = 81% DOES; catches a future edit that swapped `>` for `>=` at the conventional-conforming threshold), (4) `computeLTV` strictly decreasing in `value` at fixed loan_amount (inverse-in-V pin), (5) `computeCapRateDSCR` (Group X) `cap_rate_percent` strictly increasing in NOI and strictly decreasing in property value at fixed others (NOI / V * 100 pin), and (6) `computeBackcountryNeeds` (Group P Field) `trip_kcal` strictly increasing in both `trip_days` and `group_size` with a doubling-in-group identity pin (`trip_kcal(group=4) = 2 * trip_kcal(group=2)` at fixed body weight, days, ambient, exertion).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 87 -> **93 passing**. Combined §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans twenty-two catalog groups (the original twenty-one plus P Field) and pins ten more compute functions at the consumer level. The remaining catalog groups without a direct sweep (H References, Q Historical, S Legal) are predominantly categorical / lookup tiles outside the §10.3 contract.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4120 / 4120 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact (pure unit-test additions against existing exports).

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F sixth monotonicity batch: 7 new sweeps across Groups A / C / O / Y 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** seven more strict-monotonicity sweeps extend §10.3 coverage at the consumer level: (1) `computeVoltageDrop` (Group A) `drop_V` strictly increasing in `length_ft` with a doubling identity pin (`drop(2L) == 2 * drop(L)` to 1e-9), (2) `computeVoltageDrop` strictly increasing in `current_A` at fixed length (pins the second linear dimension), (3) `computeBatteryRuntime` (Group A) `hours` strictly increasing in `amp_hours` with a doubling identity pin (Peukert k=1 reduces to a linear-in-Ah relationship), (4) `computeBatteryRuntime` strictly decreasing in `load_W` (inverse-proportional), (5) `computeBaseboardOutput` (Group C) `btu_total` strictly increasing in `length_ft` with a doubling identity pin (linear `btu_per_ft * length`), (6) `computeRecipeScale` (Group O Kitchen) `factor` is exactly `target / original_yield` and each row's `quantity` scales by the same factor (doubling-identity pin: `target 2x -> quantity 2x` to 1e-9), and (7) `computeBellCurve` (Group Y Educators) `percentile` strictly increasing in `raw_score` with the symmetry pin that the percentile at the mean is 50 to 1e-6 (A&S 26.2.17 7-digit accuracy floor; the z-score at the mean is exactly 0).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 80 -> **87 passing**. Combined §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans twenty-one catalog groups (the original twenty plus O Kitchen) and pins eleven more compute functions at the consumer level. The remaining catalog groups without a direct sweep (H References, P Field, Q Historical, S Legal) are predominantly categorical / lookup tiles outside the §10.3 contract.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4114 / 4114 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact (pure unit-test additions against existing exports).

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F fifth monotonicity batch: 7 new sweeps across Groups K / J / R / N 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** seven new strict-monotonicity sweeps extend §10.3 coverage to four previously-untouched catalog groups: (1) `computeFuelRange` (Group K Mechanic) strictly increasing in `tank_gal` at fixed mpg with a doubling identity pin (`range(2*tank) == 2 * range(tank)` to 1e-9), (2) `computeFuelRange` strictly increasing in `mpg` at fixed tank (linear-in-mpg pin catches a future multiplication-vs-division swap), (3) `computeDIM` (Group J Trucking) `dim_lb` strictly increasing in each of length / width / height at fixed others (catches a future edit that swapped multiplication for addition or dropped a factor in the L * W * H / divisor identity), (4) `computeStoppingSightDistance` (Group J Trucking) `total_ssd_ft` strictly increasing in `speed_mph` with the v^2 braking-distance ratio pin (`braking(2v) / braking(v) == 4` at fixed friction), (5) `computeStraightLine` (Group R Accounting) `annual_depreciation` strictly increasing in cost at fixed salvage / life (catches a future edit that dropped the salvage subtraction or swapped life into the numerator), (6) `computeAmortization` (Group R Accounting) `payment` strictly increasing in principal at fixed rate / term with a doubling identity pin (linear in P at fixed annuity factor), and (7) `computeSPL` (Group N Stage) `L2_dB` strictly decreasing in d2 with the per-doubling 6.0206 dB drop pin (`L2(2x) - L2(x) == -20*log10(2)` to 1e-9 in free field).
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 73 -> **80 passing**. Combined §10.3 monotonicity coverage now spans twenty catalog groups (A Electrical, B Plumbing, C HVAC, D Restoration, E Construction, F Fire-direct, G Cross-trade, J Trucking, K Mechanic, L Agriculture, M Water, N Stage, R Accounting, T Lab, U Veterinary, V EMS, W Aviation, X Real Estate, plus the primitive-level sweeps in F and Y). The remaining catalog groups (H References, O Kitchen, P Field, Q Historical, S Legal, Y Educators directly) are mostly categorical / lookup tiles outside the §10.3 contract; any new monotonic compute function landed in those groups appends per the §16.1 phase order.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4107 / 4107 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact (pure unit-test additions against existing exports).

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F IRS-mileage per-consumer pin closeout (Groups G / P / R) 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** four new tests close the last of the five spec-v14 §10.1 shared-computation classes (the IRS standard mileage rate). The new pins: (1) `IRS_STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATE` in [calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) matches exactly one of the business rates in `STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATES` in [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) (catches a hand-typed drift like `0.7` instead of `0.67`, or `0.067` cents-vs-dollars), (2) `computeMileageCost` (Group G mileage-cost tile) produces `reimbursement = miles * IRS_STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATE` and re-surfaces the scalar in its `irs_rate_per_mile` output (catches a future edit that broke the destructuring default), (3) `computeTimesheet` (Group P timesheet tile) produces `reimbursable = miles * IRS_STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATE` at the matched job input, and (4) `computeMileageRollup` (Group R mileage-rollup tile) at whichever tax-year matches `IRS_STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATE` produces `deductible_amount = miles * IRS_STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATE` and surfaces the rate in its `standard_rate` output. The four pins together tie the Group R per-year-table consumer back to the Group G / P scalar consumer through the matched-year input, so any future drift in either direction surfaces at CI rather than slipping past as a silent default-value change.
- **Coverage.** Cross-tile invariants suite: 69 -> **73 passing**. Combined with the existing shard-against-JS-const bijection pin and the JS-const-covers-shard-years pin, the IRS-mileage row now matches the bit-level discipline already in place for the other four §10.1 classes (AWG->cmils, CFM<->m^3/s, NFA hose friction, psi<->feet-of-head water). Phase F campaign substantively complete; residual work is per-tile §10.3 monotonicity sweeps for any newly-landed monotonic compute function and per-consumer pins for any future Group J trucking owner-operator-expense tile that takes the rate as a default.
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK; 4100 / 4100 tests pass; build 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist. Zero runtime impact (pure unit-test additions against existing exports).

- **[scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs)** `SOURCE_PATTERNS` gains 69 more regex entries surfaced by the third-pass untracked-tiles list. The largest sub-categories: federal-statute / regulatory surface (IRS Publication / Form / Schedule with the `Forms?` and `Schedules?` plural fix; 26 USC / IRC §; 29 USC / FLSA; 23 CFR / 24 CFR / 29 CFR / 49 CFR; FMVSS), fire-service training surface (NFA / National Fire Academy; IFSTA; NFPA 1901 fire apparatus; NFPA 1965 fire hose appliances; Pierce / E-One / Sutphen apparatus manufacturers; Dave Dodson Reading Smoke), medical-protocol surface (ATLS; PALS; APLS Advanced Paediatric Life Support; AARC respiratory; ABA / ABLS Advanced Burn Life Support; Surviving Sepsis Campaign; Holliday-Segar; Merck Veterinary Manual; IDEXX / Antech / Abaxis; DiBartola vet fluids; landmark clinical studies Kothari / Kline / Allgower / Figge / Payne / Baxter / Vandromme / Mutschler; Wells DVT/PE; Apgar; Lund-Browder), construction-trade surface (AWS American Welding Society; Machinery's Handbook; BIA / NCMA masonry; USG / National Gypsum / GA-216; ARMA asphalt roofing; CAGI compressed air; NHLA / WWPA lumber grading; carpentry framing references Steel Square / Audel's), HVAC / refrigeration deepening (ACCA Manual S; ASHRAE 34 refrigerant safety; ASHRAE 170 healthcare ventilation; IICRC S520; HVAC equipment manufacturers Daikin / Carrier / Trane / Rheem / Lennox), agriculture / outdoor (ASABE; ARCSA rainwater catchment; AIARE avalanche), mechanical / marine / freight (ABYC boat; AAM / Spicer / Dana driveline; Gates / Goodyear / Bando belts; Sandvik / Kennametal / Niagara tooling; NMFTA / NMFC freight class; Wabash / Great Dane / Utility trailers; Thermo King / Carrier Transicold reefer; ICC Incoterms), aviation deepening (FAA Advisory Circular AC; Jeppesen / ASA Pilot's Manual; FAA Aeronautical Chart User's Guide), accessibility (ADA Standards / ICC A117.1), travel (GSA Federal Travel Regulation), education (AACRAO; Marzano proficiency scales; readability formulas SMOG / Coleman-Liau / Flesch / Kincaid), generic ISO numbered standards (covers ISO PPC, ISO 9613-2, ISO 8573, ISO 668), Fed. R. Civ. P. long form (complements the FRCP abbreviation), per-state statutory code reference forms (CA / NY / TX / AK / NV), landmark mathematical / statistical works (Bessel / Cramer / Wald), US Census / SBA, US Army Field Manual, Wayfair (sales-tax nexus), veterinary reproduction textbooks (Feldman-Nelson / Senger), and the NOAA WMM pattern broadened to match the `WMM<year>` short form. The pattern set now covers every regulatory + standards-body + trade-association + handbook + textbook + manufacturer-attribution + landmark-study surface in the catalog; the 50 remaining untracked tiles are "first-principles arithmetic", "classical geometry / trigonometry", "engineering practice", or "original plain-English summary" cites where no specific publisher is named (a corpus-level invariant per spec-v14 §6.2: every formula is either traceable to a tracked source or is first-principles).
- **Coverage.** Tracked-source inverse map: 117 -> 201 -> 280 -> 328 -> **478** tile-source edges across 29 -> 46 -> 72 -> 91 -> **160** tracked sources. Per-tile coverage: 42.3% -> 54.8% -> 63.9% -> **87.0%** (335 / 385 tiles match at least one tracked source; 50 untracked, all in the first-principles / classical / engineering-practice / original-summary category).
- **Lint / test / build.** Lint stays in measurement mode per spec-v14 §11.2 design. `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs 4096 / 4096 green; `npm run build` produces 591 files / 7.9 MB at dist (zero runtime impact; build-time documentation lint only). `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK.

### spec-v14 §11.2 Phase G citation-coverage expansion: untracked-tile measurement + 26 more source patterns; 42.3% -> 54.8% tile coverage 2026-05-22

- **[scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs)** the lint gains an "untracked tiles" measurement step that counts the CITATIONS tiles whose `edition` field matches no `SOURCE_PATTERN` entry. These tiles will not surface on any per-source edition-rollover row in [docs/v6-audit.md](docs/v6-audit.md), so the count is a direct indicator of Phase G coverage holes. The new summary line reads `<n>/<N> tile(s) matched at least one tracked source (<pct>%); <untracked> untracked.` `--verbose` mode now also dumps the first 25 untracked tile_ids with their edition prefix (truncated to 60 chars) so a maintainer can scan for sources that still need a SOURCE_PATTERN entry. At 2026-05-22 the lint reports `163 / 385 (42.3%) matched; 222 untracked` before the same-day pattern expansion and `211 / 385 (54.8%) matched; 174 untracked` after.
- **[scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-citation-coverage.mjs)** the `SOURCE_PATTERNS` array gains 26 more regex entries surfaced by the new untracked-tiles measurement step: NEMA, IEEE numbered standards (matches the `IEEE 802.3 / 802.3bt / Std 1584 / etc.` form), ASME (B31.1 / B31.9 / etc.), SMACNA, ASTM, AASHTO, ACI (318-19 / 211 / etc.), AISC, APA Engineered Wood (with a wood/span guard to avoid the generic APA prefix), IMC (Mechanical Code), ICC-ES, CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, wire / cable manufacturer datasheets (Southwire / Encore Wire / Belden / Corning / AFC), Bussmann / Eaton, Hydraulic Institute, WEF / Water Environment Federation, NWS / National Weather Service, ICAO, AOPA, NFPA 96 (commercial cooking), NFPA 25 (sprinkler ITM), NFPA 72 (fire alarm), NFPA 101 (Life Safety), NFPA 70E (arc flash), ICC code family + IECC, CRSI, SAE, DOL / Department of Labor, SSA / Social Security, ACVECC, AVECCT (re-asserted), ASA Physical Status, APSP (pool / spa), NSPF, NDS (timber design), and Joukowsky 1898. Coverage moves from 117 -> 201 -> **280** tile-source edges (the second-pass +79 from the first-pass +84 expansion 2026-05-22 earlier) across 29 -> 46 -> **72** tracked sources; per-tile coverage from 42.3% to **54.8%**.
- **Lint / test / build.** No source-of-truth change; the lint stays in measurement mode per spec-v14 §11.2 design. `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs 4096 / 4096 green; `npm run build` produces 591 files / 6053.0 KB at dist (zero runtime impact; this is a build-time documentation-lint expansion).

### spec-v14 §12 Phase H lint extension: check-audit-trail.mjs parses the per-group status table and reports per-status counts 2026-05-22

- **[scripts/check-audit-trail.mjs](scripts/check-audit-trail.mjs)** the §12 lint gains a structured-table parser that reads the "Per-group signoff status (v0.14)" table appended to [docs/audit-trail.md](docs/audit-trail.md) on 2026-05-22 and reports per-status counts in measurement mode. The parser walks the data rows, extracts the Group letter (column 2) and Status (column 4), and accumulates counts under five canonical status keys (`open`, `under-review`, `signed-off`, `renewal-due`, `exempt`) plus an `other` bucket for any value outside that set. Output line: `per-group signoff status: <N> row(s) in table; open=<n>, under-review=<n>, signed-off=<n>, renewal-due=<n>, exempt=<n>`. At 2026-05-22 the lint reports `24 row(s) in table; open=22, under-review=0, signed-off=0, renewal-due=0, exempt=2` (matching the table state). When the table is absent (e.g., on an older branch), the lint falls back to the prose-only scaffolding message and does not fail. A missing group row in the table is reported as a warning (advisory; does not fail at scaffolding); the warn graduates to error when the first row transitions to `under-review` or `signed-off` so the table cannot lose a row once it starts being maintained per the spec-v14 §16.2 ratchet pattern.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** gate 8 cell updated with one sentence naming the lint extension and the per-status counts the lint now reports.
- **Lint / test / build.** No source-of-truth change. `npm run lint` passes clean; the new line `per-group signoff status: 24 row(s) in table; ...` appears under the existing `audit-trail: 24 active group(s); ...` summary. `npm test` runs 4096 / 4096 green. `npm run build` produces 591 files at dist (unchanged from prior).

### spec-v14 §12 Phase H scaffolding: structured per-group signoff status table in docs/audit-trail.md 2026-05-22

- **[docs/audit-trail.md](docs/audit-trail.md)** adds a new "Per-group signoff status (v0.14)" subsection under §"Open solicitations for v0.14 (spec-v14 §12 per-group signoff)". The new structured table replaces the prior prose-only solicitations text with one row per active catalog group (24 rows: 22 non-exempt + 2 exempt) carrying columns Group / Letter / Credential / Status / Last review / Next renewal / Notes. Status values per spec-v14 §12 / §18.3: **open** (no reviewer engaged), **under-review** (reviewer engaged; signoff pending), **signed-off** (signoff appended under a YYYY-MM-DD section heading), **renewal-due** (signoff older than the v6 quarterly 90-day cadence), **exempt** (H References and Q Historical per spec-v14 §12.1 paragraph 2; reference tiles use the v6 source-stamp recheck cadence instead). Per-row tile count and primary-source list are recorded in the Notes column so a reviewer-of-record can see the scope and the citation surface in one place without a calc-*.js dive: e.g., Group A (Electrical, 33 tiles, NEC 2023 primary source), Group U (Veterinary, 18 tiles, Plumb's + AAHA + AAFP primary sources, v12 §13.1 override scope), Group X (Real Estate, 15 tiles, FNMA / FHFA / HUD / CFPB primary sources). At table close (2026-05-22): 24 active groups; 22 non-exempt; 0 signed-off; 22 open.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** gate 8 cell rewritten to name the structured-table scaffolding step explicitly. The gate stays **partial** (was: "Phase H scaffolding landed 2026-05-19") with the qualifying parenthetical "structured state machine scaffolded; reviewer signoffs pending". The §18.3 "warn if no completed review within 100 days" upgrade and the §12 gate-flip to pass land when the per-row status column starts moving (first **open** -> **under-review** -> **signed-off** transition).
- **Lint / test / build.** `npm run lint` clean (audit-trail lint reports `24 active group(s); 22 non-exempt; 24 named in audit-trail; 2 completed-review section(s)` -- the two completed-review sections are the v12 §13.1 override-scope mentions for Groups U and V that have always been there; the new table does not change the named-group set the lint asserts against). `npm test` runs 4096 / 4096 green. `npm run build` produces 591 files at dist.

### spec-v14 §17 launch-checklist gate refresh: gate 7 (cross-tile invariants) flipped partial -> pass after the Phase F §10.1 / §10.2 / §10.3 closeouts 2026-05-22

- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** the v14 §17 launch-gates table moves gate 7 ("Every shared computation passes the cross-tile invariant tests") from **partial** to **pass (Phase F campaign substantively complete)** after the §10.1 NFA hose-friction closeout (commit 8f2a5c7), §10.1 psi<->feet-of-head water closeout (ddeefb4), §10.3 first / second / third / fourth monotonicity batches (49e7939, b4ef57f, 105ed6d, e9e9a10) all landed 2026-05-22 on top of the §10.2 round-trip closeout (b8170f8, 2026-05-22) and the 2026-05-19 IRS-mileage shard pin. The new gate-7 cell summarizes the §10.1 / §10.2 / §10.3 surface: §10.1 has four of five shared-computation classes pinned at the bit / consumer level (AWG->cmils, CFM<->m^3/s, NFA hose-friction, psi<->feet-of-head water) with the IRS-mileage row carrying a shard / JS-const bijection pin (the per-consumer extension lands when shared Group J / P / R call sites grow); §10.2 round-trip identities are complete across every SI-base conversion pair at 1e-12 absolute plus three named-constant exact-bit pins (international foot, 1959 international pound, mechanical horsepower); §10.3 monotonicity covers twenty-eight compute-function sweeps across sixteen catalog groups directly (A B C D E F G L M T U V W X plus the cross-tile primitives in F and Y), with the remaining uncovered groups (R Accounting, S Legal, H References, J Trucking, K Mechanic, N Stage, O Kitchen, P Field, Q Historical) being mostly categorical / lookup tiles outside the §10.3 contract. **69 passing** in [test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js). The 2026-05-18 scaffolding prose is retained inline behind a "historical text follows" lead so the v0.14 audit-trail of how the gate moved through the ratchet stays readable.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** the "Remaining v14 work" closing paragraph is rewritten to name six complete campaigns (C, D, E, F, I, with G in measurement mode), accurately count Phase F as a campaign closure (the prior text described it as "scaffolding for the pure-math primitives plus the 2026-05-18 follow-ups"), and identify the residual work: per-consumer pins for the IRS-mileage row once Groups J / P / R consumers grow shared call sites, and per-tile §10.3 monotonicity sweeps for any newly-landed monotonic compute function.
- **Lint / test / build.** No source-of-truth change; the gate-status edit is a documentation reconciliation against the seven Phase F closeout commits landed 2026-05-22. `npm run lint` passes clean; `npm test` runs 4096 / 4096 green; `npm run build` produces 591 files / 6045.9 KB at dist (the launch-checklist is not shipped in the runtime payload).

The fourth batch covers four additional catalog groups (M Water, D Restoration, L Agriculture, F Fire-direct) and seven compute functions: (22) `computePoundsFormula` `pure_lb_day` strictly increasing in `flow_mgd` across [0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 25] MGD (AWWA pounds-formula linear-in-Q pin), (23) `computePoundsFormula` strictly increasing in `dose_mg_l` across [0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0] mg/L (linear-in-dose pin), (24) `computeAirMovers` `air_mover_count` monotonic non-decreasing in `affected_area_ft2` across [50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, 2000] ft^2 (IICRC S500 step-function pin; a refactor with the wrong ceil/floor direction surfaces here), (25) `computeGPA` `gpa` strictly increasing in `gpm` across [0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.6, 3.2] (boom-spray linear-in-gpm pin per the public USDA NRCS Agronomy Technical Note 5 formula gpa = 5940 * gpm / (speed * spacing)), (26) `computeGPA` strictly decreasing in `speed_mph` across [2, 3, 5, 8, 10, 15] (inverse-proportional in speed pin), (27) `computeDrawbarPower` `drawbar_hp` strictly increasing in `pull_lb` across [500, 1000, 2000, 4000, 6000, 8000] lb (ASABE D497 DBHP = pull * speed / 375 linear-in-pull pin), and (28) `computeFireFriction` `friction_loss_psi` strictly increasing in `gpm` at the consumer level across [100, 150, 250, 400, 600] gpm at fixed 2.5" hose / 100 ft (Group F consumer-side monotonicity pin; complements the primitive-level Q^2 pin already in place).

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F monotonicity sweep (third batch): seven more strict-monotonicity invariants across calc-construction / calc-cross / calc-hvac 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains seven more strict-monotonicity tests per spec-v14 §10.3. The third batch spans three additional catalog groups (C HVAC, E Construction's ASCE 7 surface, G Cross-trade) and seven compute functions: (15) `computeWindPressure` `q_psf` strictly increasing in V across [70, 90, 110, 130, 150, 170, 190] mph (ASCE 7 q = 0.00256 * V^2 dynamic-pressure pin), (16) V^2 ratio pin asserting `q(200)/q(100) == 4` exactly to 1e-9 (pins the V^2 exponent against a future swap to V or V^3), (17) `computeSnowLoad` `Pf_psf` strictly increasing in `Pg_psf` across [10, 20, 40, 60, 80, 100] psf at unit Ce / Ct / Is (ASCE 7 Pf = 0.7 * Ce * Ct * Is * Pg linear-in-Pg pin), (18) closed-form pin `Pf == 0.7 * Pg` at unit factors via strict `assert.equal` (catches any drift in the 0.7 base factor), (19) `computeWindChill` strictly decreasing in wind speed across [5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 40, 60] mph at T_F = 0 (NWS 2001 piecewise-power-law: WC = 35.74 + 0.6215T - 35.75*v^0.16 + 0.4275*T*v^0.16; the 0.16 exponent is the key pin), (20) `computeLoanPayment` `monthly_payment` strictly increasing in APR across [3.0, 4.5, 6.0, 7.5, 9.0]% at 300k / 360 months (mirrors the calc-realestate `computePITI` monotonicity pin so the two amortization entry points cross-check), and (21) `manualJCooling` `tons` strictly increasing in `outdoor_design_F` across [80, 85, 90, 95, 100, 105, 110] F at a fixed 1500 ft^2 / 4-occupant envelope (more outdoor heat -> more cooling tons; the dT = max(0, outdoor - indoor) clamp keeps the sweep above the 75 F indoor set point).

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F monotonicity sweep (second batch): seven more strict-monotonicity invariants across calc-ems / calc-vet / calc-lab / calc-electrical 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains seven more strict-monotonicity tests per spec-v14 §10.3 ("where the calculator's output is monotonic in an input, the test asserts monotonicity over a sweep; a non-monotonic result for a monotonic relationship is a transcription error"). The second batch spans four additional catalog groups (V EMS, U Veterinary, T Lab / bench science, A Electrical) and seven compute functions: (8) `computeParkland` `total_24hr_mL` strictly increasing in `weight_kg` across [40, 60, 80, 100, 120] kg at fixed TBSA=30 (4 * wt * TBSA linear-in-wt pin), (9) `computeParkland` strictly increasing in `tbsa_percent` across [10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60] at fixed weight=70 kg (linear-in-TBSA pin), (10) `computeEnergyRequirement` `RER_kcal_per_day` strictly increasing in `weight_kg` across [2, 5, 10, 20, 40, 60] kg (RER = 70 * wt^0.75 Kleiber-allometric pin; a refactor to 1.0 linear or 0.67 Brody would still be monotonic but the per-bit pin in the calc-vet worked-example fixture covers that), (11) `computeBeerLambert` `concentration` strictly increasing in absorbance across [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0] at fixed epsilon=50000 / path=1 cm (c = A / (eps * l) linear-in-A pin; catches a future log10 swap), (12) `computeBeerLambert` `concentration` strictly decreasing in epsilon across [10000, 25000, 50000, 100000, 200000] at fixed A=0.5 / path=1 (inverse-proportional pin), (13) `computeOhmsLaw` `I = V/R` strictly increasing in V across [12, 24, 48, 120, 240, 480] V at fixed R=10 ohm (linear-in-V pin), and (14) `computeOhmsLaw` `P = I^2 R` strictly increasing in I across [1, 2, 5, 10, 20] A at fixed R=10 (quadratic-in-I, strictly increasing for positive I).
- **Test count.** Cross-tile-invariants test file moves from 48 tests to 55 (+7 net). Full `npm test` runs 4082 / 4082 green. Phase F §10.3 (monotonicity) now carries fourteen compute-function-level sweeps across eight catalog groups (A Electrical, B Plumbing, E Construction, T Lab, U Veterinary, V EMS, W Aviation, X Real Estate), well above the spec-v14 §10.3 floor.

### spec-v14 §11.2 Phase G citation-coverage inverse-map expansion: 17 new source patterns; 117 -> 201 tile-source edges across 29 -> 46 tracked sources 2026-05-22

Each pattern is keyed off the canonical edition string the CITATIONS object uses so a future v6 recheck that rolls an edition triggers the per-source rollover row via the same lint without further changes.

### spec-v14 §10.3 Phase F monotonicity sweep: seven strict-monotonicity invariants across calc-plumbing / calc-construction / calc-aviation / calc-realestate 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains seven new strict-monotonicity tests per spec-v14 §10.3 ("where the calculator's output is monotonic in an input, the test asserts monotonicity over a sweep; a non-monotonic result for a monotonic relationship is a transcription error"). The new tests span five compute functions and four catalog groups (B Plumbing, E Construction, W Aviation, X Real Estate): (1) `computeFrictionLoss` `pressureLoss_psi` strictly increasing in `flow_gpm` across a 5-point sweep [5, 10, 20, 40, 80] gpm at fixed 3/4" copper / 100 ft (Hazen-Williams Q^1.852 scaling: a future refactor that swapped the exponent's sign or broke the scaling surfaces here), (2) `computeFrictionLoss` `pressureLoss_psi` strictly decreasing in nominal SCH 40 pipe size across [0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2] at fixed 10 gpm / 100 ft (larger diameter -> lower velocity -> lower head loss), (3) `computeBeamLoading` `delta_in` (max deflection) strictly increasing in uniform load across [50, 100, 200, 400, 800] lb/ft (the 5wL^4/384EI form is linear in w; a future clamped-boundary substitution would break this), (4) `computeBeamLoading` `delta_in` strictly increasing in span across [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 16] ft (pins the L^4 exponent against a unit-conversion error), (5) `computeDensityAltitude` `density_altitude_ft` strictly increasing in OAT across [-10, 0, 10, 20, 30, 40] C at fixed PA=3000 ft (pins the FAA 120 ft/C coefficient against any drift toward 100 or 150), (6) `computePITI` `piti` strictly increasing in principal across [100k, 200k, 400k, 800k] at 6.5% / 30 yr (the amortized-payment formula is linear in P; the fixed escrow keeps strict monotonicity), and (7) `computePITI` `piti` strictly increasing in APR across [3.0, 4.5, 6.0, 7.5, 9.0]% at 400k / 30 yr (the standard amortization formula `(P*r)/(1 - (1+r)^-n)` is monotonic in r at fixed P, n).
- **Test count.** Cross-tile-invariants test file moves from 41 tests to 48 (+7 net). Full `npm test` runs 4075 / 4075 green. Phase F §10.3 (monotonicity) now carries seven compute-function-level sweeps on top of the four prior primitive-level monotonicity tests (voltage drop in length / current, conductor resistance in temperature, hose friction in flow); the §10.3 surface is well-seeded for additional compute-function sweeps to append as new spec-v15 / v16 / v17 tiles land.

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F psi<->feet-of-head water closeout: bit-level consumer pins + rounded-literal tolerance band 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains four new tests closing the spec-v14 §10.1 psi<->feet-of-head-water shared-computation row (the fourth of the five §10.1 classes; AWG->cmils, CFM<->m^3/s, and NFA hose-friction are already pinned). Per spec-v14 §10.1 the conversion `1 psi = 2.31 ft of head water at 60 F` is shared across Group B plumbing (`computeFrictionLoss` and `computeRecircPumpHead` both call the pure-math `feetOfHeadToPsi` primitive), Group F fireground (`computeStandpipeFriction` hardcodes the rounded `0.434 psi/ft` NFPA water-column literal), and Group C HVAC + Group L agriculture (the inverse `2.31 ft/psi` literal lives inside `vaporPressureFt` in [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)). The new tests assert: (i) calc-plumbing `computeFrictionLoss` produces `pressureLoss_psi` bit-equal to `feetOfHeadToPsi(headLoss_ft)` via strict `assert.equal` across a 27-point pipe-size / flow / length sweep on the Hazen-Williams branch (size in {0.5, 0.75, 1} x flow_gpm in {10, 30, 60} x length_ft in {50, 100, 200}) so a future refactor that inlined the `rho/144` factor would surface immediately at CI, (ii) calc-plumbing `computeRecircPumpHead` `pressure_psi` is bit-equal to `feetOfHeadToPsi(head_ft)` at the spec-recircPumpHeadExample input (the second Group B consumer of the primitive), (iii) calc-fire's hardcoded `0.434 psi/ft` stays within 0.5% of the primitive `62.4/144 = 0.43333...` and the standpipe spec-example pin `elevation_psi = 100 * 0.434 = 43.4 psi` round-trips through `computeStandpipeFriction` -- a future edit that swapped `0.434` for `0.5` (the NFA fireground rule-of-thumb that lives in `computePDP`, a 15% inflation) would surface here even though the two consumers use different coefficients, and (iv) calc-hvac's hardcoded `2.31 ft/psi` inverse literal stays within 0.5% of the primitive inverse `144/62.4 = 2.3076923...`. The rounded-literal tolerance is set at 0.5%, comfortably below the worst-case published-table drift (the NIST water density at 60 F rounds to `62.4` to three significant figures and to `62.366` to five) and well above any floating-point noise. Group L agriculture consumes the same `2.31` factor implicitly through irrigation pressure-to-head conversion; the test pins the literal once and Group L tracks any future drift via the shared band.
- **Test count.** Cross-tile-invariants test file moves from 37 tests to 41 (+4 net). Full `npm test` runs 4068 / 4068 green. Phase F §10.1 moves from "three of five shared-computation classes pinned" (AWG, CFM, NFA hose-friction) to "four of five pinned" (the psi<->feet-of-head water row joins); only the IRS standard mileage row remains, and that row already has its shard-vs-JS-const bijection asserted today via the 2026-05-19 `STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATES` pin and would extend naturally as Group J / P / R consumers grow shared call sites.

### spec-v14 §10.1 Phase F NFA hose-friction coefficient closeout: shared-computation invariants across calc-fire consumers 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains five new tests closing the spec-v14 §10.1 NFA-hose-friction-coefficient shared-computation row (the third of the five §10.1 classes; AWG->cmils and CFM<->m^3/s are already pinned). Per spec-v14 §10.1 the `HOSE_FRICTION_COEFFICIENTS` table in [calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js) is shared across Group F hose friction (`computeFireFriction`), pump discharge pressure (`computePDP`, which threads `computeFireFriction`), reverse-lay friction (`computeReverseLayFriction`), and standpipe friction (`computeStandpipeFriction`); all four must agree on the per-diameter coefficient at the bit level. The new tests assert: (a) `computeFireFriction` produces the same `friction_loss_psi` as a direct `fireHoseFrictionLoss` primitive call at every diameter (1.75 / 2.5 / 3 / 4 / 5 in) using strict `assert.equal` so a coefficient drift in either side surfaces immediately, (b) `computeReverseLayFriction` at `n_pumps=1` equals `computeFireFriction` (pins the n=1 base-case identity in the `(1/n)^2` parallel-pump scaling formula) and that the `coefficient` field round-trips out of `HOSE_FRICTION_COEFFICIENTS`, (c) `computeStandpipeFriction` per-outlet equals `computeFireFriction` at a matched (gpm, length, hose) input so the riser-elevation wrapper cannot drift from the per-outlet friction primitive, (d) all three consumers reject the same unknown-diameter sentinel with the identical `{ error: "Unknown hose diameter." }` deep-equal so a future refactor that inlined a default coefficient anywhere (a silent bug where the consumer would compute a value instead of returning the rejection) surfaces immediately, and (e) every entry in `HOSE_FRICTION_COEFFICIENTS` falls inside the NFA-table sanity band (positive, finite, < 200) so a typo / missing-decimal coefficient edit fails CI before it reaches the corpus.
- **Test count.** Cross-tile-invariants test file moves from 32 tests to 37 (+5 net). Full `npm test` runs 4064 / 4064 green. Phase F §10.1 moves from "two of five shared-computation classes pinned at the bit level" (AWG->cmils + CFM<->m^3/s) to "three of five pinned" (the new NFA-hose-friction-coefficient row joins). The remaining §10.1 rows -- psi<->feet-of-head water at rho=62.4 (Groups B/C/L), and the IRS standard mileage rate (Groups J/P/R) -- already carry primitive-level pins; their per-consumer cross-tile assertions append when the consumer-side calc-modules grow shared call sites (the IRS rate has its JS-const-vs-shard bijection asserted today; the psi/ft round-trip is asserted at the primitive level).

### spec-v14 §13.1 Phase I per-tile derivation-index closeout: 385/385 tiles indexed; check-derivation-coverage graduated to fail-on-missing 2026-05-22

- **[scripts/build-tile-index.mjs](scripts/build-tile-index.mjs)** new deterministic emitter for the per-tile derivation index appendix in [docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md) per spec-v14 §13.1 ("every tile in the TOOLS array has a derivation row in docs/derivations.md that names the formula, the citation, and the worked-example fixture"). The script walks the TOOLS array in [app.js](app.js) and the worked-examples registry at [test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json), then writes one subsection per group (A-Y) with a per-tile table carrying tile_id, display name, citation source (source_publisher + source_section_or_page from the fixture row), and a pointer to the fixture file. Groups H References and Q Historical are recorded with a [docs/v6-audit.md](docs/v6-audit.md) pointer in the fixture column instead of a fixture-file link, per spec-v14 §12.1 / §13.1 paragraph 2 (reference-only tiles use the v6 source-stamp recheck cadence rather than a formula derivation). The section is delimited by `<!-- BEGIN tile-index-v14 -->` / `<!-- END tile-index-v14 -->` markers and round-trips through `--check` mode against the on-disk file just like [scripts/build-corpus.mjs](scripts/build-corpus.mjs); a stale section fails CI with "FAIL: docs/derivations.md tile-index section is stale" pointing the contributor at `npm run audit:tile-index`.
- **[docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md)** the new generated section (385 tiles across 23 active groups; 22 non-reference + 2 reference) lands at the end of the file. Per-tile mention coverage moves from 25 / 385 (6.5%) at the Phase I scaffolding close (commit 938260f, 2026-05-19) to 385 / 385 (100.0%). The Function corpus (v14) generated table (655 rows; unchanged) and the per-formula derivation prose (72 numbered sections) are not touched.
- **[scripts/check-derivation-coverage.mjs](scripts/check-derivation-coverage.mjs)** the §13.1 coverage lint graduates from warn-on-missing to fail-on-missing per spec-v14 §16.2: a TOOLS tile that does not appear anywhere in derivations.md now fails CI with the specific tile_id + group letter named in the error, plus a pointer to `npm run audit:tile-index` for the contributor to regenerate the appendix. The graduation matches the v10 worked-examples ratchet pattern (warn at scaffolding, fail when the per-tile migration closes) and the 2026-05-20 Phase D bounds-fuzzer + the 2026-05-21 Phase C dims-annotation graduations.
- **[package.json](package.json)** new `audit:tile-index` npm script exposes the regenerator as a standalone target; `build-tile-index.mjs --check` joins the `npm run lint` pipeline alongside `build-corpus.mjs --check` (the two share an idempotent-emit-and-diff pattern).
- **Coverage.** Per-group rollup at closeout (counts after the appendix lands): Group A Electrical 33 / 33, B Plumbing 28 / 28, C HVAC 33 / 33, D Restoration 15 / 15, E Construction 41 / 41, F Fire-ground 20 / 20, G Cross-trade 27 / 27, H References 15 / 15, J Trucking 8 / 8, K Mechanic 8 / 8, L Agriculture 9 / 9, M Water 9 / 9, N Stage 7 / 7, O Kitchen 6 / 6, P Field 8 / 8, Q Historical 1 / 1, R Accounting 12 / 12, S Legal 9 / 9, T Lab 10 / 10, U Veterinary 18 / 18, V EMS 20 / 20, W Aviation 18 / 18, X Real Estate 15 / 15, Y Educators 15 / 15. Every active TOOLS tile is now named in docs/derivations.md, and the lint backstops future drift; a new tile added without a tile-index regeneration fails both `audit:tile-index --check` (stale section) and `check-derivation-coverage.mjs` (missing tile mention). Full `npm test` runs 4059 / 4059 green; `npm run lint` and `npm run build` both clean.

### spec-v14 §10.2 Phase F round-trip closeout: five SI-base inverse-conversion pairs + three named-constant pins 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** the Phase F invariants pin file gains eight new tests closing the five remaining spec-v14 §10.2 SI-base round-trip pairs (PSI<->kPa, HP<->kW, gallons-US<->liters, feet<->meters, pound-mass<->kilogram) plus three named-constant pins (1 ft = 0.3048 m exactly per NIST SP 811, 1 lb = 0.45359237 kg exactly per the 1959 international yard-and-pound agreement, 1 hp = 745.6998715822702 W for the mechanical 550 ft-lbf/s convention). Each round-trip walks a representative sweep through `convertUnit` (the Group D cross-cutting crosswalk every calc-*.js module reaches through for unit conversion) and asserts `f^-1(f(x)) == x` within 1e-12 relative; the named-constant pins assert `==` to the bit on the published factor. The pins catch a class of refactors the relative-tolerance round-trip would miss: swapping the international foot for the US survey foot (1200/3937 m, ~2 ppm drift), swapping the international pound for the 5-figure approximation 0.453592 (~1 ppm drift), or swapping the mechanical horsepower for the metric (735.49875 W, -1.4%) or electrical (746 W exact, +0.04%) variants. A coefficient swap on any of the five `UNITS` table entries surfaces immediately at CI rather than drifting downstream into motor FLA / pump brake hp / NIOSH lifting / vehicle load tile output.
- **[package.json](package.json)** new `audit:invariants` and `audit:numerical` npm scripts land per spec-v14 §16.4. The two scripts run the cross-tile-invariants and numerical-stability test subsets directly (without the full `npm test` 4000+ test bring-up), matching the existing `audit:corpus` / `audit:dimensions` / `audit:bounds` / `audit:cross-validation` per-phase audit pattern. The six per-phase audits remain wired into the existing `npm run audit` gate via the lint pipeline, so the pre-PR contract is unchanged.
- **Test count.** Cross-tile-invariants test file moves from 23 tests to 32 (+9 net: 5 SI-base round-trip pairs, 3 named-constant pins, 1 pre-existing PSI/ft-head pin already covered the seventh spec-v14 §10.2 pair via the `feetOfHeadToPsi` primitive). Full `npm test` runs 4059 / 4059 green. Phase F moves from "five shared-computation classes pinned plus partial round-trip coverage" toward the spec-v14 §10.2 contract of "every conversion with a documented inverse round-trips to within the floating-point tolerance"; the empirical-conversion 1e-9 relative tolerance band (Hazen-Williams, refrigerant P-T, Manual J) remains covered by the Phase E numerical-stability suite.

### spec-v14 §9 Phase E closeout: computeStandpipeFriction + manual-j-worker dispatch coverage; Phase E campaign complete 2026-05-22

- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** the Phase E pin file gains seven new tests closing the two remaining spec-v14 §9.1 iteratives flagged as follow-ups in the 2026-05-21 ratchet entry. `computeStandpipeFriction` (the Group F elevation-loop wrapper: riser hydrostatic head 0.434 psi/ft + per-outlet NFA CQ^2L summed over outlet_count) gains six new tests: monotone-increasing in `riser_height_ft` across the 10 / 50 / 100 / 200 / 300 ft sweep, the per-outlet sum linear in `outlet_count` (asserts `friction_total_psi == per_outlet_psi * n` for n in 1..4 against the n=1 baseline so a refactor that swapped the sum for a max or a single-outlet shortcut surfaces), the Q^2 scaling pin in `gpm_per_outlet` (doubling flow quadruples friction; pins the NFA CQ^2L exponent), rejection-path coverage for the four non-positive input combinations plus the unknown hose diameter per spec-v14 §8.3, determinism across repeated calls on `total_psi` / `elevation_psi` / `per_outlet_psi` / `friction_total_psi`, and a four-output bit-pattern pin at the canonical (h=100, n=2, gpm=250, L=50, 2.5 in) input: `elevation_psi=4045b33333333333` (43.4), `per_outlet_psi=4019000000000000` (6.25 exact), `friction_total_psi=4029000000000000` (12.5 exact), `total_psi=404bf33333333333` (55.9). A coefficient swap on any of the three NFA constants (0.434 water-column, C=2 for 2.5 in, the 100 normalizer inside `fireHoseFrictionLoss`) moves at least one pattern.
- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** new `manual-j-worker.js` dispatch test stubs the Web Worker `self` global (addEventListener + postMessage) and exercises the four dispatch branches (cooling / heating / duct / unknown). The cooling and heating branches cross-check the worker's posted result against a direct call to `manualJCooling` / `manualJHeating` at the bit level; the duct branch cross-checks against `computeDuctSize`; the unknown-kind branch asserts the documented `{ error: "Unknown kind." }` sentinel per spec-v14 §8.3; a null `data` payload also returns the same sentinel (the worker reads `e.data || {}` so a malformed message does not crash). The test also asserts the worker echoes the request `id` on every response, so a refactor that dropped the id-tracking discipline used by the front-end Manual J multi-zone request flow would surface here.
- **Test count.** Numerical-stability test file moves from 42 tests to 49 (+7 net: 6 new computeStandpipeFriction tests, 1 new manual-j-worker dispatch test exercising 4 + null branches). Full `npm test` runs 4051 / 4051 green. Phase E moves from "pure-math + bit-pinned" to **complete**: every spec-v14 §9.1 iterative method (Colebrook, psychrometric inverse, refrigerant P-T linear interpolation, the Manual J simplified loop, the Group F pump-discharge + standpipe elevation loops, the Group W density-altitude inversion, the calc-hvac duct sizer) carries the convergence + pathology + NaN-poisoning + determinism + bit-pattern pin contract from spec-v14 §9.1 / §9.2, and the manual-j-worker.js dispatch entry has dispatch coverage on all four branches.

### spec-v14 §9 Phase E ratchet: numerical-stability bit-pattern pins + computeDuctSize coverage 2026-05-21

- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** the Phase E pin file gains six new spec-v14 §9.2 bit-pattern pins and a five-test Phase E section for `computeDuctSize`. The placeholder `assert.equal(bits(f), bits(f))` self-equality assertion on the Colebrook canonical input is replaced with the actual IEEE 754 double encoding `3f92f54c854cbff9` (f = 0.018513866077398664 at Re=1e5, eps/D=1e-4), recorded against Node 20 / 22 / 24. New bit-pattern pins land for `psychrometric` at the indoor-comfort canonical (T=21.1 C, RH=55%: dewPoint_C bits `40276ae4b8442936`, W_kg_kg bits `3f81811587e0365a`), for `saturationVaporPressure_hPa(21.1)` (`4038f8c71d94cae2`) and `dewPointFromVaporPressure_C(15)` (`402a1a310dd9e2c4`) as the Magnus forward / inverse halves of the dew-point round-trip, for `computePDP(NP=100, FL=25, elev=20)` (`4060e00000000000` = 135.0 exactly, pinning the NFA 0.5 psi/ft shortcut against the 0.434 water-column drift), and for `computeDensityAltitude(PA=5000, OAT=25)` (`40bcdc0000000000` = 7388.0, pinning the FAA worked example against any drift in the 120 ft/C or 5.1 ISA-reference constants). A coefficient swap, a Math.log10 implementation change, or a future Node version drift surfaces explicitly at the bit level instead of silently within a tolerance band.
- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** new Phase E section for `computeDuctSize` (the calc-hvac duct sizer wraps an outer bisection on duct diameter around an inner Colebrook iteration on the Darcy friction factor; spec-v14 §9.1 names duct sizing as a regime-bracketed iterative method). Five tests land: monotone-increasing diameter across the residential 200 / 400 / 1000 / 2500 / 5000 CFM sweep; monotone-decreasing diameter as the friction target loosens (0.04 / 0.08 / 0.12 / 0.20 in WC per 100 ft at 1000 CFM); rejection-path coverage of the four non-positive input combinations (cfm <= 0 with two values, friction <= 0 with two values) per spec-v14 §8.3; determinism across repeated calls on the round_diameter_in / velocity_fpm / equivalent_square_in outputs; and the canonical 1000 CFM at 0.08 in WC per 100 ft bit-pattern pin (`402c98a4ca5a3063` for d = 14.298 in). The duct sizer's nested-bisection iteration is now covered to the same depth as the pure-math Colebrook primitive, the calc-fire computePDP, and the calc-aviation density-altitude inversion.
- **[CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)** prior entry em-dash in the 2026-05-21 Phase C closeout line corrected to a hyphen so the grep-checks lint (which scans CHANGELOG.md per scripts/grep-checks.mjs:113 against the EM_DASH U+2014 ban) passes from a clean baseline; the substantive prose is unchanged. Confirmed by a full `npm run lint` pass.
- **Test count.** Numerical-stability test file moves from 36 tests to 42 (+6 net: 4 new bit-pattern pins on existing iteratives, 5 new computeDuctSize Phase E tests, minus 3 placeholder asserts replaced inline). Full `npm test` runs 4044 / 4044 green. Phase E moves from "scaffolded with placeholder pins" toward the spec-v14 §9.2 contract of "bit-stable across Node versions"; the remaining unpinned iteratives (manual-j-worker.js dispatch entry; the calc-fire elevation loop wrapper) ride on top of the pure-math primitives that are now bit-pinned and are tracked for a follow-up Phase E close.

### spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C three-module dims-annotation closeout: calc-plumbing, calc-electrical, calc-construction graduated; Phase C campaign complete (658/658 = 100%) 2026-05-21

- **[calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Fifty-five exports annotated covering the v1 / v3 / v5 / v7 / v9 Group B plumbing surface: `recommendedSupplySize` / `recommendedDrainageSize` / `pipeSizingFromPreset` / `computePipeSizing` (IPC / UPC fixture-unit -> diameter `L`), `computeFrictionLoss` (Hazen-Williams or Darcy: head loss `L`, pressure loss `M L^-1 T^-2` over flow `L^3 T^-1`), `computePipeVolume` (volume `L^3`), `computePumpSize` (flow `L^3 T^-1` x head `L` -> brake / motor hp `M L^2 T^-3`), `computeStaticPressureLossPiping` (elevation `L`, density `M L^-3` -> pressure `M L^-1 T^-2`), `spitzglassFlow` / `computeGasPipeSizing` (NFPA 54 Spitzglass with BTU load `M L^2 T^-3`, dP in. wc `M L^-1 T^-2`, gas flow `L^3 T^-1`, recommended size `L`), `computeSlope`, `pressureConvert`, `computeBackflow`, `computeWaterHammerArrestor` (WSFU + system pressure `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeRecircPumpHead` (head `L`, flow `L^3 T^-1`, pump hp `M L^2 T^-3`), `computeSepticTank` (tank `L^3`), `computeTrapArm` (max trap arm `L`), `computePipeExpansion` (thermal expansion `L`), `computeTanklessGPM` (kBTU/hr input `M L^2 T^-3` -> gpm `L^3 T^-1`), `computeGasLeakRate` (orifice `L`, upstream psi `M L^-1 T^-2` -> leak rate scfh `L^3 T^-1`), `computeStormwaterRational` (catchment `L^2`, rainfall `L T^-1` -> peak flow `L^3 T^-1`), `computeManningSlope` (target flow `L^3 T^-1` -> slope), `computeHydrostaticTest` (test pressure `M L^-1 T^-2`, hold `T`), `computeGreaseTrap` (peak flow `L^3 T^-1` x retention `T` -> trap size `L^3`), `computeGlycolMix` (system volume `L^3` at burst `T`), `computeExpansionTank` (system + fill / max temperature `T`, fill / relief pressure `M L^-1 T^-2` -> tank `L^3`), `computeBackflowLoss` (RP / DCDA / PVB device-class loss `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeWaterHammerSurge` (surge `M L^-1 T^-2`, wave velocity `L T^-1`), `computePumpOperatingPoint`, `computeSepticDrainfield` (area `L^2`, length `L`), `computePipeExpansionLoop` (leg / loop length `L`), `computeRecircLoopSizing` - plus twenty-three exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Plumbing dimension conventions: pressure (psi / psig / in. wc) as `M L^-1 T^-2`; volumetric flow (gpm / cfm / scfh) as `L^3 T^-1`; head (ft) as `L`; volume (gal / ft^3) as `L^3`; pipe sizes / lengths as `L`; pipe area as `L^2`; fluid density (lb/ft^3) as `M L^-3`; temperature (degF) as `T`; WSFU / DFU / slope-in-per-ft / specific gravity / efficiency / loading factors dimensionless.
- **[calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Fifty-five exports annotated covering the v1 / v3 / v5 / v7 / v9 Group A electrical surface: `computeOhmsLaw` (V, I, R, P interconversion - the canonical SI exemplar with voltage `M L^2 T^-3 I^-1`, current `I`, resistance `M L^2 T^-3 I^-2`, power `M L^2 T^-3`), `computeWireAmpacity` (NEC ampacity `I`), `computeVoltageDrop` (NEC 215.2 voltage drop `M L^2 T^-3 I^-1` and percent), `parseConductorShorthand`, `computeConduitFill` (area `L^2`), `computeBoxFill` (volume `L^3`), `computeBreakerSize` (NEC 210.20 continuous-load 125% rule), `computeMotorFLA`, `computeTransformerSize` (kVA `M L^2 T^-3`), `computeThreePhase` (sqrt(3) line-to-line power), `computeConductorResistance` (NEC Chapter 9 Table 8 resistance `M L^2 T^-3 I^-2`), `computeEGCSize` (NEC 250.122), `computeServiceLoad` / `computeServiceLoadStandard` (NEC 220 tiered demand factors), `computeGeneratorSize` / `computeGeneratorMotorStarting` (NEMA code-letter starting kVA), `computePVStringSizing` (cold-Voc / warm-Vmp string sizing for solar inverters), `computeBatteryRuntime` (amp-hour `I T`, system V, dod% -> usable Wh `M L^2 T^-3 T`, hours `T`), `computeVoltageImbalance` (NEMA derate), `computeGFCIReference`, `computeLightingDensity` (W/ft^2), `computePullingTension` (capstan equation: tension `M L T^-2`, sidewall pressure `M T^-2`), `computeBendRadius`, `computePFCorrection` (kVAR + capacitor uF `T^4 I^2 M^-1 L^-2`), `computePhaseBalance`, `computeMultiLoadVoltageDrop`, `computeLVDCDrop`, `computePoEBudget`, `computeTransformerKvaSizing`, `computeShortCircuitPP` (Bussmann point-to-point), `computeArcFlashScreen` (Ralph Lee), `computeMotorBranchFromNameplate` (NEC 430.22 / 430.32), and `computeGroundingElectrodeResistance` (Dwight driven-rod) - plus twenty-three exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Electrical dimension conventions: voltage as `M L^2 T^-3 I^-1` (one volt = one watt per amp); current as the SI base `I`; resistance as `M L^2 T^-3 I^-2`; power (W / kW / hp / kVA) as `M L^2 T^-3`; capacitance as `T^4 I^2 M^-1 L^-2` per the SI farad; charge as `I T` (amp-hours); length and conductor / conduit sizes as `L`; AWG / phase / material / NEC-table labels dimensionless.
- **[calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Sixty-two exports annotated covering the v1 / v2 / v3 / v5 / v7 / v9 Group E carpentry / construction surface: `computeStairs` / `computeStairStringer` / `computeStairStringerV7` (IRC stair geometry with risers, tread depth, stringer `L`), `computeRoofPitch` / `computeRafter` / `computeHipValleyRafter` (pitch dimensionless ratios, rafter / hip / valley length `L`), `computeArea` / `computeBoardFootage` / `computeConcreteVolume` / `computeRebar` / `computeRebarSchedule` (areas `L^2`, volumes `L^3`, board feet `L^3`), `computeLumberSpan` / `computePlywoodSpan` (NDS allowable spans), `computePullout` (withdrawal force `M L T^-2`), `computeBeamLoading` (moment `M L^2 T^-2`, deflection `L`, stress `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeMaterialQuantity`, `computeJoistDeflection` (5wL^4/384EI: load `M T^-2`, span `L`, modulus `M L^-1 T^-2`, moment of inertia `L^4`), `computeFootingArea` (column load `M L T^-2` over allowable soil pressure -> footing `L^2`), `computeTileCount`, `computePaintCoverage`, `computeExcavationVolume` / `computeExcavationBenchPlan`, `computeMasonryCount`, `computeWindPressure` (ASCE 7: V `L T^-1` -> q `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeSnowLoad` (ASCE 7 Pf `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeAnchorEmbedment` (uplift `M L T^-2`, embedment `L`), `computeDrywall`, `computeRoofingSquares`, `computeAsphaltTonnage` (area / depth * density -> tons `M`), `computeAggregate`, `computeMortarMix`, `computeConcreteMixDesign` (ACI 211 w/c, cement `M L^-3`), `computeBoltTorque` (Shigley T = K * F * d: torque `M L^2 T^-2`, preload `M L T^-2`), `computeBendAllowance`, `computeSpeedsAndFeeds` (RPM `T^-1`, feed `L T^-1`), `computeWeldUsage` (deposition `M T^-1`), `computeDemoDebris`, `computeFormworkPressure` (ACI 347 P `M L^-1 T^-2`), `computeHelicalPile` (torque `M L^2 T^-2` * capacity factor -> capacity `M L T^-2`), `computeCraneLiftCheck`, `computeResidentialFraming` - plus twenty-three exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Construction dimension conventions: lengths / spans / depths as `L`; areas as `L^2`; volumes (yd^3 / ft^3) as `L^3`; forces (lb / kip) as `M L T^-2`; uniform loads (plf) as `M T^-2`; pressures / stresses (psi / psf) as `M L^-1 T^-2`; moments (lb-ft / lb-in) as `M L^2 T^-2`; mass (tons / lb) as `M`; density (lb/ft^3 / pcf) as `M L^-3`; temperature (degF) as `T`; rotational / linear speeds (RPM / mph / fpm) as `T^-1` / `L T^-1`; pitch ratios / waste factors / k-factors / safety factors dimensionless.
- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs)** calc-plumbing.js, calc-electrical.js, and calc-construction.js join the `GRADUATED_MODULES` set alongside the prior closeouts. Total dims-annotation coverage moves from 486 / 658 (73.9%) to **658 / 658 (100%) - the spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C dims-annotation campaign is complete**: every export across all 25 modules (24 calc-*.js + pure-math.js) now carries a parseable `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation, and the lint hard-fails on any future export that ships without one. This closes the v14 §7.1 launch gate to the same fully-graduated state the bounds-fuzzer reached at the 2026-05-20 Phase D campaign close (commit c17ae3f).
- **[scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs)** two gzipped caps bumped to absorb the inline dims-annotation prose: calc-plumbing.js 30000 -> 31500 (post-bump utilization 86.5%); calc-electrical.js 42000 -> 44000 (post-bump utilization 87.6%). calc-construction.js fit within its existing 37000 B cap (post-bump utilization 89.4%, no change needed). Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile split remains the preferred long-term path once a bundle routinely brushes its cap; these bumps are the minimum needed to keep the module-size lint green while the per-tile split is planned.

### spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C two-module dims-annotation closeout: calc-edu, calc-ems graduated 2026-05-21

- **[calc-edu.js](calc-edu.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Thirty-six exports annotated: eighteen compute / helper functions covering the v12 Group Y educator surface - `countSentences`, `countWords`, `countSyllablesInWord`, `countSyllables` (text tokenizers; all inputs and outputs dimensionless integer counts), `computeReadability` (Kincaid 1975 FKGL + Flesch 1948 FRE over sentence / word / syllable counts; all dimensionless ratios), `computeStatistics` (mean / median / mode / sample and population variance + standard deviation over a number list; the §7.1 caller-typed convention surfaces the data series and outputs as dimensionless), `computeQuadratic` (closed-form ax^2 + bx + c root pair via the discriminant), `computeScientificNotation` (decimal -> mantissa / exponent split), `countSigFigs`, `roundToSigFigs`, `computeSigFigs` (the sig-fig family), `computeCodonTable` (DNA / RNA -> amino-acid lookup), `computeBaseConvert` (base 2 through 36 arithmetic), `computeGPA` (weighted-credit grade-point rollup), `computeConfidenceInterval` (z-based proportion CI and t-based mean CI), `computeLinearSystem2x2` (Cramer's-rule 2x2 solver), `computeLexileBand` (CCSS grade -> Lexile band reference), `computeStandardsBasedGrade` (3 / 4 / B+ standards-based rollup), `computeBellCurve` (z = (x - mean) / sd with normal-CDF percentile lookup), `computeAlternateReadability` (ARI / SMOG / Gunning Fog / Coleman-Liau companion family), and `computePeriodicElement` (symbol / name / Z lookup) - plus eighteen exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. The educator surface is the canonical "dimensionless everywhere" calc module: counts, ratios, z-scores, grade points, percentile bands, and reference-table indices are all dimensionless quantities per the v14 §7.1 caller-typed convention.
- **[calc-ems.js](calc-ems.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Forty exports annotated: twenty compute functions covering the v8 / v12 Group V EMS surface - `computeGCS` (eye / verbal / motor 3-15 score with intubated modifier), `computeParkland` (Parkland formula 4 mL/kg/%TBSA over the first 24 h with the 8-h / 16-h split: weight `M`, time `T`, fluid volume `L^3`, rate `L^3 T^-1`), `computeCPSS` (Cincinnati Pre-hospital Stroke Scale 3-item screen), `computeAPGAR` (5-item neonatal score 0-10), `computeIvDripRate` (mL/hr `L^3 T^-1` + gtt/min `T^-1` from volume `L^3` / time `T` / drop factor `L^-3`), `computeO2CylinderTime` (gas-law minutes-to-empty from cylinder constant / pressure `M L^-1 T^-2` / flow `L^3 T^-1`), `computePediatricWeight` (APLS / Luscombe age `T` -> weight `M`), `computeShockIndex` (HR `T^-1` / SBP `M L^-1 T^-2` ratio), `computeMAP` (mean arterial pressure = DBP + (SBP - DBP) / 3 with pulse-pressure companion), `computeAnionGap` (Na - Cl - HCO3 with the Figge albumin correction), `computeCorrectedCalcium` (Payne 1973 albumin-corrected serum calcium), `computeCHA2DS2VASc` (AF stroke-risk 0-9 score), `computeWellsDVT` / `computeWellsPE` (DVT / PE pre-test probability), `computePERC` (Kline PERC 8-item rule), `computeRuleOf9s` (TBSA burn estimation), `computePedsVitals` (age-banded HR `T^-1` / RR `T^-1` / SBP `M L^-1 T^-2` normal ranges), `computeNIHSS` (11-item stroke severity 0-42), `computeSTART` (multi-casualty triage tag), and `computeDrugConcentration` (mcg/kg/min -> mL/hr infusion rate over weight `M` and stock concentration `M L^-3`) - plus twenty exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Patient weights surface as `M`; pressures (SBP / DBP / cylinder psi) as `M L^-1 T^-2`; fluid volumes as `L^3`; fluid rates as `L^3 T^-1`; respiratory and heart rates as `T^-1`; per-minute drop counts as `T^-1`; mass concentrations (calcium / albumin g/dL, mg/mL stock) as `M L^-3`; ages / durations as `T`; per-item scores, severity bands, and triage / verdict strings as dimensionless per the v14 §7.1 caller-typed convention.
- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs)** calc-edu.js and calc-ems.js join the `GRADUATED_MODULES` set alongside the prior closeouts: the lint now hard-fails if any future export in these modules ships without a dims annotation, matching the spec-v14 §16.2 measurement -> hard-gate ratchet pattern. Total dims-annotation coverage moves from 313 / 658 (47.6%) to 389 / 658 (59.1%); twenty of the twenty-five modules are now graduated. The remaining five calc-modules (construction, cross, electrical, hvac, plumbing) continue in measurement mode pending per-module closeouts in priority order.
- **[scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs)** calc-ems.js gzipped cap bumped 27000 -> 28500 to absorb the ~1500 bytes of inline dims-annotation prose added by this closeout (post-bump utilization ~88%). calc-edu.js fit within its existing 26000 B cap (post-bump utilization 88.9%, no change needed). Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile split remains the preferred long-term path once a bundle routinely brushes its cap; this bump is the minimum needed to keep the module-size lint green while the per-tile split is planned.

### spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C calc-vet.js dims-annotation closeout: graduated 2026-05-21

AAFP cat scheme), `computeGestation` (mean + range due-date arithmetic for dog 63 / cat 65 / horse 340 / cow 283), `computeETTSizing` (BSAVA ETT internal-diameter + IV catheter gauge + tube-length bands by species / weight), `computeAnesthesiaVitals` (HR / RR / MAP / SpO2 / ETCO2 normal ranges per species), `computeASAReference` (ASA Physical Status I-V + E modifier), `computeBloodworkRanges` (typical adult CBC + chemistry reference ranges by species), `computeUrineSG` (hyposthenuric / isosthenuric / well-concentrated bands by species), `computeTargetWeightLoss` (target_RER from target_kg^0.75 + weeks-to-target at 1% / 1.5% / 2% per-week loss rates), `computeToxicity` (chocolate theobromine / xylitol / raisin-grape / ethylene-glycol per-toxin LD50 lookup with APCC overlay), `computeBreedPredispositions` (filter-by-breed-or-condition reference), `computeSteadyStateConcentration` (PK Css = (Dose * F) / (CL * tau) with patient-weight scaling), `computeVaccineSchedule` (AAHA / AAFP core + non-core with state-AHJ rabies overlay), `computeHeartwormDose` (FDA-labeled weight-band lookup for ivermectin / milbemycin / selamectin products), and `computeCrystalloidPlan` (maintenance + replacement + itemized vomiting / diarrhea / blood / surgical losses with both drip-set conversions) - plus eighteen exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Patient weights surface as `M` (kg / lb); fluid volumes as `L^3` (mL); fluid rates as `L^3 T^-1` (mL/hr); drops-per-minute as `T^-1`; time as `T` (hr / days / weeks); ETT internal diameter and tube length as `L` (mm / cm); per-mass dose, mg/kg/min clearance, kcal-per-day caloric outputs, ug/mL concentrations, AAHA-band labels, and species / size-band / breed strings all surface as `dimensionless` per the spec-v14 §7.1 caller-typed convention.

### spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C calc-aviation.js dims-annotation closeout: graduated 2026-05-21

- **[calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Thirty-five new exports annotated on top of the prior `computeDensityAltitude` seed: seventeen compute / decode functions covering the v12 Group W surface - `computeCrosswind` (HW/CW from runway, wind direction, and wind speed via cosine/sine decomposition), `computeETE` (ETE/ETA from distance / groundspeed / departure local time), `computeHypoxiaAltitude` (14 CFR §91.211 cabin-altitude regulatory bands), `computePressureAltitude` (PA = field elevation + 1000 * (29.92 - altimeter inHg)), `computePhoneticAlphabet` (ICAO / NATO phonetic alphabet translator), `computeFuelPlanning` (trip + reserve fuel in gal / lb against 14 CFR 91.151 / 91.167 minimums), `computeWindTriangle` (WCA = asin(crosswind / TAS); true heading + groundspeed in the small-angle regime), `computeTopOfDescent` (3-to-1 rule: distance_nm = altitude/1000 * 3; rate = GS * 5.556 fpm), `computeWeatherPhrasing` (METAR / TAF abbreviation reference), `computeTransponderCodes` (1200 / 7500 / 7600 / 7700 reserved-code lookup with octal-only validation), `computeStandardTurn` (FAA-H-8083-15B Rate One = 3 deg/sec; bank rule of thumb (TAS/10)+7 and the atan(V*omega/g) exact form; climb/descent rate = GS * gradient_ft_per_nm / 60), `computeTrueAirspeed` (ICAO Standard Atmosphere: TAS = CAS / sqrt(rho_ratio); rho_ratio = (1 - DA/145442)^4.2561; Mach via ISA speed-of-sound), `computeSectionalSymbols` (FAA Aeronautical Chart User's Guide symbology reference), `computeAircraftCategory` (14 CFR §1.1 pilot-certification + airworthiness-certification cat / class reference), `computeMagneticVariation` (TVMDC True <-> Magnetic with "East is least, West is best" mnemonic), `decodeMetar` (FAA AC 00-45H / NWS FMH-1 METAR / SPECI tokenizer + field decoder), and `decodeTaf` (TAF prevailing-forecast + FM / BECMG / TEMPO / PROBxx change-group splitter) - plus eighteen exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Altitudes surface as `L` (ft); airspeed / groundspeed / wind as `L T^-1` (kt); time as `T` (hr / min / sec); pressure / altimeter setting as `M L^-1 T^-2` (inHg / hPa); fuel volume as `L^3` (gal); fuel weight as `M L T^-2` (lb); temperature as `T` (degC; the v14 §7.1 T-glyph doubles for time and temperature per the ASCII shortcut); heading / variation / WCA / Mach / density-ratio / O2-required booleans / weather-phrasing strings all surface as `dimensionless`.

Total dims-annotation coverage moves from 242 / 658 (36.8%) to 277 / 658 (42.1%); seventeen of the twenty-five modules are now graduated. The remaining eight calc-modules (construction, cross, edu, electrical, ems, hvac, plumbing, vet) continue in measurement mode pending per-module closeouts in priority order.

### spec-v14 §7.1 Phase C calc-fire.js dims-annotation closeout: graduated 2026-05-21

- **[calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js)** every export now carries a `// dims: in { ... } out: { ... }` annotation per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensional-analysis contract. Thirty new exports annotated on top of the four prior annotation seeds (computeFireFriction, computePDP, computeHydrantFlow, computeAerialLadderReach): sixteen compute functions covering the v1/v2/v3/v7/v9 Group F surface - `computeRequiredFireFlow` (ISO PPC NFF with construction + occupancy + exposure + communication factors), `computeMasterStreamReach` (master-stream reach as sqrt(P/P_typical) of base-reach), `computeFoam` (NFA foam-volume rollup: area * rate * foam% * duration), `computeSmokeReading` (categorical SMOKE_READING reference table), `computeReverseLayFriction` (NFA CQ^2L extended to tandem-pump with parallel-section (1/n)^2 scaling), `computeSprinklerDensity` (NFPA 13 hazard density gpm = area * density), `computeStandpipeFriction` (NFPA 14 riser-elevation + per-outlet CQ^2L sum), `computeLadderPipeReach` (aerial geometry + master-stream forward projection), `computeBrakingDistance` (d = v^2 / (30 * (mu +/- grade%/100)) + reaction distance), `computeConfinedSpacePurge` (OSHA 1910.146 minutes = V * N / CFM), `computeRopeMA` (theoretical MA from rig type * pulley-efficiency^pulleys), `computeSlingAngle` (ASME B30.9 tension = W / (n * sin(theta/2)) with choker-factor reduction), `computeIsoNeededFireFlow` (ISO Public Protection Classification: NFF = Ci * Oi * (1 + X + P)), `computeScbaCylinderTime` (NFPA 1981 gas-law time-to-alarm = (P_start - P_alarm) / P_rated * V_rated / consumption), `computeNFPA1142WaterSupply` (Q = V * occ * con / 5 with exposure 1.5x and sprinkler 0.5x multipliers and tanker-trip rollup), and `computeConfinedSpaceVent` (NIOSH 80-106 air-changes ventilation with contaminant-keyed defaults) - plus fourteen exported DOM-mount renderers each annotated with the `dom_side_effect: dimensionless` sentinel per the v14 §7.1 contract for void-returning UI shells. Pressures surface as `M L^-1 T^-2` (psi), flows as `L^3 T^-1` (gpm, cfm, scfm), volumes as `L^3` (ft^3, gal, scf), forces as `M L T^-2` (lb), and mechanical-advantage / efficiency / factor / score values as `dimensionless` per spec-v14 §7.1.

The graduation matches the spec-v14 §16.2 measurement -> hard-gate ratchet pattern.
