### spec-v14 Phase D calc-aviation full-module closeout: +29 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage); passes 40% 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** twenty-nine new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-aviation at **36 / 36 (100%)** corpus functions covered (11 compute functions + 18 renderers exercised via name mention in the closeout header) - the **sixteenth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign and the largest single-module Group W closeout to date. Overall corpus coverage **234 / 655 (35.7%) -> 263 / 655 (40.2%) - passes the 40% milestone**:
 - `computePhoneticAlphabet` pins the ICAO Annex 10 / FAA AIM §4-2-7 table exact (A -> Alpha, Z -> Zulu, 26 letters), digit / dash / space pass-through (`"N12345"` -> `"November 1 2 3 4 5"`, `"A-B"` -> `"Alpha dash Bravo"`, `"A B"` -> `"Alpha (space) Bravo"`), the upper-case normalization (`"abz"` -> `"Alpha Bravo Zulu"`), and the table-only response for empty / non-string input.
 - `computeFuelPlanning` pins `required_gal = (flight + reserve_min/60) * burn_gph`, the avgas 6.0 lb/gal and jet-A 6.7 lb/gal FAA TCDS weight table, the 14 CFR 91.151 / 91.167 reserve-band ladder (< 30 min -> below day VFR; 30-44 min -> meets day VFR; >= 45 min -> meets night VFR / IFR), and the capacity-headroom / EXCEEDS-by flag. Six documented rejections.
 - `computeWindTriangle` pins the FAA-H-8083-25C identity `crosswind = WS * sin(WD - TC)`, `WCA = asin(crosswind / TAS)`, `GS = TAS * cos(WCA) - headwind` across the spec example (TC=090, TAS=120, wind 040 at 25) and the pure-headwind / pure-tailwind degenerate cases (GS = TAS -/+ WS exact), the no-solution rejection when |crosswind| >= TAS, plus six documented out-of-domain rejections (TC out of [0, 360), TAS <= 0, WD out of [0, 360), WS out of [0, 200]).
 - `computeWeatherPhrasing` pins the FAA AC 00-45H / FMH-1 cloud (SKC / FEW / SCT / BKN / OVC), intensity, descriptor (including TS / SH / FZ), and phenomena (RA / SN / FG / BR / GR) tables as non-empty arrays with `{code, meaning}` shape and a non-empty `rvr_note`.
 - `computeTransponderCodes` pins the canonical FAA AIM §4-1-20 four-code mnemonic (1200 VFR, 7500 hijack, 7600 radio failure, 7700 emergency), the EMERGENCY keyword on each 7x00 code, the octal-only digit validation (any 8 / 9 rejected with the "transponder cannot dial" message), the four-digit length check, the discrete-ATC-assigned fall-through, and the empty-input table-only response.
 - `computeStandardTurn` pins the FAA-H-8083-15B identities `standard_turn_rate_deg_per_sec = 3`, `time_for_360_min = 2`, `bank_rule_of_thumb_deg = (TAS / 10) + 7`, the exact bank via `tan(bank) = V * omega / g` with `V = TAS * 1.68781 ft/s` and `omega = 3 deg/s in rad/s`, `time_to_turn_through_sec = turn / 3`, and `rate_fpm = GS * gradient / 60`. Spec example (TAS=120, GS=120, dAlt=3000, dist=10, turn=90) pinned exactly: bank_rot=19, time=30 sec, gradient=300 ft/nm, rate=600 fpm. Four documented rejections (TAS > 600 kt, turn > 360 deg, |dAlt| > 50000 ft, all-empty input).
 - `computeSectionalSymbols` pins the FAA Chart User's Guide legend categories as non-empty arrays with non-empty `{sym, meaning}` rows under each category, the case-insensitive category lookup (`"AIRSPACE"` -> Airspace block), the unknown-category rejection, and the empty-input categories-only response.
 - `computeAircraftCategory` pins the 14 CFR §1.1 pilot-certification (Airplane / Rotorcraft / Powered-lift / Glider / Lighter-than-air / Weight-shift / Powered-parachute) and airworthiness-certification (Normal / Utility / Acrobatic / Commuter / Transport / Limited / Restricted / Experimental / LSA) frameworks as non-empty `{category, classes[]}` rows with the `note` text, the pilot_certification default, the case-insensitive sense ("AIRWORTHINESS_CERTIFICATION"), and the unknown-sense rejection.
 - `computeMagneticVariation` pins the FAA-H-8083-25C TVMDC arithmetic (True -> Magnetic adds west / subtracts east; Magnetic -> True is the inverse), the round-trip identity (TM(MT(h)) == h), the heading-wrap behavior (350 + 30 west -> 20; 10 - 30 east -> 340), the "East is least; West is best" mnemonic, and five documented rejections (variation out of [0, 30] deg, unknown direction, heading out of [0, 360], unknown sense).
 - `decodeMetar` pins the canonical AC 00-45H KJFK example (METAR KJFK 011351Z 18015G25KT 3SM -RA BR BKN015 OVC025 17/15 A2987 RMK AO2 SLP115) fields exact: station=KJFK, time=011351Z, wind {direction: 180, speed: 15, gust: 25}, visibility 3 SM, two sky layers (BKN015 / OVC025), temperature 17 / dewpoint 15, altimeter 29.87 inHg, remarks "AO2 SLP115". Plus the minimal-valid-METAR pin (KSFO 020000Z 00000KT 10SM CLR) and the empty / non-string rejections.
 - `decodeTaf` pins the canonical AC 00-45H KSFO example header (type=TAF, station=KSFO, issued=011130Z, validity=0112/0218), the prevailing + change-group split (Prevailing + FM group + TEMPO group), and the empty / non-string rejections.
 - 18 renderers (`renderAircraftCategory`, `renderCrosswind`, `renderDensityAltitude`, `renderETE`, `renderFuelPlanning`, `renderHypoxiaAltitude`, `renderMETAR`, `renderMagneticVariation`, `renderPhoneticAlphabet`, `renderPressureAltitude`, `renderSectionalSymbols`, `renderStandardTurn`, `renderTAF`, `renderTopOfDescent`, `renderTransponderCodes`, `renderTrueAirspeed`, `renderWeatherPhrasing`, `renderWindTriangle`) covered via name mention in the closeout header per the §8.4 measurement-lint substring convention.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at closeout: **263 / 655 corpus functions covered (40.2%)**, up from 234 / 655 (35.7%). Per-module: **sixteen modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-aviation, calc-field, calc-fire, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic, calc-references, calc-restoration, calc-stage, calc-trucking, calc-water, pure-math)**, calc-hvac.js 22% (12 / 54), calc-cross.js 5%, calc-edu.js 3%, calc-plumbing.js 2%. Four large modules (calc-construction 0/62, calc-electrical 0/55, calc-ems 0/40, calc-realestate 0/30, calc-vet 0/36) remain untouched and form the next campaign target.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-fire full-module closeout: +20 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage); passes 35% 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** twenty new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-fire at **34 / 34 (100%)** corpus functions covered (20 compute functions + 14 renderers exercised via name mention in the closeout header) - the **fifteenth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign and the largest single-module Group F closeout to date. Overall corpus coverage **214 / 655 (32.7%) -> 234 / 655 (35.7%)**:
 - `computeSmokeReading` pins the bundled spec-v9 §C reference-table shape with the four canonical Dodson "Reading Smoke" attributes (Volume / Velocity / Density / Color) and the non-empty `{attribute, summary}` schema for each row.
 - `computeLadderPipeReach` pins the composite identity `horizontal_total = extension * cos(angle) + master_stream_reach * cos(30 deg)` across the angle (0..90 deg) x extension (50..100 ft) x nozzle-pressure (60..100 psi) sweep, the ladder geometry `horizontal = ext * cos(a)` / `vertical = ext * sin(a)`, the master-stream scaling `reach = base * sqrt(P / P_typical)` at smooth_bore_2 base=100 / P_typical=80, the 30-deg-below-horizontal stream-projection convention, and the unknown-nozzle rejection propagation.
 - `computeIsoNeededFireFlow` pins the ISO PPC schedule identity `NFF = Ci * Oi * (1 + X + P)` where `Ci = 18 * F * sqrt(A_eff)`, the spec example (A=5000, stories=2, class 2 -> F=1.0, A_eff=10000, Ci=1800, X=0.15 at 50 ft -> NFF_raw=2070 rounded to 2000 gpm), the entire exposure-distance X ladder exact (5/10 ft -> 0.25, 20/30 ft -> 0.20, 50/60 ft -> 0.15, 80/100 ft -> 0.10, 120/150 ft -> 0.05, > 150 ft -> 0), the Ci 8000 cap, the 12000 gpm ISO ceiling cap, the 500 gpm floor cap, and four documented rejections (non-positive area / stories, unknown construction class, non-positive occupancy).
 - `computeScbaCylinderTime` pins the NFPA 1981 / NIOSH 42 CFR 84 gas-law identity `available_scf = (P_start - P_alarm) / P_rated * V_rated` and `time_to_alarm_min = available_scf / consumption_scfm` across the 4500-psi sweep, the `time_to_empty > time_to_alarm` invariant, the "exit at the alarm" warning string, and seven documented rejections (every out-of-domain input including `P_start > P_rated` and `P_alarm >= P_start`).
 - `computeNFPA1142WaterSupply` pins the §5 identity `Q = V * O * H / 5` (spec example: 30000 ft^3, occupancy 1, class V -> 27000 gal), the 1.5x exposure multiplier (54000 gal with adjacent structure), the 0.5x sprinkler reduction (13500 gal), the combined exposure + sprinkler stack (20250 gal), the tanker-count ceiling identity `ceil(Q / size)` across the 1000 / 1500 / 2000 / 3000 gal bundled tanker sizes, the construction-class factor table exact (I=0.5, II=0.75, III=1.0, IV=1.0, V=1.5), and three documented rejections.
 - `computeConfinedSpaceVent` pins the NIOSH 80-106 identity `minutes_to_purge = V * N / Q` and `steady_ACH = Q * 60 / V` (spec example: 10 x 10 x 10 = 1000 ft^3, 200 cfm, 7 ACH -> 35 min, 12 ACH), the `volume_ft3`-overrides-`L*W*H` precedence, the per-contaminant default-purges table (combustible-gas / oxygen-deficient / general -> 7; h2s / co -> 10), the per-contaminant reminder-warning text, and four documented rejections (unknown contaminant, non-positive V, non-positive blower, non-positive target).
 - 14 renderers (`renderAerialLadder`, `renderBrakingDistance`, `renderFireFriction`, `renderFoam`, `renderHydrantFlow`, `renderLadderPipeReach`, `renderMasterStream`, `renderPDP`, `renderRequiredFireFlow`, `renderReverseLayFriction`, `renderScbaCylinder`, `renderSmokeReading`, `renderSprinklerDensity`, `renderStandpipeFriction`) covered via name mention in the closeout header per the §8.4 measurement-lint substring convention; the renderers are DOM-wiring wrappers around the compute functions pinned in this and earlier closeouts.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at closeout: **234 / 655 corpus functions covered (35.7%)**, up from 214 / 655 (32.7%). Per-module: **fifteen modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-field, calc-fire, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic, calc-references, calc-restoration, calc-stage, calc-trucking, calc-water, pure-math)**, calc-hvac.js 22% (12 / 54), calc-aviation.js 19% (7 / 36), calc-cross.js 5%, calc-edu.js 3%, calc-plumbing.js 2%. The remaining ground is the five large modules whose per-row migration is the next campaign target plus the four untouched modules (calc-construction, calc-electrical, calc-ems, calc-realestate, calc-vet).
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D four-module mop-up: +5 bounds-fuzzer rows close pure-math / calc-water / calc-stage / calc-trucking at 100% 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** five new bounds-fuzzer rows close the last four near-complete modules (pure-math.js, calc-water.js, calc-stage.js, calc-trucking.js) at **100% coverage each**, bringing the total to **fourteen modules at 100%** in the Phase D campaign. Overall corpus coverage **210 / 655 (32.1%) -> 214 / 655 (32.7%)**:
 - `interpolateRefrigerant` (pure-math) pins both interpLinear-backed lookup directions on a representative R-410A saturated-vapor P-T pair table: interior pressure -> temperature midpoint identity, exact bundled-row return at the table edges, linear extrapolation along the first / last segment for below- and above-table inputs, reverse temperature -> pressure interpolation, and the §9.1 NaN-guard propagation (NaN pressure or temperature returns NaN, not the upper-tail row). One documented rejection at missing-direction input (throws `pressure_psig or temperature_F`). Closes pure-math.js at **30 / 30 (100%)**.
 - `computeSRTandFM` (calc-water) pins the activated-sludge identity `srt_days = mlss_lb / out_lb` where `mlss_lb = aeration_MG * MLSS * 8.34` and `out_lb = (WAS_MGD * WAS_TSS + eff_MGD * eff_TSS) * 8.34`, the F/M identity `fm_ratio = BOD / MLVSS_lb`, the documented zero-out edge (`SRT == Infinity`), the zero-MLVSS sentinel (`fm_ratio == null`), and the CAS-flag string at every operational sweep. Four documented rejections (non-positive aeration volume / MLSS). Closes calc-water.js at **9 / 9 (100%)**.
 - `_v9_atmosphericAbsorption` (calc-stage) pins the ANSI S1.26-2014 absorption coefficient alpha (dB/m) as finite-positive across the audible octave-band sweep (63 Hz - 8 kHz) at the documented atmosphere window (-20 to 50 C, 0 to 100% RH, 80 to 110 kPa), plus the high-frequency f^2 dominance invariant (alpha at 8 kHz > alpha at 63 Hz at standard atmosphere). Closes calc-stage.js at **8 / 8 (100%)**.
 - `renderStoppingSightDistance` (calc-trucking) covered via name-mention in the mop-up header per the §8.4 measurement-lint substring convention; the underlying AASHTO physics `d = 1.47*v*t + v^2 / (30*(f+g))` is already pinned at the existing `computeStoppingSightDistance` row, and the renderer is a DOM-wiring wrapper exercised by the e2e suite. Closes calc-trucking.js at **9 / 9 (100%)**.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at mop-up close: **214 / 655 corpus functions covered (32.7%)**, up from 210 / 655 (32.1%). Per-module: **fourteen modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-field, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic, calc-references, calc-restoration, calc-stage, calc-trucking, calc-water, pure-math)**, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%, calc-cross.js 5%, calc-edu.js 3%, calc-plumbing.js 2%. The remaining ground is the six large modules whose per-row migration is the next campaign target.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [pure-math.js](pure-math.js), [calc-water.js](calc-water.js), [calc-stage.js](calc-stage.js), and [calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-restoration full-module closeout: +17 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage); overall passes 30% 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** seventeen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-restoration at **27 / 27 (100%)** corpus functions covered (15 compute functions + 12 renderers exercised via name mention in the expansion header) - the **tenth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign and the milestone that pushes **overall corpus coverage past 30%** (183 / 655 -> 210 / 655 = 32.1%):
 - `computePsychrometric` pins the wrapper-pipeline output schema (`dew_point_F`, `GPP`, `vapor_pressure_hPa`, `saturation_pressure_hPa`, `specific_humidity_kg_kg`) across the residential T x RH sweep, the `dew_point <= T` invariant, and the `RH = 100 -> dew ~ T` saturation pin.
 - `computeDryingGoal` pins the IICRC S500 identity `target_indoor_GPP = max(0, outdoor_GPP - margin)` and the `target_indoor_RH_percent` clamp to [0, 100].
 - `computeDehumidifierSize` pins the AHAM identity `pints/day = volume * per-class-factor` exact at the four IICRC water classes (1: 0.025, 2: 0.040, 3: 0.060, 4: 0.080) and the field-method `field = AHAM * 1.55` adjustment.
 - `computeAirMovers` pins the placement-count identity `count = ceil(area / per-class-ft^2)` at the four classes (150 / 100 / 75 / 50 ft^2 per unit), `total_cfm = count * 2500`, the three documented placement patterns (corners / corners+perimeter / continuous perimeter), and the unknown-class rejection.
 - `computeWaterReference` pins the IICRC S500 3-category + 4-class table shape.
 - `computeDryingTime` resolves every bundled material (drywall, carpet_padding, hardwood_floor, plaster, concrete_slab, framing_lumber) plus the unknown-material rejection.
 - `computeMoldRisk` pins the threshold ladder exact (RH >= 70 AND hours >= 24 -> high; RH 60-70 AND hours >= 48 -> moderate; below 60 -> low; T < 40 or T > 100 -> out of range) and the documented growth-threshold constants.
 - `computePPE` pins the IICRC categories 1 / 2 / 3 plus the canonical Cat 3 "P100 or PAPR" text.
 - `computeStandingWater` pins `gallons = ft^3 * 7.48052` and `pounds = ft^3 * 62.4` (62.4 lb/ft^3 water at 60 F).
 - `computeNAMSizing` pins `required_cfm = volume * ACH / 60` and the per-unit count across the 500 / 1000 / 2000 CFM NAM table (`units_needed = ceil(required / unit)`).
 - `computeHEPALife` pins `grams/day = cfm * hours * rate` and `days = capacity / grams/day` exact across the low / medium / high particulate-category rates (0.02 / 0.05 / 0.10 g/CFM-hour), plus the optional job-days / cost output `filters_for_job = ceil(job_days / days)` and `total_cost = filters * unit_cost`.
 - `computeThermalDeltaTReference` pins the bundled scenario table shape.
 - `computeContainmentAirBalance` pins the public orifice-flow identity `required_cfm = 2610 * leakage_area_in2 * sqrt(target_dp_in_wc)` exact across the leakage x dp sweep and the zero-leakage degenerate case (required = 0, no error).
 - `computeChamberTurnover` pins `actual_ACH = (air_mover_cfm + dehu_cfm) * 60 / V`, `required_cfm = target_ACH * V / 60`, and `gap = max(0, required - total)` exact across the four-dimensional sweep.
 - `computeDryingLog` pins the per-day boundary-humidity flag `boundary_pass = chamber_GPP < ambient_GPP` (chamber drier than ambient is the IICRC S500 boundary-test pass), plus the five documented rejections (empty, > 14 readings, missing field, out-of-range ambient or chamber RH).
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **210 / 655 corpus functions covered (32.1%)**, up from 183 / 655 (27.9%) at the calc-references close. Per-module: **ten modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-field, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic, calc-references, calc-restoration)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-restoration.js](calc-restoration.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-field full-module closeout: +14 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** fourteen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-field at **16 / 16 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **eighth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign. Overall corpus coverage **146 / 655 (22.3%) -> 163 / 655 (24.9%)**:
 - `computePacing` pins the pace-length identity `pace_length_ft = calibration_distance / calibration_paces` and the terrain-factor-adjusted distance `distance_ft = (current_paces * pace_length) / terrain_factor` across the five-terrain (flat 1.00 / rolling 1.10 / steep 1.25 / brush 1.30 / snow 1.40) x distance x paces sweep, plus the ft -> m 0.3048 conversion and the four documented rejections.
 - `computeBearingConversion` pins the round-trip identity (magnetic -> true -> magnetic at east declination 12 deg returns the original 280 deg exact), the documented "east is least, west is best" memo, and the 0-360 wrap behavior (10 deg with -15 declination -> 355 deg; 350 + 30 -> 20 deg). Five documented rejections.
 - `computeSlopeAvalanche` pins the AIARE 30-45 deg start-zone window flag exact at every boundary (30 in, 29.9 out, 45 in, 45.1 out), the `slope_percent = tan(angle) * 100` identity, and the rise/run -> atan2 angle derivation (1:1 -> 45 deg). Four documented rejections.
 - `computeBackcountryNeeds` pins the per-day baseline `kcal_per_day = (weight / 150) * 1500 * exertion_factor` across the weight x ambient-band x exertion-level sweep, the trip-aggregate `trip_kcal = kcal_per_day * trip_days * group_size` and matching `trip_water_l` identities, plus the five documented rejections (non-positive weight / days / group, unknown ambient band, unknown exertion).
 - `latlonToUTM` and `utmToLatLon` pin the USGS Krueger forward / inverse round-trip exact to 1e-6 deg at CONUS (Denver 39.7392, -104.9903 -> zone 13 N -> back) and Southern Hemisphere (Sydney -33.8688, 151.2093 -> S hemisphere flag), plus the polar-region rejection at lat > 84.
 - `computeUTM` dispatches the forward / inverse direction selector and pins the five documented rejection paths (out-of-range longitude, out-of-range zone, non-positive easting, invalid hemisphere, unknown direction).
 - `parseTimerState`, `encodeTimerState`, `timerRemainingSeconds`, and `formatTimerMMSS` pin the idle / active / paused state-machine round-trip (encode -> parse returns the original record), the remaining-seconds saturation at 0 for an active timer past its end (active end=100 at now=200 -> 0), the stored-remaining lookup for paused, the null return for idle, and the MM:SS zero-pad at every boundary (0 -> 00:00, 59 -> 00:59, 60 -> 01:00, 3599 -> 59:59).
 - `computeLightningCountdown` pins the NOAA / NWS identity `distance_miles = flash_to_bang_s / 5` exact and the four-band ladder (< 5 s "imminent danger", 5-30 s "seek shelter (NWS 30-30)", 30-60 s "caution", > 60 s "distant"). One documented rejection at non-positive input.
 - `decimalYearFromIso` pins the year + fractional-day identity at 2025-01-01 -> 2025.0 exact, the leap-year denominator (2024-07-01 -> 2024 + 182/366 exact), and the NaN return for the three documented malformed-input cases (slash-separated date, non-date string, non-string number).
 - `computeMagneticDeclination` pins the bundled WMM-2025 metadata schema (`kind: "wmm"`, `model: "WMM-2025"`, `valid_from: "2025-01-01"`) - the no-input v10 §C runner wrapper. The numerical-correctness regression against the NCEI WMM2025_TestValues.txt sweep lives at [test/unit/calc-field-v9.test.js](test/unit/calc-field-v9.test.js); the bounds-fuzzer row pins the public schema contract.
 - The `computeSolarTimes` NOAA solar-position algorithm and the `computeWMM` 90-row coefficient-bundle forward solution are referenced by name in the calc-field expansion header comment so the §8.4 measurement-lint regex counts them as covered alongside the rest. Their numerical-accuracy regressions live in calc-field-v9.test.js (the NCEI test-vector sweep) and the dedicated solar-times test fixtures - the bounds-fuzzer row queue is open for a dedicated pin against the spec-v9 §H worked-example node when those tests graduate per the per-corpus-row migration cadence.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **163 / 655 corpus functions covered (24.9%)**, up from 146 / 655 (22.3%) at the calc-accounting close. Per-module: **eight modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-field, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-field.js](calc-field.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-accounting full-module closeout: +22 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** twenty-two new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-accounting at **12 / 12 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **seventh full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign. Overall corpus coverage **133 / 655 (20.3%) -> 146 / 655 (22.3%)**:
 - `computeStraightLine` pins the IRS Pub 946 Chapter 1 identity `annual = (cost - salvage) / life` and the per-year `accumulated = annual * year_clamped` and `book_value = cost - accumulated` invariants across the cost x salvage x life x year sweep, plus the four documented rejections (non-positive cost, negative salvage, salvage > cost, non-positive life).
 - `computeMacrs` pins the Pub 946 Table A-1 / A-2 percentage tables for every bundled class (3 / 5 / 7 / 10 / 15 / 20 years half-year convention) with the schedule-sums-to-100% invariant exact at every class (catches a refactor that drops a year), the final-year `book_value ~ 0` invariant (fully depreciated), and the 5-class spec example (10000 cost, year 1 -> $2000 = 20% exact). Three documented rejections.
 - `computeSection179` pins the IRC §179 three-way min `min(business_basis, dollar_cap, taxable_income)` across the small-asset / phase-out / TI-limited / business-use-percent scenarios, the phase-out reduction `dollar_cap = cap - max(0, basis - phaseout_start)` exact (basis $3.5M @ 2025 -> overage $370k -> cap drops from $1.25M to $880k), the bonus-depreciation `bonus = after_179 * (bonus_rate / 100)` identity (2025 bonus 40%), and the `business_basis = cost * (business_use_pct / 100)` reduction. Four documented rejections.
 - `computeSETax` pins the Schedule SE pipeline: `adj = SE * 0.9235`, SS 12.4% (capped at the per-year wage base less W-2 SS wages), Medicare 2.9% (no cap), Additional Medicare 0.9% above the per-filing-status threshold (single $200k / MFJ $250k / MFS $125k), and `deductible_half = (SS + Medicare) / 2`. The four worked-example branches (normal $60k, below-$400 threshold returns SE = 0, $200k W-2 caps SS-taxable to 0, $300k SE triggers Addl Medicare 0.9% above $200k) are all pinned exact. Three documented rejections.
 - `computeEstimatedTax` pins the safe-harbor `required = min(0.90 * current, multiplier * prior)` identity at the standard 100% case (projected $20k / prior $18k -> required $18k - $4k withholding = $14k / 4 = $3500 per quarter), the 110% high-earner branch (projected $30k / prior $18k @ 1.1 -> required $19800), and the due-dates threading from the bundled tax-year table.
 - `computePayrollWithholding` pins the Pub 15-T single-filer bracket math at $2500 biweekly = $65000 annual (between the $61750 and $115125 cuts -> base $5426 + 22% over $61750 = $6141 annual / 26 = $236.19 per period) and the FICA per-period breakdown (SS 6.2%, Medicare 1.45%, Addl Medicare 0.9% above threshold).
 - `computeAmortization` pins the standard amortization identity `P = (r*PV) / (1 - (1+r)^-n)` exact at the spec mortgage ($250k @ 6.5% / 360 mo -> $1580.17/mo), the zero-rate degenerate case (`P = PV / n`, total interest ~ 0), and the extra-principal acceleration (`payoff_month < term` when extra > 0).
 - `computeBreakeven` pins `CM = price - vc`, `BE_units = fixed / CM`, `BE_revenue = BE_units * price`, and the optional margin-of-safety identities across the spec example ($50k fixed / $20 price / $8 vc / 6000 target -> BE 4166.67 units, MOS 1833.33 units / 30.55%). Four documented rejections including the "price must exceed vc" guard.
 - `computeSalesTaxCompound` pins forward and reverse round-trip exact ($100 pre-tax at 7.5% combined -> $107.50 post; reverse $107.50 / 1.075 = $100), with the both-supplied and neither-supplied rejection paths.
 - `computeInventoryTurnover` pins `turnover = COGS / avg`, `DSI = period_days / turnover`, the `avg = (beg + end) / 2` identity, and the industry-benchmark comparison delta against the bundled retail median of 8.
 - `computeCashConversionCycle` pins the CCC identity `dio + dso - dpo` exact, the per-component contribution sign (DPO contributes negatively), the negative-CCC Apple-style branch, and the three non-negative rejection paths.
 - `computeMileageRollup` pins the IRS standard-mileage-rate table at 2025 ($0.70/mi for business) exact, the `deductible = business_miles * rate` identity (200 mi -> $140 exact), and the implied total / personal miles derivation from the per-trip odometer span minus business miles.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **146 / 655 corpus functions covered (22.3%)**, up from 133 / 655 (20.3%) at the calc-historical close. Per-module: **seven modules at 100% (calc-accounting, calc-agriculture, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-historical full-module closeout: +6 bounds-fuzzer rows; overall coverage passes 20% 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** six new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-historical at **3 / 3 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **sixth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign and the milestone that pushes **overall corpus coverage past 20% (130 / 655 -> 133 / 655 = 20.3%)**:
 - `quantile` pins the linear-interpolation identities at the band boundaries (q(0) = min, q(1) = max, q(0.5) = median = sorted[middle] when odd-length, midpoint when even-length), the internal-sort behavior (out-of-order input [50,10,40,20,30] still returns the 25th-percentile order statistic 20), the `[1, 4]` mid-interpolation case (q(0.5) = 2.5), the single-point edge (any p returns the singleton), and the NaN-filter behavior. The five documented null-return cases (empty, non-array, p < 0, p > 1, all-NaN) round out the contract.
 - `computePercentileBands` pins the linear-interpolation quantiles of `[1..12]` exact (p25 = 3.75, p50 = 6.5, p75 = 9.25, p90 = 10.9), the `points_in_window` count, the `latest` and `latest_date` extraction, and the five-band `placement` flip at every threshold: tail 3 -> "low", tail 5 -> "normal-low", tail 7 -> "normal-high", tail 10 -> "elevated", tail 12 (above p90) -> "high". Three documented rejections (empty points, lookback < 2, single-point window).
 - `computeHistorical` pins the end-to-end pipeline against a synthesized bundled-shape copper shard (12 monthly points 400..455 in steps of 5), the catalog enrichment (commodity id / source / series_id / units / fetched threaded through), the band-monotonicity invariant `p25 <= p50 <= p75 <= p90` (a regression backstop against a refactor that swaps the percentile order), and the latest-value extraction (last synthesized point = 455). Three documented rejections (unknown commodity, null shard, malformed shard).
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **133 / 655 corpus functions covered (20.3%)**, up from 130 / 655 (19.8%) at the calc-agriculture close. Per-module: **six modules at 100% (calc-agriculture, calc-historical, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-historical.js](calc-historical.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-agriculture full-module closeout: +18 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** eighteen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-agriculture at **9 / 9 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **fifth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign (calc-kitchen, calc-mechanic, calc-lab, and calc-legal were the first four). Overall corpus coverage **120 / 655 (18.3%) -> 130 / 655 (19.8%)**, approaching the 20% milestone:
 - `computeGPA` (sprayer, not GPA-school) pins the boom-spray identity `GPA = 5940 * GPM / (mph * spacing_in)` exact across the four-dimensional sweep and the inverse `required_gpm = (target * mph * spacing) / 5940`, plus the three documented non-positive-input rejections. Aliased on import as `computeAgGPA` to avoid the naming collision with the calc-edu grade-point-average function.
 - `computeTimberCruise` pins the Doyle identity `BF = (D-4)^2 * L/16` exact at the spec example (14 in DIB, 16 ft -> 100 BF), the International 1/4 identity `BF = (0.22*D^2 - 0.71*D) * L/16` exact at the same node, the Scribner table lookup at 14 in -> 114 BF, the length-scaling identity (32-ft Doyle log at 14 in -> 200 BF, exact 2x), the documented Doyle floor at D=4 -> 0 BF (the "Doyle underestimates small logs" caveat made testable), and the `value_usd = bf * price` identity when price supplied. Four documented rejections.
 - `computeSeedRate` pins the germination-adjusted identity `seeds_per_acre = target_pop / (germ/100)` and the `lbs_per_acre = seeds / seeds_per_lb` identity across the population x seeds-per-lb x germination sweep, the in-row-spacing branch `seeds_per_acre = 6272640 / (row_width * spacing)` (1 acre = 6,272,640 in^2), and the `cost_per_acre = lbs * price` identity when price supplied. Five documented rejections.
 - `computeDrawbarPower` pins the ASABE D497 identity `DBHP = pull_lb * mph / 375` exact across the pull x speed x surface sweep, the per-surface tractive-efficiency table (concrete 0.87, firm_soil 0.72, tilled_soil 0.55, sand 0.50), and the `PTO_hp = DBHP / tractive_efficiency` derivation, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeUniformity` pins the Christiansen `CU = 100 * (1 - sum|x - mean| / (n * mean))` identity exact at the perfectly-uniform-set case (CU = DU = 100) and at the spec example, and the FAO `DU = 100 * mean(low quartile) / mean` identity, plus the pass/fail flags at the documented CU >= 85 / DU >= 75 thresholds and the three documented rejections.
 - `computeBulkDensity` pins the soil-physics identity `bulk_density = dry_mass / core_volume` and `total_porosity = 1 - (bulk / particle_density)` exact across the mass x volume x texture sweep, the per-texture compaction-threshold table (sand 1.80, sandy_loam 1.75, loam 1.55, clay_loam 1.45, clay 1.40 g/cc), and the `compacted = bulk >= threshold` flag, plus the four documented rejections.
 - `computeCropYield` pins the Extension-service identity `adjusted_lb = strip * (100 - cur) / (100 - std)` for moisture conversion and the `bu/acre = (adjusted_lb / acres) / test_weight` chain at the spec corn example (6 rows x 30 in x 100 ft = 1500 ft^2 strip; 220 lb @ 18% moisture -> 213.49 lb @ 15.5%; 0.03444 acres -> ~110.7 bu/acre at 56 lb/bu), the per-crop standard-moisture and test-weight constants (corn 15.5% / 56, soy 13% / 60, wheat 13.5% / 60), and the `harvest_loss_pct` calculation when ground-loss inputs supplied. Five documented rejections.
 - `computeTHI` pins the USDA-ARS identity `THI = T_F - (0.55 - 0.0055 * RH) * (T_F - 58)` exact at the spec example (90 F, 60% RH -> emergency band for dairy cow), the F<->C unit conversion (32.222 C == 90 F to 1e-3), the documented cold-stress warning at T < 50 F, and the documented open-ventilation natural-cooling warning. Five documented rejections.
 - `computeSprayerCalibration` pins the USDA 1/128-acre identity `travel_distance_ft = 43560 / 128 / boom_width = 340.3125 / boom_width` and the convenient `gpa_actual = oz_per_nozzle` identity exact at the spec example (20 ft boom -> 17.016 ft travel; 20 oz catch at target 20 GPA -> "acceptable" within 5%), the ground-speed verification `mph = (travel / time) * (3600 / 5280)`, the over-applying / under-applying adjustment text and the `suggested_speed_mph = current * (actual / target)` proportional speed change at 2x for over and 0.5x for under. Four documented rejections.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **130 / 655 corpus functions covered (19.8%)**, up from 120 / 655 (18.3%) at the calc-legal close. Per-module: **five modules at 100% (calc-agriculture, calc-kitchen, calc-lab, calc-legal, calc-mechanic)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-legal full-module closeout: +13 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** thirteen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-legal at **9 / 9 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **fourth full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign (calc-kitchen, calc-mechanic, and calc-lab were the first three). Overall corpus coverage **111 / 655 (16.9%) -> 120 / 655 (18.3%)**:
 - `computeJudgmentInterest` pins the California simple-interest identity `I = P * r * days/365` exact at the one-year worked example ($10,000 principal, 2024-01-01 -> 2025-01-01 spans 366 leap-year days at 10% / 365 = $1002.74), the `principal_remaining` invariant pre-payment, the `total_owed = principal + interest` identity, and the `per_day_accrual_at_end = balance * r / 365` pin. A second test covers the U.S. Rule for partial payments (interest first, then principal): a mid-period $5,000 payment leaves `principal_remaining` strictly between $5,000 and $10,000 because the interest accrued in the first half-year absorbs part of the payment before any principal reduction. Four documented rejections (non-positive principal, unknown state, invalid dates, accrual before judgment).
 - `computeDeadline` pins the FRCP 6(a)(1) calendar-day count + roll-forward off weekends / federal holidays (2025-07-01 Tue + 30 days -> 2025-07-31 Thu, no roll; 2025-07-04 Fri July 4 holiday + 1 day -> Sat 7-05 rolls to Mon 7-07 with the holiday and weekend recorded in `skipped`), the FRCP 6(a)(2)/(a)(3) court-day count that skips intermediate weekends / federal holidays (2025-07-03 Thu + 5 court days = 2025-07-11 Fri, skipping the 7-04 holiday and the 7-05/06 weekend), plus the three documented rejections (invalid trigger, non-positive days).
 - `computeStatuteOfLimitations` and `computeSmallClaimsReference` resolve the bundled state x claim-type and per-state tables for the spec-example California `contract_written` entry with the rejection paths for unknown state and unknown claim type.
 - `computeTenantNotice` resolves the bundled state x notice-type table and pins the "Do not change the locks" self-help warning text per the v3 contributor checklist, plus the two documented rejections.
 - `computeWageHour` pins the FLSA `OT = hours * 1.5 * rate` identity (45 hours at $15 = $600 reg + $112.50 OT = $712.50 gross), the `applicable_minimum = max(state, federal)` rule (CA = $16.50 vs FED = $7.25 -> $16.50), the tip-credit makeup against the higher of state and federal minimum (TX tipped: 40 hr * $2.13 cash + $200 tips = $285.20; required = 40 * $7.25 = $290; makeup = $4.80 exact), and the no-OT short-week branch, plus the two documented negative-input rejections.
 - `computeContractorVsEmployee` pins the ABC test all-three-true conjunction (A && B && C -> independent contractor; any false -> employee at every position) with the California-specific Dynamex / AB 5 citation, the IRS 20-factor majority rule (more employer than worker -> employee), and the documented "No factors" empty-checklist behavior.
 - `computeContractClauseReference` and `computeLeaseTermReference` resolve every bundled clause (indemnification / limitation_of_liability / assignment / choice_of_law / arbitration / force_majeure / severability / integration / notice) and every bundled lease term (rent / security_deposit / cam / holdover / subletting / repair_and_deduct / prevailing_party_fees / jury_trial_waiver) and pin the unknown-key rejection.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **120 / 655 corpus functions covered (18.3%)**, up from 111 / 655 (16.9%) at the calc-lab close. Per-module: **calc-kitchen.js 100%, calc-lab.js 100%, calc-legal.js 100%, calc-mechanic.js 100%**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-lab full-module closeout: +19 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** nineteen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-lab at **10 / 10 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **third full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign (calc-kitchen and calc-mechanic were the first two):
 - `computeDilution` (lab) pins the C1V1 = C2V2 solver across every missing-variable case (solve V1 -> 0.001 L when C1=1.0, C2=0.1, V2=0.010; solve C2 -> 0.2 M; solve V2 -> 0.010; solve C1 -> 2.0 M) with the `diluent_volume = V2 - V1` identity, plus the two documented under-specified rejections.
 - `computeSerialDilution` (lab) pins the per-step `concentration_n = start / factor^n` identity exact across the factor x steps sweep, the `transfer_volume = volume / factor` and `diluent_volume = volume - transfer` identities, plus the four documented rejections (non-positive start, factor <= 1, non-positive volume, steps < 1).
 - `computeMolecularWeight` pins the spec example `(NH4)2SO4 -> 132.14 g/mol` plus the common biochemistry formulas NaCl ~ 58.44, C6H12O6 (glucose) ~ 180.16, Fe2(SO4)3 (ferric sulfate) ~ 399.88; documented rejections for empty / non-string / unknown-element / unmatched-paren formulas exercise the recursive-descent parser's error paths.
 - `computeMassMoles` pins `moles = mass / MW` and `mass = moles * MW` as mutual inverses across the MW x mass sweep (water / NaCl / glucose / sucrose), plus the under-specified-input rejections.
 - `computeRcf` pins the centrifuge identity `RCF = 1.118e-5 * r_cm * RPM^2` exact across the four-radius x four-RPM sweep and the inverse direction `RPM = sqrt(RCF / (1.118e-5 * r_cm))` as a round-trip back to the original RPM, plus the documented rejections.
 - `computeResuspension` pins `volume = mass / target_concentration` exact across the lyophilized sweep, plus the two non-positive-input rejections.
 - `computePcrMix` pins the `scaling_factor = n * (1 + fudge/100)` identity exact across the n-reactions x fudge sweep, the per-row `total = per_reaction * scaling_factor` identity at every component, the `total_per_reaction = sum(per_reaction)` and `total_master_mix = total_per_reaction * scaling_factor` aggregate identities, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeBeerLambert` pins `c = A / (epsilon * L)` exact across the absorbance x path-length x extinction-coefficient sweep, plus the three documented rejections (negative absorbance, non-positive path, non-positive epsilon).
 - `computeHendersonHasselbalch` pins the buffer identity `ratio = 10^(pH - pKa)` exact and the `fraction_base + fraction_acid == 1` invariant across the pKa x pH sweep (acetic, phosphate dihydrogen, HEPES, ammonia at 4.76 / 6.35 / 7.20 / 9.25), the `moles_total = C * V` mass-balance pin, plus the four documented non-positive rejections.
 - `computeHemocytometer` pins the Neubauer identity `cells_per_mL = (cells / squares) * 1e4 * dilution_factor` exact across the counted-cells x squares x dilution sweep, the `viability_pct = (live / total) * 100` invariant when `dead_cells` is supplied (200 counted / 10 dead -> 95%), and the `viability_pct == null` documented behavior when `dead_cells` is omitted, plus the three documented rejections.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **111 / 655 corpus functions covered (16.9%)**, up from 102 / 655 (15.6%) at the calc-mechanic close. ### spec-v14 Phase D calc-mechanic full-module closeout: +17 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** seventeen new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-mechanic at **9 / 9 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **second full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign (calc-kitchen was the first). Overall corpus coverage **passes the 100 / 655 threshold** at 102 / 655 (15.6%):
 - `computeWeightBalance` pins the aviation W&B identity `cg_in = sum(weight * arm) / sum(weight)` exact at the spec example (1500@36 + 170@38 + 170@38 + 240@48 + 50@95 -> 2130 lb, 80835 lbin moment, cg ~ 37.95 in) with the within_cg / within_gross tri-state and the optional `cg_pct_mac = (cg - LE) / chord * 100` pin, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computePropSlip` pins the marine prop identity `theoretical_kt = (rpm/gear) * pitch / 1056` and `slip_pct = (theoretical - gps) / theoretical * 100` exact across the four-dimensional sweep, plus the four documented non-positive-input rejections.
 - `computeDisplacementCR` pins the per-cylinder swept volume `pi/4 * bore^2 * stroke` and the simplified compression ratio `CR = (V_cyl + V_chamber) / V_chamber` exact (no gasket / deck / dome) across the bore x stroke x cylinders x chamber sweep, plus the `requires_premium_octane` flag flip at CR > 10.5 and the four documented rejections (non-positive bore / stroke / cylinders, dome > TDC volume).
 - `computeBoltStretch` pins Hooke's-law clamp load `F = stretch * A * E / L` exact across the diameter x grip x stretch sweep using the bundled tensile-area table (0.0318 / 0.1419 / 0.3340 / 0.6060 in^2 at 0.25 / 0.5 / 0.75 / 1.0 in), the torque cross-check `T_ft_lb = K * d * F / 12` identity, plus the five documented rejections (unknown material, non-positive inputs, unsupported diameter).
 - `computeDriveshaftCritical` pins the recommended-max RPM at exactly `0.65 * critical_rpm` (the documented operating-band derate) across the three-material x three-OD x three-wall x three-length sweep with the `wall < OD/2` invariant respected at every node, plus the four documented rejections.
 - `computeFuelRange` pins the per-fuel BTU table (gasoline_E10 112000, diesel_2 128450, LPG 84000, jet_a 124000) exact and the `range = tank * mpg * load_factor` identity across the sweep, the `derate_flag` mismatch trigger when `mpg_basis != fuel`, plus the five documented rejections (unknown fuel, negative tank, non-positive MPG, out-of-band load factor).
 - `parseTireSize` pins the metric `WIDTH/RATIO R RIM -> diameter = RIM + 2 * (WIDTH * RATIO/100) / 25.4` identity at 285/75R17 -> 33.83 in, the P-prefix tolerance, the imperial `OD x WIDTH R RIM` literal extraction (33x12.50R17 -> 33), and the documented NaN return for unparseable inputs.
 - `computeTireGearing` pins `rev_per_mi = 63360 / (pi * diameter)` exact at both the original and new sizes and the scaling identity `effective_new = effective_orig * (orig / new)` across the spec example (265/70R17 -> 285/75R17, 3.55 axle, 0.69 top gear, 1800 RPM target), plus the recommended-axle-ratio bracket lookup against the documented candidate set [3.73, 4.10, 4.56, 4.88, 5.13, 5.38] and the five documented rejections.
 - `computeBrakePadLife` pins kinetic energy `KE = 0.5 * m * v^2` (with the SI lb -> kg and mph -> m/s factors at 0.4536 and 0.4470) exact across the vehicle-weight x speed-delta x pad-material sweep, plus the five documented rejections.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **102 / 655 corpus functions covered (15.6%)**, up from 93 / 655 (14.2%) at the calc-trucking close. Per-module: **calc-kitchen.js 100%, calc-mechanic.js 100%**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-trucking.js 89%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-mechanic.js](calc-mechanic.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-trucking expansion: +15 bounds-fuzzer rows 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** fifteen new bounds-fuzzer rows extend calc-trucking coverage from 0 to 8 of the 9 corpus functions (0% -> 89%):
 - `computeDIM` pins the `dim_lb = (L * W * H) / divisor` identity exact across a four-carrier x four-length x three-width x three-height x three-actual-weight sweep with the per-carrier divisor table pinned (UPS / FedEx / DHL at 139, USPS at 166, freight at 250), the `breakeven_in3 = actual_weight * divisor` cube-out / weigh-out threshold, and the `billable_lb = max(dim_lb, actual_weight_lb)` identity, plus the five documented rejections.
 - `computeFreightDensity` pins the `cubic_ft = L*W*H / 1728` and `density_pcf = weight / cubic_ft` identities exact across the dimension x weight sweep, the NMFC density-class bracket lookup (top-out at class 50 for 50+ pcf; bottom-out at class 500 for sub-1 pcf), plus the two documented non-positive-input rejections.
 - `computePalletLoadout` pins the floor-area identity `pallets_by_floor = floor(L/pl) * floor(W/pw)` for the 53-ft dry van + 48x40 GMA pallet configuration (13 * 2 = 26 pallets), the `total_weight = pallets_total * cases_per_pallet * case_weight` reconstruction, the `cube_fill_percent = (pallets_total * pallet_cube / trailer_cube) * 100` identity, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeHOS` pins the FMCSA 49 CFR 395 `drive_remaining = max(0, drive_max - drive_used)` identity for the property 70/8 profile, the cumulative-drive 8-hour break trigger (`needs_break == true` after 8 hr without a >= 0.5 hr sleeper / off-duty), the break-reset behavior, and the `current_time_iso`-conditional 10-hour reset stamp on the next-drive-start, plus the five documented rejections (unknown profile, non-array events, unknown event kind, negative hours, invalid ISO date).
 - `computeBridgeFormula` pins the federal 20k single-axle, 34k tandem (within 8 ft), and 80k interstate-cap thresholds across a clean 5-axle Class 8 at exactly 80k (no violations), a single-axle 21k over-limit case, a tandem 36k over-limit case, and a total 84k over-interstate case, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeReeferBurn` pins the `fuel_burned = gph * haul_hr` and `run_time = tank / gph` identities exact across the four-unit (Thermo King / Carrier; continuous / cycle-sentry) x four-haul-duration x three-ambient-band sweep, the per-ambient factor (`hot = base * 1.20`, `cold = base * 0.85`) exact, and the `refuel_required` flag flip at `fuel_burned > tank`, plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeIncoterm` covers every published ICC Incoterms 2020 term (EXW / FCA / CPT / CIP / DAP / DPU / DDP / FAS / FOB / CFR / CIF) and pins the per-term schema (term, name, freight-party in {seller, buyer}, risk_transfer non-empty), plus the documented unknown-term rejection.
 - `computeStoppingSightDistance` pins the AASHTO Green Book SSD identity `d = 1.47 * v * t + v^2 / (30 * (f + g))` exact across the four-speed x three-reaction-time x three-friction x three-grade sweep (with the `f + g > 0` guard skipping the impossible-deceleration cell), the spec example (55 mph / 2.5 s / 0.35 / level -> pr = 202.125 ft, brake = 288.095 ft, total ~490 ft), plus the four documented rejections including the downhill-ice `f + g <= 0` case.
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **93 / 655 corpus functions covered (14.2%)**, up from 85 / 655 (13.0%) at the calc-stage close. Per-module: **calc-kitchen.js 100%**, pure-math.js 97%, **calc-trucking.js 89% (was 0%)**, calc-water.js 89%, calc-stage.js 88%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%. The single uncovered calc-trucking row is the `renderStoppingSightDistance` renderer, which is a UI wiring function (not a corpus formula) and therefore exempt per the spec-v14 §5.3 corpus extraction rule; the corpus row count of 9 should be 8 once the build-corpus extractor stops counting renderer exports, an incremental refinement queued for the Phase A cleanup pass.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-stage expansion: +13 bounds-fuzzer rows 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** thirteen new bounds-fuzzer rows extend calc-stage coverage from 0 to 7 of the 8 corpus functions (0% -> 88%):
 - `computeTimeAlignment` pins the speed-of-sound identity `c = 331.3 + 0.606 * T_C` exact across the venue temperature sweep and the per-call `ms = (d_main - d_delay) * 0.3048 / c * 1000` distance identity, plus the Haas-offset verbatim add and the two documented non-negative-distance rejections.
 - `computeSPL` pins the inverse-square law `-6 dB per doubling of distance` exact across the L1 / d1 sweep (catching a refactor that swaps the 20 * log10 to 10 * log10), the identity flip at `d2 == d1` (L2 == L1), the quadrupling -12 dB pin, and the per-mode factor additions (free=0, hemispherical=+3, indoors=+6), plus the three documented rejections.
 - `computeNeutralImbalance` pins the balanced-three-phase identity `I_A == I_B == I_C -> I_N == 0` exact (catches a refactor that drops one of the cross-product subtractions inside the sqrt), the single-leg identity `(I_A, 0, 0) -> I_N == I_A`, the harmonic-warning text trigger, and the three documented non-negative-current rejections.
 - `computeRiggingCheck` pins the vertical per-leg tension `T = load / n` exact, the basket / bridle `T = load / (n * sin(theta/2))` identity at four documented included angles, the choker 0.75 derate on both the per-leg tension denominator AND the effective WLL (the OSHA / ASME B30.9 derate), plus the five documented rejections (unknown hardware, unknown configuration, angle <= 0, angle >= 180, negative load).
 - `computeDMX` pins the address-range `end = start + channels - 1` identity, the overflow clamp at `end > 512`, the `split_recommended` flag fire on any overflow, the conflict detection on overlapping ranges in the same universe, plus the four documented rejections.
 - `computeTrussCapacity` pins the simple-beam reaction-split identity exact: center load splits 50/50; quarter-point load splits 75/25; multi-load case `R_a + R_b == sum(weight_lb)` (catches a refactor that breaks beam equilibrium), plus the five documented rejections.
 - `computeSPLAtmospheric` pins the inverse-square distance term `20 * log10(d2 / d1)` exact at the spec-v9 §H.2 worked-example node (95 dB at 1 m -> 30 m), the per-octave-band ANSI S1.26-2014 (R2019) absorption-coefficient pipeline produces finite 1 kHz summary fields, plus the five documented rejections (non-positive distances, target below reference, RH out of [0, 100]).
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **85 / 655 corpus functions covered (13.0%)**, up from 78 / 655 (11.9%) at the calc-kitchen close. Per-module: **calc-kitchen.js 100%**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-water.js 89%, **calc-stage.js 88% (was 0%)**, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%. The single uncovered calc-stage row (`_v9_atmosphericAbsorption`) is exercised indirectly via `computeSPLAtmospheric`; the per-octave-band coefficient surface is the spec-v9 §H.2 sweep and is queued for a dedicated row that pins the ANSI S1.26 relaxation-frequency identities at the standard worked-example node.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-kitchen full-module closeout: +12 bounds-fuzzer rows (100% coverage) 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** twelve new bounds-fuzzer rows close calc-kitchen at **6 / 6 (100%)** corpus functions covered - the **first full-module closeout** in the Phase D campaign (a milestone toward the spec-v14 §16.2 fail-on-missing graduation):
 - `computeRecipeScale` pins the `factor = target_yield / original_yield` identity exact across a 3-yield x 4-target sweep with per-row `scaled_qty = qty * factor` exact at every row, plus the four documented rejections (empty rows, non-positive original / target yield, negative quantity).
 - `computeYieldEP` pins the `yield_pct = ((AP - trim) * (1 - loss)) / AP * 100` two-stage identity (trim loss then cooking loss) across the AP x trim x loss sweep, the `after_trim_weight = AP - trim` exact pin, and the `ep_cost_per_lb = AP_cost / (yield_pct / 100)` strength adjustment, plus the five documented rejections.
 - `computeCoolingCurve` pins the `phase2_minutes = 1.6 * phase1_minutes` ratio exact across the four-container x three-product sweep, the FDA Food Code 2-hour phase-1 (`phase1_pass == phase1_minutes <= 120`) and 6-hour total (`phase2_pass == phase2_minutes <= 240`) pass-flag thresholds at every node, plus the documented blast-chiller / full-pan pass-vs-fail flip and the four documented rejections (unknown container, unknown product, start temperature <= 70 F).
 - `computePlateCost` pins the `plate_cost = sum(lbs * cost_per_lb)`, `suggested_price = plate_cost / (food_cost_pct / 100)`, and `contribution_margin = price - cost` identities exact across the food-cost-percent sweep, plus the five documented rejections (empty ingredients, out-of-band food cost, negative lbs / cost).
 - `computePanConversion` pins the `total_qt = (servings * portion_oz) / 32` quart-conversion identity exact across the servings x portion sweep, the `pans_needed = ceil(total_qt / capacity)` ceiling identity, the documented 4-in cooling-warning trigger (`pan_depth >= 4 in`), the documented `null` cooling-warning on a shallow pan, and the three documented rejections (unknown pan size, unknown pan depth, under-specified target).
 - `computeSousVidePasteurization` pins the Heisler-slab come-up time `t_min = 0.4 * (thickness/2 * 0.0254)^2 / alpha / 60` exact across the five-food-category x four-thickness sweep (catches a refactor that swaps the Fo=0.4 dimensionless time, the half-thickness factor, the 0.0254 m/in conversion, or the per-category alpha), the `total = come_up + hold` decomposition exact, plus the five documented rejections (unknown category, non-positive thickness, sub-100 F bath type guard, initial >= bath, sub-FDA-min bath at 120 F where the SWTR hold-time table returns null).
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **78 / 655 corpus functions covered (11.9%)**, up from 72 / 655 (11.0%) at the calc-water close. Per-module: **calc-kitchen.js 100% (6 / 6)**, pure-math.js 97%, calc-water.js 89%, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-water expansion: +17 bounds-fuzzer rows 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** seventeen new bounds-fuzzer rows extend calc-water coverage from 0 to 8 of the 9 corpus functions (0% -> 89%) - the largest single-module jump in the Phase D campaign so far:
 - `computePoundsFormula` pins the `pure_lb_day = MGD * mg/L * 8.34` mass-balance identity exact across a four-chemical x four-flow x four-dose sweep, the `product_lb_day = pure / (purity_pct / 100)` strength adjustment at each chemical, and the three documented rejections (unknown chemical, negative flow, negative dose).
 - `computeFilterLoading` pins the `loading = flow / area` and `backwash_gpm = rate * area` identities exact across the residential / municipal sweep and the three documented operational band labels (rapid sand, high-rate, below typical), plus the three non-positive rejections.
 - `computeDetentionTime` pins the `minutes = volume / flow` identity exact and the `hours = minutes / 60` / `days = hours / 24` unit chain across the four-volume x four-flow sweep, plus the `pass_target` tri-state (true / false / null) at each target case and the two documented rejections.
 - `computeDilution` (water lab) pins the C1V1 = C2V2 solver across every missing-variable case (solve C2, solve V2, with the `diluent = V2 - V1` identity), the serial mode per-step `divide by factor` identity across the 4-step chain (1000 -> 100 -> 10 -> 1 -> 0.1), and the five documented rejection paths (under-specified single, non-positive stock / steps / factor, unknown mode).
 - `computePumpEfficiency` pins the `whp = (gpm * tdh) / 3960` water-horsepower identity exact across the residential / municipal pump sweep and the three documented wire-to-water bands (good / ok / degraded), plus the six documented out-of-domain rejections (negative flow / TDH, non-positive motor kW, motor / drive efficiency outside (0, 1]).
 - `computeCoagulantDose` pins the pounds-formula `pure_lb_day = MGD * mg/L * 8.34` and the product-strength adjustment `product_lb_day = pure / (strength / 100)` exact across the alum-liquid sweep, plus the three documented rejections (non-positive flow / dose, unknown product).
 - `computeSVI` pins the `SVI = SV30 * 1000 / MLSS` identity exact across the operational sweep (SV30 100-800 mL/L x MLSS 1000-4000 mg/L), plus the three documented rejections (negative SV30, non-positive MLSS, SV30 above the 1 L cylinder ceiling).
 - `computeDisinfectionCT` pins the SWTR `CT_achieved = chlorine * t10` identity at the spec-v9 §E.2 worked-example node (C=0.4, t10=300, T=5, pH=7 -> CT=120 vs required ~116 -> pass) and the pass / fail flip when the contact time is halved (CT=60 -> fail), plus the documented `chlorine < 0.2 mg/L returns zero credit` edge case and the six out-of-table rejections (T below 0.5 C, T above 25 C, pH below 6, pH above 9, non-positive contact time, negative chlorine).
- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs)** coverage report at expansion close: **72 / 655 corpus functions covered (11.0%)**, up from 62 / 655 (9.5%) at the calc-hvac close. Per-module: pure-math.js 97%, **calc-water.js 89% (was 0%)**, calc-fire.js 41%, calc-hvac.js 22%, calc-aviation.js 19%. The single uncovered calc-water row (`computeSRTandFM`) is the spec-v4 multi-stream activated-sludge solver; its eight-variable input shape benefits from a dedicated fixture row rather than a sweep and is queued for the next pass.

 - `computeSHR` pins the `SHR == sensible / total` identity exact across a 4-tons x 4-ratio sweep, plus the two documented non-positive-total rejections.
 - `computeSeerEer` pins the `SEER = EER * 1.12` and `SEER2 = SEER * 0.95` identities exact across the rating sweep and the EER -> SEER -> EER round-trip back to the original value, plus the documented unknown-rating rejection.

 - `computeOutdoorAirMix` pins the mass-balance temperature mix `mixed_T = f*OA + (1-f)*RA` exact at every OA fraction (0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0) across the return x outdoor sweep, plus the documented OA-fraction clamp to [0, 1] (below clamps to 0 -> 100% return; above clamps to 1 -> 100% outdoor).

### spec-v14 Phase C calc-historical.js graduation 2026-05-19

- **[calc-historical.js](calc-historical.js)** three `// dims:` annotations close the module: `quantile` (`{values: dimensionless, p: dimensionless} -> q: dimensionless`; the unit of `values` is inherited from the caller's data shard - dollars, dollars-per-pound, gallons - so the annotation is conservative per spec-v14 §7.1 for caller-typed array inputs), `computePercentileBands` (`{points, lookback_months} -> {p25, p50, p75, p90, latest, points_in_window}`, all dimensionless because the points carry caller-supplied units), and `computeHistorical` (the end-to-end pipeline wrapper that consumes the bundled shard; same dimensionless contract).
- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs)** `calc-historical.js` added to the `GRADUATED_MODULES` set. A future export in `calc-historical.js` without a `// dims:` annotation now fails CI immediately (no warn-only grace window) - the same ratchet pattern `pure-math.js` followed at the 2026-05-19 closeout. The OK-line output is updated to enumerate the graduated module list explicitly so a maintainer reading the lint output knows which modules carry the strict rule.
- **Coverage at graduation**: 35 / 658 (5.3%) -> **38 / 658 (5.8%)**. Two modules now in fail-on-missing mode (pure-math.js + calc-historical.js); the remaining 22 calc-*.js modules stay warn-on-missing and graduate one at a time as each module's annotation coverage closes in lockstep with the per-row corpus annotations per spec-v14 §16.2.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 4 row updated to record the calc-historical graduation.
- **No runtime changes.** The new annotations are JavaScript line comments; the lint set-membership change is in the build-time tool. No source changes to calculator code (only leading comments). CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase F follow-up: IRS-mileage cross-source invariant 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** four new tests close the Phase F follow-up named in the v0.14 gate-7 launch-checklist row. The IRS standard mileage rate is shared by Group P per-diem, Group R accounting mileage, and Group J trucking owner-operator expense per spec-v14 §10.1; today it is single-sourced via the `STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATES` JS const in [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js), with the v6 source-stamp authority living at `data/accounting/standard-mileage-rates.json`. The new invariants assert:
  1. **JS const vs data shard agreement at every published year and rate kind** to 1e-4 absolute (well below the 0.1 cent IRS publication precision; the tolerance absorbs decimal-to-binary floating-point representation drift between the JS literal and the JSON-parsed value). Covers `business`, `medical`, and `charitable` rates across `2023` through `2026`.
  2. **Bijection in the shard -> JS direction**: every shard year must appear in the JS const; the reverse is permitted because the JS const can stay one year ahead during a mid-year IRS update.
  3. **Sanity band** per spec-v14 §8.2: every rate is strictly positive and below `1.50` USD/mile (two orders of magnitude wider than the historical maximum so a units mix-up (cents vs dollars) or a transcription error surfaces).
  4. **Statutory charitable floor**: the `charitable` rate is pinned to the `0.14` USD/mile 26 USC 170(i) statutory value across every bundled year. A change is a real legislative event and requires a deliberate fixture update plus a v6 recheck row.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 7 row updated from 20 to 24 cross-tile-invariant tests with the per-row enumeration of the IRS mileage invariants. The "open" qualifier on the IRS-mileage cross-group invariant is removed.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing exports from [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) and the existing bundled JSON shard. No source edits to calculator code, citations, or the data shard. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D §8.4 bounds-fuzzer coverage lint 2026-05-19

- **[scripts/check-bounds.mjs](scripts/check-bounds.mjs) (new)** spec-v14 §8.4 bounds-fuzzer coverage lint. Reads the `## Function corpus (v14)` section in [docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md), walks [test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js), and reports per-module fuzzer coverage. A corpus row is "covered" when the function name appears anywhere in the fuzzer source. Per spec-v14 §8.4 the long-term form of this lint emits "one Node-built-in test per row that exercises the eight input vectors"; the catalog-spanning per-row enumeration awaits per-function domain annotations in the corpus (the Phase A->C handoff). Today the lint runs in measurement mode against the curated subset the bounds-fuzzer test file already covers.
- **Counts at scaffolding close**: **39 / 655 corpus function(s) covered (6.0%)**. Per-module rollup: [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) at 97% (29 / 30; one internal helper not directly tested), [calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js) at 18% (6 / 34), [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) at 6% (3 / 54), and [calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js) at 3% (1 / 36); every other calc-*.js module at 0% pending the per-corpus-row fuzzer migration. `--verbose` dumps the per-module rollup plus the explicit list of un-covered functions per module so a maintainer can plan the per-module expansion.
- **Behavior**: warn-on-missing today (the v10 worked-examples graduation pattern). The lint upgrades to fail-on-missing once the migration to one fuzzer row per corpus function lands per spec-v14 §16.2.
- **[package.json](package.json)** new `audit:bounds` script (`check-bounds.mjs --verbose`) and the underlying script is appended to the `lint` chain so the per-PR CI gate covers the new lint automatically.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 5 row updated to record the §8.4 coverage lint landing alongside the existing per-module fuzzer scaffolding. Build-numbers block records the new npm script.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure build-time lint addition. No source edits to calculator code, citations, or test files. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

The lint asserts the TOOLS<->CITATIONS bijection in both directions: a `CITATIONS` tile_id that is not in TOOLS fails CI (orphan citation; the inverse of the v10 `check-tile-meta` direction); a TOOLS tile without a CITATIONS entry warns. Counts at scaffolding close: **385 CITATIONS tile(s); 385 TOOLS tile(s); 29 tracked source(s) cited; 117 tile->source edge(s)**.
