### spec-v14 Phase C pure-math closeout: every export annotated; lint graduated to fail-on-missing 2026-05-19

- **[pure-math.js](pure-math.js)** every function and arrow-const export now carries a `// dims:` annotation. Nine new annotations closed the file: `ampacityFromPhysics` (single-output `I`), `threePhasePower` (multi-output `{P_W, S_VA, Q_var, kW, kVA, kVAR}` each `M L^2 T^-3`), `singlePhasePower` (multi-output, mirror of three-phase), `beamUniformLoadSimplySupported` + `beamCenterPointLoadSimplySupported` (multi-output `{M_lbft: M L^2 T^-2, delta_in: L}`), `rectangularSection` (multi-output `{I_in4: L^4, S_in3: L^3, c_in: L}`), `psychrometric` (multi-output `{e_s_hPa, e_hPa, W_kg_kg, dewPoint_C, GPP}`), and the two generic table-interpolation primitives `interpolateRefrigerant` + `interpLinear` (annotated conservatively per spec-v14 §7.1 because their output unit is selected by the caller's xs/ys; the annotation is `dimensionless` with an inline note that a future per-call-site dimensional check would dispatch on the calling function's annotation, not the primitive's).
- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs)** two robustness changes:
  1. **Extractor termination**: the annotation extractor used to take every comment line from `dims:` through end-of-block; trailing parenthetical notes (e.g., "(the output unit is conditional; the annotation is conservative per spec-v14 §7.1 ...)") were being concatenated into the dimension expression and tripping the parser on the unbalanced parenthesis. The extractor now walks forward from the `dims:` line and stops at the first blank line, the first parenthetical-note line, or the line that closes `out:` (single-output `out: name: <expr>` or balanced multi-output `out: { ... }` block), whichever comes first.
  2. **Per-module fail-on-missing graduation**: a new `GRADUATED_MODULES` set names the source files whose annotation coverage has closed; a missing-annotation row in any graduated module is an error (lint fails), not a warning. The set is seeded with `pure-math.js` at this closeout. Calc-*.js modules graduate one at a time as each module's annotation coverage closes in lockstep with the per-row corpus annotations per spec-v14 §16.2. The pattern mirrors the v10 worked-examples lint graduation: warn during migration, error after closure, no grace window.
- **Coverage at pure-math closeout**: **35 / 658 functions (5.3%)**, up from 26 / 658 (4.0%) at the 2026-05-19 expansion close. The pure-math.js export surface is fully annotated; the calc-*.js modules carry the existing seed (5 rows in calc-fire.js + 1 in calc-aviation.js) and remain warn-on-missing for the rest.
- **[scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs)** `calc-fire.js` per-module cap bumped 16000 -> 16500 to absorb the ~83 B gzipped delta from the four leading `// dims:` annotations on `computeFireFriction`, `computePDP`, `computeHydrantFlow` wrapper, and `computeAerialLadderReach`. Per spec-v10 §H.1 the per-tile split remains the preferred long-term remediation; this is a deliberate intentional growth to host the Phase C seed.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 4 row updated to record the pure-math closeout, the parser-termination fix, and the new graduation pattern. Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 closeout landing.
- **No runtime changes.** The new annotations are JavaScript line comments; the parser changes are in the build-time lint. No source changes to calculator code (only leading comments). No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase C expansion: multi-output annotation grammar + 11 additional rows 2026-05-19

- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs)** parser extended to accept the multi-output `out: { name: <expr>, name: <expr>, ... }` form alongside the original single-output `out: name: <expr>` form. Most calc-module compute functions return JavaScript object literals with several named outputs (e.g., `computePDP` returns `{ pdp_psi, elevation_psi }`); the single-output grammar from the Phase C scaffolding could not annotate them. The extension preserves the spec-v14 §7.1 token grammar unchanged (L / M / T / I / N / J / `dimensionless` with integer exponents and `*` / `/` / whitespace operators) and adds the brace-bracketed multi-output form as a second top-level alternative. The parser fails fast on a malformed multi-output entry (no `name: <expr>` shape, unparseable dimension expression, or empty `out: { }`).
- **[pure-math.js](pure-math.js)** six additional single-output annotations: `awgToNumber` (`{awg: dimensionless} -> n: dimensionless`), `conductorResistancePerKft` (mirrors `conductorResistance` with per-kft output), `hazenWilliamsFrictionLoss` (`{flow_gpm: L^3 T^-1, internal_diameter_in: L, length_ft: L, C: dimensionless} -> headLoss_ft: L`), `darcyWeisbachFrictionLoss` (the full seven-input SI form with `g: L T^-2` and the M/L/T expanded for density, viscosity, roughness, velocity), and the two beam-span functions `allowableSpanByBending` / `allowableSpanByDeflection` (each `{w_lb_ft: M T^-2, modulus: M L^-1 T^-2, b_in: L, d_in: L} -> L_max_ft: L`).
- **[calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js)** four calc-module multi-output annotations using the new grammar: `computeFireFriction` (`{hose_diameter: dimensionless, gpm: L^3 T^-1, length_ft: L} -> { friction_loss_psi: M L^-1 T^-2, coefficient: dimensionless }`), `computePDP` (`{ nozzle_pressure_psi, friction_loss_psi, elevation_ft, appliance_loss_psi } -> { pdp_psi, elevation_psi }`), `computeHydrantFlow` wrapper (`{ pitot_psi, outlet_diameter_in, c } -> { flow_gpm, coefficient_of_discharge }`), and `computeAerialLadderReach` (`{ angle_deg: dimensionless, extension_ft: L } -> { horizontal_reach_ft: L, vertical_reach_ft: L }`).
- **[calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js)** one calc-module multi-output annotation: `computeDensityAltitude` (`{ pressure_altitude_ft: L, oat_c: T } -> { density_altitude_ft: L, isa_temp_c: T, isa_deviation_c: T, takeoff_distance_factor_vs_sl: dimensionless }`). The `band` string output is excluded per the spec-v14 §7.1 dimensionless-token discipline (the lint covers numeric outputs; string-tag outputs are not dimensional).
- **Coverage at expansion close**: 26 / 658 functions (4.0%), up from 15 / 658 (2.3%) at the 2026-05-19 scaffolding close. The lint remains in warn-on-missing mode per spec-v14 §16.2; the per-row enumeration of missing functions stays suppressed for readability (single-line summary instead) until coverage reaches the graduation threshold.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 4 row updated to record the 26-function coverage and the multi-output grammar extension. Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 expansion landing.
- **No runtime changes.** The new annotations are JavaScript line comments; the parser change is in the build-time lint. No source changes to calculator code (only leading comments). No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase C scaffolding: dimensional-analysis lint + pure-math seed annotations 2026-05-19

- **[scripts/check-dimensions.mjs](scripts/check-dimensions.mjs) (new)** spec-v14 §7 Phase C dimensional-analysis lint. Reads each calc-*.js and [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) source, walks each export, and parses any `// dims: in { name: <expr>, ... } out: name: <expr>` annotation against the SI base-dimension grammar per spec-v14 §7.1: tokens `L` (length), `M` (mass), `T` (time / temperature - the ASCII shortcut the spec carries), `I` (current), `N` (amount), `J` (luminous intensity), or the literal `dimensionless`; integer exponents written `^<int>`; products and ratios written with whitespace / `*` / `/`. The lint fails on a malformed annotation today (any unparseable input list, missing `out:` line, or unknown dimension base); it warns (single-line summary plus per-function detail when 5 or fewer functions are missing) on a function without an annotation. Per spec-v14 §16.2 Phase C ratchet, the warn-on-missing graduates to fail-on-missing once corpus + annotation coverage land in lockstep (mirroring the v10 worked-examples lint graduation pattern). Pure read-and-report; no network, no mutation.
- **[pure-math.js](pure-math.js)** 15 seed annotations on the well-understood primitives: `awgDiameterInches` (`{awg: dimensionless} -> diameter_in: L`), `awgAreaCmils` / `awgAreaM2` (`-> area: L^2`), `conductorResistance` (`{length_m: L, temperature_C: T} -> resistance_ohm: M L^2 T^-3 I^-2`), `voltageDrop` (`{length_ft: L, current_A: I} -> voltage_drop_V: M L^2 T^-3 I^-1`), `feetOfHeadToPsi` (`{feet: L, fluid_density_lb_ft3: M L^-3} -> psi: M L^-1 T^-2`), `saturationVaporPressure_hPa` (`{T_C: T} -> e_s_hPa: M L^-1 T^-2`), `dewPointFromVaporPressure_C` (`{e_hPa: M L^-1 T^-2} -> dewPoint_C: T`), `colebrookFrictionFactor` (dimensionless in / dimensionless out), `fireHoseFrictionLoss` (`{gpm: L^3 T^-1, length_ft: L} -> friction_loss_psi: M L^-1 T^-2`), `hydrantFlow` (`{pitot_psi: M L^-1 T^-2, outlet_diameter_in: L} -> flow_gpm: L^3 T^-1`), and the four temperature converters (`F_to_C`, `C_to_F`, `C_to_K`, `K_to_C`, all `T -> T`). Coverage at scaffolding close: 15 / 658 functions (2.3%); the rest carry the warn-only single-line summary.
- **[package.json](package.json)** new `audit:dimensions` script (`check-dimensions.mjs`) and the script is appended to the `lint` chain so the per-PR CI gate covers the new lint automatically.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 4 promoted from `gate` to **partial** with the per-cell enumeration of the lint behavior, the 15-annotation seed, and the §16.2 graduation pattern. Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 Phase C scaffolding landing. Build-numbers block records the new npm script.
- **No runtime changes.** The annotations are JavaScript line comments; the lint is build-time-only. No source changes to calculator code (the functions are unchanged, only their leading comments). No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged. `dist/` is regenerated bit-identical modulo the source-comment additions which strip out of the production bundle.

### spec-v14 Phase D calc-module extensions: Group F + Group W + Group C compute-function bounds 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js)** Phase D coverage extended from the pure-math primitives to the named calc-module compute functions per spec-v14 §8. Fourteen new tests, all passing:
 - **Group F `computePDP`** ([calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js)): operational sweep across nozzle pressure 50-200 psi × friction loss 0-200 psi × elevation -50-500 ft, asserting finite `pdp_psi` and finite `elevation_psi` at every node.
 - **Group F `computeStandpipeFriction`**: finite-positive `total_psi` across typical riser geometry (height 10-300 ft × outlet count 1-4 × per-outlet flow 100-500 gpm).
 - **Group F `computeFireFriction`**: documented `{error}` on unknown hose diameter; finite-nonnegative `friction_loss_psi` across the supported diameter table (1.75 in, 2.5 in, 3 in, 5 in) and flow sweep.
 - **Group F `computeHydrantFlow` wrapper**: finite-positive `flow_gpm` across operational pitot pressures (1-80 psi) and outlet diameters (2.5 in, 4 in, 4.5 in).
 - **Group F `computeRequiredFireFlow`**: documented `{error}` on unknown construction class; the 12000 gpm ISO ceiling-clamp pin (even a 1e8 ft^2 input clamps at 12000).
 - **Group F `computeAerialLadderReach`**: across the 0-90 deg angle sweep with the `h^2 + v^2 = extension^2` Pythagorean invariant pinned to 1e-6 absolute; boundary identities at 0 deg (all horizontal) and 90 deg (all vertical).
 - **Group W `computeDensityAltitude`** ([calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js)): full documented PA × OAT sweep (PA from -1000 to 50000 ft × OAT from -40 to 50 C) plus the four out-of-domain boundary rejections (PA below -2000 ft, PA above 60000 ft, OAT below -60 C, OAT above 60 C).
 - **Group C `manualJCooling`** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)): finite-positive `total_BTU_hr` and `tons` across typical residential envelopes (outdoor design 80-115 F × floor area 600-4000 ft^2).
 - **Group C `manualJHeating`**: finite-positive `total_BTU_hr` across winter-design envelopes (outdoor design -10 to 50 F × insulation level poor / average / good).
 - **Group C `computeDuctSize`**: documented `{error}` rejection on non-positive `cfm` and non-positive `friction_in_wc_per_100ft`; convergence across typical CFM (50-5000) and friction rates (0.05-0.12 in WC/100 ft) with the `round_diameter_in` / `equivalent_square_in` / `velocity_fpm` output triple pinned finite-positive (catches a future refactor that renames the output fields and breaks the v9 renderer).
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 5 row updated from 30 to 44 tests with the per-calc-module enumeration of the new rows. Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 Phase D calc-module extension landing. Test-suite total updated from 3,526 to 3,540.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js), [calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js), and [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) exports. No source edits. No new dependencies. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged.

### spec-v14 Phase D scaffolding: bounds-and-edge-case fuzzer for pure-math primitives 2026-05-19

- **[test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js](test/unit/bounds-fuzzer.test.js) (new)** spec-v14 §8 Phase D fuzzer for the pure-math primitives. 30 tests, all passing. Each row exercises the lower bound, the upper bound, the midpoint, the documented regime transition (laminar / transitional / turbulent for Colebrook; sub-saturation through near-saturation for psychrometrics; standard-ambient through max-insulation-rating for ampacity), and the documented out-of-domain rejection path. The fuzzer asserts the spec-v14 §8.2 sensible-result contract (finite output, sign-correct, magnitude-banded) per row, the documented sentinel where one is named in [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) (`colebrookFrictionFactor` returns `0` for `Re<=0`; `hazenWilliamsFrictionLoss` returns `0` for any non-positive input; `ampacityFromPhysics` returns `0` when `T_c <= T_a`), or the documented thrown error per spec-v14 §8.3 (`awgToNumber` throws on a non-AWG string; `conductorResistance` and `voltageDrop` throw on an unknown material).
- **Coverage at scaffolding close**: AWG geometric / area (`awgDiameterInches`, `awgToNumber`, `awgAreaCmils`, `awgAreaM2`), conductor resistance + ampacity (`conductorResistance`, `conductorResistancePerKft`, `ampacityFromPhysics` including the bundle-derate monotonicity check across the 1..20 sweep), voltage drop (`voltageDrop`), single- and three-phase power (`threePhasePower` with the `P^2 + Q^2 = S^2` invariant pinned for `pf in [0, 1]`, `singlePhasePower`), Hazen-Williams + Darcy-Weisbach + Colebrook with full regime ladder coverage `Re=1` through `Re=1e8` and `relativeRoughness=0` through `0.1` (regime-dispatched upper bound: the laminar branch returns `f = 64/Re` up to 64 at `Re=1`; the turbulent branch stays well below 0.5), `feetOfHeadToPsi` (including the suction-side negative-head pass-through), beam mechanics + section properties + allowable spans across common joist sizes and load sweeps, psychrometrics across the indoor-comfort sweep (six dry-bulb x five RH points), linear interpolation including the empty / single-point / two-point / out-of-range edges, NFA fire-hose friction with table coefficients and flows, hydrant flow across typical pitot pressures and outlet diameters, and the temperature converters from absolute zero through high-temperature regimes.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 5 promoted from `gate` to **partial** with the per-row enumeration of what landed and a pointer to the deferred work (the dedicated `scripts/check-bounds.mjs` Phase D lint per spec-v14 §8.4 lands when the corpus carries explicit per-function domain annotations - the Phase A->C handoff is the precondition). Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 Phase D landing. Test-suite total updated to 3,526.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) exports. No source edits to pure-math.js or any calc-*.js module. No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged. `dist/` is regenerated bit-identical modulo the documentation rows.

### spec-v14 Phase B scaffolding: cross-validation lint + tolerance-ceiling backstop 2026-05-19

- **[scripts/check-cross-validation.mjs](scripts/check-cross-validation.mjs) (new)** spec-v14 §6.4 Phase B cross-validation lint. Walks the formula corpus (`## Function corpus (v14)` section in [docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md)) and the worked-example registry ([test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json)) and reports per-module corpus row counts, per-tile fixture coverage, and per-fixture tolerance vs the spec-v14 §14.1 per-group default ceiling. The group-ceiling table is hard-coded against spec-v14 §14.1 (A/F/G/J/K/M/N/P/T/U/V/W/Y at 5 percent, B at 25, C/D/L at 10, E at 20, O at 1, R/X at 1, with H/Q/S exempt because their tiles are not numeric). A fixture whose tolerance.pct exceeds the ceiling without a `tolerance.justification` (per-output) or `tolerance_justification` (per-row) note is warned; once the per-row review pass closes, the warnings graduate to errors using the same pattern as [scripts/check-worked-examples.mjs](scripts/check-worked-examples.mjs). Pure read-and-report; no network, no mutation.
- **[scripts/check-worked-examples.mjs](scripts/check-worked-examples.mjs)** graduated-state header. Coverage hit 100 percent at v13 close and remains there through the v14 audit pass, so the migration target declared in spec-v10 §5.2 is closed and the lint operates in fail-on-missing mode (a tile that drops its fixture fails CI immediately, no grace window). The 80-percent threshold remains as a regression backstop; the comment block is updated to reflect the graduated state.
- **[test/fixtures/worked-examples.json](test/fixtures/worked-examples.json)** three rows over the Group A 5 percent ceiling now carry the spec-v14 §14.2 `tolerance.justification` note: `voltage-drop.percent` (10 percent band: the `percent` output divides `drop_V` by the source voltage so the source-voltage assumption carries an additional published-rounding band on top of the underlying `drop_V` tolerance), `copper-resistance.resistance_ohm` (10 percent band: the expected value is the NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 8 75 C published value hand-corrected to 20 C; alpha for copper has a ~2-3 percent figure-of-merit band per NIST SP 811 and the NEC table is rounded to three significant figures), and `copper-resistance.resistance_ohm_per_kft` (the same justification carried through unchanged because the per-kft figure is a unit-scaling of the same value).
- **[package.json](package.json)** new `audit:cross-validation` script (`check-cross-validation.mjs`) and the script is appended to the `lint` chain so the per-PR CI gate covers the new lint automatically.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 2 promoted from `gate` to **partial** with the per-cell enumeration of what landed (lint coverage, tolerance-ceiling backstop, three justifications). Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-19 Phase B scaffolding landing. Build-numbers block records the new npm script.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure build-time documentation + lint additions. No calc-*.js source edits. No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged. `dist/` is regenerated bit-identical modulo the documentation rows.

### spec-v14 Phase E calc-module extensions: Group F PDP + Group W density altitude + Group C Manual J 2026-05-18

- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** Phase E coverage extended from the pure-math primitives to the named calc-module entry points per spec-v14 §9.1. Fifteen new tests, all passing:
 - **Group F `computePDP`** ([calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js)): monotonicity in each of the three positive inputs (nozzle pressure, friction loss, elevation), the NFA-shortcut `0.5 psi/ft` elevation constant pinned to an exact-equal identity for `ft in {-10, 0, 10, 50, 100}` (catches a refactor that swaps `0.5` for the more precise `0.434` water-column value used elsewhere), and per-call determinism on the bit pattern.
 - **Group F `computeStandpipeFriction`** ([calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js)): the contrasting `0.434 psi/ft` water-column constant pinned exactly for `riser_height_ft = 100`. The two pins (`0.5` for PDP, `0.434` for standpipe) together codify that the two paths use deliberately different constants: PDP per the NFA hydraulics shortcut, standpipe per the more precise water-column relation cited in [docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md).
 - **Group W `computeDensityAltitude`** ([calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js)): the ISA-standard-day identity (`PA=0, OAT=15C -> DA=0`), monotonicity in OAT at fixed PA (warmer air -> higher DA), the pinned FAA worked-example fixture (`PA=5000, OAT=25C -> DA=7388 ft` exactly), the documented out-of-domain rejection path for both PA and OAT bounds (returns an `error` object, not a numeric DA - the bounds-fuzzer §8.3 contract), NaN-poisoning behavior (returns `{error: ...}` rather than `{density_altitude_ft: NaN}` so a downstream tile does not treat NaN as a number), and per-call determinism.
 - **Group C `manualJCooling`** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)): monotonicity in outdoor design temperature over the 80-110 F sweep, the `dT >= 0` clamp at the indoor-set crossover (asserts `conductive_BTU_hr == 0` when outdoor equals indoor while solar + internal gain still contribute - catches a refactor that drops the `Math.max(0, dT)` clamp), per-call determinism on `total_BTU_hr` and `tons` bit patterns, and the pinned-tons-band invariant against the `manualJCoolingExample` `expectedRange.tons` band (1.5-6 tons for the 1500-ft^2 reference envelope).
 - **Group C `manualJHeating`** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)): monotonicity in the opposite direction over the -10 F to 40 F sweep (colder OAT -> larger heating load by construction).
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gate 6 row updated from `18 passing` to `33 passing` with the per-calc-module enumeration of the five named methods. Implementation-status header records the 2026-05-18 calc-module extension landing. Test-suite total updated from 3,481 to 3,496. The "Remaining v14 work" paragraph moves the named iteratives out of the open list (manual-j-worker.js noted as a thin worker host around the same pure functions covered here).
- **No runtime changes.** Pure additive unit-test coverage against existing exports from [calc-fire.js](calc-fire.js), [calc-aviation.js](calc-aviation.js), and [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js). No calc-*.js source edits. No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP / service worker / home-view payload all unchanged. `dist/` is regenerated bit-identical modulo the documentation rows.

### spec-v14 Phase E + Phase F follow-ups: interpLinear NaN-guard + CFM<->m^3/s cross-group invariant 2026-05-18

- **[pure-math.js](pure-math.js)** spec-v14 §9.1 hardening for `interpLinear`. The function now propagates `NaN` explicitly via a function-head guard rather than falling through to the upper-tail branch (every comparison against `NaN` evaluates `false`, so the prior implementation returned `ys[ys.length - 1]` - a real number that a downstream tile would treat as a valid result). Three lines of code; one branch; no behavior change for any non-NaN input. Per spec-v14 §3 the change ships with this stanza because a runtime guard against silent NaN propagation is a transcription-correctness fix, not an optimization.
- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js)** the previously-skipped Phase E §9.1 row now runs and passes. A second test (NaN-poisoning consistency across single-point, two-point, and multi-point tables) asserts the guard sits ahead of every branch. The test-file count goes from 17 / 16-passing / 1-skipped to **18 / 18-passing / 0-skipped**.
- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js)** spec-v14 §10.1 CFM<->m^3/s cross-group invariant landed. Four new tests assert (a) the Group G unit converter's `cfm -> L/s` factor matches the NIST-derived `0.471947443` value to `< 1e-12` relative, (b) the Group G crosswalk agrees with the inline `cfm * 0.000471947` constant in [calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js) to the 7-figure truncation floor (so the duct-sizing tile and the unit-converter tile do not disagree on a shared physical quantity), (c) `CFM -> L/s -> CFM` round-trips through [calc-cross.js](calc-cross.js) `convertUnit` to `< 1e-12` relative across the indoor-comfort flow sweep (10-2500 CFM), and (d) the flow-category base unit is pinned to `L/s` so a future refactor that swaps the base silently rescales every downstream consumer surfaces here. The test count goes from 16 to **20**, all passing. The IRS-mileage cross-group invariant remains an open scaffolding row: the rate is single-sourced from `STANDARD_MILEAGE_RATES` in [calc-accounting.js](calc-accounting.js) today and the cross-group invariant lands when Group J / P consume the same export.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gates 6 and 7 updated with the new test counts and the per-row enumeration of the two follow-ups; the implementation-status header and the "Remaining v14 work" paragraph both record the 2026-05-18 follow-up landing. Test-suite total updated to 3,481.
- **No runtime changes to calculators beyond the one-line `interpLinear` NaN-guard.** The guard is a defensive add at a single primitive in [pure-math.js](pure-math.js); no calc-*.js module was edited. No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP unchanged. Service worker unchanged. Home-view payload unchanged (the guard adds 3 lines of source; gzipped delta is below noise). `dist/` regenerates with the same hash set modulo the one-line source change.

### spec-v14 Phase E + Phase F scaffolding: pure-math iteratives + cross-tile invariants 2026-05-18

- **[test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js](test/unit/numerical-stability.test.js) (new)** spec-v14 §9 Phase E pin for the pure-math iterative and transcendental primitives. 17 tests (16 passing, 1 skipped follow-up): Colebrook convergence across five regime bands (laminar / transition / turbulent-smooth / turbulent-rough / fully-rough), the laminar-branch exact-identity assertion `f == 64/Re` for `Re < 2300`, the smooth-pipe pathological case (`relativeRoughness == 0`), the documented `Re <= 0` zero-flow sentinel, the Darcy-Weisbach `velocity_m_s == 0` short-circuit, psychrometric inverse coverage over the indoor-comfort sweep (six dry-bulb x five RH points) with the physical invariant `T_d <= T`, the `RH == 100` saturation identity, monotonicity in RH at fixed dry-bulb, the saturation-pressure round-trip through `dewPointFromVaporPressure_C`, refrigerant P-T table-point identity in both directions, the linear-midpoint identity, out-of-range extrapolation safety (no NaN), the documented `throw` when neither pressure nor temperature is supplied, and IEEE 754 bit-stability across repeated calls into four pure-math primitives. The skipped row pins a NaN-guard follow-up at `pure-math.js` `interpLinear` (today silently falls through to the upper-tail branch on `x = NaN`); the runtime fix lands as a focused change with a CHANGELOG row per spec-v14 §3.
- **[test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js](test/unit/cross-tile-invariants.test.js) (new)** spec-v14 §10 Phase F invariants for the pure-math primitives shared across Group A / B / C / F / L tiles. 16 tests, all passing: `awgAreaCmils` determinism across the twelve-AWG sweep, the `awgAreaCmils` / `awgAreaM2` consistency invariant through the `cmil -> m^2` factor (`pi/4 * (2.54e-5)^2`) to `< 1e-9` relative, `awgAreaCmils` strict monotonicity in AWG number, voltage-drop monotonicity in length and in current, conductor-resistance monotonicity in temperature, NFA hose-friction monotonicity in flow with the `Q^2` and `L^1` scaling identities pinned exactly (catches a future refactor that swaps the exponent), `psi <-> ft-of-head` water round-trip at `rho = 62.4 lb/ft^3` to `< 1e-12` relative, the `2.31 ft == 1 psi` published-handbook invariant to three sig figs, F<->C and C<->K round-trips to `< 1e-12` absolute, the F->C->K->C->F three-trade chain identity, the AWG-diameter-inches pinned-formula invariant (`d = 0.005 * 92^((36 - n) / 39)` to `< 1e-12` relative), and the `-40` crossover named-value invariant. The CFM<->m^3/s and IRS-mileage cross-group invariants append with the Group C/D/G and Group J/P/R Phase F closeouts.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.14 gates 6 and 7 promoted from `gate` to **partial** with per-test enumeration and the per-row remainder (calc-module iteratives + cross-group invariants) named. Build-numbers block updated to record the two new tests, the 3,476 test-suite total (was 3,443), and the zero-runtime-bytes posture (the Phase E / F tests are pure unit tests; nothing ships to `dist/`). The "Remaining v14 work" paragraph moves Phase E and Phase F into the "carry scaffolding; per-row extensions append" track and keeps Phases B, C, D, G, H in the open-gate track.
- **[docs/correctness.md](docs/correctness.md)** implementation-status header updated to record the Phase E + Phase F scaffolding landing date; the per-phase table for rows E and F now points at the live test files with the "scaffolding landed; calc-module extensions append" qualifier.
- **No runtime changes.** Pure unit-test additions against existing [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) primitives. No calc-*.js module was edited. No new dependencies. No new fetch origins. No new storage keys. CSP unchanged. Service worker unchanged. Home-view payload unchanged. `dist/` unchanged. Per spec-v14 §3, runtime changes to calculators ship only when a Phase B cross-check identifies a transcription error; the Phase E / F scaffolding here surfaces the `interpLinear` NaN-guard follow-up as a skipped row to be addressed in a focused commit, not bundled into the scaffolding.

### spec-v14 Phase A scaffolding: formula corpus + docs reconciliation 2026-05-18

- **[scripts/build-corpus.mjs](scripts/build-corpus.mjs) (new)** spec-v14 §5.2 Phase A extractor. Walks [pure-math.js](pure-math.js) and the 24 calc-*.js modules, captures every exported calculator function name and parameter list (destructured-object signatures preserved), and writes a deterministic `## Function corpus (v14)` table into [docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md) between fenced HTML comment markers so a subsequent regeneration is a clean splice rather than an append. Idempotent. The default mode rewrites the section; `--check` mode (wired into `npm run lint`) fails CI if the on-disk section is stale. Named-data exports (Example fixtures, Presets, all-caps constants) are excluded per spec-v14 §5.3 - their correctness posture is the v6 source-stamp recheck, not a calculation cross-check. 655 calculator-function rows at scaffolding close.
- **[docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md)** new `## Function corpus (v14)` section (generated). The Inputs / Output / Expression / Citation / Fixture / Tolerance columns are seeded as `_` placeholders to be filled in incrementally by Phase B (cross-validation fixtures), Phase C (dimensional-analysis annotations), Phase G (citation-to-formula round-trip), and Phase H (per-group reviewer signoff). The section is regenerated by `npm run corpus:build`.
- **[docs/correctness.md](docs/correctness.md) (new)** spec-v14 §13.2 - the contributor reference for the v14 correctness pass. Per-phase artifact map, per-group reviewer credential list, new-tile and tile-retirement flows, and the explicit "what this document does not do" closer (no per-tile fixture enumeration, no aggregate quality score, no runtime cost, no posture change). Cross-links to spec-v14, derivations.md, v6-audit.md, audit-trail.md, contributor-checklist.md, and the v0.14 launch-checklist section.
- **[README.md](README.md)** updated per spec-v14 §13.5 - added `spec-v14.md` to the spec inventory line, a new "Correctness posture" subsection under "System design and architecture" summarizing the v14 audit phases and naming the Phase A artifacts that landed (build-corpus.mjs script, `npm run audit:corpus` lint, `npm run corpus:build` regen), and a docs-list entry pointing at docs/correctness.md.
- **[docs/audit-trail.md](docs/audit-trail.md)** new "Open solicitations for v0.14 (spec-v14 §12 per-group signoff)" section under the 2026 heading. Names the credential sought per active group A through Y per spec-v14 §12.1, calls out Group H References and Group Q Historical as exempt (v6 source-stamp recheck instead), and notes that the v12 §15 gate-10 solicitations for Groups U and V are the seed. The append template and prior solicitation block are unchanged.

Gate 1 (corpus coverage) is **partial** (Phase A scaffolding shipped; per-row annotations land with Phases B/C/G/H); gate 9 (docs/correctness.md) is **pass**; gate 11 (CHANGELOG stanza-per-phase) is **partial**; remaining gates are open as the corresponding phases land. Build-numbers block records 655 corpus rows, the new scripts, the new docs, and the npm script + lint wiring.

No calc-*.js module was edited (per spec-v14 §3, runtime changes to calculators ship only when a Phase B cross-check fails and identifies a transcription error; none of Phase B has landed yet).

### spec-v13 §5.5 SPA canonical + title + meta-description emission 2026-05-18

- **[app.js](app.js)** new `updateHeadForTool(id)` and `updateHeadForHome()` helpers called from `applyRoute()`. On tile open the SPA sets `document.title` to `{Tile Name} - Rough Logic`, `<meta name="description">` to `tool.desc`, and `<link rel="canonical">` to `/tools/<id>/` so a crawler that lands on the hash URL still reads the canonical shell URL per spec-v13 §5.5. On return to home the three reverts to `Rough Logic` / the home meta description / `/`. The helpers create the `<meta>` / `<link>` elements on first call so a future deployment cannot lose them via an index.html edit. The title-format mapping is the simple `{name} - Rough Logic` form (the §6.1 profession-bearing variant is reserved for the shell, where the crawler reads it); the SPA carries the simpler form so the trade→profession map does not need to ship in the home-view payload.
- **[test/unit/spa-canonical-emission.test.js](test/unit/spa-canonical-emission.test.js) (new)** source-text invariant covering the eight call-site shapes (helper presence, applyRoute wiring, canonical href format for tile + home, title format for tile + home, meta-description update from `tool.desc`, and the element-creation fallback in `setHeadLink` / `setHeadMeta`). Source-text rather than DOM-stub because app.js is the unbundled home-view module; the full handler is exercised by the Playwright integration suite, and the source-text test holds the shape so an accidental rename / removal fails before CI runs the browser tests.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** v0.13 §16 gate #9 now references the actual handler + the new test, replacing the prior pass-by-assertion entry.
- **Home-view payload**: 55,307 B (prior commit) → **55,723 B** (+416 B, 54.4 % of 100 KB cap). JS sub-budget at **99.8 %** of 45 KB cap (was 98.9 %; the next non-trivial home-view JS addition needs a per-tile split or refactor). Per-asset HTML 8.8 % / CSS 31.0 % unchanged.
- **No new dependencies, no new runtime fetch, no new storage.** The canonical / title / description updates run synchronously inside the existing hash-route handler. CSP unchanged.
- `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK (lint, test 3,443 passing -- +8 for the new canonical test, build 591 files, check:dist clean, check:shells 385 + 24 valid, data:verify 123 entries).

### spec-v13 §15.4 documentation reconciliation: performance / threat-model / accessibility / deployment / launch-checklist 2026-05-18

- **[docs/performance.md](docs/performance.md)** new "v13 per-shell budgets (spec-v13 §12.1)" section captures the per-shell LCP / FCP / TBT / CLS targets (< 0.8 s / < 0.6 s / 0 ms / < 0.01), the 6 KB tile / 12 KB group gzip caps enforced by [scripts/check-shells.mjs](scripts/check-shells.mjs), the live measurements at v13 close (~1.8 KB tile / ~3.9 KB group gzipped), the ~2.5 MB aggregate `dist/` growth budget, and the unchanged home-view 100 KB cap. Records the Lighthouse-CI extension to two tile shells + one group shell with SEO ≥ 100 + Performance / Accessibility / Best Practices ≥ 95.
- **[docs/threat-model.md](docs/threat-model.md)** new "v13 additions and threat surface" section confirms shells inherit the same CSP via the inline `<meta http-equiv>` tag and the `_headers` file, ship zero JavaScript (the inline `<script type="application/ld+json">` block is a non-executable data block per the HTML spec, with the generator escaping `<` / `>` / `&` to `\u` form so a tile name cannot smuggle a `</script>` close), perform no runtime fetch (the "Run the calculator" link is a same-origin anchor to the SPA hash route), and that Search Console / Bing verification artifacts are one-time bootstrap files that observe the search engine, not the user. The closed schema.org allowlist is restated with the validating lint named.
- **[docs/accessibility.md](docs/accessibility.md)** new "v13 shells" section captures the single-h1 invariant per shell, the real `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` + `aria-current="page"` pattern, the real `<ul>` related-tiles block, the zero-JavaScript posture (so screen readers / voice-input / keyboard-only users see a static document), the shared 48 px touch-target token, the high-contrast / `prefers-color-scheme` propagation through `styles.css`, the JSON-LD-as-data invariant, and the axe-core verification pathway through both the `check-shells.mjs` lint and the Lighthouse CI Accessibility ≥ 95 threshold.
- **[docs/deployment.md](docs/deployment.md)** new "v0.13.0 release notes" section records the build-output growth (~2.5 MB uncompressed for the shell tree), the unchanged CSP / service-worker precache (shells are not added to `SHELL_FILES`; they are reached by crawlers and direct navigation, not by SPA route changes), the sitemap regeneration step, and the Lighthouse-CI extension. A new sub-section "Search Console / Bing Webmaster Tools (spec-v13 §10.4 / Phase I)" documents the verification method (DNS TXT preferred; HTML-file fallback), the sitemap submission step, the monthly review cadence, and the no-cookie / no-visitor-identifier posture per §13.1.
- **[docs/launch-checklist.md](docs/launch-checklist.md)** new "v0.13 (spec-v13 discoverability)" section enumerates the spec-v13 §16 launch gates (1-11) as a table mirroring the v0.12 §15 gates: 10 of 11 pass (shell presence per `/tools/<id>/` and `/groups/<slug>/`, sitemap with 411 URLs, axe-core via `check-shells.mjs` + Lighthouse, §11 authoring lint, JSON-LD allowlist, Lighthouse SEO ≥ 100, `npm run audit` clean, SPA canonical-emission, seo.md + seo-log.md present); gate 11 (post-deploy sitemap submission + Search Console / Bing verification) remains the only post-deploy gate. Records the v0.13 build numbers (385 tile shells / 24 group shells / 411 sitemap URLs / `dist/` 5,633 KB / RELATED registry at 385 / 385) and notes the lint additions (`check-shells.mjs` + `check-related-tiles.mjs`).
- **No runtime or build-pipeline changes.** Pure documentation reconciliation against spec-v13 §15.4. No new dependencies. No shell-payload growth. CSP unchanged. Home-view payload unchanged.
- `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files / 5,633 KB, check:dist 0 orphans, check:shells 385 + 24 valid, data:verify 123 entries).

bridging to Group F rigging), F (Fire-ground: aerial-ladder / ladder-pipe-reach pair, smoke-reading / confined-space-vent / confined-space-purge / scba-cylinder-time confined-space cluster, reverse-lay-friction / standpipe-friction / sprinkler-density supply cluster, sling-angle / iso-nff / nfpa-1142-water-supply, braking-distance bridging to Group J), G (Cross-trade: sales-tax / tip-out / upgrade-roi / mileage-cost / overtime / per-diem / timesheet cost-and-labor cluster, geometry / slope-from-level / ramp-slope / trench-slope geometry cluster, dilution / wind-chill / fall-protection-clearance / vehicle-load / noise-dose / rainwater-yield long-tail), H (References: inspection-checklist / tool-maintenance / emergency-contacts / hand-signals / defensible-space / storm-shelter), J (Trucking: reefer-burn, incoterm-decoder, stopping-sight-distance pairs into Group F braking-distance), K (Mechanic: bolt-stretch / driveshaft-crit / tire-gearing / brake-pad-life), L (Agriculture: timber-cruise, drawbar-power, bulk-density, crop-yield, thi-livestock bridging to Group U vet-energy-requirement), M (Water: lab-dilution, pump-eff-w2w, srt-fm-ratio / svi-sludge-index pair), N (neutral-imbalance bridging to Group A phase-balance), Q (historical-pricing), and U (Vet: vet-gestation, vet-urine-sg, vet-breed-predispositions, vet-plasma-css). Every entry remains within the 6-id §9.1 cap; the typical entry shape is 2-4 ids reflecting the actual reference graph rather than padding for SEO juice.
- **Fallback retained as safety net.** The "first 5 in same group" fallback in [scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) is unchanged; with 385 / 385 coverage no tile in the current catalog uses it, but a future tile that lands before its registry entry will still ship a related-tiles block. The fallback also remains the place that handles the cap-6 truncation for any future curated entry that briefly exceeds the cap during editorial work (the lint catches it before commit).
- **[scripts/check-related-tiles.mjs](scripts/check-related-tiles.mjs)** unchanged; runs against all 385 entries and passes (every key is a real TOOLS tile id, no self-references, no duplicates, every list at most 6 entries, every referenced id resolves to a real TOOLS tile).
- **Hard limits preserved.** Pure editorial expansion. No runtime behavior change (the SPA does not import the registry). No new dependencies. No shell-payload growth (curated edges replace fallback edges 1:1; both render as a `<ul>` of `<li><a>` items, same number of items per shell). CSP unchanged. Home-view payload unchanged.
- **Build numbers (refreshed 2026-05-18)**: `dist/` unchanged at 5,653.6 KB. Tile shells, group shells, sitemap, JSON-LD: all unchanged in size. `scripts/related-tiles.mjs` is build-time only and does not ship to `dist/`.
- `npm run audit` reports all stages OK (lint -- including check-related-tiles on 385 curated entries, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files, check:dist / check:shells / data:verify clean).

### spec-v13 Phase E: split RELATED out of tile-meta.js into a build-time-only module 2026-05-18

- **[scripts/related-tiles.mjs](scripts/related-tiles.mjs) (new)** absorbs the `RELATED` const that previously lived inside [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js). The map is build-time editorial metadata consumed only by [scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs); the SPA does not import it. Moving it out of the runtime surface (a) stops tile-meta.js from growing toward its module-size cap as the editorial map fills in, and (b) frees the SPA service-worker precache from carrying ~10 KB of build-time-only data the runtime never executes. All 206 curated entries carry over byte-for-byte; the entry shape is unchanged.
- **[tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js)** drops the inline `RELATED` const and the `related: RELATED[id] || []` field on every TILE_META row. TILE_META now carries only the v10 §B.2 runtime fields (id, group, simplified, requires_field_meter, a11y_verified_on). The module shrinks from 9,917 B gzipped to 5,060 B (49% reduction). Lives in the SPA precache list per its v10 contract; the size drop is real bytes the precache no longer ships.
- **[scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs](scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs)** drops the `related` field validation (the field is gone); the comment block records the move and points at the new lint.
- **[scripts/check-related-tiles.mjs](scripts/check-related-tiles.mjs) (new)** validates the moved registry: every key is a real TOOLS tile id, every value is an array of strings, no entry references its own tile, no duplicates, every list at most 6 entries per spec-v13 §9.1, every referenced id is a real TOOLS tile. The lint runs as part of `npm run lint` between check-tile-meta and check-wiring; a curated entry that drifts (typo, retired tile) fails CI.
- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs)** updated to `import(resolve(ROOT, "scripts/related-tiles.mjs"))` instead of `tile-meta.js`. The `relatedTiles()` helper signature changes from `(tool, tools, tileMeta)` to `(tool, tools, related)`; behavior is unchanged. Shell output is byte-equivalent.
- **[scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs](scripts/check-module-sizes.mjs) tile-meta.js cap dropped 11000 → 7000 B gzipped.** The module no longer carries the editorial map; the prior cap-bump arithmetic (7000 → 9000 → 11000 B across the day's Phase E expansion) reverts. tile-meta.js sits at 5,060 B / 7,000 B (72.3 % of cap) at this commit, with normal headroom for future v10 §B.2 fields.
- **[docs/seo.md](docs/seo.md) + [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md)** updated to point at the new location.
- **Hard limits preserved.** No runtime behavior change. Tile shell rendering is unchanged (the curated edges still surface in the curated order). SPA hash routing unchanged. CSP unchanged. Home-view payload unchanged.
- **Build numbers (refreshed 2026-05-18)**: `dist/` 5,670 KB → **5,650 KB** (-20 KB; tile-meta.js shrank from ~10 KB gzipped + ~38 KB raw to ~5 KB gzipped + ~18 KB raw, and scripts/related-tiles.mjs does not ship to dist/). `tile-meta.js` 5,060 B / 7,000 B gzipped. Tile shells, group shells, sitemap, JSON-LD: all unchanged.
- `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK (lint -- now including check-related-tiles on 206 seeded entries, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files / 5,650 KB, check:dist 1,407 refs / 0 orphans, check:shells 385 tile + 24 group shells valid, data:verify 123 entries).

### spec-v13 Phase E expansion + docs/seo.md + docs/seo-log.md scaffolds 2026-05-18

- **[docs/seo.md](docs/seo.md) (new)** spec-v13 §15.4 -- the operations-side companion to spec-v13.md documenting the per-tile shell model, the authoring rules (verb-first descriptions, profession-bearing titles, no-marketing-language posture with the exempted-engineering-terms note), the JSON-LD structured-data allowlist, the internal-link-graph posture, the Phase H performance budgets (6 KB tile / 12 KB group gzip caps; LCP <0.8s, FCP <0.6s, TBT 0ms, CLS <0.01 targets), the Phase I no-telemetry measurement stance (Cloudflare edge metrics + Google Search Console + Bing Webmaster Tools, all source-side, no visitor instrumentation), the closed list of what v13 does not do (no SSR, no prerender framework, no AMP / PWA install prompt / hreflang / author bylines / review microdata / backlink solicitation / paid placement), and the per-file map for contributor reference.

Schema and intent only at this commit; the first entry lands after the v13 deploy + Search Console / Bing Webmaster Tools verification (the §10.4 / Phase I bootstrap). The registry is build-time data, not runtime logic; the right remediation per the v10 §H.1 cap doc is "raise the cap with a CHANGELOG note" (the alternative -- per-tile split -- is appropriate when the data grows by orders of magnitude, not when one editorial pass adds 30 lines of map entries). The new 9000 B cap carries the same ~20% headroom convention the other module caps use.

### spec-v13 Phase E (related-tiles registry) + Phase H (Lighthouse extension) 2026-05-18

- **[tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js) `RELATED` const + `related` field on TILE_META** ships the spec-v13 §5.2 / §9.1 per-tile related-tiles registry. The seed set covers ~21 high-traffic tiles across Groups A (ohms-law, wire-ampacity, voltage-drop, conduit-fill, breaker-sizing, motor-fla), B (pipe-sizing, friction-loss, pump-sizing, pipe-volume), C (manual-j-cooling, manual-j-heating, duct-sizing, refrigerant-pt, superheat-subcool), D (psychrometric, drying-goal, dehumidifier), and F (fire-friction, pdp, hydrant-flow, required-fire-flow). Each entry is the editorial cross-reference set the citation graph + worked-example narratives + unit-test fixture neighbors already imply (the spec.md §10 / spec-v3 §HVAC / v6 §F worked-example sequences). Tiles outside the seed continue to use the build-shells.mjs fallback ("first 5 other tiles in the same group, by TOOLS order"); populating the remaining ~360 tiles is contributor editorial work, tracked separately.
- **[scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs](scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs)** extended to validate the new `related` field: must be an array, max 6 entries per §9.1, every entry must be a string and a real TOOLS id, no self-reference, no duplicates. The lint runs as part of `npm run lint` so a curated set that drifts (a typo in an id, a stale reference to a retired tile) fails CI.
- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) `relatedTiles()`** now imports `TILE_META` from tile-meta.js dynamically and uses the curated `related` array when present, falling back to the Phase A "first 5 in same group" order otherwise. The shells of the seeded tiles now render the editorial cross-references (ohms-law's "Related tiles" leads with Voltage Drop, not Wire Ampacity; pump-sizing leads with Friction Loss, not Pipe Sizing).
- **[lighthouserc.json](lighthouserc.json) Phase H** switched `staticDistDir` from `./` to `./dist` so Lighthouse serves the built site (the prerendered tile and group shells live under `dist/tools/<id>/` and `dist/groups/<slug>/`; the home document and every SPA module is already copied into dist/ by `scripts/build.mjs`, so the existing SPA URLs continue to resolve). Three new URLs added to the audit set: `/tools/wire-ampacity/` (a representative tile shell, Group A), `/tools/friction-loss/` (a representative tile shell, Group B), and `/groups/electrical/` (a representative group shell). The existing six SPA URLs (home + five tiles via hash route) carry forward unchanged.
- **[.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml) `lighthouse` job** prefixed with `npm ci` + `npm run build` so dist/ exists at the moment `lhci autorun` runs. The previous configuration served the repo root and did not need a build step; the v13 switch to `./dist` makes the build a hard precondition.
- **No production code changes** in the runtime. tile-meta.js is a build-time + future runtime registry; the SPA does not yet read `related` (the renderer uses search-discovery + the existing tile-link grid; a Phase E follow-up could surface the curated edges in the live tool view, but that is out of scope for the discoverability spec).
- **Build numbers (refreshed 2026-05-18)**: tile shells stay ~1.8 KB gzipped / 5.4 KB raw; the curated edges replace the fallback edges 1:1, so the per-shell payload is unchanged. `dist/` 5,670 KB unchanged. The home view is unchanged. `npm run lint` runs the extended tile-meta lint against the seeded `related` entries; all 21 pass.
- `npm run audit` reports all 6 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files / 5,670 KB, check:dist 1,407 refs / 0 orphans, check:shells 385 tile + 24 group shells valid, data:verify 123 entries).

### spec-v13 Phase B + C + G: shell authoring, JSON-LD, payload-budget lint 2026-05-18

- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) Phase B** wires the spec-v13 §6.1 / §6.2 authoring caps into the generator so every shell ships within the contract the lint enforces. `buildTitle()` falls back from the full `{Name} - {Profession Noun} - Rough Logic` to `{Name} - Rough Logic` when the full form exceeds 70 characters, then truncates the tile name only as a last resort (the brand suffix is preserved either way). `metaDescription()` truncates against the HTML-escaped string length so the value the search-engine snippet reads out of the rendered `<meta content="...">` attribute stays under the §6.2 220-char cap (a previous off-by-escape draft truncated raw length, which let apostrophe-containing descriptions overflow once HTML-escaped to `&#39;`). Marketing tail rephrased from "Free, client-side, ad-free, account-free reference" to "Client-side, ad-free, account-free reference" so the snippet leads with the verb-first calculation and the audience-bearing tail fits in the remaining budget.
- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) Phase C** appends one `<script type="application/ld+json">` block to every shell `<head>`. Tile shells carry a `WebApplication` item (name, description, canonical URL, applicationCategory `BusinessApplication`, operatingSystem `Any (browser)`, `isAccessibleForFree: true`, `Offer` with `price "0"` / `priceCurrency "USD"`, author `Clay Good` linking to `https://claygood.com`) plus a 3-step `BreadcrumbList` (Home > Group > Tile). Group shells carry `CollectionPage` + 2-step `BreadcrumbList` + `ItemList` (one `ListItem` per tile, in TOOLS order). JSON-LD is JSON-encoded with `<`, `>`, `&` escaped to `<`, `>`, `&` so the inline `</script>` close cannot be smuggled by a tile name or description (the existing grep-checks ngram lint already screens upstream, but the escape here is the structural guard regardless). No `HowTo` block in Phase C: that requires a per-tile input schema that tile-meta.js does not yet carry; the registry build-out is a Phase E follow-up.
- **[scripts/check-shells.mjs](scripts/check-shells.mjs) (new) Phase G** is a standalone Node 20 lint that walks `dist/tools/<id>/index.html` and `dist/groups/<slug>/index.html` and asserts: every TOOLS tile has a shell; every shell's `<title>` is within the §6.1 70-char cap and ends with " - Rough Logic"; every shell's `<meta name="description">` is within the §6.2 220-char cap; every shell carries a `<link rel="canonical">` under the site origin; every shell carries the four OG / Twitter Card meta tags; every JSON-LD block parses, has `@context: "https://schema.org"`, and uses only types on the closed allowlist (`WebApplication`, `WebPage`, `CollectionPage`, `BreadcrumbList`, `ItemList`, `ListItem`, `Offer`, `Person`, `HowTo`, `HowToStep`); no banned marketing word appears in any title or description; every tile shell gzips under the §5.4 6 KB cap and every group shell under the §8.3 12 KB cap. The banned-word list is narrowed to unambiguously-marketing terms (`awesome`, `amazing`, `killer`, `badass`, `revolutionary`, `game-changing`, `world-class`); the §11.3 list also names `best` / `easy` / `simple` / `fast` / `fastest` / `ultimate` / `powerful` but those have legitimate engineering / pedagogical uses in the live TOOLS descs ("east-is-least / west-is-best" mnemonic, "simple or compound interest", "ultimate axial capacity", future "Fast Fourier") and are exempted; the contributor remains responsible for avoiding marketing copy.
- **[package.json](package.json) `check:shells` script** added and wired into [scripts/audit.mjs](scripts/audit.mjs) between `check:dist` and `data:verify` so the shell lint runs against the freshly-built `dist/` tree on every pre-PR `npm run audit`.
- **Build numbers (refreshed 2026-05-18, post Phase B + C + G)**: tile shells ~5.4 KB raw / ~1.8 KB gzipped (up from ~4.5 KB raw / ~1.5 KB gzipped at Phase A close, driven by the JSON-LD block); group shells ~15.2 KB raw / ~3.9 KB gzipped (up from ~10.3 KB raw / ~3.3 KB gzipped, same driver). Both safely under the §5.4 / §8.3 6 KB / 12 KB gzip caps. `dist/` 5,224 KB → **5,670 KB** (+446 KB across 409 shells, ~1.1 KB per shell from the inlined JSON-LD).
- **Hard limits preserved.** No new third-party dependency. JSON-LD is generated from the same TOOLS array and GROUP_NAMES the shells already read; no new data source. CSP `default-src 'self'` continues; the `<script type="application/ld+json">` block is an inline data island, not executable script, so it falls under the existing `style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'` carve-out's same character-set discipline (the grep-checks ngram lint already screens the upstream TOOLS strings). Home-view payload unchanged.
- `npm run audit` reports all **6** stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files / 5,670 KB, check:dist 1,407 refs / 0 orphans, check:shells 385 tile + 24 group shells valid, data:verify 123 entries).

### spec-v13 Phase A + D + F: per-tile shells, per-group shells, sitemap expansion 2026-05-18

- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) (new)** is a standalone Node 20 script using only built-ins. Parses TOOLS (id, name, group, trades, desc) and GROUP_NAMES out of [app.js](app.js) via the same regex pattern [scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs](scripts/check-tile-meta.mjs) already uses, then emits one HTML shell per tile under `dist/tools/<id>/index.html` and one per group under `dist/groups/<slug>/index.html`. Each shell carries the same CSP `<meta http-equiv>` tag the home document carries, a profession-bearing `<title>` (Tile Name - Profession Noun - Rough Logic per spec-v13 §11.2), a verb-first meta description (spec-v13 §11.1; tiles whose `desc` does not lead with an admissible verb get a "Reference for" prefix), a `<link rel="canonical">` to the shell's own URL, Open Graph + Twitter Card meta, a breadcrumb (Home > Group > Tile), one h1 with the tile name, the tile description as a lead paragraph, a "Run the calculator" link to the SPA's hash form (`/#<id>`), an "Audience" block naming the profession, a "Related tiles" block (Phase A fallback: first 5 other tiles in the same group; Phase E will replace with the v10 §B.2 `related` field), and a footer matching the home document's footer plus the spec-v13 §5.2 inline disclaimer. Shells carry zero JavaScript: a person who arrives at a shell from a search result reads a static reference page; clicking "Run the calculator" loads the SPA from the home URL with the existing hash route.
- **[scripts/build.mjs](scripts/build.mjs)** wires `build-shells.mjs` into the build pipeline after the verbatim copy + build-info stamp so it can read `dist/build-info.json` for the sitemap `<lastmod>`. The shell builder fails fast (non-zero exit) on any I/O or parse error so a regression surfaces as a build failure rather than as a silent skip.
- **[scripts/check-dist.mjs](scripts/check-dist.mjs)** adds two prefix exemptions to the orphan-warning allowlist: `tools/<id>/index.html` and `groups/<slug>/index.html`. The shells are crawler-facing reference pages enumerated in `sitemap.xml`; they are not referenced by any other HTML / JS / CSS asset (the v12 §G.3 lint scans only `<link>` / `<script>` / `<img>` / `<source>` / `<video>` / `<audio>` href/src, not `<a href>`), so without the exemption every shell would surface as an orphan. The shell's `<link rel="stylesheet" href="../../styles.css">` still resolves through the lint as today.
- **[styles.css](styles.css) §spec-v13 Phase A + D** appended a shell-only selector set: `.shell-page .shell-main`, `.shell-page .shell-breadcrumb`, `.shell-page .shell-h1`, `.shell-page .shell-lead`, `.shell-page .shell-run-link` (48 px touch target preserved), `.shell-page .shell-section`, `.shell-page .shell-related`, `.shell-page .shell-disclaimer`. Reuses the same `--space-*`, `--border`, `--accent`, `--bg`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--radius`, `--bg-tertiary` CSS custom properties the SPA already defines; no new color tokens, no new layout primitives, no `@media` queries beyond what the existing stylesheet provides via the shared properties.
- **[scripts/build-shells.mjs](scripts/build-shells.mjs) sitemap.xml regenerator** writes the full URL set at build time. The pre-v13 `sitemap.xml` enumerated exactly one URL (the home page); the post-Phase-F sitemap enumerates 411 URLs (home + changelog + 24 group shells + 385 tile shells), each with a `<lastmod>` drawn from `dist/build-info.json`, a `<changefreq>` of `weekly` for home/changelog and `monthly` for groups/tiles, and a `<priority>` of 1.0 home / 0.5 changelog / 0.8 group / 0.7 tile per spec-v13 §10.1. The repo-root `sitemap.xml` is still copied verbatim by the existing FILES list (so the dev-server `npm run dev` still serves a sitemap); the build then overwrites it in `dist/` with the expanded version.
- **[specs/spec-v13.md](specs/spec-v13.md) implementation-status banner** marked Phases A + D + F as landed 2026-05-18. Phases B, C, E, G, H follow in subsequent commits; Phase I lands after first deploy.
- **Build numbers (refreshed 2026-05-18)**: `dist/` 182 files / 3,211.1 KB (pre-v13) → **591 files / 5,224.1 KB** post-v13 Phase A + D + F (+409 files, +2,013 KB; +409 shells averaging ~5 KB raw / ~1.5 KB gzipped per tile shell and ~10 KB raw / ~3.3 KB gzipped per group shell, per the §5.4 / §8.3 caps of 6 KB and 12 KB gzipped respectively). The home-view payload is unchanged because the shells are separate documents.
- **Hard limits preserved.** No new third-party dependency. CSP `default-src 'self'` continues; the shells inherit it via the same `<meta http-equiv>` tag the home document carries. No telemetry. No JavaScript on shells (zero TBT). No new storage keys (shells are stateless). No new fetch origins. The home view's 100 KB-after-gzip payload budget is unchanged (the shells are not loaded by the home document and are not in the precache list).
- `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build 591 files / 5,224.1 KB, check:dist 1,407 references resolved with 0 orphan warnings, data:verify 123 entries).

### spec-v13 drafted: search discoverability and crawlable surface 2026-05-18

- **[specs/spec-v13.md](specs/spec-v13.md) (new)** drafted, status: drafted, not yet landed. v13 is a discoverability spec. It addresses the structural defect every prior spec has carried: the site is a single-page application with hash-based routing, which means every URL a crawler can fetch returns the same home document, and every per-tile heading, citation, and worked example lives behind a JavaScript route fragment that crawlers do not execute. The fix is one build-time prerendered HTML shell per tile at `/tools/<id>/index.html` and one per group at `/groups/<slug>/index.html`, each carrying a `<title>`, a `<meta name="description">`, a `<link rel="canonical">`, Open Graph + Twitter Card meta, a JSON-LD `WebApplication` / `HowTo` / `BreadcrumbList` block, the inline notice variant, the source-stamp citation, the worked-example fixture as readable text, and an internal link graph that connects shells to group landing pages and to related tiles. Sitemap.xml is expanded from one URL to one per tile + one per group. The SPA continues to live at the home URL with no change to its hash-routing behavior; the shells are stateless reference pages with no JavaScript.
- **Hard limits preserved.** No new third-party dependencies, no new licenses, no new storage keys, no new fetch origins, no new runtime state channels, no telemetry, no AI, no advertising. CSP `default-src 'self'` continues; the shells inherit it via the same `<meta http-equiv>` tag and the same `_headers`. The home view's 100 KB-after-gzip payload budget is unchanged because shells are separate documents.
- **Phase order documented.** Phase A (per-tile shells) → B (metadata authoring) → C (JSON-LD) → D (group shells) → E (internal linking) → F (sitemap) → G (authoring lint) → H (performance budget) → I (post-deploy measurement via Search Console / Bing Webmaster Tools, the only third-party observation introduced and exclusively maintainer-side).
- **[README.md](README.md) §Utility groups paragraph** appended a one-sentence pointer to spec-v13 so a reader of the README has the up-to-date pointer alongside the v1-v12 inheritance line.
- **Documentation-only commit.** No code change, no test change. The Phase A-I implementation lands in a subsequent release once the spec is reviewed.
- `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build, check:dist, data:verify 123 entries).

### v12 §H follow-up: check-sw-precache.mjs inverse-direction lint 2026-05-18

- **[scripts/check-sw-precache.mjs](scripts/check-sw-precache.mjs) (new)** closes the omission class the prior 2026-05-18 sw.js fix surfaced. Walks the live `data/` tree and asserts every `data/<folder>/manifest.json` is named in `sw.js` `DATA_MANIFESTS`; walks the `FILES` array in [scripts/build.mjs](scripts/build.mjs) and asserts every shipped repo-root `.js` module is named in `sw.js` `SHELL_ASSETS`. The v12 §G.3 [scripts/check-dist.mjs](scripts/check-dist.mjs) walks the opposite direction (precache entries → dist files), so the realestate-manifest omission was a class the prior gate could not catch by construction; this script is the missing inverse and fails CI when a new `data/<folder>/` lands without its precache line, or when a new repo-root `.js` lands in `FILES` without its `SHELL_ASSETS` line.
- **[package.json](package.json) `lint` script** wired the new lint in between `check-wiring.mjs` and `check-module-sizes.mjs` (alphabetically adjacent to the other wiring lints and ordered so the SW precache check runs after the runtime-wiring gates have passed). `npm run audit` picks it up through `npm run lint` without an audit-stage rewire.
- **Standalone Node 20 script using only built-ins.** No network, no deps. Reads `sw.js`, `data/<folder>/`, and `scripts/build.mjs` directly; parses the `const NAME = [...];` string-literal arrays out of `sw.js` with a single regex. Reports `check-sw-precache OK: 18 data/<folder>/manifest.json entries and 44 calc-*/support .js entries all present in sw.js precache lists.` on success.
- **No production code changes.** The realestate-manifest line landed in the prior commit; this commit only adds the gate that would have caught it.
- `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build, check:dist, data:verify 123 entries).

### v12 §H follow-up: sw.js precache list missing v12 realestate manifest 2026-05-18

- **[sw.js](sw.js) `DATA_MANIFESTS`** added the missing `./data/realestate/manifest.json` entry. The list pre-fetches one manifest per `data/` folder at service-worker install time so the runtime startup integrity check ([integrity.js](integrity.js)) finds every manifest in the SW cache even on a cold offline load. The v12 `data/realestate/` folder (added 2026-05-16 with `loan-limits.json` + `hud-fmr.json`) was wired into the SW shell precache for the calc-realestate.js module and into `data/integrity.json` for the expected-hashes registry, but the per-folder manifest line in `DATA_MANIFESTS` was omitted. Without this line a user who installs the SW before ever opening an X.* tile online, then goes offline, would hit a network-fetch failure on the realestate manifest at startup and surface a spurious integrity banner. With this line, the manifest pre-caches on install alongside the other seventeen data folders.
- **No docs change.** The deployment.md §v0.12 release-notes stanza already names the `data/realestate/` folder; performance.md §Page weight strategy already names it in the 18-folder enumeration; architecture.md ASCII diagram already lists it under `data/`. Only the SW precache list was missing it.
- **No test change.** No test currently asserts the DATA_MANIFESTS list against the live `data/` folder set; the omission slipped past the v12 §G.3 dist/-vs-runtime check because that lint walks references the other direction (from precache entries to dist files). A future spec follow-up could add a `scripts/check-sw-precache.mjs` inverse-direction lint, but the immediate fix is one line. (Follow-up: [scripts/check-sw-precache.mjs](scripts/check-sw-precache.mjs) landed 2026-05-18 in the next commit, wired into `npm run lint`.)
- `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK (lint, test 3,435 passing, build, check:dist, data:verify 123 entries).

### v12 §G.3 follow-up: check-dist scanner now recognizes sw.js precache list 2026-05-17

- **Bug fix in [scripts/check-dist.mjs](scripts/check-dist.mjs).** The G.3 dist/-vs-runtime cross-check was emitting three spurious orphan warnings for [cost-output.js](cost-output.js), [search-discovery.js](search-discovery.js), and [tile-meta.js](tile-meta.js). All three are referenced from the [sw.js](sw.js) service-worker precache list (`SHELL_ASSETS`), but the JS scanner in `scannersFor()` only matched `import` / `import()` / `declare()` / `new Worker()` / `fetch()` syntax. The plain `"./<file>.js"` string entries in the precache array did not count as inbound references, so the three modules that the runtime resolves only via the SW shell tripped the warning.
- **Fix.** Adds an `sw.js`-only scanner that matches `"./<file>.<ext>"` string literals across the precache arrays. The scanner is scoped to `rel === "sw.js"` so it does not relax the import-discipline check for any other JS file. With the scanner in place the G.3 lint now resolves **179 same-origin references** (up from 109) and emits **0 orphan warning(s)**; a typo in the SW precache list would now surface as a G.3 dangling-reference *failure* in the same channel as a typo in an `import` or `fetch`.
- **No behavior change to the runtime.** Pure lint-tightening on the build-time audit. `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK.

### v12 inline-notice fix: per-group rules for Groups U / V / W / X / Y 2026-05-17

- **Bug fix in [app.js](app.js).** Group U Veterinary, V EMS, W Aviation, X Real Estate, and Y Educators tiles were falling through the per-group notice selector to `NOTICE_DEFAULT` ("Local codes, manufacturer specifications, and the authority having jurisdiction govern all installations and inspections"). That language is wrong for veterinary practice, prehospital medicine, flight planning, mortgage underwriting, and classroom assessment. The structured CITATIONS / GOVERNANCE map in [citations.js](citations.js) already carried the right per-group governance language (`veterinary`, `ems_prehospital`, `aviation`, `real_estate`, `education`), but the inline notice rendered above the input region did not.
- **Fix.** Adds five new `NOTICE_<X>` constants in [app.js](app.js) (`NOTICE_VETERINARY`, `NOTICE_EMS`, `NOTICE_AVIATION`, `NOTICE_REAL_ESTATE`, `NOTICE_EDUCATION`) and five new per-group rules in the notice-selection priority block (between the existing Group T rule and the fire-trade fallback). Each constant matches the corresponding GOVERNANCE string in voice and content: vet names the attending veterinarian + RVT / LVT; EMS names the receiving facility + EMS medical director + agency protocol; aviation names PIC + AFM / POH; real estate names lender + appraiser + program guidelines; education names the classroom teacher.
- **Group U / V tiles continue to render the spec-v10 §B.1 limitation banner** below the per-group notice per the spec-v12 §13.1 override. The banner is the long-form, screening-tile-specific replacement copy; the per-group notice is the short, every-tile governance reminder. Both render.
- **[docs/notice-variants.md](docs/notice-variants.md) extended** with the five new variants in the wording table and the five new per-group rules in the selection-rules block. The "Group U / V tiles also render the §B.1 limitation banner" note carries the cross-reference.
- **Home-view payload**: 54,357 B (pre-fix) -> 54,817 B (post-fix; 53.5% of 100 KB cap). JS sub-budget tightens from 97.9% to 98.9% of cap; still under. Each new NOTICE constant is ~150 B raw + a one-line per-group rule. `npm run audit` reports all 5 stages OK.

### Spec-v12 §14.4 follow-up: derivations + citation-discipline v12 sections 2026-05-16

- **[docs/derivations.md](docs/derivations.md) extended** with five new first-principles sections (§§68-72) covering the v12 math families: §68 vet energy and fluid (Kleiber RER + Holliday-Segar fluid + Riviere-Papich Css), §69 burn fluid resuscitation and TBSA (Parkland + Rule of 9s + Lund-Browder), §70 aviation density altitude / true airspeed / crosswind / wind triangle (FAA-H-8083-25C + ICAO Standard Atmosphere), §71 mortgage and CRE ratios (annuity P&I + amortization + DTI / LTV / cap rate / DSCR), §72 educator readability formulas and z-score (Kincaid 1975 + McLaughlin SMOG + Coleman-Liau + Abramowitz-Stegun 26.2.17). Each section names a public-domain or pre-DMCA primary source and a worked verification.
- **[docs/citation-discipline.md](docs/citation-discipline.md) extended** with per-group source-stamp tables for Groups U / V / W / X / Y (43 new rows). Tile ids match the live `TOOLS` array (e.g. `vet-weight-based-dose`, `glasgow-coma-scale`, `parkland-formula`, `crosswind-component`, `exchange-1031-timeline`, `readability`, `gpa-calculator`); the v10 §A.3 [scripts/build-citation-strings.mjs](scripts/build-citation-strings.mjs) generator regenerated [docs/citation-strings.generated.json](docs/citation-strings.generated.json) from 52 -> 95 rows and the `--check` mode passes. Group U / V tiles cite the §B.1 limitation-banner discipline (professional-governs banner + free-access URL); Group W / X / Y tiles use cite-strong governance verbiage (PIC governs / lender governs / teacher governs) without the banner.
