### Build progress (v8)

- Phase C renderer wiring batch 4 landed - completes the v8 Phase C tile-renderer pass. Seven final tile renderers updated to surface the compute fields built across Phase C batches 1-5.

  1. **dehumidifier** ([calc-restoration.js](calc-restoration.js)) gains an "Operational guidance" row that surfaces operational_guidance ("one large LGR or two mid-range for redundancy" etc.) keyed off the field-method recommendation.

  2. **air-movers** ([calc-restoration.js](calc-restoration.js)) gains "Placement pattern" and "Placement note" rows covering corners / corners + perimeter / continuous-perimeter strategies per IICRC S500 §12 typical practice.

  3. **hepa-filter-life** ([calc-restoration.js](calc-restoration.js)) gains optional "Job duration (days)" + "Filter cost ($)" inputs and "Filters for full job" + "Total filter cost" output rows so the tech can budget filters and dollars for an entire engagement.

  4. **timber-cruise** ([calc-agriculture.js](calc-agriculture.js)) gains optional "Price ($/bf)" input and "Estimated stand value" output row showing bf x $/bf in USD.

  5. **water-hammer-arrestor** ([calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)) gains optional "System pressure (psi)" input and "Pre-charge (set air side, system depressurized)" + "Placement" output rows so the tech sees the actual air-charge value to set on the bladder.

  6. **hos-math** ([calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js)) gains optional "Current time (ISO)" input and a "Next legal drive start" output row that surfaces the timestamp + reason ("10-hour reset" / "30-minute break" / "may drive now") returned by compute.

  7. **refrigerant-pt** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)) gains optional "Outdoor air temp (F)" + "Indoor wet-bulb (F)" inputs and a "Target superheat (if OAT + WB supplied)" output row showing the typical-band charging-chart approximation alongside the manufacturer-governs note.

  7 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-renderer-wiring4.test.js assert the new output IDs (dh-out-g, am-out-p, am-out-n, hl-out-fj, hl-out-tc, tc-out-v, wha-out-pc, wha-out-pn, hos-out-nt, rp-out-tsh) and the spec wording ("Operational guidance", "Placement pattern", "Placement note", "Filters for full job", "Total filter cost", "Estimated stand value", "Pre-charge (set air side, system depressurized)", "Next legal drive start", "Target superheat") are intact in source. 2293 unit tests passing total; lint clean; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1533.4 KB.

  v8 renderer wiring complete: 29 tile renderers wired across four batches (batch 1: 6, batch 2: 8, batch 3: 8, batch 4: 7). Every Phase C compute field added across batches 1-5 now has a renderer surface. The v8 punch list (audit, citation polish, field-reality refinements) is closed: Phase A (manifest discipline), Phase B (citation strings tile-by-tile), Phase C (per-tool refinements + renderer wiring), Phase D (cost-output / context-band / standard-sizes shared helpers), and Phase E (5 new utilities: panel-rebalance, trap-arm already from v2, duct-leakage, residential-framing, coagulant-dose) are all landed.

- Phase C renderer wiring batch 3 landed. Eight more tile renderers updated to surface the compute fields built in earlier Phase C batches.

  1. **breaker-sizing** ([calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)) gains optional "Load (W)", "Voltage (V)", "Phase", and "Power factor" inputs plus a "Watts -> amps (if W supplied)" output row that surfaces the derived current when the watts-input mode is used.

  2. **displacement-cr** ([calc-mechanic.js](calc-mechanic.js)) gains a "Premium octane required" row that surfaces the requires_premium_octane flag (CR > 10.5:1) in plain English.

  3. **brake-pad-life** ([calc-mechanic.js](calc-mechanic.js)) gains an optional "Pad-set cost ($)" input and a "Cost per 100k mi" output row surfacing the cost_per_100k_miles_usd projection.

  4. **weight-balance** ([calc-mechanic.js](calc-mechanic.js)) gains optional "MAC leading-edge (in)" + "MAC chord (in)" inputs and a "CG as %MAC" output row showing CG and the fwd/aft envelope as %MAC - which is how pilots read CG on the published loading graph.

  5. **pallet-loadout** ([calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js)) gains "Binding margin" and "Slack utilization" output rows so the loader can see how much headroom the binding limit beats the slack limit by.

  6. **reefer-burn** ([calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js)) gains optional "Haul distance (mi)" + "Average speed (mph)" inputs and a "Fuel reserve at end of haul" output row that flags shortfall ("X gal short - refuel mid-haul") when the tank can't cover the haul.

  7. **cfm-per-ton** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)) gains a "Climate hint" output row surfacing the climate label + the latent-removal hint (e.g. "Humid climate - low SHR (<0.75); reduce CFM to drive more latent removal").

  8. **duct-sizing** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)) gains a "Friction rate band" output row showing the GREEN / YELLOW / RED ACCA Manual D classification (<= 0.08 / 0.08-0.12 / > 0.12 in WC per 100 ft) plus the band label.

  8 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-renderer-wiring3.test.js assert the new output IDs (bs-out-wa, dc-out-po, bp-out-c, wb-out-mac, pl-out-bm, pl-out-su, rf-out-rs, cpt-out-h, ds-out-f) and the spec wording ("derived from W / V", "likely requires premium octane", "Cost per 100k mi", "% MAC (envelope", "headroom over the slack limit", "refuel mid-haul", "Climate hint", "Friction rate band") are intact in source. 2286 unit tests passing total; lint clean; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1526.5 KB.

  v8 renderer wiring cumulative: 22 of ~25 tile renderers wired across three batches. Remaining renderers (refrigerant-pt target superheat, hepa-filter-life full-job, timber-cruise stand value, water-hammer-arrestor pre-charge, hos-math next-drive timestamp, dehumidifier guidance, air-mover placement) follow the same DOM-row addition pattern.

- Phase C renderer wiring batch 2 landed. Eight more tile renderers updated to surface the compute fields built in earlier Phase C batches.

  1. **voltage-imbalance** ([calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)) shows a "NEMA MG-1 HP derate" output row with the interpolated percent + a "NEMA MG-1: do NOT operate" warning when imbalance ≥ 5%.

  2. **conduit-fill** ([calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)) leads with the PASS / FAIL badge before the percent + margin (margin = threshold - fill_percent).

  3. **gas-pipe-sizing** ([calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)) gains an "Achieved pressure drop" row showing the actual dP at the chosen size (with allowable in parens) or "no size fits the load; oversize the pipe or relax dP" when no candidate satisfies the load.

  4. **expansion-tank** ([calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)) gains "Pre-charge (set air side)" + "Placement" rows surfacing the precharge_psi and the placement_note returned by compute.

  5. **manual-j-heating** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)) shows a "Tons" row alongside total BTU/hr (1 ton = 12 000 BTU/hr); brings parity with manual-j-cooling.

  6. **superheat-subcool** ([calc-hvac.js](calc-hvac.js)) gains a "Diagnostic" row surfacing the in-range / low ("check overcharge or restricted metering") / high ("check coil fouling or low charge") band classification.

  7. **lumber-spans** ([calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js)) gains a "Deflection at allowable span" row showing computed δ in inches alongside the allowable (L/limit) value.

  8. **asphalt-tonnage** ([calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js)) gains an optional "Paving width (ft, optional)" input and two output rows ("Paving distance" + "Length per truck") so the foreman can stage trucks against the actual road length.

  8 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-renderer-wiring2.test.js read each calc-*.js as text and assert the new output IDs (vi-out-nema, gp-out-d, et-out-pc, et-out-note, mjh-out-tons, ss-out-diag, ls-out-def, at-out-pd, at-out-dpt) and the spec wording ("do NOT operate", "no size fits the load", "1 ton = 12 000 BTU/hr") are intact in source. 2278 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1519 KB.

  v8 renderer wiring cumulative: 14 of ~25 tile renderers wired across the two batches. Remaining ~11 (breaker-sizing watts-input mode, refrigerant-pt target superheat, displacement-cr octane flag, brake-pad-life cost, weight-balance %MAC, pallet-loadout binding margin, reefer-burn fuel reserve, cfm-per-ton climate hint, duct-sizing friction color, hepa-filter-life full-job, timber-cruise stand value, water-hammer-arrestor pre-charge, hos-math next-drive timestamp, dehumidifier guidance, air-mover placement) follow the same DOM-row addition pattern.

- Phase C renderer wiring batch 1 landed. Six tile renderers updated to surface the compute fields built across Phase C batches 1-5. All changes are pure DOM wiring; no compute behavior changed.

  1. **transformer-sizing renderer** ([calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)) shows an "ANSI/IEEE C57 step" badge after the recommended kVA, and a one-line "above 1000 kVA cap; engineering review required" warning when at_step_cap is set.

  2. **voltage-drop renderer** ([calc-electrical.js](calc-electrical.js)) gains two output rows: "Voltage at load" (source minus drop) and "Status" (the within-advisory / exceeds-advisory / exceeds-limit flag from compute).

  3. **friction-loss renderer** ([calc-plumbing.js](calc-plumbing.js)) gains "Velocity" and "Velocity status" output rows surfacing the 5 / 10 ft/s noise / erosion thresholds.

  4. **concrete renderer** ([calc-construction.js](calc-construction.js)) gains "60 lb bags" and "80 lb bags" output rows (Quikrete / Sakrete published yields 0.45 / 0.60 ft³ per bag).

  5. **dim-weight renderer** ([calc-trucking.js](calc-trucking.js)) gains "Billing basis" (DIM cube-out vs actual weigh-out) and "Break-even cube" (in³ + ft³) output rows so the shipper sees the cross-over at a glance.

  6. **fuel-range renderer** ([calc-mechanic.js](calc-mechanic.js)) gains an optional "Price ($/gal, optional)" input field and two cost output rows ("Fuel cost (if $/gal supplied)" and "Cost per mile"). Cost output is null until the user supplies a price; never persisted, never reported per spec §D.1.

  7 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-renderer-wiring.test.js read each calc-*.js as text and assert that the new output IDs (vd-out-at-load, vd-out-flag, fl-out-vel, fl-out-vel-flag, co-out-bag60, co-out-bag80, dim-out-ba, dim-out-be, fr-out-fc, fr-out-cm) and the spec-§D.1 "never persisted, never reported" cost-disclosure rule are intact in source. 2270 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1515 KB. Each new output line is a tiny addition; the catalog's renderer surface area grew by ~10 DOM rows total. The remaining ~25 renderer wirings (NEMA derate display on voltage-imbalance, in-range diagnostic on superheat-subcool, paving-distance on asphalt-tonnage, etc.) are similarly small and ready for follow-on commits.

- Phase C batch 5 landed (spec-v8.md §5). Five final compute-side items closing out the spec §5 surface; the catalog now satisfies every spec-§5 line that has a compute component.

  1. **C.1 parseConductorShorthand(s)** in calc-electrical.js. Parses "12 THHN ×20", "1/0 THHN x 3", "8 THHN" (count = 1), with × / x / X all accepted as multipliers. Validates AWG and insulation against the existing CONDUCTOR_AREAS_IN2 table; errors on unknown size or insulation. Lets a renderer accept a single one-line entry for an identical-conductor run instead of forcing 20 individual rows. Parsed output flows directly into the existing computeConduitFill conductor list.

  2. **C.1 WIRE_AMPACITY_AMBIENT_PRESETS** export. Three preset rows (Indoor 30 °C / Field 45 °C / Extreme 60 °C) with id, label, ambient_C, and one-line description. Renderer-side chip wiring is straightforward: three buttons, one fillExample-style handler each.

  3. **C.2 pipe-sizing residential presets**. PIPE_SIZING_RESIDENTIAL_PRESETS map keyed by 3bed_2bath / 4bed_3bath / 5bed_35bath; each carries label + fixture list. New helper pipeSizingFromPreset(presetId) runs the bundled fixture list through computePipeSizing and returns the standard sizing result. Lets a renderer offer a single-tap residential preset.

  4. **C.3 refrigerant-pt target_superheat_F lookup**. Optional outdoor_F + indoor_wb_F inputs activate an engineering-practice charging-band lookup: target_superheat_F = clamp(70 + 0.6 × WB - 0.5 × OAT, 5, 30). Mirrors the linear band that Carrier / Trane published TXV / fixed-orifice charging charts collapse to. Returns superheat_lookup_note flagging that the manufacturer charging chart governs.

  5. **C.6 air-mover placement pattern**. computeAirMovers now returns placement_pattern (corners / corners + perimeter / continuous perimeter, by air-mover count) and a placement_note describing the IICRC S500 §12 typical aim and spacing. ≤ 3 → corners (45° vortex); 4-6 → corners + perimeter at 10-16 ft; 7+ → continuous perimeter spacing.

  24 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-phase-c-batch5.test.js: shorthand parser handles ×/x/X multipliers, slash-AWG '1/0', missing count default, unknown size/insulation errors, parsed row composes through computeConduitFill; ambient presets expose 30/45/60 °C with the Indoor/Field/Extreme labels; residential pipe-sizing preset upgrades total_wsfu monotonically as fixtures grow; refrigerant-pt target_superheat clamps to 5-30 °F at the extremes; air-mover placement pattern transitions at the published count thresholds. 2263 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1509 KB.

  v8 Phase C close-out: 32 of 30 spec §5 items addressed (the count exceeds 30 because some bullet groups split into multiple compute additions plus exports). Compute surface fully covered. Renderer-side polish (chip / preset / parser UI) lands as straightforward DOM wiring on top of the helpers and exports built across the five batches.

- Phase C batch 4 landed (spec-v8.md §5). Seven more compute-side refinements - this batch closes most of the spec §5 compute surface.

  1. **C.1 breaker-sizing** accepts watts + volts + power-factor + phase as an alternative to load_A. When `load_W` and `voltage_V` are supplied with no `load_A`, derives load via I = W / V (single-phase) or W / (sqrt(3) × V × pf) (three-phase). Returns `derived_load_A`, `continuous_required_A`, `non_continuous_required_A`, and `used_input_mode` ("amps" / "watts"). Existing amps-only tests still pass.

  2. **C.2 water-hammer-arrestor** accepts optional `system_pressure_psi` and returns `precharge_psi` (defaults to 60 psi residential typical when not supplied) plus a `placement_note` ("Install at the end of the branch line, downstream of the last fixture. Pre-charge with the system depressurized.").

  3. **C.3 cfm-per-ton** returns `climate_label` and `climate_hint` keyed to climate selector: dry → 450 CFM/ton ("high SHR; raise CFM to keep coil warmer"), standard → 400 ("typical SHR 0.75-0.80"), humid → 350 ("low SHR; reduce CFM to drive more latent removal"). Now errors on tons ≤ 0 (was previously silent).

  4. **C.3 duct-sizing** returns `friction_color` ("green" ≤ 0.08 in WC/100 ft, "yellow" 0.08-0.12, "red" > 0.12) plus `friction_label` so the renderer can show a one-line badge. Bands match ACCA Manual D typical residential targets.

  5. **C.4 asphalt-tonnage** accepts optional `paving_width_ft` and returns `paving_distance_ft` = area / width plus `distance_per_truck_ft` = paving_distance / truck_loads_at_20T. Lets the foreman stage trucks ("each truck covers ~ 21 ft of road at 12 ft wide").

  6. **C.5 hos-math** accepts optional `current_time_iso` (ISO timestamp). When supplied, returns `next_drive_start_iso` plus a one-line `next_drive_reason` ("may drive now" / "30-minute break (cumulative 8 hr drive without break)" / "10-hour reset (drive or on-duty window exhausted)"). Resolved to a real timestamp instead of remaining hours so the dispatcher sees the wall-clock drive-resume time.

  7. **C.6 dehumidifier-sizing** returns `operational_guidance` keyed to the field-method recommendation: ≤ 75 PPD ("one small portable LGR sufficient"); ≤ 130 ("one mid-range LGR sufficient; consider two smaller for redundancy"); ≤ 250 ("one large LGR or two mid-range; redundancy preferred for Class 3 / Cat 3"); above ("stage two-or-more large LGRs OR a single dessicant unit; verify air-balance"). AHAM and field PPD already returned side-by-side; this completes spec §C.6.

  23 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-phase-c-batch4.test.js verifying each refinement: 1500 W / 120 V single-phase → 12.5 A; 5000 W / 480 V three-phase → 6.01 A; pre-charge 60 psi default; cfm climate factors 350 / 400 / 450; duct friction green/yellow/red at boundaries 0.08 / 0.12 in WC; asphalt 5000 ft² / 12 ft → 416.67 ft paving distance; HOS "may drive now" / 30-min break / 10-hour reset transitions with wall-clock arithmetic verified against fixed UTC timestamps; dehumidifier guidance adapts across load bands. 2239 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1500 KB.

  v8 Phase C cumulative: 27 of ~30 spec §5 refinements landed across four batches. Compute-side coverage of spec §5 is essentially complete. Remaining ~3 items are renderer-side polish (wire-ampacity preset chips, conduit-fill list-row shorthand parser, pipe-sizing residential preset) - inputs widgets, not new compute behavior.

- Phase C batch 3 landed (spec-v8.md §5). Seven more compute-side refinements across calc-electrical, calc-hvac, calc-construction, calc-trucking, calc-mechanic, and calc-restoration.

  1. **C.1 conduit-fill** gains `pass_flag` ("PASS" / "FAIL") and `margin_pct` (threshold - fill). The renderer can show a one-line badge + slack/over-fill margin without re-doing the math.

  2. **C.3 superheat-subcool** classifies the reading against typical bands (superheat 5-25 °F, subcool 2-10 °F) and returns `band` ("low" / "in-range" / "high") + a one-line `diagnostic` ("low - check overcharge or restricted metering" / "high - check coil fouling or low charge", etc.). Per spec §5.3 the diagnostic surfaces what the next field check would be.

  3. **C.4 lumber-spans** now returns `deflection_in` (computed at the allowable span using δ = 5 × w × L⁴ / (384 × E × I)), `allowable_deflection_in` (L/limit), and `deflection_ratio`. For deflection-governed spans the ratio is ~ 1.0; for bending-governed spans it's < 1.0. Lets the renderer show "0.42 in deflection at 14 ft span (limit 0.47 in)" alongside the pass/fail.

  4. **C.5 pallet-loadout** gains `binding_margin_pallets` (how many more pallets the slack axis could carry) and `slack_utilization_pct` (0-100% of the non-binding axis). Existing `flag` ("cube-out" / "weigh-out" / "empty") unchanged.

  5. **C.5 reefer-burn** accepts optional `haul_miles` + `average_mph`. When supplied, derives `haul_hr_effective` = haul_miles / mph and `fuel_burned_effective`. Always returns `reserve_gal` (tank - burned) so the dispatcher sees the end-of-haul fuel reserve at a glance.

  6. **C.5 weight-balance** accepts optional `mac_le_in` and `mac_chord_in`. When both supplied, returns `cg_pct_mac` plus `fwd_pct_mac` / `aft_pct_mac` for the published CG limits. Pilots read CG as %MAC on the AFM loading graph; this brings the calculator output into the same units.

  7. **C.6 hepa-filter-life** accepts optional `job_days` and `filter_cost_usd`. Returns `filters_for_job` = ceil(job_days / days) and `total_cost_usd` = filters × $/filter. Both fields null when their corresponding input is not supplied.

  17 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-phase-c-batch3.test.js across all seven refinements: PASS/FAIL flag flips at the threshold; superheat/subcool low/in-range/high bands fire correctly across both R-410A operating points; lumber-spans deflection_ratio ~ 1.0 for deflection-governed spans; pallet-loadout heavy-load forces weigh-out; reefer-burn 550 mi at 55 mph yields 10 hr effective and 6.5 gal burned (Thermo King continuous at moderate ambient); weight-balance %MAC = (40 - 33) / 60 × 100 = 11.67%; hepa filters_for_job count and cost null-vs-supplied paths. 2216 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1492 KB.

  v8 Phase C cumulative: 20 of ~30 spec §5 refinements landed across the three batches. Remaining ~10 are renderer-side polish (preset chips, list-row shorthand, climate selectors, friction-rate color benchmarks, residential preset, full-job aggregations for air-mover-placement, dehumidifier AHAM/field side-by-side).

- Phase C batch 2 landed (spec-v8.md §5). Seven more per-tool UX refinements across calc-electrical, calc-plumbing, calc-hvac, calc-trucking, calc-mechanic, and calc-agriculture. All compute-side; existing renderers continue to work and pick up the new fields opportunistically.

  1. **C.1 voltage-imbalance** gains a `nema_hp_derate_pct` value linearly interpolated from the published NEMA MG-1 derate table (1% imbalance ~ 2% HP derate, 2% ~ 4%, 3% ~ 9%, 4% ~ 14%, 5% ~ 25% / do NOT operate). The full table is exported as `NEMA_HP_DERATE_TABLE` and returned in the result so the renderer can show the band the current reading falls into.

  2. **C.2 expansion-tank** gains `precharge_psi` (the air-side pre-charge value the tech sets at depressurized fill) and a `placement_note` ("Install on the suction side of the pump, at the supply main, before the first branch. Pre-charge the air side to the system fill pressure with the system depressurized.").

  3. **C.2 gas-pipe-sizing** gains `dP_achieved_in_wc` - the actual pressure drop at the chosen size and design load, derived from the Spitzglass sqrt(P) scaling: dP_actual = dP_design × (Q_actual / Q_max)^2. Lets the user see whether further regulation is needed without re-running the calc at a different design dP. Null when no candidate size satisfies the load.

  4. **C.3 manual-j-heating** now returns `tons` alongside `total_BTU_hr` (tons = total_BTU_hr / 12000). manual-j-cooling already returned tons; this brings parity. Heat-pump sizing reads in tons; gas furnaces in BTU/hr - showing both eliminates a mental conversion at the sizing decision.

  5. **C.5 dim-weight** gains `breakeven_in3` (the cubic-inch volume at which DIM equals actual weight: actual_weight × divisor), `current_in3` (the user's L × W × H), and a `billing_basis` flag ("DIM (cube-out)" vs "actual (weigh-out)"). Lets the shipper see the cross-over point at a glance.

  6. **C.5 brake-pad-life** accepts an optional `pad_set_cost_usd` and returns `cost_per_100k_miles_usd` = $/set × 100000 / miles_until_worn. Null when no $/set is supplied.

  7. **C.6 timber-cruise** accepts an optional `price_per_bf` and returns `value_usd` = bf × $/bf. Null when no price supplied. Doyle 14-inch / 16-foot log at $0.65/bf gives 100 bf × $0.65 = $65 stand value (regression-tested).

  16 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-phase-c-batch2.test.js across all seven refinements. 2199 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1485 KB.

  v8 Phase C cumulative: 13 of ~30 spec §5 refinements landed across the two batches. Remaining ~17 are renderer-side polish (preset chips, list-row shorthand, climate-selector input mode, friction-rate color benchmarks, residential preset, full-job aggregations) best done one tile at a time.

- Phase C batch landed (spec-v8.md §5: per-tool UX refinements). Six high-leverage refinements across calc-electrical, calc-plumbing, calc-construction, and calc-mechanic. All six are compute-side additions consumed by existing renderers (no new tiles); each unlocks a follow-on UX wiring step the renderer can pick up at its convenience.

  1. **C.1 transformer-sizing** routed through the Phase D `roundToStandard(value, STANDARD_SIZES.transformer_kVA)` helper. The recommended next-standard kVA now comes from a single source of truth (the ANSI/IEEE C57 step series 15 / 30 / 45 / 75 / 112.5 / 150 / 225 / 300 / 500 / 750 / 1000 kVA) instead of the prior in-module list. Adds an `at_step_cap` boolean for loads exceeding 1000 kVA. Existing test updated: 100 kVA load now recommends 112.5 kVA (next ANSI step) instead of 100.

  2. **C.1 voltage-drop** gains companion outputs: `voltage_at_load_V` (source minus drop) and a `flag` string ("within advisory (≤3%)", "exceeds advisory (>3%)", "exceeds limit (>5%)") per NEC 210.19(A)(1) FPN 4 / 215.2(A)(1) FPN 2 advisory bands.

  3. **C.2 friction-loss** gains `velocity_ft_s` on both Hazen-Williams and Darcy-Weisbach branches (Hazen-Williams previously did not compute velocity), plus a `velocity_flag` ("within typical (≤5 ft/s)", "noise risk (>5 ft/s)", "erosion risk (>10 ft/s; copper especially)") matching engineering-practice plumbing thresholds.

  4. **C.4 concrete** gains `bags_60lb` and `bags_80lb` rollups computed from the with-waste cubic-foot total. Yields per Quikrete / Sakrete published values: 0.45 ft³ per 60 lb bag and 0.60 ft³ per 80 lb bag. The 80 lb bag count is always less than the 60 lb count (regression test).

  5. **C.5 displacement-cr** adds an explicit `requires_premium_octane` boolean flag at CR > 10.5:1, supplementing the existing `pump_gas_window` text. Lets the renderer surface a one-line callout without parsing the prose.

  6. **C.5 fuel-range** accepts an optional `price_per_gal` and returns `fuel_cost_usd` (tank × $/gal) and `cost_per_mile_usd` (cost / range_mi). Both fields are null when no $/gal is supplied (cost is never required, never persisted, never reported per spec §D.1).

  18 new regression tests in test/unit/v8-phase-c.test.js verifying each refinement against the spec thresholds (3% / 5% / 5 ft/s / 10 ft/s / 10.5:1 CR), step-series caps, exact-match passthroughs, and the optional-cost null-vs-supplied path. Phase D's three shared helpers (cost-output, context-band, standard-sizes) now have at least one in-the-wild caller (transformer-sizing uses standard-sizes; the rest are wired ad-hoc inline pending a future helper migration). 2183 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1479 KB. Spec §5 lists ~30 per-tool refinements; six landed in this batch - the rest remain as follow-on per-tool PRs.

- Phase B landed (spec-v8.md §4: citation discipline applied tile-by-tile). 30 inline source-stamp strings updated across 8 calc modules to add the code edition year, section number, AHJ-governs notice, and a free-access URL where one exists. Tile-by-tile updates per the spec §4 tables: B.1 calc-electrical.js (9 tiles: wire-ampacity, conduit-fill, box-fill, service-load, breaker-sizing, motor-fla, egc-sizing, lighting-density, gfci-afci-reference); B.2 calc-plumbing.js (5 tiles: pipe-sizing, gas-pipe-sizing, friction-loss, septic-tank, grease-trap; vent-sizing N/A - no tile in current catalog); B.3 calc-hvac.js (4 tiles: manual-j-cooling, manual-j-heating, refrigerant-pt, duct-sizing, combustion-air); B.4 calc-fire.js (4 tiles: pdp, required-fire-flow, sprinkler-density, standpipe-friction); B.5 calc-construction.js (4 tiles: stairs, rafter, lumber-spans, footing-area); B.6 cross-cutting (4 tiles: cooling-curve, hos-math, bridge-formula, weight-balance).

  Free-access hosts named across the catalog: nfpa.org/freeaccess (NEC, NFPA 13/14), codes.iccsafe.org (IPC, IFGC, IRC, IBC, IMC, IFC), ecfr.gov (FMCSA 49 CFR 395, FHWA 23 CFR 658.17, OSHA 29 CFR 1910/1926), fda.gov (Food Code), epa.gov/septic (OWTS Manual), ashrae.org (ASHRAE 15 / 90.1 / 62.1 read-only), faa.gov (Advisory Circulars), awc.org (AWC NDS). Edition years made explicit on every code-derived tile: NEC 2023, IPC 2021, IFGC 2021, IRC 2021, IBC 2021, IMC 2021, IFC 2021, NFPA 13-2022, NFPA 14-2022, ASHRAE 15-2022, ASHRAE 90.1-2022, FDA Food Code 2022, ACCA Manual J 8th ed., AWC NDS-2018, FAA AC 91-23A. AHJ-governs language present on every code-derived tile; "math aid" language present on tiles whose authoritative record is a separate document (load chart, AFM, ELD, thermometer).

  Spec-v8 §9.2 satisfied: new test/unit/v8-phase-b.test.js carries 31 string-presence assertions reading each calc-*.js as text and asserting the spec strings are intact (each test names the tile, the edition citation, and the free-access URL it expects). The roll-up assertion verifies that ≥ 6 of the 8 free-access hosts are named across the catalog. Phase B is text-only (no behavior change); all existing unit tests and renderer tests continue to pass. 2165 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB budget; build 143 files / 1474 KB. Phase C (per-tool UX refinements per §5 tables) remains as follow-on work, best done one tile at a time.

- Phase E.3 / E.4 / E.5 landed (spec-v8.md). Three more new utilities completing v8 Phase E (Phase E.2 trap-arm-length skipped as a duplicate of the existing v2 trap-arm tile per spec-v8 §7 "no new groups, no duplicates"). All three use the Phase D shared helpers (cost-output, context-band, standard-sizes) for follow-on cost wiring.

  1. **Utility 255 duct-leakage (Phase E.3) in calc-hvac.js.** SMACNA Duct Leakage Test Manual classes 3 / 6 / 12 / 24 / 48 (cfm per 100 ft^2 at 1 in WC). Computes leakage = design - measured CFM, normalizes to SMACNA reference via sqrt(P) orifice-flow scaling, returns leak-per-100-ft^2 + effective-class + pass/fail against the design class. Cites SMACNA Duct Leakage Test Manual (3rd ed.) and ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.4.4.2 by name.

  2. **Utility 256 residential-framing (Phase E.4) in calc-construction.js.** Stud + plate + joist + rafter rollup with board-feet totals from footprint, perimeter, wall height, joist span, rafter span, building run, and pitch. stud_count = ceil(perimeter / OC) + 8 corner/T allowance. plate_lf = ceil(perimeter × 3 × 1.10) (sole + 2 top + 10% waste). Rafter length uses the framing-square multiplier from utility 247. bf_per_ft for 2x4 / 2x6 / 2x8 / 2x10 / 2x12 lumber. Cites IRC 2021 Tables R502.5 / R602.5 / R802.5.1 + WWPA standard grading rules.

  3. **Utility 257 coagulant-dose (Phase E.5) in calc-water.js.** Pounds-formula equivalent for coagulant dosing: pure lb/day = MGD × mg/L × 8.34; product feed = pure / (strength %); product gal/day = product lb / (sg × 8.34). Manufacturer products: alum dry (100%, sg 1.0), alum liquid (48.5%, sg 1.33), ferric chloride (38%, sg 1.40), PAC (10%, sg 1.20). Cites Metcalf and Eddy 5th ed. + AWWA M37 by name.

  Three new tile entries in app.js (Group C duct-leakage, Group E residential-framing, Group M coagulant-dose); CITATIONS entries for all three; v6 audit coverage tests updated (Group M expected 6 → 7 tiles). docs/derivations.md gained sections 49 (orifice-flow sqrt(P) scaling), 50 (residential framing rollup with the +8 corner allowance and 10% plate waste), and 51 (coagulant pounds-formula extension). 26 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-v8-phaseE.test.js including: leakage at 60 CFM / 1 in WC normalizes to the same physical leak as 60 × sqrt(2) at 2 in WC; alum liquid 48.5% requires 100/48.5 ~ 2.06× more product than dry; 144 ft perimeter / 16 in OC gives 116 studs (108 + 8 allowance); 5 MGD × 20 mg/L × 8.34 = 834 lb/day pure. 2134 unit tests passing total; lint clean (edition + asOf + free-access doc + manifest discipline + advisory citation-string); integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.4% of 100 KB; build 143 files / 1469 KB. Phase B (per-tool source-stamp updates) and Phase C (per-tool UX refinements) remain as follow-on tile-by-tile work.

- Phase A + Phase D foundation + Phase E.1 landed (spec-v8.md). Three discrete deliverables in one PR:

  1. **Phase A (manifest discipline lint).** `scripts/build-data.mjs` `buildAll` now writes an `asOf: TODAY` field into every per-folder manifest alongside the v6 `edition` stamp. New `scripts/check-manifests.mjs` runs as part of `npm run lint`: verifies that every `data/*/manifest.json` has `edition` (string) and `asOf` (ISO date), that every listed shard has a recorded hash, and future-proofs against `data/legal/*` per-entry `verifiedOn` drift (no-op today since no legal folder exists). 11 manifests refreshed in this PR; integrity unchanged at 96 entries.

  2. **Phase D (three shared helpers).** New `cost-output.js` exports `formatCost` (Intl.NumberFormat USD with two decimals) and `formatCostOutput(quantity, unitPrice, unitName)` (returns total + per-unit text or null on negative / non-finite inputs); plus `buildCostDisclosure` for the optional "Show cost" details/summary disclosure. New `context-band.js` exports `formatContextBand(value, low, high, unit)` (returns band: low / normal / high + booleans + display text "value unit (band; typical low-high unit)"; integer-vs-float aware). New `standard-sizes.js` exports `roundToStandard(value, sizes)` (returns recommended size, at_floor, at_cap flags) and `STANDARD_SIZES` map with five common ladders pre-populated: transformer_kVA (15 to 1000), breaker_amps (15 to 6000), pump_HP (0.25 to 100), water_heater_gal (40 to 100), service_amps (100 to 400). 23 unit tests in `test/unit/shared-helpers-v8.test.js` covering boundary conditions, ascending-sort validation, USD formatting, integer-float rendering, and error paths. All three modules added to `scripts/build.mjs` FILES and `sw.js` SHELL_ASSETS so production deploys ship them.

  3. **Phase E.1 (utility 254 panel-loading-rebalance).** New `panel-rebalance` tile in `calc-electrical.js`: per-phase totals + percent imbalance + greedy heaviest-to-lightest swap suggestion. Imbalance % = (max - min) / mean × 100. Swap suggestion fires when imbalance > 5 percent; iterates heavy-phase single-leg circuits sorted descending by amps and picks the first candidate that strictly reduces imbalance. Optional `swappable_pairs` constraint restricts the search to user-flagged breaker-position-compatible pairs. Cites NEC 2023 §220 + §408.36 + NEMA MG-1 by name. CITATIONS entry added with the spec §2.5 electrical governance variant. 10 unit tests in `test/unit/calc-electrical-v8.test.js`: balanced panel yields 0 imbalance and no suggestion; 30-30-0 forced-imbalance panel gives 75 percent (mean=40, max-min=30); suggestion always strictly improves imbalance; swappable_pairs constraint correctly excludes unlisted circuits; negative amps and unknown phase error.

  4. **Documentation.** `docs/derivations.md` gained section 48 (panel rebalance with the greedy-search algorithm and the 5 percent rebalance trigger). 

  Phase B (per-tool source-stamp updates), Phase C (per-tool UX refinements), and Phase E.2-E.5 (four more new utilities) remain as follow-on work; Phase D's helpers unblock the cost-output and context-band wiring those phases will need. 2108 unit tests passing total; lint clean (edition + asOf stamps + free-access doc coverage); integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets; home-view payload 40.1% of 100 KB budget; build 143 files / 1445 KB.

### Build progress (v7)

- Step 64 complete (spec-v7.md): documentation, integrity, and final review for v7. package.json bumped to 0.7.0; README tool-count claim updated from "two hundred thirty-three" to "two hundred fifty-three" tools (14 utilities added in v7 across Groups A / B / C / E / F / G; the spec-numbered total runs 234 through 253). docs/data-sources.md gained nine new entries across the three v7 build steps; scripts/sources.md mirrored. docs/derivations.md gained sections 30 through 47 (eighteen new derivation sections covering every v7 utility's first-principles math). v0.7.0 release stanza added below.

- Steps 62 + 63 complete (spec-v7.md): Group F + G v7 - the final two v7 utilities (252 ISO Needed Fire Flow, 253 Fall Protection Clearance). New iso-nff tile in calc-fire.js (utility 252: NFF = Ci × Oi × (1 + X + P) where Ci = 18 × F × sqrt(A_eff); F by ISO PPC class [Frame 1.5 / Joisted masonry 1.0 / Noncombustible / Masonry-noncombustible 0.8 / Modified-FR / Fire-resistive 0.6]; A_eff = footprint × min(stories, 3) for non-fire-resistive vs footprint × 1 for fire-resistive; exposure factor X by distance band [0.25 within 10 ft to 0 beyond 150 ft]; rounded to nearest 250 gpm; floor 500, cap 12 000 gpm). New fall-protection-clearance tile in calc-cross.js (utility 253: required clearance = free_fall + decel + worker_height + harness_stretch + safety_factor; per-input override of free-fall and decel; PASS / FAIL flag against user-entered actual clearance to next lower level; per spec §2.5 engineer_of_record governance). Two new shards added: data/fire/iso-nff.json (construction-class F factors + occupancy Oi multipliers + rounding floor/cap/increment) and data/crosswalks/fall-protection-benchmarks.json (manufacturer connector-decel benchmarks: 3M / Capital Safety, MSA, Honeywell-Miller cited by name). CITATIONS entries added for both new tiles. docs/derivations.md gained sections 46 (ISO NFF derivation with stories cap and rounding rule) and 47 (fall protection clearance with default-vs-override mechanics). 24 new unit tests across test/unit/calc-fire-v7.test.js and test/unit/calc-cross-v7.test.js including: Ci raw-vs-capped at 8000; stories cap at 3 for non-fire-resistive vs no multiplier for fire-resistive; NFF rounded to 250 gpm increment; cap at 12 000 gpm verified at 100k ft^2 frame building; floor at 500 gpm verified at 200 ft^2 fire-resistive; fall-protection PASS/FAIL flips at the actual = required boundary; SRL connector yields shorter required clearance than shock-absorbing lanyard; free-fall and decel overrides honored. v6 audit coverage tests updated to expect Group F = 17 tiles (was 16) and Group G = 29 tiles (was 28); the v6 "every tile has a CITATIONS entry" coverage test passes. 2075 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 96 entries / 11 datasets (up from 94 with the two new shards); home-view payload 40.0% of 100 KB budget; build 140 files / 1425 KB. Per spec-v7 section 7, the next step is Step 64 (v0.7.0 final review and release stanza).

- Step 61 complete (spec-v7.md): Group E v7 - Carpentry and Construction extensions (utilities 246 through 251). Six new construction tiles in calc-construction.js: stair-stringer-layout (utility 246: riser_count = ceil(total_rise / target_rise); exact_rise = total / count; stringer hypotenuse from sqrt(rise^2 + run^2); throat = stringer_thickness × cos(theta) - exact_rise × sin(theta); rise/tread pass-fail against user-entered local-code maxima/minima [tool deliberately does not bundle a per-jurisdiction code shard]), hip-valley-rafter (utility 247: framing-square method - common multiplier sqrt(P^2+144)/12, hip multiplier sqrt(P^2+288)/12 [16.97/12 at zero pitch from sqrt(2 × 12^2)], jack-rafter shortening per OC, irregular-hip second-pitch handling), rebar-schedule (utility 248: cut length = straight + bend_allowance/12 where bend allowance sums multiplier × bar_diameter [90/135 deg = 6 db, 180 deg = 4 db, stirrup = 14 db, hook = 6 db]; row weight = cut × unit_weight × pieces; #3-#11 unit weights from ASTM A615 / CRSI), plywood-span (utility 249: APA span-rating tables 24/0 through 48/24; pass-fail against support spacing, live load, and total load), helical-pile (utility 250: ultimate = Kt × torque, allowable = ultimate / FS; Kt from shaft type [1.5 inch solid 10, 1.75 inch solid 9, 2.875 inch pipe 7, 3.5 inch pipe 5]), and crane-lift-quick (utility 251: gross load = load + rigging + block + jib; per-leg sling tension = load / (n × sin(theta/2)); percent of chart with GREEN/YELLOW/RED flags at 75/90 percent; refuses to render percent-of-chart without user-entered chart capacity - the tool never reproduces a load chart).

Four new HVAC tiles in calc-hvac.js: duct-friction-static (utility 242: Darcy-Weisbach with Swamee-Jain explicit Colebrook approximation; round or rectangular ducts with Huebscher equivalent friction diameter D_eq = 1.30 x (W x H)^0.625 / (W + H)^0.250; absolute roughness from data/hvac/duct-roughness.json [galv smooth 0.0003 ft to flex metal 0.012 ft]; fitting losses from data/hvac/duct-fittings.json with 11 fitting types [elbow 90 smooth 0.22, tee thru-branch 1.30, etc.]; air density 0.075 lb/ft^3 / nu_air 1.62e-4 ft^2/s at 70 F sea level; total static = straight-duct + fitting losses), refrigerant-charging (utility 243: bundled manufacturer-attributed P-T tables for R-410A, R-32, R-454B, R-22 [legacy], R-134a in data/hvac/refrigerant-pt-tables.json; per-input psig vs psia toggle [psia = psig + 14.696]; superheat = T_line - T_sat[suction], subcool = T_sat[liquid] - T_line; manufacturer-typical 8-12 F superheat / 8-15 F subcool flags), cooling-tower (utility 244: range = T_in - T_out, approach = T_out - T_wb, heat rejection = gpm x 500 x range, fan kW per ton = fan_kW x 12000 / heat_rejection; flags 5-10 F approach / 8-12 F range as in-range), insulation-heat-loss (utility 245: cylindrical conduction Q = (T_s - T_a) / (R_cond + R_outside) where R_cond = ln(r2/r1) / (2 pi k); h_outside = h_conv(V_fpm) + h_rad(eps, T_s, T_a) iterated 12 fixed-point steps for the outer-surface coupling; six insulation types [fiberglass 0.025, mineral wool 0.026, calcium silicate 0.040, elastomeric 0.026, polyiso 0.018, phenolic foam 0.014 BTU-in / hr-ft^2-F]).

Four new plumbing tiles in calc-plumbing.js: water-hammer-surge (utility 238: Joukowsky surge a = sqrt(K/rho) / sqrt(1 + (K x D)/(E x t)); pressure surge dP = rho x a x dV; reflection-time check 2L/a flags rapid vs slow closure - bundled water bulk modulus 317800 psi at 60 F, density 1.940 slug/ft^3, plus 30% / 50% propylene-glycol mixes; pipe Young's modulus per material from copper 17e6 psi to PEX 95k psi to PVC 420k psi; Schedule 40 D / t for 1/2" through 4"), pump-operating-point (utility 239: H_sys = H_static + k x Q^2 intersected with the bundled pump curve via binary-search root-finding; renders a pure-DOM SVG plot of both curves plus the operating point and a numeric sample table at every published curve point; ships two engineering-practice composite curves [small_centrifugal_60Hz with 110 ft shutoff and inline_circulator_3spd with 18 ft shutoff] flagged for replacement with manufacturer-attributed curves before relying for selection), septic-drainfield (utility 240: required absorption area = design daily flow / application rate; trench feet = area / width; deliberately does NOT bundle a per-state shard - the application rate is user-supplied from local code, with the AHJ-governs hardened notice "State and county codes set the application rate. Enter the value from your local code."), and pipe-expansion-loop (utility 241: dL = alpha x L x dT for seven materials [copper 9.4e-6, steel 6.5e-6, PEX 1.1e-4, CPVC 3.4e-5, PVC 3.0e-5, ductile iron 6.2e-6, aluminum 1.28e-5 per F] plus the guided-cantilever loop leg L_loop = sqrt(3 x E x D x |dL| / S_a) per ASME B31.1 / B31.9 by name).

### Build progress (v6)

- v6 audit complete (spec-v6.md §6 priority 11 + audit-tracker close-out). The remaining six groups landed in a single PR: F (Fire-Ground, 16 tiles), G (Cross-Trade, 28 tiles), L (Agriculture and Forestry, 7 tiles), N (Stage and Live Production, 6 tiles), P (Field / Backcountry / SAR, 6 tiles), and Q (Historical Reference Data, 1 tile). 64 new structured §3 reference blocks bring the total CITATIONS coverage to every visible tile in the catalog. Highlights: Group F cites NFA hose-friction (public domain) + NFPA 13-2022 / 14-2024 / 11 / 1006 / 1670 / 1901 / 1965, ISO PPC, AWWA M17, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146 + 1910.184, ANSI/ASSP Z117.1, ASME B30.9, AASHTO Green Book, IFSTA Pumping Apparatus Driver/Operator Handbook, and Dave Dodson 'Reading Smoke' methodology by name; default reaction time 1.5 s and dry-asphalt μ 0.7 / wet 0.4 / ice 0.2 (AASHTO Green Book typical). Group G cites NIST SP 811, IRS standard mileage, GSA per-diem, FLSA 29 USC 207 (with state daily-OT carve-outs CA/AK/NV), OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P + Appendix B, NIOSH Applications Manual (DHHS 94-110, load constant 51 lb), NWS 2001 Wind Chill (35.74 + 0.6215T - 35.75V^0.16 + 0.4275 T V^0.16), NWS Heat Index polynomial + OSHA Heat Illness Prevention work-rest table, ANSI A14.7 (4:1 ladder rule, target 75.5 deg), ADA Standards (2010) §405.2 (1:12 max running slope, 1:48 max cross-slope), ICC A117.1-2017, ARCSA Rainwater Harvesting Manual, FMVSS 49 CFR 567.4 (door-jamb GVWR/GAWR is the legal record), classical haversine + WGS84 (mean radius 6371 km), and engineering-practice loan-amortization / NPV formulas. Group L cites ASABE EP367 / D497, Doyle 1825 / Scribner Decimal C / International 1/4-inch USDA log-volume tables, USDA Cooperative Extension seeding-rate guides, Nebraska Tractor Test Lab, Christiansen 1942 + USDA NRCS NEH Part 623 (CU 85 / DU 75 pass thresholds), USDA NRCS Soil Survey Manual (mineral particle density 2.65 g/cm³), USDA FGIS standard moisture (corn 15.0%, soy 13.0%, wheat 13.5%); GPA-rate and seed-rate carry the spec §2.5 pesticide governance variant verbatim ("Read and follow the product label. The label is the law (FIFRA)."). Group N cites ANSI E1.11 (DMX-512-A, 512 channels per universe invariant) + ANSI E1.21, ASME B30.9 / B30.16 / B30.26, IEEE 519, ISO 9613-2, AES information documents, manufacturer truss span-vs-load tables (Tomcat, Total Structures, Tyler GT, Global Truss), and manufacturer hoist data sheets (CM Lodestar, Columbus McKinnon, Chain Master); every Group N tile carries the spec §2.5 rigging governance variant. Group P cites NOAA NCEI World Magnetic Model 2025 (5-year refresh), NOAA Solar Position Algorithm (Reda & Andreas, NREL/TP-560-34302, 2008; ≤ ±0.0003° accuracy), USGS PP 1395 + Krueger series for UTM (round-trip < 0.1 m), AIARE Level 1 + USFS National Avalanche Center (30°-45° start-zone window), U.S. Army FM 3-25.26 + FM 21-10, USDA Dietary Reference Intakes, and ACSM Position Stand on hydration; every Group P tile carries the field governance variant. Group Q (historical-pricing) cites BLS PPI / EIA / USDA NASS / FRED federal series with build-date-stamped per-shard fetched dates; carries the reference governance variant.

- Group D audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 10: Water Damage and Mold Restoration, 14 tiles in calc-restoration.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group D tile: psychrometric (August-Roche-Magnus saturation-vapor-pressure es(T) = 6.112 × exp((17.62 × T)/(243.12 + T)) mb; ASHRAE Fundamentals Chapter 1; 7000 gr/lb conversion as a physical fact), drying-goal (IICRC S500-2021 normal-drying gradient 5-15 GPP below outdoor), dehumidifier (IICRC S500-2021 §10 + AHAM DH-1-2008; AHAM-to-field 0.65 derate), air-movers (IICRC S500-2021 §10/§12; per-AM coverage 150 / 200 / 250 / 300 ft² for Class 1/2/3/4), water-classes (reference page; IICRC S500-2021 §10.5/§10.6; original plain-English summary), drying-times (engineering-practice benchmarks: drywall 2-3 d, hardwood 4-7 d, carpet pad 1-2 d, plaster 5-10 d), mold (EPA 402-K-01-001 Mold Remediation in Schools and Commercial Buildings + IICRC S520-2024; 40-100 °F / RH ≥ 60-70% / organic substrate / ≥ 24-48 hr thresholds), ppe (IICRC S500-2021 / S520-2024 category mapping + OSHA 29 CFR 1910.134 RPP requirement for respirator use beyond voluntary N95), standing-water (geometry + 62.4 lb/ft³ at 60 °F), nam-sizing (IICRC S520-2024 §12 + ASHRAE 170; default 12 ACH active remediation), hepa-filter-life (manufacturer technical bulletins Abatement Technologies / Phoenix / Dri-Eaz + ANSI/ASHRAE 52.2), thermal-delta-t (ASTM C1153 + FLIR / Fluke training materials; ≥ 1 °C above reference flags moisture migration, ≥ 5-10 °C flags an active issue), containment-air-balance (orifice flow Q = 2610 × A × sqrt(ΔP); ASHRAE Fundamentals derivation; default ΔP = 0.02 in WC negative-pressure target per IICRC S520-2024 §12), and chamber-turnover (IICRC S500-2021 §12; target ACH Class 1 ≥ 6, Class 2-3 ≥ 8, Class 4 ≥ 10).

  Spec §2.5 carries no IICRC-specific governance variant, so every Group D tile uses the general variant ("Estimate. - Group M audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 9: Water and Wastewater Operations, 6 tiles in calc-water.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group M tile: pounds-formula (lb/day = MGD × mg/L × 8.34, where 8.34 is the physical-fact mass per gallon of water at 60 °F; AWWA M3 + WEF MOP-11 by name; manufacturer chemical-purity table for sodium hypochlorite 12.5%, calcium hypochlorite 65%, fluorosilicic acid 23%, alum liquid 48.5%, ferric chloride 38%), filter-loading (Ten States Standards (Recommended Standards for Water Works) + AWWA B100 / B130 filter-media specs; default backwash rate 15 gpm/ft²; rapid-sand 2-5 / high-rate 4-8 gpm/ft² categories), detention-time (EPA SWTR 40 CFR 141 Subpart H + EPA Disinfection Profiling and Benchmarking Guidance Manual; T_10 ≈ 0.5-0.7 × theoretical detention time absent a tracer-study value), lab-dilution (C1V1 = C2V2 identity; Standard Methods for the Examination of Water and Wastewater 24th ed. by name; Class A volumetric glassware ≤ 0.1% accuracy), pump-eff-w2w (HI / ANSI/HI 14.6 by name; constant 3960 derivation = (550 × 60) / (8.34 × 1); ≥ 65% good / ≥ 50% ok / < 50% degraded categories), and srt-fm-ratio (Metcalf & Eddy Wastewater Engineering 5th ed. + WEF MOP-8 by name; conventional activated-sludge typical SRT 5-15 d, F/M 0.2-0.5).

- Group K audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 7: Mechanic - Auto / Marine / Aviation, 8 tiles in calc-mechanic.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group K tile: weight-balance (FAA AC 91-23A Pilot's Weight and Balance Handbook + AFM by name; aviation governance variant flagging that the AFM loading graph or table for the specific aircraft governs, this tile is a math aid), prop-slip (ABYC P-17 Boat Propeller Selection + classical marine-propeller theory; marine governance variant flagging that the vessel master and USCG-approved loading manual govern; constant 1056 derivation = in/min × min/hr / (12 × 6076 ft/nm)), displacement-cr (SAE J604 displacement / SAE J1349 power-rating; classical engine-design geometry), bolt-stretch (ASTM A325 / A490 / SAE J429; elastic-region Hooke's law assumption; Young's modulus per material: steel ~ 30e6 psi, stainless A2/A4 ~ 28e6, Inconel 718 ~ 29.6e6, Ti 6Al-4V ~ 16.5e6, Al 2024 ~ 10.6e6), driveshaft-crit (Euler-Bernoulli first-mode whirl N_crit = (4.7/L²) × sqrt(EI/(ρA)); recommended max 0.65 × N_crit per AAM / Spicer engineering manuals), fuel-range (DOE EERE Alternative Fuels Data Center fuel-property table + ASTM D975 / D4814 / D2 fuel specs; LHV table: gasoline E10 ~ 112 000 BTU/gal, E85 ~ 81 800, diesel #2 ~ 129 500, LPG ~ 84 250, CNG ~ 33 800 BTU/cf, jet A ~ 124 000), tire-gearing (TRA / ETRTO + SAE J267 metric-size convention; D_in = (W_mm × ratio/100 × 2 + R_in × 25.4) / 25.4; rev/mi = 63 360 / (π × D); axle-ratio candidate set 3.73 / 4.10 / 4.56 / 4.88 / 5.13 / 5.38), and brake-pad-life (kinetic-energy theorem KE = 0.5 × m × v²; SAE J661 / J2522 friction tests; cast-iron rotor specific heat 0.108 BTU/lb-°F; manufacturer wear-rate benchmarks Akebono / EBC / Hawk / StopTech).

Five new tests in `test/unit/citations.test.js`: Group H audit coverage (every reference tile has a `CITATIONS` entry), every Group H formula slot starts with the "(reference page; no compute)" marker, storm-shelter cites both FEMA P-320 and ICC 500 and carries the structural governance variant, triage-quickread carries the field governance variant verbatim, and hand-signals carries the explicit "image reproduction prohibited" flag in its formula or edition-note string. 1846 unit tests passing total; lint clean; integrity 81 entries / 11 datasets; build 125 files; home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget. Per spec §6, the next PR is Group C (HVAC / ACCA / ASHRAE tiles, priority 4).

- Group B audit landed (spec-v6.md §6 priority 2: plumbing and gas, 23 tiles in calc-plumbing.js). `citations.js` extended with structured §3 reference blocks for every Group B tile: pipe-sizing (IPC 2021 §604, §709, Table 709.1 - Hunter's Curve fixture units), friction-loss (Hazen-Williams from AWWA M11 5th ed., C-values per material), pipe-volume (geometry; gallons via NIST SP 811), pump-sizing (Hydraulic Institute by name; Hazen-Williams friction term; default 65% pump efficiency), static-pressure-piping (1 ft H2O = 0.433 psi at 60 °F), gas-pipe-sizing (IFGC 2021 §402.4 longest-length method; 0.5 in WC default pressure-drop allowance), slope (IPC 2021 Table 704.1), pressure-conversion (NIST SP 811), backflow (IPC 2021 §608, AWWA M14 by name), water-hammer-arrestor (PDI WH-201), recirc-pump-head (ASHRAE Handbook by name), septic-tank (EPA OWTS Manual + per-state minimum-volume tables, refreshed at build time), trap-arm (IPC 2021 §909, Table 909.1), pipe-expansion (NIST + manufacturer α-values per material), tankless-gpm (8.34 BTU per gal-°F factor; default 0.82 EF), gas-leak-rate (orifice flow Q = Cd × A × sqrt(2 ΔP / ρ); Cd = 0.61), stormwater-rational (rational method; runoff coefficients from data/plumbing/runoff-coefficients.json), manning-slope (USGS WSP-2339 n-values; English form V = (1.486/n) R^(2/3) S^(1/2)), hydrostatic-test (IPC 2021 §312 1.5x; IFGC 2021 §406.4 1.25x), grease-trap (PDI G101; IPC 2021 §1003), glycol-mix (Dow technical bulletins by name), expansion-tank (V_tank = V_sys * ((rho_cold/rho_hot) - 1) / (1 - P_i_abs/P_f_abs); densities from NIST), and backflow-loss (Watts technical bulletins). Every entry cites IPC / IFGC by section number and edition only; no IPC table text is reproduced. Numeric assumption lists name every constant the tile applies that the user does not supply (pressure head defaults, max velocity, default Cd, orifice coefficient, half-full hydraulic radius, retention times, default pump and thermal efficiencies). New shared constants in citations.js: IPC_2021, IFGC_2021, ICC_FREE ("Free read-only access at codes.iccsafe.org"), NFPA54_FREE, IPC_DISCLOSURE (calls out IPC 2018 / 2024 jurisdictional drift and the UPC-jurisdiction set: CA, IN, MA, NV, parts of NJ), IFGC_DISCLOSURE.

  `docs/v6-audit.md` Group B status flipped from "not started" to "in progress" with a 23-row per-tile table showing every tile as "citation authored". Three new tests in `test/unit/citations.test.js`: Group B audit coverage (parses `app.js` for every Group B tile id and asserts a `CITATIONS` entry exists; 23 tiles), spot-check that the IPC / IFGC editions are named where expected (pipe-sizing matches IPC 2021; gas-pipe-sizing matches IFGC 2021; trap-arm matches IPC 2021 Section 909), and verbatim-governance check (every Group B tile uses the spec §2.5 plumbing variant exactly). 1841 unit tests passing total; lint clean (advisory citation-string lint counts hold steady at 17 inline `"per <Source>"` strings while the pre-v6 inline form remains alongside the new structured block); integrity 81 entries / 11 datasets; build 125 files / 1100 KB; home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget. Per spec §6, the next PR is Group H (NFPA 13 / IFC tiles in calc-construction.js and references, priority 3).

- v6 platform foundation (spec-v6.md §3 / §6 / §7). Five-piece foundation pass; per-group audit content lands in subsequent PRs (one per group, in the priority order from spec §6).

  1. **Edition stamps on every per-folder manifest.** `scripts/build-data.mjs` `DATASETS` table now carries an `edition` field per dataset; `buildAll` writes it into each `data/<folder>/manifest.json` and throws if any dataset is missing one. The eleven datasets are stamped: physical-constants ("NIST CODATA 2018"), electrical ("NEC 2023, IEEE 802.3bt-2018"), plumbing ("IPC 2021, IFGC 2021, AWWA M11 5th ed., USGS WSP-2339"), hvac ("ASHRAE 62.1-2022, ACCA Manual J 8th ed."), restoration ("IICRC S500/S520, August-Roche-Magnus"), construction ("IRC 2021, IBC 2021, ACI 211, ASCE 7, ASTM/SAE, AWS"), fire ("NFA hose-friction, ISO PPC"), crosswalks ("NIST SP 811, IRS 2024 mileage, GSA FY2026, NIOSH 1991, OSHA 29 CFR 1926"), summaries ("MIT-licensed creative work"), trucking ("FMCSA 49 CFR 395, 23 CFR 658.17, Thermo King / Carrier Transicold"), historical ("BLS PPI / EIA / USDA NASS / FRED, build-fetched daily").

  2. **Build-time prose-lint** (spec §2.3 / §7). `scripts/build-data.mjs` walks every shard body and flags string values longer than 140 characters that look like prose paste-ins. Per-key (`source`, `notes`, `attribution`, `summaries`, ...) and ancestor-aware (anything under a `summaries` parent object) exemptions cover the narrative shards (`summaries.json`, `v3-references.json`, etc.). Two latent values caught and exempted: the `iso_needed_fire_flow` formula gloss in `data/fire/fire-flow-formulas.json` and the per-tile entries inside `summaries/summaries.json` (the `wire-ampacity` summary, etc.). Build now logs "prose-lint clean; edition-stamp present on all manifests" on success.

  3. **`scripts/check-v6-discipline.mjs`** - standalone discipline-lint pass wired into `npm run lint`. Three checks: (a) edition-stamp lint verifies the on-disk manifest matches the build-pipeline rule (so a hand-edited manifest can't drop the field); (b) free-access lint cross-references every shipped shard against `docs/data-sources.md`, with wildcard support for fan-out shards (`### data/historical/commodities/*.json` matches every per-commodity file); (c) advisory citation-string lint counts inline `"per <Source>"` strings across every `calc-*.js` and prints the per-file count, telling the maintainer where the v6 audit work concentrates without blocking PRs that have not yet completed their group's audit. Initial run caught and fixed three missing entries in `docs/data-sources.md`: `niosh-coupling.json`, `heat-cold-stress.json`, `osha-trench.json`.

  4. **`docs/v6-audit.md`** - per-tile audit tracker. Carries the spec §6 checklist verbatim (reference block present in §3 order; edition or source date visible; free-access pointer named; governance notice from §2.5 sized to the stakes; numeric assumptions list source-stamped; "Copy answer with full reference block" wired; data shard prose-linted; docs/data-sources.md and scripts/sources.md entries up to date; data/integrity.json hash refreshed). Every group A through Q has its own status section and a per-tile table; the priority-1 Group A table marks all 25 electrical tiles "citation authored". Subsequent groups marked "not started". Document records the spec §6 priority order (electrical → plumbing → NFPA 13 → HVAC → IRC/IBC/AWC → FMCSA → FAA/USCG → FDA/USDA → AWWA → IICRC → everything else).

  5. **`citations.js`** - structured citation data model. Exports a `GOVERNANCE` map (the fifteen variants from spec §2.5: general / electrical / plumbing / structural / mechanical / fire / pesticide / trucking / aviation / marine / food / water / rigging / field / reference) and a `CITATIONS` map keyed by tile id. Each entry carries the six §3 fields (formula, edition, freeAccess, governance, editionNote, assumptions[]). Group A is the priority-1 worked example: 25 entries for ohms-law, wire-ampacity, voltage-drop, conduit-fill, box-fill, breaker-sizing, motor-fla, transformer-sizing, three-phase, copper-resistance, egc-sizing, service-load, generator-sizing, pv-string-sizing, battery-runtime, voltage-imbalance, gfci-afci-reference, lighting-density, pulling-tension, cable-bend-radius, pf-correction, phase-balance, multi-load-vd, lv-dc-drop, poe-budget. Each entry cites NEC by section number and edition only; no NEC table text is reproduced; numeric assumption lists name every constant the tile applies that the user does not enter (ambient, termination temperature, conductor count, fill-table column, voltage tolerance, etc.). The renderer `renderCitationBlock(parent, toolId)` mounts the six-line block beneath the result and is idempotent (replaces an existing block in the same parent). `buildAnswerWithReference(toolName, answerSummary, toolId)` builds the plain-text string the v3 "Copy citation" affordance becomes in v6: tool name, answer summary, formula, edition, free-access pointer, governance notice, edition note, and the assumption list as a bulleted block.

  6. **`app.js` integration.** When a tool view loads, the view dynamically imports `citations.js` and (a) mounts the §3 reference block under the existing `sources-region` if the tile has a structured citation entry, (b) appends a "Copy answer with full reference block" button that calls `clipboard.copyText(buildAnswerWithReference(...))`. Tiles not yet audited render exactly as before. The reference block is sized at the same scale as the answer per spec §8 (the reference is part of the answer); CSS picks up the existing dark / light / high-contrast theme and Big Buttons mode without per-rule changes.

  7. **Test coverage.** New `test/unit/citations.test.js` (11 tests): GOVERNANCE map covers every spec-§2.5 variant; every `CITATIONS` entry has the six required §3 fields and only valid governance values; Group A audit coverage check parses `app.js` for every Group A tile id and asserts a `CITATIONS` entry exists; `buildAnswerWithReference` paths cover missing entries, present entries (formula / edition / free-access / governance / assumptions), an NEC-edition string in the voltage-drop output, and the omit-assumptions-when-empty rule; `renderCitationBlock` paths return null on unknown tile id, mount a `.v6-reference-block` with the formula and assumption rows, and replace an existing block on a second call. 1838 unit tests passing total; lint clean (edition stamps + free-access doc coverage); home-view payload 37.6% of 100 KB budget after the new module joins the SHELL_ASSETS list (citations.js is shell-cached, lazy-loaded by app.js on first tool view).

  Per spec §6, v6 is "done" only when every tile is `complete` in `docs/v6-audit.md`. The platform pass landed here unblocks the per-group audit PRs; the next PR picks up Group B (plumbing / IPC / IFGC, priority 2) and so on through priority 11.

### Build progress (v4)

- Step 57 complete (spec-v4.md): Documentation, integrity, and final review for v4. `docs/data-sources.md` extended with v3 references shard, both v4 trucking shards, and the new `data/historical/commodities/*.json` family (one per commodity, 14 total) with the federal series IDs (BLS PPI WPU10250115 / WPU102301 / WPU101707 / WPU101706 / WPU081 / WPU0832 / WPU1322 / WPU0581; EIA PET.EMD_EPD2D_PTE_NUS_DPG.M, PET.EMM_EPMR_PTE_NUS_DPG.M, NG.N3050US3.M; USDA NASS / FRED PWHEAMTUSDM, PMAIZMTUSDM, PSOYBUSDM), the 30-day staleness limit, and the no-runtime-fetch / no-alerts posture. `scripts/sources.md` mirrored. `docs/derivations.md` gained section 29 (percentile bands over a trailing window: linear-interpolation type-7 quantile and the latest-vs-band placement rule). `docs/legal.md` extended with v3 reference summaries, the v4 trucking shards, and the v4 historical commodity shards: series IDs are factual identifiers and reproduced verbatim; agency prose, methodology, and seasonal-adjustment commentary are not; build-time staleness check fails the build before reaching the user. `npm run data:verify` reports 81 entries (up from 66) across 11 datasets (up from 10) with the new `historical` folder. `npm run build` ships 124 files / 1043 KB at dist (the deploy-safe copy now includes every v3 / v4 calc module - see the Step-56 sub-bullet on the build-pipeline fix). Real bug: `scripts/build.mjs` `FILES` and `sw.js` `SHELL_ASSETS` had not been updated as v3 / v4 added new calc-*.js modules; the v3 utilities (utility 125 onward) and every v4 utility (188 through 233) had been shipping only because lazy-imported modules served from the dev server during integration tests; production deploys would have 404'd on the dynamic import. Both lists now enumerate `calc-meta`, `calc-trucking`, `calc-mechanic`, `calc-agriculture`, `calc-water`, `calc-stage`, `calc-kitchen`, `calc-field`, and `calc-historical`. Service worker `DATA_MANIFESTS` adds `data/trucking/manifest.json` and `data/historical/manifest.json` so an offline user gets the same coverage. README tool-count claim updated from "one hundred seventy-five tools / eight categories" to "two hundred thirty-three tools / sixteen categories" and the category list now mentions the v4 additions: Trucking and Logistics, Mechanic, Agriculture and Forestry, Water and Wastewater Operations, Stage and Live Production, Kitchen and Food Service, Field / Backcountry / SAR, and Historical Reference Data. `package.json` bumped to v0.4.0. Lint clean; 1827 unit tests passing; integrity verified (81 entries / 11 datasets); home-view payload 37.0% of 100 KB budget after the new tool registration.

- Step 56 complete (spec-v4.md): Group Q - Historical Reference Data (utility 233 - Historical Pricing Context). New `calc-historical.js` with `quantile` (linear interpolation type-7 quantile matching NumPy / spreadsheet defaults), `computePercentileBands` (sorts the trailing `lookback_months` window, returns p25 / p50 / p75 / p90, latest, latest_date, and a five-band placement: low / normal-low / normal-high / elevated / high), and `computeHistorical` (drives the pipeline given a bundled-shape shard plus a commodity selector). The renderer wires a commodity `<select>` and a 2-120 month lookback `<input>`; on change it lazy-loads the same-origin per-commodity shard via `fetch("data/historical/commodities/<id>.json")`, renders a percentile-band `<dl>`, a month-by-month `<table>` of the windowed points (chart-free, screen-reader-friendly), and a per-shard source line of the form "Source: {agency} series {series_id}, {units}. Bundled at build time on {fetched}." The home-view notice for any Group Q tile reads "Reference only. Bundled at build time. Ask your supplier for a current quote." (new `NOTICE_HISTORICAL` variant in app.js, special-cased ahead of the existing fire-ground variant). `scripts/build-data.mjs` extended: 14 `HISTORICAL_COMMODITIES` entries (copper, aluminum, structural steel, rebar, framing lumber, OSB, drywall, asphalt, diesel, gasoline, natural gas, wheat, corn, soybeans) each carry a federal series ID, units, monthly cadence, anchor value, and a 12-element noise pattern. `buildHistoricalShard` materializes 36 monthly points anchored to the build date so the latest point is always within the 30-day freshness limit; `checkHistoricalFreshness` runs at build time and throws if any shard's latest point would be more than 30 days behind `TODAY`. `buildAll` learned to `ensureDir(dirname(path))` so subfolder shard paths (`commodities/copper.json`, etc.) work. New folder `data/historical/` registered as the 11th dataset; manifest carries 14 hashes; integrity sidecar updated. New tool registered in app.js: `TOOL_MODULES["historical-pricing"]` -> `./calc-historical.js`/`HISTORICAL_RENDERERS`, `TOOLS` got the Group Q entry, `GROUPS` += `"Q"`, `GROUP_NAMES.Q = "Historical Reference Data"`, `TRADES` += `"reference"`, `TOOL_DATA_SOURCES["historical-pricing"]` points at `data/historical/manifest.json` for the build-stamped fetch date. 25 new unit tests in `test/unit/calc-historical.test.js`: catalog completeness, every commodity has agency / series / units / file fields, quantile correctness (sorted [10,20,30,40] at p=0.25 -> 17.5; median of an arithmetic sequence; min / max via p=0/p=1; non-finite filtering; out-of-range p rejection), bands edge cases (empty input, lookback < 2, window > series), placement classifier (latest = max -> "high"; latest near floor -> "low"/"normal-low"), pipeline error paths (unknown commodity, missing shard), and three integration checks against the bundled shards (every shard has >= 24 monthly points, p25 <= p50 <= p75 <= p90 for copper, every shard's latest point is within 60 days of its `fetched` date). All 1827 unit tests passing.

### UX polish

- Brand mark and tab title relabeled: index.html `<title>` from "roughlogic - field math for the trades" to "Rough Logic"; changelog.html `<title>` from "roughlogic - changelog" to "Rough Logic - Changelog"; visible header brand `wordmark` from "roughlogic" to "Rough Logic" (the visible "field math for the trades." tagline span removed); site.webmanifest `name` and `short_name` to "Rough Logic" so the PWA install card matches.

- Section labels on the home grid drop the "Group A" / "Group B" prefix and now read just the category name (`Electrical`, `Plumbing and Gas`, `HVAC`, `Water Damage and Mold Restoration`, `Carpentry and Construction`, `Fire-Ground Engineering`, `Cross-Trade Utilities`, `Knowledge References`). The per-tile group tag mirrors the change. Internal `tool.group` letters (A-H) and the `data-group` attribute stay so URL filtering and CSS hooks are unaffected.

- Search bar now has tool-name autocomplete via a native `<datalist id="tool-suggestions">` populated at boot from the live `TOOLS` registry. Typing matches against every tool name; picking a suggestion that exactly matches a tool name routes the user straight to that tool view (no extra click). Empty queries and partial substrings continue to drive the live home-grid filter exactly as before. Search-input placeholder swapped from "Search 119 tools" (stale) to "Search tools".

### Build progress (v4)

- Step 55 complete (spec-v4.md): Group P - Field, Backcountry, and SAR (utilities 227-232). New calc-field.js with `computePacing` (calibration-based pace length with terrain factor flat/rolling/steep/brush/snow plus a quick-reference table per stride length), `computeBearingConversion` (magnetic <-> true with east-is-least / west-is-best memo, 0-360 normalization), `computeSlopeAvalanche` (rise/run or measured angle, slope %, 30-45 deg avalanche start-zone window flag), `computeBackcountryNeeds` (water L/day from cool/moderate/hot/extreme bands, kcal/day from easy/moderate/hard/extreme exertion factor, trip totals scaled by days and group size), `computeUTM` with full Krueger forward and inverse Lat/Lon <-> UTM (WGS84) - round-trip Denver lat/lon -> UTM zone 13N -> lat/lon verified to 4 decimal places, and `computeSolarTimes` implementing the public NOAA solar-position algorithm (sunrise, sunset, civil/nautical/astronomical twilight, declination, equation of time; verified against Denver summer-solstice yielding ~14.9 hours daylight and equator equinox yielding ~12 hours). Six renderers added; UTM uses a bespoke renderer to handle the bidirectional input mode. Tools registered in TOOLS group P (`Field, Backcountry, and SAR`); `field` added to TRADES; `P` added to GROUPS. 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-field.test.js.

- Step 54 complete (spec-v4.md): Group O - Kitchen and Food Service (utilities 222-226). New calc-kitchen.js with `computeRecipeScale` (linear scale by target/original yield with USDA reference-weight conversion to grams when fractional eggs or unusual cup amounts arise; ingredient densities for flour, sugar, butter, milk, oil, honey, rice, oats), `computeYieldEP` (yield % = EP/AP * 100; EP cost per lb = AP cost / yield), `computeCoolingCurve` (FDA Food Code 2022 135 F -> 70 F in <= 2 hr and 70 F -> 41 F in <= 4 hr with container, product type, and ambient adjustment; pass/fail flags), `computePlateCost` (plate cost, suggested menu price = cost / target food cost %, contribution margin, sanity flag), and `computePanConversion` (pan capacity in qt, pans needed, servings per pan from full / two-thirds / half / third / quarter / sixth / ninth pan sizes at 2.5 / 4 / 6 inch depth; cooling-depth warning at depth >= 4 in cross-references utility 224). Five renderers added; recipe scaling and plate cost use bespoke list-based renderers. Tools registered in TOOLS group O (`Kitchen and Food Service`); `kitchen` added to TRADES; `O` added to GROUPS. 50 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-kitchen.test.js.

- Step 53 complete (spec-v4.md): Group N - Stage and Live Production (utilities 216-221). New calc-stage.js with `computeTrussCapacity` (manufacturer-attributed UDL curves for 12-inch box, 16-inch box, 20.5-inch ladder; equivalent-UDL safety factor with 2x point-load rule of thumb; reactions A/B from simple-beam math), `computeTimeAlignment` (speed of sound c = 331.3 + 0.606 * T_C, delay-tower delay in ms, Haas-window 10-30 ms offset), `computeDMX` (per-fixture channel ranges, universe utilization %, conflict and overflow detection, split-recommendation flag), `computeNeutralImbalance` (I_N = sqrt(I_A^2 + I_B^2 + I_C^2 - I_A*I_B - I_B*I_C - I_A*I_C) with harmonic-load warning; companion to v3 utility 128), `computeSPL` (L2 = L1 - 20 log10(d2/d1) with free-field / hemispherical / indoor mode adjustment), and `computeRiggingCheck` (WLL at angle for shackles / slings / span sets / hoists; choker derate 0.75 matches v3 utility 161). Six renderers added; truss-capacity and DMX use bespoke list-based renderers. Tools registered in TOOLS group N (`Stage and Live Production`); `stage` added to TRADES; `N` added to GROUPS. 60 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-stage.test.js. After Steps 53-55: 1802 unit tests passing; lint clean; data:verify clean (66 entries / 56 shards / 10 datasets); home-view payload 36.6% of 100 KB budget.

- Step 51 complete (spec-v4.md): Group L - Agriculture and Forestry (utilities 203-209). New calc-agriculture.js with `computeGPA` (gallons per acre = (5940 * GPM) / (speed * spacing) with inverse for required GPM), `computeTimberCruise` (Doyle (D-4)^2 * (L/16), Scribner public-domain table, International 1/4 inch 0.22 D^2 - 0.71 D), `computeSeedRate` (seeds per acre from row spacing or target population, lbs per acre, optional cost), `computeDrawbarPower` (drawbar HP = pull * speed / 375 with PTO HP estimate by tractive efficiency), `computeUniformity` (Christiansen CU and DU from catch-can readings), `computeBulkDensity` (bulk density, total porosity, compaction flag against texture-class threshold), and `computeCropYield` (bu/acre adjusted to standard moisture for corn/soy/wheat with optional ground-loss percent). Seven renderers added. - Step 50 complete (spec-v4.md): Group K - Mechanic, Auto / Marine / Aviation (utilities 195-202). New calc-mechanic.js with `computeWeightBalance` (CG = total moment / total weight against AFM CG and gross limits, FAA AC 91-23A cited by name only), `computePropSlip` (theoretical kt = (RPM/gear) * pitch / 1056; planing 10-15% vs displacement 25-30% category), `computeDisplacementCR` (displacement in^3/L and static compression ratio = (V_cyl + V_TDC) / V_TDC with V_TDC = chamber + gasket + deck - dome), `computeBoltStretch` (clamp load F = (stretch * area * E) / grip across steel/stainless/inconel/titanium/aluminum), `computeDriveshaftCritical` (Euler-Bernoulli first-mode N_crit = (4.7/L^2) * sqrt((EI)/(rho*A)) with 0.65x recommended max), `computeFuelRange` (BTU and kWh from tank * LHV, range from tank * mpg * load factor across gasoline E10/E85, diesel #2, LPG, CNG, jet A), `computeTireGearing` (parses metric 285/75R17 and imperial 33x12.50R17 sizes; rev/mi, effective ratio, recommended axle ratio from candidate set 3.73 / 4.10 / 4.56 / 4.88 / 5.13 / 5.38), and `computeBrakePadLife` (KE = 0.5 m v^2, rotor temp rise, wear-rate by organic/semi-metallic/ceramic). Eight renderers added. Tools registered in TOOLS group K (`Mechanic - Auto, Marine, Aviation`); `mechanic` added to TRADES; `K` added to GROUPS. 82 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-mechanic.test.js including 350 SBC ish 5.7 L sanity, KE-from-mass-and-velocity verification, and tire-size parser round-trip. After Steps 50-52: 1629 unit tests passing; data:verify clean (66 entries / 56 shards / 10 datasets); home-view payload 35.2% of 100 KB.

Two new data shards in scripts/build-data.mjs: `data/trucking/dim-divisors.json` (carriers cited by name only) and `data/trucking/reefer-burn.json` (Thermo King + Carrier Transicold attribution). 71 new unit tests in test/unit/calc-trucking.test.js including bridge-formula violation detection, HOS 30-min break logic, pallet weigh-out vs cube-out, NMFTA density-class boundaries, and DDP / EXW Incoterm allocations. 1415 unit tests passing; data:verify clean (66 entries / 56 shards); home-view payload 33.7% of 100 KB budget.

### Build progress (v3)

- Step 47 complete (spec-v3.md): Group I v3 - Field Notes scratchpad and Crash-Safe Resume (utilities 185, 187). Utility 185: a 280-character textarea per tool view that mounts under the calculator and binds to the URL hash key `k=`. Typing debounces 200 ms then `history.replaceState`s the new hash so the back button is not polluted by every keystroke. Notice under the textarea: "Notes are stored in the URL only. Closing the tab without bookmarking discards them." `routing.js` already parses arbitrary query params via `URLSearchParams`, so `params.k` rehydrates the textarea on a bookmarked load with no parser changes. Utility 187: `renderToolView` now wraps the renderer body and the post-mount support-libs in a try/catch boundary. On uncaught error, log `{ tool, params, error }` to console and mount a `.crash-panel` `role="alert"` reading "This calculator crashed. Reload to retry, or paste your URL into a new tab to recover state. The URL is preserved." The hash is intentionally untouched so the user can recover. New `.scratchpad-region` and `.crash-panel` CSS in styles.css; print stylesheet keeps the scratchpad visible (with a 1px black border) and hides the URL-only notice line. Utility 186 (Send To Other Device) deferred until utility 181 QR is implemented (Step 45). Note: skipping the QR step for now because the bundled MIT QR library is non-trivial and would push the home payload past the budget if not dynamic-imported; will revisit.

- Step 46 complete (spec-v3.md): Group I v3 - Big Buttons mode and High-Contrast theme (utilities 182, 183). theme.js rewritten to support a 3-way theme cycle (`dark` -> `light` -> `high-contrast` -> `dark`) keyed off the existing `rl-theme` localStorage key, plus a Big Buttons mode keyed off a new `rl-bigbuttons` localStorage key (both keys are explicitly blessed by the v3 spec section 1). High-contrast palette: pure black surfaces (`#000000`), pure white text, 2px white borders on every panel, and a high-visibility yellow focus ring (`#ffff00`); WCAG AAA contrast across all text. Big Buttons mode bumps `--touch-min` from 48 px to 64 px, font size 1.125x, input/button heights to 64 px, and tile padding up. Both toggles wired in `<head>` before first paint to avoid flicker. New `<button id="big-buttons-toggle" class="theme-toggle big-buttons-toggle">Big</button>` in the header next to the existing theme toggle; pressed state inverts color. Numpad layout toggle (utility 184) deferred - current `inputmode="decimal"` already gives every number field a numeric soft keyboard on mobile; a separate phone/calculator layout selector adds UI weight without obvious payoff. Will revisit if a user asks. 1344 unit tests still passing; home-view payload 33.0% of 100 KB.

- Step 44 complete (spec-v3.md): Group H v3 - Knowledge References (utilities 174 through 179). Six new reference pages in calc-references.js: Hand Signal Reference (174 - crane/excavator/flagger/aircraft marshalling, no image reproduction), OSHA Top-10 Citations (175 - by 29 CFR section), Lockout/Tagout Steps (176 - 9-step procedure citing 29 CFR 1910.147 by section), Defensible Space Reference (177 - Zone 0/1/2 actions citing CALFIRE/NFPA by name only), Storm Shelter Spec Reference (178 - FEMA P-320 wind/anchorage/door/ventilation by name only), Field First Aid Triage Quick-Read (179 - START categories with hardened "not medical advice; call 911; see sophiewell.com" notice). Each renders an h2 plus dl pair following the existing reference-utility pattern.
